But I do eat lots of coconut oil.

### Andrea — 2014-01-27T20:01:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-107055>

I'm still amused by how well I tolerate the cold outside...
The temperature is below freezing and there's snow and ice on the ground. My new favorite winter shoes are [FiveFingers Lontra](http://www.vibramfivefingers.it/product_details.aspx?model=LONTRA). The last several years (or whole my life?) my feet have been lumps of ice most of the time and I've been wearing warm boots with thick and warm socks in the winter. Now, when I have been outdoors in the cold and take the FiveFingers off... my feet are surprisingly warm! Even the toes.

However, people look at me as if I were an alien...  Perhaps the fact that I walk between the buildings at work only wearing a t-shirt on my upper body contributes...
Wimps...!

Off to the shower.....

### Andrea — 2014-02-02T18:54:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-107935>

How ironic...

I visited some people I know this weekend. Within a few hours of arrival it felt like my face was on fire. The skin was red and dry. Actually, the skin got pretty dried out all over my body.
EMF? Well, they have 2 smartmeters in their house, a microwave oven they cook half their food in, an induction stove, wifi, iPhones and iPads all over the place, DECT phones, wireless transfer of TV signals all over the house, etc, etc, etc. You name it, they got it. We spent the evening in front of the TV...

I'm still not up to telling others too much about what I'm doing, so I didn't bring my ridiculously looking blue blocker glasses with me. Otherwise I've been using them everyday at home.

Dinner about 2 hours before bedtime, by the way.
Eventually, I went to bed. Fell asleep quickly and slept like a baby for over 8 hours. I haven't slept this well for months!

Sometimes, I just want to give up.
Could I have some funny mutation that makes me function better in a world full of EMF and blue light 24/7?

### sooperb — 2014-02-02T19:09:43+0200

### Andrea — 2014-02-13T20:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-110014>

I’m sorry to be whining again, but things do seem to get worse now. It started a couple weeks ago with CT becoming more and more unpleasant. Now, I find myself freezing most of the time, and especially at home in the evenings. Also, it feels like my brain has slowed down a little.

I’ve been thinking about it and I’ve realized that my appetite has decreased quite a bit lately.

It reminds me a lot about the time I had my low thyroid issues, before I got my medication. Back then, I didn’t have much appetite at all. I know that calories in themselves don’t matter, but to give you a picture... If I only ate after my appetite, I would eat about 600-700 kcal a normal day (and gain weight!)
Later on, when I was on T4 supplements and added T3 supplements as well, my appetite increased. I stopped feeling so bloody cold all the time, the brain fog disappeared, I had more energy and I even lost some weight.

So what’s happening now?

I’m not hungry in the morning, but I try to eat anyway. I eat more fat than proteins for breakfast since fat is easier for me to get down.
Yesterday, for instance, I ate 2 eggs and maybe 5 tablespoons coconut oil plus some kelp for breakfast. I wasn’t really hungry when I got home from work, but as I was feeling cold I decided to eat anyway. I had 100-150 g of shrimps, half an avocado and a tablespoon of mayo. I literally forced myself to eat all that, because if I hadn’t done that, I would have ended up wearing warm sweaters and socks and extra blankets all evening (been there, done that).

I measured my temperature when I woke up last night; it was at 36.0°C (96.8F). However, I wasn’t feeling cold at that time - on the contrary, actually. Before I started with the T4 and T3 meds, my night temperature used to be 35.5°C (95.9F) or sometimes less and I often woke up freezing and shivering, no matter how warm it was in my bedroom. With the help of the meds my night temp got up to 36.5°C (97.7F) and I stopped waking up freezing.

So what is going on? Why am I getting so much worse, when things started so well with both Leptin Rx and CT?

### Andrea — 2014-02-15T13:12:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-110396>

Oh, by the way, I've finally had a positive experience to write about again.

Since I started on my low carb/high fat diet a couple years ago, my periods became pretty regular, which they definitely haven't been all my life. The downside of it is that they are accompanied by awful, incapacitating cramps and pain the first 1-2 days. I rarely take painkillers, but for this I really have to.

Anyway, the other night I woke up at 1 am and this delightful pain was developing. I walked to the freezer and took out a couple freezer blocks, went back to bed and put them on my belly. Some time later I woke up again, pain free, rolled over on my stomach with the blocks under my belly and I didn't wake up until 5 am. Wow! I actually slept longer and better than I have for some time!

I woke up pain free and the pain hasn't returned, so I haven't had to take even one of my pills this time.

Maybe I should bring a frozen leg of lamb to bed after all? Hit myself in the head to fall asleep quickly, cuddle during the night - and have breakfast in bed?

### sooperb — 2014-02-15T13:21:16+0200

### Andrea — 2014-02-18T20:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-111042>

Ok... So I have met my endocrinologist, which was an... interesting experience...
Here are my latest labs, all from serum, number in () is the reference value:

Potassium: 4.3 mmol/L (3.6-4.6)
Sodium: 136 mmmol/L (137-145)
Creatinin: 73 µmol/L (45-90)
Bilirubin: 8.8 µmol/L (5-35)
ALP: 1.2 µkat/L (0.6-1.8)
ASAT: 0.40 µkat/L (0.25-0.60)
ALAT: 0.36 µkat/L (0.15-0.75)

T4: 47 nmol/L (66-181)
Free T4: 6.4 pmol/L (12-22)
T3: 0.91 nmol/L (1.3-3.1)
Free T3: 2.4 pmol/L (3.1-6.8)
TSH: 0.90 mIU/L (0.3-4.2)
Hb: 143 g/L (117-153)

HDL: 2.5 mmol/L (1.0-2.7)
LDL: 9.7 mmol/L (1.4-4.7) (this value is measured, not calculated)
Trigl.: 0.84 mmol/L (0.45-2.6)
ApoA1: 1.9 g/L
ApoB: 2.4 g/L
ApoB/ApoA1: 1.3

The only thing he could offer me was some different sort of test for T4 and T3 (he spoke about interference) - mainly to prove that I am in fact fine. He did react a little when I spoke of my crappy sleep, and offered to take a "morning cortisol" test from serum (I'm not sure what it's supposed to show, since I wake up at 1-2 am and do whatever I do, before I rush to the crowded tram to the hospital at 8 am). Any other test is irrelevant (!?).
He said some other shocking things and it's needless to say that I'm not going to see this so called doctor again. I'm not sure if I should do these last tests, maybe, but I will definitely not go see him personally.

Anyway, looking at these labs, even without other important labs, it's no wonder I'm not well.
The question is what do I do? I've already played around with my doses of synthetic T4 and T3, and doses I take now are probably as "optimal" as they can be. Taking more or less of either one makes me feel worse.

I know I have to move from my apartment in the city; I am looking for a house without too much civilization too close, but unfortunately, this doesn’t happen from one day to the next. I do what I can to reduce EMF at home. And, of course, I wear blue blockers after the sun has set.

I’m trying to force myself eat more; I eat shrimps or other sea food at least once a day. And I’m struggling on with CT. Did 10 minutes in the shower today, which is better than for a while.
I haven’t started weight lifting yet, and I don’t think I will. The last time I did HIT with weights with low T3 I developed some worrisome changes on my ECG and I also got weaker and weaker for every time. However, I go out and run some sprints a few times a week.

### Andrea — 2014-02-24T20:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-111960>

I might be getting back on track again... I hope, anyway...
I suppose I'm doing better at least partly because I feel that my endocrinologist is a bloody ?#¤% &@$£#! and I want to prove that he's wrong. I darn WILL get better!

The last week I've managed to eat more again. I've figured out that if I eat breakfast early enough after staring at my dawn simulator, I can get down more food than if I wait for a while. So BAB works fine again and I don't need to struggle too much with dinner either. Funny, the more I eat for breakfast, the more I can eat for dinner. And vice versa.

The past week I've done CT every day (taking a planned break today though), although I don't do very long sessions in the shower, just 10-15 minutes. It's not that it's so awful right now, but mainly it's... uhmm... boring...  But, I rather admit that I'm bored than tell myself that I freeze and start shivering because of my imagination.
And I went to have a look at the ocean this weekend. The water temperature was about 2.5°C (36.5F). I couldn't help taking my shoes off and dip my feet. My feet got a sort of numb within a few seconds, but it felt quite ok to keep standing with the cold water up to my ankles for a couple minutes. Back in the car, when I was taking my shoes on again, I was surprised that my feet were actually a lot warmer than my hands, that hadn't even touched the cold water.

But, yeah, I know, I'm a wuss...

I have been thinking about it. But I'm so confused. I got my [thyroid labs](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/page-2#post-111042) last week and my T4 and T3 (both free and total) are very low, so it feels counter intuitive to decrease my doses, which otherwise would be the most logic step.
My labs tell me that the meds aren't working for me. If I understand it correctly, if I have inflammation in my body, most of the T4 and T3 are transformed to rT3 - which would explain my low serum T3 levels, and also that I'm not feeling great. If this is the case, increasing my doses would probably help for a brief moment, but it would worsen my condition in the long run. But maybe I should decrease my doses anyway? Which one - T4 or T3? Or both at once?

Thanks! Grounding outside and barefeet is tricky, since the temperature is only around freezing. But, thinking about it, even though the FiveFingers aren't made to be grounding, they ought to be grounding anyway. Not as good as swimming in the ocean, but at least something.
Will have to find a conifer tree to hug... Do I need to get undressed when hugging it?
And thanks for reminding me, I need to get out for a walk on my lunch break. I don't eat anyway, so I can spend all my break walking outside.

I'm not sure leather soled shoes are so much better at grounding than my thin-soled-not-so-dry-FiveFingers.
And as I've been wearing FiveFingers only for quite some time now, I find normal shoes with thicker sole and/or toes together uncomfortable. Another option I have for grouding is using my work shoes (heavy sneakers type of shoes). At work we have sensitive equipment which could be damaged by static electricity, so when we're anywhere close we wear antistatic shoes, that is, grounding shoes. But again, I find this type of shoes really uncomfortable.

As for my non native EMF exposure, it's actually better than I feared. I've been measuring RF around my apartment and office, and the main culprits are my computer monitors. And my cell phones, which I try to keep far away from myself. There's wifi all around my apartment, there's a 3g tower close to my office, etc., but these don't seem to do much with the microwave spectrum near me. But, of course, there are other frequencies as well. I really am looking for a house far enough from civilization, so that I can at least somewhat control my environment when I'm not at work.

And yes, I'm on the 90 day optimal reset. Unfortunately, the webinars start at 2 am my time, so I can't participate and only listen to them afterwards. There's pretty much info there, but since English isn't my first language, I'd need to listen to them several times to understand all that's being said. Reading text in my own pace is much easier for me.

### Andrea — 2014-03-05T20:07:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-113628>

I moved from ordinary socks to toe sox and loved them but now I'm a no socks person, my feet are seldom cold and I only wear socks with my walking boots and I'm not sure why. I might try without at the weekend. Leather is fine for grounding but I suspect would wear out quite quickly.

Actually, I wear toe socks in my FiveFingers now that it's cold. But wearing socks inside shoes doesn't solve my problem. I've had low arches all my life, always being told to always wear shoes. Then, about 13 years ago, I badly injured one of my feet, extended and damaged the ligaments, and when I stood on it it only had a vaguely footlike shape... The doctors' verdict was to always wear soft but firm shoes and specially made insoles. Never ever walk barefoot. That's what I did for years. But then it hit me - do they really KNOW that's the right thing to do? And I started to try to walk barefoot (well, wearing socks) as much as I could. After a while I realized that I didn't feel any undefined pain from my hips, knees and feet, which I had taken for granted before. So I started wearing the FiveFingers and that's where I am now.
I had my feet scanned on a "foot scanning machine" recently, by the way, and my arches are now considered normal. And both my feet look quite alike, like normal feet.

So, socks or no socks - I want to be more or less barefoot.

### Andrea — 2014-03-11T20:07:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-114826>

All my adult life (at least), I've had quite some drinking problems...
But I've been working on it lately.

Most days I drank about 0.5 liters of water. Some days even less. Rarely felt thirsty.

Lately, I've upped my water intake and for the last couple weeks I've been able to get down slightly more than 3 liters each day. And I've been peeing like crazy!
They say that the tap water in my country is good. And I suppose it is far better than many other countries. I've looked at the water analysis report of my local tap water and it really doesn't look bad; all values are within the limits. Fluoride is less than 0.1 mg/l, pH around 8. The chloride level isn't very high either, even though the water does taste chloride, in my oppinion, anyway. Some metals, like aluminum, manganese, iron and copper have relatively high readings in some of the samples, but still below what's allowed or not far off, and on average they're far below the limits.

Anyway, I have been drinking only distilled water the last 3 days. Suddenly, I don't pee as much. It's difficult to compare...  but I would guess that I now pee less than half of what I did before. Not as frequently either.

Hmmm... Has my body got used to this amount of water all of a sudden, or does this change depend on a change in the water chemistry? I suppose I now should start drinking normal tap water again to see if I start peeing a lot again... Sigh.

### Andrea — 2014-03-24T18:43:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-117615>

I've done some water testing now.
A couple days with regular tap water: I drink less and pee more.
Then distilled water again: I drink much more and pee less frequently.

But is it the water or is it just my head that's playing with me?  I wish I could do a blind test, having someone give me water, without me knowing what kind of water it is...

Anyway, I visited my parents not far from the east coast this weekend. Dad and I went to the sea to have a walk on the beach. The water temperature is around 5°C (40F), but I couldn't help taking my FiveFingers off, rolling my pants up and walking with my feet in the water. How nice and relaxing that was! Wish I could do that more often.
When we got back to the car, dad wondered if my feet hadn't frozen to ice... He soon found out that my feet were much warmer than his hand!
Had a couple nice cold showers at their house too. The water temperature in their shower is 10°C (50F), but it doesn't really feel worse than my own shower water, which is 16°C. But their house, unfortunately, is an EMF hell.
It pains me to see how they live and how they eat, and I now see (partly, anyway) why they have the problems/illnesses they have. I'm afraid their future is not very bright and long, but there's not much I can do. Maybe if I manage to get well one day, they could be open for suggestions from me. But for the time being... My mom still thinks that gluten intolerance is an imaginary illness... Enough said.

### sooperb — 2014-03-26T19:46:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-118093>

We have a waterbed at present. It's about 13 years old and although I like it a lot, I feel it's past its best and we should change it. OH loves it, largely because it's heated, I have started turning the heater off before I get in it and I still get hot and have to throw off the covers. I'd like a new bed with a memory foam mattress, we have one in our weekend home and it's so comfortable. Funny thing though, I've tried lots of memory foam pillows and I can't make them work for me at all, mostly they are too thick, I only use one pillow and it has to be a soft feather. I hate broken nights and I've had plenty, mostly I tend not to be too tired when I finally get up but sometimes I just want to sit down and doze off. I try not to, I think it just sets me up for another less than optimal sleep.

### Andrea — 2014-03-26T20:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-118100>

Oh, pillows! That's a whole story by itself. I have several pillows: two different memory foam pillows, one thin soft down pillow and one thick and heavy down pillow. I use them all, periodically. All of a sudden the one I'm using becomes uncomfortable, so I start using one of the others. That one works fine for several weeks, but then I have to change again. For the time being I'm sleeping without a pillow...

Thinking about it, I don't think I have been tired in the morning upon waking up for a long long time, if ever. I have no idea how it feels to be tired in the morning. I've "always" been one of those obnoxious morning people, always waking up early and being most clear in my head in AM. At my previous job I used to enjoy getting to work at 6 am.
It's just that now I wake up at around 1 am - and I'm obnoxiously alert.

### sooperb — 2014-03-30T13:00:08+0300

I'm always concerned about customs and Vat being added on if you order above the £18 limit which is very easy to do lol. I know I order from a company called Health Monthly a lot, not a hint that they're not in the UK but all their orders are dispatched from Gibraltar. No postage over £40 but everything is despatched in a separate bag! I once ordered seven items, they all came separately! I baulk at the wastefulness of the bags and labels etc., but it must have something to do with avoiding duty.

### Andrea — 2014-04-02T19:03:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-119429>

I don't think iHerb.co.uk exists. But on iHerb.com you can pick which currency you like. Nevertheless, they are in the States...

I know about the duties and VAT. Here in Sweden, the customs normally don't bother to deal with shipments where the VAT is less than about 10-11 EUR. So far, when I have ordered from iHerb, the value has been at around $50 at most, and our customs have let each package through. Getting things from iHerb, even in several packages, is far cheaper than similar things from Sweden. Even the shipment cost itself is far cheaper than from any online shop in Sweden!

### Andrea — 2014-04-02T19:14:35+0300

I have invested in a home distiller which makes 3.5-4 liters per batch, so I run one batch per day, and possibly one extra in the weekends. Nice to have my own "water well".

### Andrea — 2014-04-03T21:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-119649>

Thanks for looking by!

I'm a bit confused. About light, circadian rhythm and all.
During the winter months the sun rises sometime in late AM hours, and sets at around 3-4 PM. But I have a normal full time job, which requires that I leave home soon after 6 AM and I get back home at around 5 PM. In some podcast or webinar with dr.K. I think I heard him say that the best thing is to get up at sunrise, stare into the sun and have a BAB within 30 minutes from that moment. And in the evening, after sunset, no blue light.
Sounds simple. But this is not very possible for me!

This winter, I have often been awake way too early, so I've been wearing my blue blockers until 5 AM, when it's a more decent time to wake up. At 5 AM I have removed the blue blockers and stared into my brightest lamp for a while. And taken my thyroid meds. Within 30 minutes I've had my BAB.
In the afternoon, I've put on the blue blockers after getting home.

Good or bad?

Now, the seasons are changing. I still get up before sunrise, but soon it will be the other way around. And now it's light outside when I go to bed; I don't need to turn the lights on at home. The only thing emitting blue light in my apartment is my computer screen, but most days I turn the computer off at around 7 PM.

So... Do I wear blue blockers in the evening all summer? Or only when there's artificial blue light around? Should I wear an eye patch while sleeping (since it's light much of the night)? And... Was my winter procedure bad?

I just don't get it...

### Inger — 2014-04-04T08:28:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-119726>

Ok Rerun, I will tell you how I do we live very close so we will have about the same light and dark hours..

In winter - I use only candles or red salt lamps until sun rises/it gets light. If I need to be in bright artificial light or computer then I use blue blockers until sun rises too. The same when it gets dark. If I have to work outside home... I do my best. That is... I put on the blueblockers as soon as I leave from work if it is dark outside, I use them in the train, when I drive.. etc. At work (waitress) I cannot use blueblockers..lol
Now I have arranged my life so that I work from home so I have lots of freedom, it is great!
In summer... I go by the natural light. I sleep with my window wide open, and the curtains away from the window, I want to wake up til the sun shining into my bedroom. My window opens up to east.... That it is kind of light the whole summer all night long does not bother me at all. I never use eye mask or curtains in front of the window because I want to feel the wind blowing... especially in summer when it is warm! Then I keep my balcony door open too so the air circulates

So if you go early to work, I guess you travel a bit? You could use blueblockers until you arrive at your work if it is still dark.

IDK if I do it right but this is how my belly tells me to do and it works great! I sleep so well in summer even if it sun is still shining when I go to bed and it is not dark in my bedroom at all.. go figure..
I guess we are adapted to it? The more darkness we need in winter!!!! Because it is so dark up here. So I really am strict about artificial lights in winter - I get sooo much darkness. But I love it

Not so gross. You have saliva in your mouth all the time...
Anyway, it's not bad to get out stuff from between the teeth. Since oil is more viscous than water, it ought to work better than plain water to do that.
But I don't understand why one shouldn't swallow it. First, I doubt anyone can go swishing a fluid around in their mouth for 20 or 30 minutes without swallowing any of it. Second, if you're using a healthy oil (which I suppose one should when doing this) what's the harm in swallowing it? Yes, the gunk from your mouth and from between your teeth is suspended in the oil, but that's stuff that you have eaten, it just got stuck in the mouth and didn't reach the gut.

### Andrea — 2014-04-10T19:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-120820>

I've been experimenting with my meds lately...
6 weeks or so ago I reduced my daily dose of T3 from 20 mcg to 15 mcg. After about a week my old brain fog and fatigue started coming back. I then increased the amount of kelp I eat more or less every day (in addition to my shrimp breakfast), which seemed to help. Then I struggled with some bloating, which I suppose has to do with the kelp.
In a couple days I started to feel better, with regard to the brain fog and tiredness. My stomach is still a bit big though.

Then, nearly two weeks ago, I reduced my T4 dose, from 25 mcg daily to 25 mcg every other day and about 12 mcg the other days. That's my intention anyway, although I always seem to forget which dose I'm supposed to take...  So far so good, but it's really too soon to tell.

I got some labs taken the other week. My thyroid values are still upside down, with "normal" TSH and very low fT4 and fT3. In fact, T4 and T3 haven't changed significantly since I started tweaking my meds in December. Only TSH has got a little higher.
My homocysteine has dropped to within the reference values, which I suppose is a good sign. However, **my B12 is 719 pmol/l (reference 150-500 pmol/l)**. I'm not sure what this means, other than that I eat a lot of seafood (and liver). I will assume that high B12 is better than low. Or not?

### Andrea — 2014-04-13T15:55:41+0300

Hmmm... There's something weird going on in my home....
For some reason, chocolate just disappears, without a trace.

I started with the Leptin Rx in November 2013, and after about 2 weeks or so, all my desire to eat anything other than Epi paleo food completely disappeared. Before Christmas, when the consultants at work brought huge amounts of chocolates and sweets, I could sit right next to one of the boxes, with my nose practically inside the box, and feel absolutely no desire to even taste. Do I have a ghost at home? Or do all those nasty EMFs make chocolate disintegrate?

### sooperb — 2014-04-15T12:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-121610>

I just had a BAB and have also eaten the last of the Ben&Jerry's ice cream I found in the freezer. What a contradiction/ mess I am, a shedload of supplements but can't get motivated to do anything even though the sun is shining and would rather eat sugar than not despite knowing it's baaaaad.

### Andrea — 2014-04-21T16:16:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-122793>

Something rather unfathomable has happened!

Due to my thyroid issues, I've been feeling cold all the time for years. I've always put on more and more clothes, even in the summer. My feet and hands are always like lumps of ice. And I've always been freezing at night, sometimes waking up from shivering. Even when the temperature in my bedroom has been over 30°C in the summer.

After I started with the Leptin RX and CT, I have gradually noticed some changes...

Now there's been changes in my bedroom too:
I have turned off the heat in my apartment, including the bedroom. I keep the ventilation in my bedroom open all night (it's basically a plain hole through the outer wall, blowing in air from the outside (now just slightly above freezing at night).
And I have removed my down duvet and sleep only under my silk duvet cover, wearing a silk night gown.

The last couple nights even that felt unnecessarily hot...
I quite like my silky stuff, but I'm afraid I will have to start sleeping in the nude.

And it's only April, when it's still cold outside at night. What's next? Buying a new, big freezer and sleep inside it during the summer?

### nonchalant — 2014-04-21T16:52:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-122802>

> I quite like my silky stuff, but I'm afraid I will have to start sleeping in the nude.

Yep!
Sounds like some good positive changes for you!

Don't worry about summer, you will find that you can now tolerate a broader range of temperatures. Though I find that when it is cloudy and I'm not grounded, I have much less tolerance of hot temps.

### Andrea — 2014-04-24T20:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-123338>

I've been thinking about my breakfasts lately. I've never been a breakfast person, but soon after I started with the BAB's I started to like eating in the morning. And I started to eat more and more. Some time ago I tried to cut down on the proteins and add more fat, but then I couldn't tolerate cold as well. So I increased the proteins (but didn't really decrease the fat...) and things got better.

A typical Rerun breakfast:
3-4 eggs - either scrambled or fried
about 150-200 g shrimps
a couple tablespoons (probably nearly 100 g) coconut oil
some kelp
(occasionaly an avocado, mushrooms and/or macadamia nut oil mayo)

Just for fun, I did some math. My breakfast consists of about 120 g of fat and 55 g protein. About 1350 kcal.  This is more than I used to eat during an entire day before... And I also have dinner when I come home from work....

Anyway, I measured the ketones in my blood this morning: 0.4 mmol/L.
Pretty low.

If I eat less for breakfast, I don't feel satisfied and I get hungry too soon.
I suppose I could try to skip the shrimps or an egg or two and add some more fat though. Maybe it will work better than last time? Should I?

### Andrea — 2014-04-26T06:30:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-123489>

### Andrea — 2014-04-27T19:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-123589>

Today I decided I didn't want to wait any longer... So I did my first outdoor CT.

And friend and I drove to the ocean, enjoyed the sun on the beach for a while, then I took my clothes off and went for it. I was there in the water, enjoying the sunshine and some people passed - wearing seemingly warm clothes.

I forgot to bring a thermometer, but the weather forecast said it ought to be 9°C (48F). It didn't feel too awful, although I only stayed in for about 10 minutes.

One funny thing is that the last time I dipped myself in the ocean here in Sweden was in August 1986, I believe. I thought it was too cold and I said "never again". For years I've been saying that the ocean needs to be at least 28°C (82F) for me to get in. And now I do it in April, in about 9°C. I must have gone nuts!

### sooperb — 2014-04-27T23:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-123602>

You most definitely aren't nuts, you chose, in my opinion, the best feel good factor available  Today the sun was shining and I was at the beach. Granted I didn't take a dip, the sea was out lol, but I did roll up my trousers and walk barefoot through all the streams and rock pools for at least half an hour. The shallower pools were warm, the big pool at the end of the pier was much deeper and much colder, not too cold to paddle. So sleeves rolled up, trousers rolled up and a breeze I thoroughly ennervated myself. By tonight you will feel glorious, it's not just the cold, or the water or the two together, it's also combined with the fact that you did it. I almost think taking the plunge, both figuratively and literally enhances the success. You feel good because you made the effort and topped up on all that this glorious earth can give if only we bother to access it. Good for you, and a smaller good for me because I managed it two days running lol.

### Andrea — 2014-04-28T11:27:11+0300

I was a bit euphoric afterwards... It's not that I would consider long cold showers "normal", but... getting into the ocean, here, in April, is something I've never ever even thought about doing. And my friend, who was there with me (and took the picture in my current avatar), was quite impressed.
Will definitely try to get to the ocean again soon, before it gets... uhmmm... too warm.

Good for you! Dipping one's feet is quite nice, isn't it?
By the way, I've noticed, that once my legs get slightly numb in the cold water, it's relatively easy to go deeper and deeper, compared to if I do it at once.

### Andrea — 2014-04-30T20:15:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-124109>

The first time is the hardest...
I did some more ocean CT last night. At sunset. In the nude.

I suppose the temperature has got up a degree or two, because this time it was actually quite pleasant.

One curious thing, though, is that I got these weird spots on my forearms afterwards. On the palm side, where the skin is more sensitive. A sort of irregular redish spots, slightly swollen. Kind of like a cross of mosquito bite and the allergic reaction I have to contact with grass, although they didn't itch or hurt. The spots were gone in the morning.
Wonder if it is some allergic reaction to seaweed, which there was plenty of in the water. Or something else...

### sooperb — 2014-04-30T20:34:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-124114>

Hmm, interesting. Could just be something in the water, maybe even some tiny sand fly that bites but isn't too significant histamine wise. Or it could just be the cold. I'm sure Jack mentions that you can get wheals from the cold too. If you're enjoying the experience and the marks don't bother you, I'd carry on. That's me of course. It does get easier the more you do it, I was under the shower for ages this morning, either I went numb and couldn't feel the cold or I can tolerate it for longer. I had no desire to stop apart from time constraints.

### Andrea — 2014-05-02T11:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-124533>

Hmmm... I suppose it could be cold urticaria... Although it felt nothing like that time, when I had placed ice on my chest (wearing a compression t-shirt) and fell asleep, with one block of ice resting on my arm, on bare skin. It looked and felt different.
I suppose I'll keep swimming in the ocean and see what happens. If it doesn't get worse I suppose I could carry on.

I feel that sometimes too, having no desire to get out from the shower. Except that it's boring...
One funny thing I started noticing some time ago is, that when I started with cold showers, the skin that wasn't exposed to the water for a moment had goose bumps and I was freezing in those areas - while now, the parts not in contact with water feel hot, and I want to get them submerged to cool them down.

### sooperb — 2014-05-02T12:53:07+0300

### Andrea — 2014-05-05T20:02:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-125322>

Finally decided to spend $$$ (or €€€) on oysters last week. They cost half a fortune here so I only bought half a dozen this time. The fish monger didn't know how to shuck them, but was kind enough to give me an oyster knife along with my oysters. I have never done this before, and the one and only time I've seen oysters being shucked the person actually used a microwave to make them open up...
Anyway, I had a go. With a small towel to hold those critters and some patience it was surprisingly easy to shuck them. I made it without any injuries. And eating them... Mmmmmmm... Addictive!

Will see if I can make some sort of a subscription on oysters with one of the fish mongers nearby... With the regular price around here I can't afford them very often.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-05-05T21:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-125334>

The irony is the oyster industry has taken a beating here in the states. Down 95% from 25 yrs ago. So most places they are pretty cheap. I can get a dozen here for 4 bucks. If I go to a restaurant here 7-8 bucks schucked.

### Andrea — 2014-05-14T19:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-126916>

I bought 1 kg of blue mussels today.
As I had suspected, these suckers are a terrible pain to open raw. But after eating my third I decided to stop. They all tasted very differently; from fresh ocean breeze to dirty mud. Unfortunately I didn't realize it until it was too late...
Obviously, I'm not competent enough to see if they are ok or not while raw. Only using the ones that are closed is apparently not enough. So I chickened out and I briefly steamed the rest of my dinner until most of them had opened up.
Now I'm waiting to see if the really yucky one is going to turn my stomach inside out or not. I only hope it won't get worse than that.

I'm not sure I will be eating raw blue mussels on a regular basis now. I'll rather try to find a fisherman who can save some herring and mackerel heads for me...

### Inger — 2014-05-14T19:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-126918>

Rerun.... i have done that too... an I am no fan of raw blue mussels either. They are hard to open (but that would not be a stopper) harder than oysters, and there is so little meat and the taste is far from an oyster!

So I just buy them frozen precooked now because they are waaay cheaper that way... and eat cold, or buy fresh, eat maybe a few raw and steam the rest very gently a few minutes

I think your belly will be fine, at least mine was after eating not so good tasting raw blue mussels

### Andrea — 2014-05-14T19:55:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-126920>

Thanks, Inger!

I have looked at frozen blue mussels, but they all come from Limfjorden in Denmark, which I'm not very fond of. So I've mostly bought canned mussels from Chile. Not sure they are better, but at least they are not from Limfjorden and they cost more...
But most of all, I eat some shrimps almost every day. They are precooked - raw seem to be impossible to find - so I fry them briefly with some spices and eat them whole, with the head and legs and all. The legs often turn out crispy and nice...

And tomorrow I'll have a couple oysters for breakfast.

### Andrea — 2014-05-15T05:19:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-127048>

The mussels from Limfjorden are not necessarily bad. I just want my seafood to be from "open waters", so to speak. If you look at a map, Limfjord looks pretty much like a lake, surrounded by agriculture.
[https://maps.google.com/maps?q=limf...pn=37.871902,54.140625&hnear=Limfjord&t=h&z=7](https://maps.google.com/maps?q=limfjorden&hl=en&ll=56.794862,9.975586&spn=3.279344,6.767578&sll=37.0625,-95.677068&sspn=37.871902,54.140625&hnear=Limfjord&t=h&z=7)
To me, conventional agriculture = fertilizers, chemicals etc.

Anyway, I have no idea if mussels from anywhere else are any better... Maybe I am avoiding crap from one place but eat crap from somewhere else...?

### Andrea — 2014-05-15T21:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-127115>

Can fish heads really taste that awful? I actually like the taste of raw mackerel... In fact, I like raw mackerel better than cooked. Herring is worse, but with some apple cider vinegar - not bad!

Looks like I have to find a fisherman who would sell me some heads. The fish mongers here in town apparently purchase - and sell - only fillets of fish. Occasionally, they get a good price on whole fish and sell that. So I need to find a fisherman and buy from them directly. After all, the heads is something they most often throw away, so they should be glad that someone wants to pay for it...

### Inger — 2014-05-15T21:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-127120>

hmmm... maybe not.. my dad drank a glass full of mackerel head smoothie without holding his nose at all, and said, ah, that was not bad at all!

but I find it yucky... so I always hold my nose. I bet that is why I have been drinking them for 2 years now lol
i find it a bit yucky with the pieces and stuff in it... when the head is not too fresh the taste is even less good
so I choose to get it down as fast as possible!

I do love raw mackerel fillets! They are just good by themselves for sure  but they are nice and clean looking, not bloody clumpy stuff

The Baltic herring heads are a bit easier, they are softer too, you should be able to get those?

### Andrea — 2014-05-17T20:19:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-127373>

I'm visiting my parents this weekend.
It is a bit funny... But mostly tragic. I used to think they had dry air in their house, because my skin gets so dry after being here for a while... Doh! My cheeks are red and it feels as if they are on fire, and I have a vague headache most of the time. And I'm constantly hungry.

Welcome to EMF hell...

Took a short swim (20 minutes) in the ocean today, which was very nice and refreshing. The water temperature here is maybe 12C or so. Yeah, it's getting too warm! And after lunch I went for a walk in a forest with my sister and her kids. I barefoot. Didn't hug a tree though... Just a couple rocks when I was climbing up on them.
I hope the weather will be decent enough for my dad to take a walk on the beach tomorrow, so that I can do some more swimming...

### sooperb — 2014-05-17T20:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-127375>

Visited the beach also today, managed a few minutes in the sea and several more in the pond, I love it, I'm a real water babe at heart. The sea here is often way out of sight and there are strong currents, swimming is not advisable unfortunately, I could bask at the edge though as long as I avoid the sinking sand ......

Do your parents have a lot of electronic gear?

### Inger — 2014-05-17T21:12:39+0300

i tell the workers at the fish counter I make fish head smoothies from the heads.. they say they sure believe it must be very healthy!  who knows... maybe they try too secretly at home  I have told it heals the brain

12 C sure is warm... the river was 9 C to 10 C today... I had to swim a few times to cool down it was so warm, air was almost 20 degree C today, way too warm!

I suppose you're right, about Baltic herring being better than mammals. At least for someone like you, who has a good redox potential. For me? Probably the Baltic herring still would be the better choice, but since I have the possibility I will definitely eat North sea herring instead.
If you and I think 20C is too warm, how are we going to survive the summer?

How brave of you to tell people what you do with your fish heads! I'm a coward, so for now, I think I'll just say fish stock.

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=127375)
>
> Visited the beach also today, managed a few minutes in the sea and several more in the pond, I love it, I'm a real water babe at heart. The sea here is often way out of sight and there are strong currents, swimming is not advisable unfortunately, I could bask at the edge though as long as I avoid the sinking sand ......

That sounds great! Except for the currents and sinking sand... Take it easy out there!

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=127375)
>
> Do your parents have a lot of electronic gear?

Oh, yeah, more gadgets than you can imagine! More wireless stuff than most.

Since I started reading dr. K.'s blogs I've had that extremely frustrating feeling of not understanding anything at all, but even though I really don't understand much, I sort of hate knowing the few things I now get. I am no longer surprised that my mom is struggling with hypothyroidism, osteoporosis, frozen shoulder and some sex hormone imbalance (and maybe more?). It's very clear to me why - on a low, simple level, of course. It pains me to watch this going on and to see where they are heading - and not being able to do anything about it.

Anyway, I got a nice swim in the ocean today. It's been blowing from the ocean, so the water was a bit cooler - 10C. I wanted to stay in the water until I started to shiver, which just didn't seem to happen. But then I realized I was clenching my teeth a lot, so I got up... 30 minutes.
I shivered afterwards for almost two hours. I had some areas with red spots near my knees and on my forearms, which disappeared in less an hour. No swelling as last time though - and there was no seaweed in the water here. Anyway, I've been "speeded" since I got back to the house, most physically but mentally too. Maybe the 30 minutes was a bit too much...?

### sooperb — 2014-05-18T14:29:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-127452>

"It pains me to watch this going on and to see where they are heading"

I can relate a little to this with my husband. He thinks anyone who stands under a cold shower without needing to is nuts lol. He's a T2D, overweight (although not as much as he used to be) and completely goes with what his GP tells him so he won't walk barefoot on the beach or even at home, because diabetics shouldn't. Such blanket advice, he may need to take care but so do I, I don't want to step on something sharp or dirty etc. I haven't in years so why should he? He's an adult though so it's up to him.

I stood under the cold shower today and realised it just didn't feel cold enough, I expect the water from the mains has warmed slightly as it's been sunny here, so either I'm more used to it or it's not cold enough to CT properly, I left my infrared thermometer thing elsewhere so couldn't test it. When I took my first couple of baths I didn't shiver or feel that cold afterwards, I felt it a lot more with next few, a little shivering not much but it was taking me several hours to warm up again too. I haven't had any since, relied on the shower because the bath is just too shallow. I am looking for something that is deeper like a plastic trough and have found one that might do, just have to decide where to put it, it won't fit in the bathroom and I'm not sure I'm ready for outside just yet  I love the feeling afterwards too, so enervating.

Since I don't have a tub in my appartment, I sometimes go visit a friend of mine to borrow his. His faucet, however, doesn't give colder water than 18°C or so (in the winter, anyway). But he has a connection for a washing machine on his cold water pipe, so we have connected a garden hose to it. When I measured the temperature last month it was 4-5°C. So I mix it with some warm water in the tub and get a nice CT temperature. I just wish I could visit his tub more often.

This may draw gasps from the oyster crowd, but last night I took a shortcut with them. Just didn't want to feel the _lumps_ of oyster sliding down my throat. So I used the good ol' Magic Bullet and blended 4-5 oz of shucked oysters, a squeeze of lemon, tsp of horseradish, couple dashes of Frank's hot sauce, sprinkle of seaweed flakes, and some green from a green onion and drank it down. Not bad is you swallow it quickly...no slurping slowly. An oyster smoothie.

### Andrea — 2014-05-22T21:09:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-127989>

My blender has made its maiden voyage!
I went by a fish monger yesterday, who had whole mackerel for a reasonable price. They are still big here, but I have to try, don't I?

So yesterday I ate mackerel intestines for dinner and today I let the blender do its magic.
Since the fish is so big I put in only one head. Wanted to do it hard core, without any flavoring, but chickened out and added a tiny dash of apple cider vinegar.
The result? Not very impressive, I have to say. Smelled a bit fishy...  Just kidding, it wasn't too bad. The consistency was interesting though: smooth with "lumps" of bones and fins. Tastewise: pretty ok, but the vinegar was a mistake. Next time I'll just add a little black pepper. Maybe add some chopped onion at the end.

But I wonder about one thing: Are girls smarter than boys?
I bought 10 mackerels. 9 boys, 1 girl. Coincidence?

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=128065)
>
> Intestines...?  what did you eat? Do you found any parasites? The mackerels I get almost always have lots of parasites. I do not care too much I just try to pick them off and rinse the roe and fish milk and liver and toss the rest of the intestines

I barely know what I ate! I suck at fish anatomy! And I'm even worse at the English teminology. I recognize the eggs and the white stuff that the boys have (roe and milt?). And I recognize the liver. But I couldn't recognize the gall bladder, so I removed all dark lumps, i.e. the gall bladder and the heart. And then I was left with I a big chunk of long thin strands, which looked like intestines. I figured that a mackerel mostly eats plankton and small fish, so why shouldn't I be able to eat that too?
As for parasites, one of them had some strange tiny curled things among the entrails. These things didn't move, so they were probably nothing, but I threw the entrails away anyway. There didn't seem to be any of it in the muscle tissue though, so I put it in the freezer with the rest of the mackerels.

### sooperb — 2014-05-23T17:54:51+0300

Omg Rerun, lol, I'm not one for sanitised food but I think I would be avoiding long white things that might be worms (unless they were attached like fibres) and just because stuff wasn't wiggling doesn't mean it was fine to eat, dead fish=dead parasites. Still, each to their own of course.

Inger you might ask your brother how he came into the world, fully dressed? I think not. Whilst I'm not up for flashing everything in public, covering it all up is a recipe for disaster as some of those covered up Asian ladies discover when they become ill because they don't get enough sunlight, it's astonishing that rickets is making a come back in this day and age. I think you mentioned he has problems? If so he's a good candidate to join a religion which especially welcomes those who are suffering. Offering support and showing kindness is a big attraction when your head is struggling to make sense of the world. It's only later that you may realise that the welcome was to enable sect members to gain your trust and get to know your weaknesses. Poor lad.

> I wish someone could explain why, when it comes to meat, fish etc. raw is considered to be better, cooked meat doesn't lose much and we've been eating it for many a century.

Better or not... Personally, I've never liked well done meat. I've always prefered much less cooked than that. When I was little, I sometimes stole small pieces of raw meat from the fridge when noone was around... In the most recent years, I've been cooking most of my meat sous vide (low temperature, long cooking time). To me a perfect steak is cooked at 40°C. A slight fever...
Fish, on the other hand, lives in much colder temperatures and - in my opinion - has best flavor and texture when eaten at a much, much lower temperature than mammalian meat.

### Inger — 2014-05-23T19:05:34+0300

Rerun... those curled white things was... just that. Anisakiasis parasites.

But do not fret.. I have had hundreds of raw mackerels infected with them and I never got any issues. I do pick the visible ones away always. Like you did.
But I have read up on them and someone can get stomach pain or the like. But they only live for I think it was 2 weeks and then they die in humans so not too bad
I have read up on every single parasite in our fish here in Scandinavia/North and I am not scared of one of them to be honest  If I were in Africa it would be a totally different story ! Their parasites are scary!

I love raw... taste better, makes me feel better, smells better... everything is better    Sue you need to try a week all aw and see if you get hooked. That is what I did many years ago and could never return - you are warned!

Yeah... you are right about weird clothes rules and not letting sun shine on the skin. Too bad! People are so covered up here too because they are scared of the sun... imagine. Even if it is very hot outside. They are scared of the sun and of parasites like ticks. And snakes. That is why they cover up and use shoes too in nature. Oh man... I am so glad I am not scared of anything like that

Rerun I have to say I often have to laugh of what you post.. you have this funny kind of humor lol makes me laugh
and your English is way better than mine!

> But I have read up on them and someone can get stomach pain or the like. But they only live for I think it was 2 weeks and then they die in humans so not too bad
> I have read up on every single parasite in our fish here in Scandinavia/North and I am not scared of one of them to be honest  If I were in Africa it would be a totally different story ! Their parasites are scary!

Don't worry, I'm not throwing all my mackerels away (I don't remember which one had this nematode). They are all in the freezer and I will keep them there for a couple days before I start eating them. By "them" I meant the fish, but, well, the parasites are included...
Anyway, not even Trichinella spiralis can survive forever in a freezer.
I haven't studied fish parasites much, but I remember the one time I bought a big piece of fresh cod. When I was at home and was cutting it to smaller pieces, it was moving! That poor fish was full of worms! I cooked something else for lunch...

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=128094)
>
> I love raw... taste better, makes me feel better, smells better... everything is better    Sue you need to try a week all aw and see if you get hooked. That is what I did many years ago and could never return - you are warned!

Yeah! Or you could start slow. Sous vide. You can even pasteurize your food at low temperature. Well, at 53°C and above anyway. And when you're used to juicy, soft and tender meat and you wonder why you have killed your meat so thoroughly before, it's not a big step to raw. Or, as I like to cook my meat: very think pieces and 30 seconds max on each side in a hot pan. Nice seared surface, raw inside.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=128094)
>
> Yeah... you are right about weird clothes rules and not letting sun shine on the skin. Too bad! People are so covered up here too because they are scared of the sun... imagine. Even if it is very hot outside. They are scared of the sun and of parasites like ticks. And snakes. That is why they cover up and use shoes too in nature. Oh man... I am so glad I am not scared of anything like that

Well, to be honest, I'm scared of ticks too. I have a relative who has been sitting in a wheel chair for 30 or so years. TBE. Another relative was more dead than alive for I think 8 years after a tick bite, until he passed away. Also TBE. I know that dr. K. has written somewhere about immunity to Lyme disease, and I suspect the same would go for TBE. But. That requires a good redox potential. I don't have that.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=128094)

### Andrea — 2014-06-01T15:44:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-129209>

I've just had the coolest weekend ever!
Yesterday, I was on OYSTER SAFARI!

We were a group of 8 people plus a guide. The guide took us by boat to a small island where we went ashore. There we put on waders, and equipped with an aquascope and a landing net we hit the water, looking for oysters. In about 20 minutes we got more oysters than we could eat.
And I'm talking about wild oysters, not farmed ones.

On a rock on the island we got a brief lesson in shucking oysters and flavoring, and then I had the greatest oyster orgie ever.

Imagine, absolutely fresh oysters, directly from the sea, picked by yourself!
It just doesn't get better than this...

Thank you, dr.K., for inspiring me to do this!
Unfortunately, this also means that I propably never will appreciate the French cultured oysters - the only stuff I can get from the fish mongers here - again. So... Tomorrow I'm going back to mackerel head smoothies...

I don't think it's a biggie with farmed oysters, but there's a huge difference between eating them fresh directly on the beach and ones that have traveled halfway around the world (ok, Europe, but still) for several days.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=129758)
>
> That safari sounds like heaven to me Rerun.... I need to do one too.. \[...\]
> Can you give me a clue where in Sweden they do those oyster safaris...? A website maybe? Have been thinking about visiting Sweden this summer anyways... my sister is there and my brother lives there too... and those oysters... they are pulling...

It was like heaven!
[http://klemmingsdyk.se/](http://klemmingsdyk.se/)
Some other companies offer oyster safaris too, but as far as I know, they don't let you catch your own oysters and eat them. Oyster fishing here in Sweden is regulated differently than fishing for other creatures in the ocean and "Allemansrätten" doesn't apply to oysters. So, choose your safari carefully, or you will be disapointed.

### Andrea — 2014-06-06T15:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-129974>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=129968)

> it is a seafood bar pretty much in the middle of the airport, close to the Danish food store with smoked salmon, liver pates and stuff. There is other places with fresh oysters too but they were a bit more expensive
> I thought 3,50 / oyster was super cheap too... here i will have to pay around 7,00 / piece in a restaurant

Thanks! The price at restaurants here is ridiculous too.

By the way, I've figured out a way to ingest MHS without it being unpleasant. Not that I had issues with it before, but to be honest, I can't say that I have toyed with it in my mouth, gurgled and swallowed it with tremendous pleasure...
The thing is, when drinking, my brain is set to just swallowing something smooth. But the consistency of MHS doesn't really allow for that. So when I've run it in my blender for a while, I pour it into a bowl and eat it with a spoon. Like a cold soup. Served this way, I actually look forward to it during the day. The only thing I like to add, except for water, is a little black pepper. Nice!

One note though... Finely chopped onion on the top was NOT a good idea. The consistency became so wrong. It's weird enough with bits of fins and other fish parts.  However, it could work if it's run in the blender together with the heads.

### Andrea — 2014-06-15T09:53:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-130886>

Hmmm... My blender smells fishy!
Wonder why...

Anyway, I've noticed one funny thing lately. I often skip words when I speak and I miss out letters when I write by hand. I notice immediately that things have come out wrong, but I just can't seem to prevent it from happening. First, I thought my brain was slowing down even more, but... Thinking about it, I rather feel like my brain has sped up and the rest of my body can't keep up. I can think more clearly now than I have been able to for several years and things simply happen faster in my brain. This is nice, but I do worry about the rest of my body not keeping up...

Perhaps the fact that I used to be left handed until I was 25 years old and then had to switch has something to do with it. Starting to learn at 25, I never attained the motor skills in my right hand as I used to have in my left, so my \[right hand\] writing has never been too great. ### Andrea — 2014-07-22T19:42:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-135201>

When I first got to this site and started with the Leptin Rx, CT and all, I was convinced that my health would only get better.
However, I was dead wrong. I just chose to notice all tiny improvements, but completely failed to see the declines and realize their importance.

I've just come back from a small time out, where I tried to figure some things out, while indulging in French cuisine. And not really enjoying it, but rather looking forward to getting home and having mackerel head smoothies...

Anyway, I've not been doing things right for a while, even though I've noticed some improvements such as:
\+ My brain works better than for ages (even though I do have a LONG way to go in that respect).
\+ I don't freeze all the time and I tolerate cold quite well.
\+ I have been able to do physical and mentally demanding activities for hours each day for 3 weeks, without needing any rest days - this has not been possible since 2007.

But on the not so great side:
\- Before I started, I used to sleep well and all night. Soon my sleep got crappy, with only about 4 hours per night. Then it got worse and eventually I started to fantasize about anaesthetics...
\- My body composition has changed a lot. I weigh slightly more than last year, but, more importantly, mass (mostly muscles, I'd like to think) from my upper body has been drawn downward by gravity and placed itself as fat on my butt and thighs. I look ridiculous and need larger pants now than I needed when I was the fattest some years back (the beginning of my so called thyroid problems).
\- I've never liked garlic or onion, but on my quest for cysteine / glutathione I embraced the discomfort and started to eat this a few times a week. For the last several weeks before my time out, I reacted badly to the smallest amounts of garlic or onion, with bloating, restlessness and total lack of sleep.
\- My homocysteine was checked for the first time in 2008 and a few times after that. The value has always been high, far above the reference value of <15 µmol/L. But in the latest test this spring/summer, it had dropped drastically to 6 µmol/L.
\- My blood pressure has risen from around 90/60 to around 125/70.
\- And some more.

### Andrea — 2014-07-22T19:44:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-135203>

Just before my time out, I got my results from 23andme.
It turns out that I am homozygous for MTHFR C677T, CBS A360A and VDR Taq.
I’m also heterozygous for MTR A2756G, MTRR A664A, BHMT-02 and BHMT-08.

I know, I know, dr.K. says that one’s epi-genome is more important than their genes, and methylation issues are not the first thing to be addressed.
But, I’m afraid that if I live a crappy life in a crappy environment, my health will spiral downwards to where I am noticeably limited by my genes and where my epi-genome can’t help me much. Of course, even if I “fixed” my methylation cycle, it wouldn’t fix the rest of my problems; the root cause would still be there. I know that.

But I thought... Now that I know which cards I have in my hand, I should figure out which game to play with them...

My thought now is to eliminate sulphur loaded food for a while. Do the Leptin Rx for a few weeks, then cut down on my animal intake. Cut down on seaweed. Eat mackerel head smoothies as often as possible and maybe the rest of the mackerel. “No carb”. And lower my standards when it comes to finding a house to get out from the city.
I wish I could do more CT, but that is pretty impossible for the time being - my tub owning friend is moving to a place without a tub, the “cold” water in my/his shower is just below 20C, the ocean temperature above 20C and my freezer is too small to make enough ice (and I need it to store mackerel heads).

### Andrea — 2014-08-05T14:21:18+0300

I think I need to think more when I'm around others.

I was having dinner with a friend the other day - a flatfish, a plaice. My friend didn't want me to eat it raw, so I got it gently warmed. I carefully ate all the muscles, and then went for the head. First I poked out the eyes and ate them, then I took the rest of the head in my hand and sucked/licked off everything edible. My friend stared at me, seemingly fascinated. But I sort of wonder if the two of us will ever have a meal together again...

What's wrong with me, when I actually prefer the heads to the flesh?!

### caroline — 2014-08-06T08:28:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-136948>

What is going on with your sleep????? you gotta sleep! tell us more.....

### Andrea — 2014-08-06T10:45:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-136958>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=136948)
>
> What is going on with your sleep????? you gotta sleep! tell us more.....

Well, I usually fall asleep like a baby in the evening, but wake up after 4 hours. I usually don't fall asleep again after that, not more than a couple minutes at a time anyway. Some nights I wake up already after 3 or 2 hours and have trouble to sleep more.

I normally turn my computer off about 2 hours before going to bed - I have installed f.lux and use blue blocker glasses.
No fake light in the evening.
No electronic devices in my bedroom, except for my cell phone (in airplane mode), more than 2m away from me, for alarm (but I do live in an electrified cage (an apartment)).
I have a black curtain in my bedroom window, or wear an eye patch (in the middle of the night when I wake up it's dark anyway though).

On the hottest nights this summer, when I couldn't fall asleep in the evening, I put an ice pack on my neck, which helped me fall asleep, but it doesn't seem to work in the middle of the night.

### caroline — 2014-08-06T11:49:10+0300

Thanks! I searched the forum a few months ago and found some thread about borax and people's success with it, so in my desperation I tried it too, but I didn't notice any difference.  But they say it can be used as a detergent, so I suppose I will have use for it anyway.

I guess I could try... Just wonder what makes sweet potatoes so special. They're hard to find around here.Wouldn't a banana do about the same thing?
I don't eat very much carbs. I have some homemade sauerkraut that I take a mouthful of a couple times a week. And when I have dinner with a friend on the weekends I might eat a slice of a red bell pepper or a small piece of brocolli or so. Until recently I ate onions and garlic a couple times a week, but my body eventually shouted at me to stop, so I've avoided that for 2-3 weeks now. Also, during July and August, I might run into some bush with berries and not be able to resist them... But basically, I've eaten very low carb for several years without any problems. On the contrary, on occasions I've eaten more carbs my stomach has got bubbly and annoying.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=137042)
>

I usually do CT after dinner. I suppose I could play with this and try it later or in the morning. I prefer not to do it in the morning though, because I'd have to get up even earlier (4 am is pretty early already) - even though I don't sleep for hours in the morning, I try to rest in bed, in darkness. But I can't do real CT for the time being, since all the water around here is too warm. However, I've just ordered a bunch of ice mats that I hope will fit in my freezer and I intend to make shorts of them... And maybe put one on my neck and shoulders as well.
Grounding is difficult, since I live in a city and work is not better EMF-wise. After the webinar with Michael Neuert I figured I'd better avoid grounding while exposed to EMF. But whenever I can, I sneak away at lunch and swim in the ocean and lie on a rock for a while.

### Andrea — 2014-08-26T08:21:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-138930>

I can't say why, but last week I decided to decrease my dose of Liothyronine (T3) to 5mcg twice a day (I used to take 3 times 5mcg). Maybe that wasn't my best move yet. My old brain fog is now coming back, I think my head hurts a bit and just walking makes me dizzy. Also, my cold tolerance is more or less gone. I'm the only person around here who walks around in shorts and a t-shirt (everyone else wears long pants and jackets), but I sure feel cold. Also, when I wake up in the middle of the night, I'm freezing. Really freezing. But I slept a total of 5 hours tonight - with a blanket - which is more than for a long, long time...

It's tempting to increase my dose again. But should I?
Why don't I like my own answer...?

### caroline — 2014-08-26T08:37:27+0300

### Andrea — 2014-08-26T10:38:13+0300

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=138933)
>
> why did you decrease the med?

I don't know why. Somehow I just felt that I should do that.
I have already slowly phased out Levothyroxine; I have been completely free of it for over a month - and each time I decreased my dose I started to feel better in some way. My heart rate slowed down a bit and I no longer experience palpitations in the evening. However, now after decreasing the Liothyronine dose my heart rate has got up! And it increases with any physical activity. That surprises me. Something is obviously wrong...

### Andrea — 2014-09-12T17:20:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-140742>

It's been a while...
So what's happened since last time I wrote?

Well, I took the easy way out this time, and raised my Lio dose back to usual.
The last three weeks, I've been out camping on work days. I found this nice, relatively dark spot, near the ocean, more than 500 m from a cell tower and power lines - I doubt I can find a better place within a reasonable distance from home or from work. After work I took a bath in the ocean (now 17-18°C), stole an oyster and devoured it, lay down and sat on a rock most of the afternoon, read the latest blog, watched the sun set in the ocean... And went to sleep under the stars. It just doesn't get better than this. And this morning I went up early and looked at the Aurora Borealis. I'm too far south to really see it, but I did see some weak, greenish light phenomena of sorts in the distance, so I tell myself that was it.

And...

I woke up after 4-5 hours each night.
Sigh. It's definitely not my environment. It is I.

It is so heartbreaking what high EMF readings are almost everywhere today - it makes me want to scream, really
Even at my parents home where i thought is nothing much.... there is a lot, and it looks pretty much like countryside. I hate hate hate what the radiation is doing to people.
I need to help the neighbor lady - but how to do it in a sensible way... it takes time and i want it done now.... people do not seem to know anything about how bad wifi etc are.... just too sad.

### Andrea — 2014-09-14T11:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-140945>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=140838)
>
> It is so heartbreaking what high EMF readings are almost everywhere today - it makes me want to scream, really
> Even at my parents home where i thought is nothing much.... there is a lot, and it looks pretty much like countryside. I hate hate hate what the radiation is doing to people.
> I need to help the neighbor lady - but how to do it in a sensible way... it takes time and i want it done now.... people do not seem to know anything about how bad wifi etc are.... just too sad.
> But I have my plans, it will just take time. I need to be more patient!  a little hard when it is about peoples health/lives

It's difficult to be patient when you see someone - especially someone you care for - whose health is suffering, when you think you could help them.
Most of the people I talk to and know are engineers, so I had hoped at least some would understand, at a basic level. I tried the simple approach:
\- Life is based on electricity and electric currents. Like for the function of the brain you look at an ELECTRO-encephalogram, for the function of the heart you look at an ELECTRO-cardiogram, etc.
\- Yeah, ok.
\- A magnetic or an electric field will affect electrons and their flow...
\- Yeah.
\- So if you expose a living organism to such a field, it will affect the electrons and their flow in it too. And this may eventually affect their health.
\- No way! That's ridiculous!

They might add that they have been exposed to EMF all their life and it hasn't affected them at all.

Speaking about helping others, is it ever ok to give up on one's parents?
My mom is the most stubborn person on this planet who knows everything about everything. Discussing health with her is like talking to a 12 year old. If she doesn't have any arguments, she makes some up. "Why would my frequent flight trips affect my health? Pilots and flight attendants fly all the time and they live longer and healthier than most..."
She lives in worse EMF hell than most can imagine. Has been on diets for years, mostly either low-calorie-powders or a-little-pasta-with-cheese-and-veggies. Has had a "frozen shoulder" for many years. Hypothyroid. Severe osteoporosis. Hasn't slept properly for years. Recently she started experiencing heart palpitations and is now on Warfarin. And there's probably more.
Basically none of the treatments or medications she has tried so far has helped, but "they are doctors, I have to do what they say!"

There simply is no point in talking to her about it. It's painful, but I just can't find any hole where I could come through. Considering her new heart issues I suspect that the end is closer than I previously thought. I'm getting used to the thought. But will I ever forgive myself if I give up already?

I've been wearing pants today, and what a relief. Well, when it comes to people looking and commenting, anyway. But how uncomfortable! My legs couldn't breathe. Oh well, I'd better get used...

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=141827)
>
> I had to withstand loads of digusting looks and even loud judgements from the fellow citizens during the last winter, because myself and my two small children, we were walking and playing in the parks barefoot. I realized lately people are putting on more and more cloths and do the same for their kids. You would regulary see during the colder summer days kids wearing caps and tights below their trousers. I feel like children are in general allowed less and less freedom in their life and choices.

To be honest, one year ago, I would have looked at you the same way and thought that you were crazy, neglecting your kids. Maybe I'd even call the social services.  I think I know better now.
The funny thing is that "everyone" seems to know better than everyone else what's best for people around them. Yet they don't even know what's best for themselves.

I think I see the same thing, people getting more and warmer dressed. Last week it was 10-15°C and people already started wearing down jackets. Wonder how they will survive winter...

### Lahelada — 2014-09-26T00:13:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-141959>

It seems to be the thing everywhere. People felt the cold more here too all winter long.

### Inger — 2014-09-27T09:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-142017>

Rerun I hate the feeling too when my legs cannot breath... haha how weird! That is why I use dresses all the time... but yeah... it will come to an end soon.... and to get used to cover the legs is no fun. I use dresses in winter too but with tights.. I just do not like at all to use pants anymore, they are way too uncomfortable

### caroline — 2014-09-27T09:28:19+0300

me too ..... hate all my skinny jeans now - my leg can't breathe!!! so uncomfortable!

### Andrea — 2014-09-27T11:05:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-142025>

I suppose that the good thing about wearing pants is that one day it will be ok to start wearing shorts again.  I quite look forward to that day...

Another good thing is that the ocean is getting colder: about 15°C this week.
But it's weird - the temperature in my shower is still above 20°C and a couple minutes in the shower feels much, much worse than bathing in the ocean for half an hour. Getting out of the ocean, however, is quite "interesting" now, since the water is warmer than the air. And more is to come...

### Andrea — 2014-10-07T19:11:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-143170>

Oooh, air drying in 13°C - after bathing in 14°C - is interesting! Fortunately  the wind was blowing hard enough to make me dry relatively fast.
One good thing about the fall and cold temperatures is that there are no people on my favorite spot by the ocean anymore, so now I wear less clothes (50%) when I'm there. I think I'll start wearing my birthday suit for bathing there from now.
But I do wonder how long I can consider it safe to go bathing there all alone...

### Andrea — 2014-10-14T12:08:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-143787>

It sure is getting more and more interesting. 13°C in the ocean and 8°C in the air today. It was surprisingly easy to get in though.
After getting dressed I walked barefoot over the rocks (of all sizes, from cliffs to pebbles) to my car. My feet were numb and shining red like Rudolph the reindeer's nose - and radiated heat. Funny! It's also amusing how difficult it is to put on FiveFingers on numb and moist feet, using fingers that lack any motor skills...

### caroline — 2014-10-15T13:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-143913>

you are an epic woman!!!

### nicld — 2014-10-15T16:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-143925>

I refuse to wear a jacket in the winter here as well and everyone thinks I am nuts too. I did wear one last winter for about 3 days with the windchill fell to -50F and hated it.

Getting cold before bed in normal and good. It means that melatonin is being produced. My issue right now is I go to bed a bit colder then heat up as the night progresses and sometimes wake up due to being too warm. We still have not turned the heat on in the house except for a couple of hours.

### Andrea — 2014-10-15T19:30:01+0300

Thanks, Nicole!  I'm always glad to see that I'm not alone.

As for being cold in bed, I just looked a couple posts back and notice a difference. Last month, I was freezing when I woke up in the middle of the night. Now, I feel a bit cold right after I've gone to bed and I tend to pull a blanket over myself. But when I wake up in the middle of the night I feel warm and throw it off. A change in my circadian biology?

### nicld — 2014-10-15T19:50:19+0300

I do the same thing. I start with two blankets on my top half and by the end of the night, just a sheet on all. I think it is warm in the house when I wake, the hubby thinks it is cold. Maybe it's a woman thing

### Andrea — 2014-10-15T19:56:09+0300

Hmmmm... First feeling cold, then warm, sometimes cold when others are warm... Menopause?
