Thinking about it... In my late teenage years I developed quite bad acne that just refused to go away. Nothing helped. Not even Roaccutane. When I was 30 or so, my dermatologist told me that unfortunately I would suffer from my acne until menopause. And, well... A few years back, after I had been on a low carb diet for a couple months, I think I got there...

### kovita — 2014-10-16T10:13:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-143992>

> [Rerun said:](/goto/post?id=143957)
>
> Ha! Exactly! Sometimes, I have a friend staying over night at my place, and in the morning when I'm about to open the windows because it's so warm inside I see that he's wearing a sweater. Feeling cold!
> Hmmmm... First feeling cold, then warm, sometimes cold when others are warm... Menopause?

I am nowhere near to menopause, so do not panic ;-) I have the same, very hot at night and morning, but sometimes just got terribly cold during the day depsite of a good temperature. And as it came to goes away. I think many people wrote here, that CT makes them to feel warm sometimes and cold the other times, but the heat during the night and early morning is definitively a good sign ;-)

### nicld — 2014-10-16T16:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-144008>

> [Rerun said:](/goto/post?id=143991)
>
> LOL! Of course not!
> I, on the other hand, am 38. So maybe?
>
> Thinking about it... In my late teenage years I developed quite bad acne that just refused to go away. Nothing helped. Not even Roaccutane. When I was 30 or so, my dermatologist told me that unfortunately I would suffer from my acne until menopause. And, well... A few years back, after I had been on a low carb diet for a couple months, I think I got there...

I had been having not really acne but bumps along the jaw line that I attribute to inflammation in the body and my HS-CRP confirmed that. I have been making sure to take all my magnesium again and the bumps are disappearing. I think the horrible cystic type acne is definitely something to do with the insides and inflammation. I see the people with that type of acne and it just breaks my heart.

### Andrea — 2014-10-16T19:52:43+0300

Thank you! I find it weird that all of a sudden I can be shivering cold, for no apparent reason, when everyone else is fine and warm - usually it's the other way around. But as you wrote, in a while it's gone and I'm good again.

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=144008)
>
> I had been having not really acne but bumps along the jaw line that I attribute to inflammation in the body and my HS-CRP confirmed that. Yes, inflammation! I guess that was my middle name. The acne is more or less gone, but as for inflammation I'm not fine yet, although better. The doctors and dermatologists used to shake their heads and say that it was a hormonal thing. I don't have my labs from those times, but I remember I had unusually high testosterone. And there's nothing you can do about your hormones...
Holy crap! I don't know much now, but if I had known what I think I know now say 20 years ago... Oh well, I'm still alive. It's not completely too late.

### Andrea — 2014-11-07T10:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-146418>

I had a serious look in my fridge this morning. On the outside, it looks like any old fridge. But on the inside... Hmmm... There's almost nothing in there, except for a jar of duck fat, 2 jars of sauerkraut, 2 mackerel heads and a bottle of organic Malbec from the Cuyo Valley in Argentina.

Let's party...

Wonder what my mom would think if she saw my fridge...

CT outdoors is getting worth the name now though. Yesterday was the best day yet.
Ocean: 11.2°C
Air: 3.6°C
Trying to air dry was interesting... However, it was less fun to sit in a meeting afterwards, finding myself clenching my teeth and shivering every now and then. I think I'll have to give this up on my lunch breaks now and try to get out to do ocean CT on the weekends instead.

### Inger — 2014-11-07T18:22:30+0200

well... I have forbid my boyfriend to open my fridge when he is here
it is not an option to let him see my fermented liver... half rotten lamb hearts... fish heads and old raw beef fat and heart laying around
so far he has not... I think he is scared....lol
you should see his house.. it is pure luxury
I am kinda really scared to show my fridge to anyone  I do keep it clean but man it looks a bit special

Rerun why not just dip shortly on the lunch breaks and walk a bit on thhe beach and breath the fresh air
you could take your lunch out there and eat... that way you can eat as yucky as you want no one is looking

### Andrea — 2014-11-08T08:03:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-146538>

> it is not an option to let him see my fermented liver... half rotten lamb hearts... fish heads and old raw beef fat and heart laying around

So he is for real?
The other night, I had a friend sleeping over at my place. He likes to have _filmjölk_ for breakfast, which I had bought for him. I simply told him that his filmjölk is next to the fish head, and added that if he wanted the head he could have it too, since I have more in the freezer...He seemed to be quite ok with it.
By the way, how do you store the beef fat? I had some in my fridge some time ago, but it got mouldy after a week or two.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=146449)
>
> I am kinda really scared to show my fridge to anyone  I do keep it clean but man it looks a bit special

LOL!

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=146449)
>
> Rerun why not just dip shortly on the lunch breaks and walk a bit on thhe beach and breath the fresh air
> you could take your lunch out there and eat... that way you can eat as yucky as you want no one is looking

I do walk on the cliffs and rocks before and after my dip - but I have to wear clothes when I do that, since it's a public place and someone could turn up. I have found a spot where I can stand (naked) and see if someone is coming before they see my body, so I like to stand there, naked, air drying after my dip. For the time being, with the air colder than the water (plus wind chill), my skin turns nicely red relatively quickly when I stand in the cold air after a dip in the ocean.
I have no appetite during the day, so I don't eat lunch. Makes life simpler.

But my problem is that after getting cold out there, I tend to shiver for up to a few hours. It's not very appropriate to sit in a meeting shivering. Also, my brain seems to be much faster than my mouth when this is going on, so I tend to skip words and combine several words when talking. I suppose my boss will send me off for another drug test one of these days...

### caroline — 2014-11-09T02:22:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-146619>

Hi! how re you doing?

### Andrea — 2014-11-10T15:30:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-146753>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=146619)
>
> Hi! how re you doing?

Thanks for looking by! I think I'm doing better.

My sleep is better, anyway. Most nights now I get 6-8 hours.  Sometimes I wake up in the middle of the night but fall asleep again, other nights I get uninterrupted sleep. There have been a few nights that I've slept for N-I-N-E uninterrupted hours!
I can point out that foot massage - really hard and tough massage - does wonders for my sleep. When I can't have someone massage my feet, I stand and move around on my acupressure mat before going to bed.

Other than that... I think I have managed to decrease my dose of Liothyronine a bit now. This time I don't notice any difference as last time. The difference between last time I tried and now is that it's darker and colder. Nice! And more to follow.

However, I have gained some weight lately. Could be me playing around with my T3, could be something else. Either way, I need to move. I saw a beautiful small house for sale the other week, one that I could afford. Everything was just great - for once even the water chemistry - except that there is a power line 100 meters from the the house. So, still looking...

Anyway, I've found a store where I can buy lamb hearts and lamb heads. The downside is that these lambs were conventionally raised (i.e. not grass fed and not organic). They normally get the lambs from Sweden and New Zealand. I'm not sure whether it really matters for these organs of such young animals or not.
Grass fed hearts are virtually impossible to find around here, unless I buy the entire animal. And brains are completely impossible, because the heads are considered high risk waste.
I got a description of an Iranian dish the other day; lamb brains with onions... Strangely, I find it appealing.

### Andrea — 2014-12-07T14:02:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-149418>

Wow! It’s been about a year since I registered my account on this forum. Time sure flies by...

So, what has happened since last year? From the top of my head…

-   I’m completely off Levothyroxine since a couple months back. So far so good.
-   I’m completely off Liothyronine since a few weeks back. So far so good.
-   I don’t freeze “all the time”, although I do have sudden bouts of shivering sometimes.
-   I can, generally, tolerate cold quite well.
-   My mental clarity has increased and my brain works faster.
-   My memory is better.
-   I can look people in the eyes now. For some reason, I haven’t been able to do that for several years. I love looking my boss in the eyes, even when telling him that I’m not comfortable with a task that he’s given me (this I’ve never done before).
-   My muscles don’t get sore for days from just carrying a grocery bag home from my car (ok, I'm cheating with D-Ribose, but I'm happy nevertheless).
-   My balance has got better. It has been really, really bad since I had a concussion about 15 years ago, but now I can stand on one foot and put a on sock on the other foot, or even stand on one foot for a short while with my eyes closed. An attempt to do this before would have got med killed.
-   My night vision is better than it has been for many years. Maybe I don't always see well in darkness, but I can sense things around me, and I can find my way around and do things with just a few stray photons' help. And I can read in the evening with very poor light.
-   I have stopped using my computer glasses. They were not very strong, but without them I would get a head ache after a while at the computer. I haven't used them at all for about two months now.

But, of course, I still have a long, loooong way to go.

Other things that have changed are:

-   I have gained a bunch of extra kg... about 6 kg since last year. (I know, I need to move.)
-   My cycle has got shorter, about 24 days, but quite regular.
-   I can clearly tell when I’m at day -2 (two days left to start of next cycle) by loss of cold tolerance (low Pg, I suppose).
-   I have become more sensitive to nnEMF, or at least more aware.
-   I tend to get too wound up for anything out of the ordinary - and even ordinary stuff. A new task at work, a meeting next week, putting up an add trying to sell some junk. My sleep crashes totally. In such periods, I think, think, think - and I love to think now that I can - and I get max 2-3 hours of sleep per night. Yet, I feel happy and enjoy every minute I'm awake (except for when I want to sleep at night), and I'm not tired.

### Inger — 2014-12-07T18:42:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-149425>

That is a lot of great stuff Rerun... love it

the last point.. do you mean you are more sensible? IDK I guess I have the same issue. I cannot stop thinking.

IDK if I am breaking down.. or lifted up. I am not sure about anything anymore.... and the boyfriend. We are just so different.... But I love him. my life is just so different from about everyone else and he seems to not get it, that it makes me happy..... and I think and think and think what do I do wrong... and darn I am so confused. I should be thankful for luxury and enjoyment but I just crave to go early to bed in the dark and to go out in the woods  I want to cry cry cry because I cannot figure it out..... I guess I am an alien  or I am just too sensible.. and the sleep deprivation and stuff is getting me finally.
Maybe I cant trust. Because of what happened to me before. But I try so hard. But how can I give up going early to bed... it makes me feel so bad when I get too little sleep. My priorities are so different... I am always looking for the truth... I cannot live moderately and luke warm  i want to do what is good for me, not what everyone else do. but I fear the boyfriend expects me to... to not be extreme.. he says that often.. and I already have done many things with him I would not normally do as In know they are not so good for me. But he kinda thinks it is all in my head. and that hurt. I am disappointed with me. I have not done a good job I guess. Or it is something else. I try to figure it out so hard.
But I so love all the oxy.. and to be close to the boyfriend.
I am just so lost right now.

Sorry to ramble in your journal Rerun... I just needed to share my pain.......

we can change ourselves.... always

### Andrea — 2014-12-08T12:10:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-149498>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=149425)
>
> Sorry to ramble in your journal Rerun... I just needed to share my pain.......

You're welcome to do that, Inger!
I hate to say this, but it is every person's own responsibility to do things that make them happy and well and to stay away from actions that do the opposite. I know you know that. But then we fall in love and we're in a pink fluffy heaven. All the hormones and substances that flow in our bodies make us pretty stupid.
You know, the "purpose" of love isn't for us to be happy and healthy. It is to produce viable offspring and help it survive until it can have its' own offspring.
I think you ought to stop once in a while, take a sober deep breath and think about where you're heading.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=149448)
>
> I love that you can look people in the eyes now Rerun. That is really huge thing IMHO. It is so valuable as you see the others soul somehow when you do that. I think. It is a connection....
> I am so happy for you

It is huge for me too. It is a connection. For me it's also a way to show my sincerity and respect for the other person. I have no idea why it has been impossible for me to look people in the eyes for a bunch of years, but I'm so happy I can do it again.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=149466)
>
> Great update rerun ...so many positives! How is your sleep???

Up and down. During the fall I thought I had sorted it out. Had a bunch of weeks with great sleep. But then I started to wind up about anything and everything, lay in bed at night thinking and thinking and thinking.
I hope we'll get a cold dry winter here. I'd love to go to my camping spot near work and sleep outdoors soon again. That brought me such tranquillity this fall.

> There is a sufi saint Hijra... an angel appears in his dream. He says to him that he should save as much
> water as possible because tomorrow morning all the water of this world is going to be poisoned by the devil,
> and everyone who will drink this water will become mad. So the fakir saves as much water, the whole night,
> as possible. And really the phenomenon happens. Everyone become mad that next morning. No one knows
> the whole city has become mad. Only the fakir is not mad, but the whole city talks as if the fakir has gone
> mad. He knows it is everyone else who is mad, but no one believes him. He goes on drinking his water and
> remains alone, but he cannot continue. The whole city is living in an altogether different world. No one
> listens to him, and now there is a rumor that he will be caught and sent to prison.
> They have come one morning to get hold of him. Either he should be treated as ill or should go to
> prison. He cannot be allowed freedom. He has become absolutely mad. Whatsoever he says is not
> understandable, he talks in a different language. The fakir is at a loss to understand. He tries to help them to
> remember their past, but they have forgotten everything. They do not know anything of the past, anything
> about before that maddening morning. They cannot understand, the fakir has become incomprehensible.
> They are around his house, they have caught hold of him.
> And the fakir says, "Give me one moment more. I shall treat myself." He runs to the common well,
> drinks the water, and he becomes all right. Now the whole city is happy. The fakir is okay now. Now he is
> not mad. Really, he has gone mad now! But now he is part and parcel of the common world. If everyone is
> asleep, you will never be aware even that you are asleep. If everyone is mad, and you are mad, you will
> never be aware of it.

### Andrea — 2015-01-04T10:29:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-151584>

Something weird is going on with my stomach. In the last 6 months, I've been sick 4 times, 2 of which this year!
Before that, the last time my stomach objected to something was in 2001: it didn't like me drinking a large glass of ice cold glacier water. And before that....? I have no idea, maybe never. My stomach has always been able to handle any garbage.

What the £$@#¤%&! is going on with me now?
And what is right and wrong to do with a sensitive stomach - what should I eat once my stomach clears out? Mackerel heads don't seem like a good choice for the time being...

### Inger — 2015-01-04T11:10:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-151587>

That is weird Rerun  try to think when it started.... could it be your gut adjusting to the new bacteria? I have a stomach like you.... could digest stones.... I do have had some minor issues maybe one or two times. I guess one time was when I ate raw prefrozen sardines and one of them looked weird with some weird skin lesions but I ate it anyways and after that I got a weird herpes on my lip and had weird pale stools for many days. But then it just healed like that and i decided to not eat sick looking sardines raw anymore

Yesterday I had a super fat butter coffee for breakfast and for lunch I did a smoothie with 2 herring heads, herring roe and fish milk.... and soaked seaweed a small handful. Then maybe 3 spoonful of butter and some pollen. I was not hungry for many hours after and indeed felt a little nausea.. a tiny bit... but it must just have been so much fat I guess! Later at 4 PM I ate a nice big dinner and everything was great!
The fish head and innards smoothies sure are bacteria bombs....err.... could it be you should better ditch the intestines? I use only fish roe and milk and sometimes liver. But then, the intestines could have its own benefits? I really am so clueless... no information to read about this stuff... we just have to try it out

What symptoms do you have exactly?

If you can get wormwood tea it could help, my mother has it in her garden and it really helps digestive issues. Very very bitter but great medicine. Helps kill parasites too... if that should be a issue.
I am a bit careful combining parasite loading with high EMF environment... i kind of have a feeling it could be not so clever......... but i could be all wrong. Maybe they help instead? Josh got help from his worms. But he live i think in a pretty good environment?

I do know high EMF exposure hurts the gut. My sister moved to a very low EMF are from the city... and from the first day on at the new place her diarrhea that no doctor could get rid of, disappeared... and everyone in her family felt so much better. It is crazy and scary stuff. You need to listen to the NYE Q&A that Yew posted. It has great stuff in it.... I listened to 60% of it yesterday and will do the rest today.....

### Andrea — 2015-01-04T12:06:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-151589>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=151587)
>
> That is weird Rerun  try to think when it started.... could it be your gut adjusting to the new bacteria? I have a stomach like you.... could digest stones....

Thanks, Inger!
I'll see if I can find some wormwood somewhere.

I suppose there are explanations to these 4 times:
1\. Late summer, I think. Chicken livers, previously frozen, quickly seared but mostly raw. I knew right away that one specific liver was different, but I couldn't imagine what it would do to me.
2\. In September or October. A piece of a fresh, raw fish. I think it might not have been as fresh as the fish monger said.
3\. New Years Day. Beef fat for breakfast, as usual. I didn't feel anything strange about it, yet it ruined the whole day.
4\. Dinner with a friend last night. I noticed that I still felt very full at 10 pm (4-5 hours after the dinner), and my stomach had somehow shut down, looking and feeling huge. The diarrhea and all started after I got up this morning. I think it was the duck liver mousse that we ate, or possibly one of the cheezes. Anyway, my friend spent much of the evening and parts of the night fraternizing with the toilet. My friend's reaction was far worse than mine, so far.

My symptoms are "usually" bloating and diarrhea about 2 hours after the meal, and about 4-6 hours after the meal I start throwing up.

I suppose my gut flora did change a bit during 2014, since I started to eat much, much more seafood then. I think I also increased my intake of completely raw food last year. But still, previously I have eaten smelly old fish or a yak that had died of natural causes and had been carried to the place where I ate it for several days without refridgeration - without any problems at all.

I know about bacteria in fish heads and innards, and I always keep that stuff on the coldest spot in my fridge (1-2°C) - as short time as possible. So far I haven't reacted to any of my fish heads though.

As for EMF, I know my neighbors have wifis all over the place and there are 3G and 4G towers on the rooftoops everywhere, not to mention electrical wires etc., but I haven't been able to measure alarmingly high values in most of my apartment. There are some spots that are worse than the rest and I avoid spending time there, but my bedroom seems surprisingly "clean". A large city in Sweden still small compared to cities on the continent or the US. I do have to move, nevertheless, I know that, and I am searching like crazy. Because to be honest, I realized I had health issues about 2 years after I had moved to the city. I changed from a skinny but very fit top athlete to a wreck in 3 years.

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=151587)
>
> ...i decided to not eat sick looking sardines raw anymore

Good decision!

### Inger — 2015-01-09T18:51:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-152226>

Anisakiasis can not reproduce in human.... they stay alive 2 weeks and then die... and thats it... I am not scared of them. I have read one can get some digestion issues tho...... i have not. I do pick the worms away that i can see......... i have had plenty of anisakiasis mackerels.... raw and unfrozen. But this is not something i would recommend to anyone, just my ownj N=1
I know context matters...........

I am just not scared pretty much at all of parasites i have to say....... i sure would go to the doc if I would see any in my stool.. or get any issues!
I actually think bugs are my friends too and they are very much alive so if i have some bugs in me maybe i just get more alive too.... lol
I always think about children.. and how they sit on the ground and eat pretty much anything  I have come to the conclusion it is wasted time to be too scared of that stuff.... so far it had served me well... who know what the future will bring! I sure will report when having any troubles

I prefer to know that they are completely harmless by freezing them first.

But I make exceptions. When I visited my parents for Christmas they had bought a whole fresh fish, including the head. Dad cleaned the fish and was preparing the "spare parts" so that mom could make a fish soup of them. When he put the head aside, I couldn't resist poking one of the eyes out (man, it was big!). He was sort of disgusted so he turned away. So I quickly ate it.

I simply can't ignore it or hide from it. Not anymore...

### Andrea — 2015-05-22T08:52:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-165013>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=164934)
>
> and what is this strong magnetic sensation you cant ignore anymore.... what is it?    that sounds so cool

It's funny, but I'm sure you have felt it too. When certain people come a bit too close, you feel uncomfortable. With other ones, usually people you like and care for, it's completely ok for them to come close.

For me, there is one person, whose presence I can feel a few meters away. I just know he's around. I have felt this on the few occasions that we have met over the years. But we never got to really talk or get to know one another. Yet I have always felt some strange joy when we've seen each other.
Now we have met and talked, started to get to know each other. The closer we are, physically, the stronger this feeling of connection is. He feels it too, it's not just my imagination. When he gave me a hug I thought my heart would explode. And when he looked into my eyes... Wow... That's just indescribable.

I've been in love before in my life, but hell, I've never ever felt this way. It reminds me so much of magnetic fields and magnetic attraction. Then again, the heart is a big magnet...
But why? Why the two of us? I have no idea.

### caroline — 2015-05-22T11:17:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-165026>

I completely understand this now ..... and there is no turning back - quantum entanglement to the max.

If you allow yourself to be fully open .....and accept what is coming into your life - it is such magic!

When your hands and heart are open - you do receive gifts! I am living, breathing proof!

The thing is - this impacts on everyone around you..... we are radiating and attracting.......

The other day a teacher friend of mine spent 2 hours talking to her class about us .... the kids were fascinated by the whole story of our citizenship ceremonies and our engagement ....and what countries we have come from ...and on and on.

It was so lovely ....and so positive... and everyone has the biggest grin on their face......

### Andrea — 2015-08-05T08:38:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-173996>

What an amazing day I had yesterday!
Started off with raw herring with onion and a mashed mackerel head.
The day was warm and shiny, so I took a very long lunch break and spent 1.5 hours on the beach. The ocean is quite cool this year, only 17-18C (63F), even though it's August already. But I don't complain!
Another good thing is that cool water = less people on the beach.

An older gentleman came there at the same time as I, but no one else was there. The gentleman and I swam in the ocean together for a while, chatting, then he got up, put on his shoes and continued his run in the forest. I took my chances... I took my swim clothes off and lay naked on a rock in the sun.  Interesting... After a while I heard voices though, so I put my bikini bottoms on.
The rest of the workday passed easily.

In the afternoon, I shared electrons with a special person. How intense! Just touching each others skin was... Brilliant! Late in the evening he said that he had completely forgotten to eat that afternoon. He always eats in the afternoon... But he wasn't really hungry...
Go figure...

Wonder if I can ever be the sun in his universe.......

### Andrea — 2015-08-27T16:54:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-175869>

I visited a cardiologist today, for some sort of yearly checkup. They've been watching my cholesterol values for a couple years, eagerly trying to sell me statins.
Last time they checked my labs was in December 2013, which was about the time I had started Leptin Rx, CT, Epi-Paleo, blueblockers ets. This time I wanted to see my labs at the end of the summer (which unfortunately has been crappy with rain most of the time, and sunshine only for 2 weeks in August)...

So...

Dec-13:
TG: 0.97 mmol/l (0.45-2.6)
HDL: 2.1 mmol/l (1.0-2.7)
LDL: 9.6 mmol/l (1.4-4.7)

Aug-15:
TG: 0.72
HDL: 2.1
LDL: 5.5

My ASAT and ALAT are lower now, too.

The doc was quite confused. He was convinced that I have familial hypercholesterolemia, and this just didn't make sense.... He didn't try to sell statins to me though.

I just wish I could have got more sunshine...

I always wish for more sunshine too....... I can never get enough! But I tan every single moment I can  and I am lucky to be able to tan a lot nude... like I do.. right now

### Andrea — 2015-09-23T19:40:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-177668>

So, I had an appointment with my endocrinologist last week. That's the guy who told me 2 years ago that there is no more help for me and that I have to accept that I'm feeling crappy, that I most probably never will get any better. Back then I thought that I'd never go see him again, but when I got the notice the other week I decided to go anyway.

He was pleased to see that my thyroid labs look good. T4, fT4, fT3 are in the reference interval. Also, TSH was 3.3 mIU/L (ref. 0.3-4.2). That's the first time for many years that TSH is within the reference interval.

My folate is on the low side of the normal interval, even though I am homozygous for MTHFR C677T. I assume that's not a bad sign.
B12 is elevated though, 740 pmol/l (ref. 140-500) (I'm heterozygous for MTRR A664A).

Anyway, the doc asked me how I was doing and what I have changed since last time we met. I smiled gently and told him that I have stopped taking the synthetic hormones he had prescribed and that I spend as much time as I can outdoors, in the sun, wearing as little clothes as possible. He just smiled. Probably thinking I was from a different planet...
But... He seemed a little interested when I mentioned MTHFR and MTRR, and said he would look it up. That's a huge change, no matter if he actually will look it up or not. Because previously, when I said something, he would ridicule me. I somehow got the feeling that now he knows that I know something.

All in all, I'm glad I went to see him. I probably won't go to see him again, but I'm glad I did this time.

### Andrea — 2015-09-26T15:11:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-177922>

So... What has Rerun been up to lately?

Well... Many of the nights between work days from mid August to mid September I camped at a nice spot near work, close to the ocean. I eventually bought myself a tent, because the air is pretty wet in the early mornings, plus that it rains often and quite a lot now... I decided to get a cheap 15 EUR tent... And it certainly sucks!  But hey, it's an adventure... Sometimes I've brought it to work and hung it up in an empty office to get it dry. People, including my boss, just smile.
But I'm done with camping for a while now. It's just way too rainy now. I hope we'll get a cold, somewhat dry winter...

Some random observations:

-   In August, I had such crazy cravings for cheese, which suddenly disappeared.

-   Ticks don't seem to be very attracted to me this year.
-   Since beginning of September I've been struggling with acne. (Hello autumn & winter, bye bye sunshine...)
-   I've eaten quite a lot of apples - still not ripe though - off an apple tree near work the last month, but the last couple of days I have had no more desire to eat them. (Which I guess is a good thing considering it's autumn now.)
-   6-12 days after a dopamine rush  (no additional dopamine during this time) I feel very crappy, lonely and depressed. It gets better after day 12 though.
-   My cycles seem to be getting shorter.

### Andrea — 2015-11-20T16:21:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-182425>

In my early 20's I found out that I have partial red-green color blindness. It hasn't been a problem to live with, except that I've always failed the color vision tests I've had to do - unless I memorized the answers to all slides of the Ishihara color vision test. Anyway, I did the Ishihara test, unprepared, the other day. I got 38 out of 38 correct. I did struggle with some slides, but I did get them all correct!
Thanks, dr. K.!

### Andrea — 2015-12-12T14:36:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-183958>

Tried something new today. For me anyway... A regular sauna.
Sat in 84°C (183F) for a while, then walked to the ocean (7°C / 45F) and took a dip to cool down, back in, out, in, out. The other ladies got a bit worried when I stayed in the water so long on my last dip....  They sort of plunged in with strange sounds and literately flew out at once.

I noticed that my skin got numb, in a different way than when I take a dip without sauna. Numb and sort of energized. Interesting sensation.

Not sure it's worth the money to pursue this though. I can have access to a sauna without ocean (just cool shower) much cheaper elsewhere, and I have free access to the ocean without sauna...

### Andrea — 2016-07-19T18:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rerun-here-i-am-and-now-what.9116/post-198601>

Time for an update... What has Rerun done and learnt the last couple of months?

\* April-May seems to be a weight dropping period for me. Last year I lost about 13 kg (28 lb) this time of year, this year another 5 kg. At that point I weighed just slightly more than when I was in excellent shape many years ago. Of course, my body composition is different now and I'm not as strong and fit, but that’s no surprise since I haven't worked out at all for several years.
\* The brain IS an energy hog. Now that my body works more as it ought to, intensive thinking and analyzing (everything else as usual) can make me lose 1 kg a day.
\* Now my body tolerates a long fast. I just happened to do 72 hours with only water and I felt ok, just a little cold. The first meal was a small serving of raw herring, but I wasn’t really hungry.
\* Crappy weather in the summer makes me behave and feel crappy. Doh.
\* Carbs in the summer with crappy weather give me acne, just as they do in winter.
\* Too much sunshine in my face gives me herpes on my lips.
\* My sleep is much, much better when I’m close to a person that I care for.
\* Love doesn’t hurt. Inability to communicate it does.

# Thread: Grounding questions.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-12-16T18:36:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103600>

Hi guys.

I have been reading up on EMFs and grounding and am rather confused.

1\. If I ground via a steak in the ground outside leading to a grounding mat on my bed, can dirty electricity jump onto this mat and lead to extra electricity going through me? If so this would be counter productive, yes.

2\. Can you test for dirty electricity by using a multimeter? If your meter reads zero volts can there still be dirty electricity present.

3\. If using a grounding mat lowers my body voltage is this good regardless or is there hidden dangers that could make it bad?

Thanks in advance!

### nonchalant — 2013-12-17T01:41:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103628>

Yeah, I understand your confusion. I used to sleep on a grounded sheet (to the earth outside) and it did wonders for my back. But after my back improved, I started getting some inflammatory issues that caused me to stop using the sheet. If you want to prevent the sheet acting like an antenna for artificial EMF, you could wrap it around you.
I still ground myself (ankle strap connected to stake in the ground) when I take ice baths; I've found that it increases my ability to comfortably tolerate the cold.

If my back were to get in the same condition it was a year or two ago, I would start using the grounding sheet at night right away.

### seanb4 — 2013-12-17T10:20:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103652>

So the problem with grounding/earthing mats is that the larger surface area picks up dirty electricity, were as a grounding strap has a smaller surface area so this doesn't happen?

You say you got inflammatory issues. How do you know this? Did you get tests or was it just symptoms? What symptoms?

### Tanya — 2013-12-17T16:22:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103661>

seanb4, I am new to this website and not an expert in any of this, but I can share with you my experience with the grounding. Please pardon my English first as it is not my first language. I’ve been using a combination of Magnetico matress, grounding bed sheet, and several grounding pads and straps - all of them connected to the outside of my house to combat my RA. After seeing a discussion here about the possibility of exposing myself to a dirty EMF, I immediately removed the grounding sheet from my bed, but my inflammation and pain increased significantly only after just one week being without it. I put it back again and measured myself with a body voltage meter. The results of that testing left no doubt for me that I definitely benefit a lot from doing the grounding the way I was doing it before. Maybe that is what you need to do yourself - test it and see how you react. Hope this helps.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-17T16:39:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103665>

Tanya, that is great advice, checking with a body voltage meter. Everyone is at a different health level, and everyone's environment is different. Plus in QED nothing can be taken for granted.
I also removed my sheet when the "antenna issue" first arose, but after just a few days, I replaced the sheet.

Sean, I started getting edema, and that was when I stopped with the grounding sheet. I also stopped using magnets during the day. Fortunately for me, my back had healed nicely, and I didn't miss the sheet at that (later) time. I think the grounding strap for me is good because I only use it for an hour. I also use magnets during CT, but again, only for an hour. I wouldn't remove my Magnetico pad for anything, on the other hand.

I do miss my daytime magnets, however. My tight throat has been creeping back. Need. to. leave. city.

### seanb4 — 2013-12-17T17:06:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103668>

Thanks for the information Tanya and Nonchalant.

Okay to clarify:
If your body voltage goes down when you ground then it is beneficial compared to no grounding at all?
If there is dirty electricity then does this show as a rise in body voltage when "grounding" or can there be damaging dirty electricity even though your body voltage drops? Can there be dirty electricity at 0V?

Also I would like to try to get free electrons from magnets but the magnetico pads are way too expensive for me. Any cheap alternatives?

### Tanya — 2013-12-17T18:50:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103673>

Sean, I’ll give it another try, but I hope that someone who is more knowledgeable will chime in. After I saw my body voltage drop to the ideal number while being grounded, I assumed that there was no dirty electricity issue to worry about at that point. As far as the magnetico pad - it is the best one there is. You can definitely find some cheaper ones, but the question is if you will get as much benefit from them as from magnetico. Magnetico’s price went up a lot since I purchased mine a few years ago... I understand how it might be difficult to be able to afford one right now. I saw a post somewhere on this site mentioning a discount arrangement with Dr. Kruse - I don’t know if it is still active.

### seanb4 — 2013-12-17T23:11:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103687>

Okay, yeah even with discount it would be too much. I think I read somewhere that putting two magnets under your bed has a similar effect...

### seanb4 — 2013-12-19T17:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103850>

Okay so I got my voltmeter today and I measure at roughly 4v in my bed room. When I use my grounding strap, that's connected to earth outside, it drops to 0.6v. So as long as there are no random spikes, does this mean I don't have to worry about dirty electricity and I should use the grounding strap without negative consequences?

### nonchalant — 2013-12-19T20:26:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103855>

Sean, there are no guarantees where EMF is concerned. Glad you got the meter, it will help you figure out your environment.
I personally think the grounding strap will help, though you might not notice the difference during sleep. If you use it during CT, you might notice a nice difference right away.

### JoeBranca — 2013-12-19T20:29:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103856>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=103850)
>
> Okay so I got my voltmeter today and I measure at roughly 4v in my bed room. When I use my grounding strap, that's connected to earth outside, it drops to 0.6v. So as long as there are no random spikes, does this mean I don't have to worry about dirty electricity and I should use the grounding strap without negative consequences?

It's something I've been wondering ... does grounding oneself to reduce body voltage necessarily mean an improvement in the situation? If I understand correctly, once you ground yourself, using the body voltmeter method you're now measuring the difference between something grounded and something grounded, so by definition yes it should be very close to zero if it's sufficient grounding. However, if you had a positive body voltage to begin with, once you ground yourself you now have an electric current, which should be measured differently than with a voltmeter (ampmeter?). Or, perhaps body voltages would measure better in that case across head to toe. Unsure how to go about that for an accurate picture.

Spending more money and focus on eliminating or reducing the sources of the electric fields (or ground them instead of oneself) that are producing significant body voltages in the first place seems like a safer approach.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-19T20:37:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-103858>

Good questions, Joe. I ground myself (other than walking barefoot) only a little in the city now. When I'm in the country, however, I sleep with a grounding sheet every night. And the yard around the house out there is getting very clean of stickers, cactus, etc. Nice to be able to walk confidently!

I think everyone would have to try grounding out to see if it helped them or not. If you have some serious issues, like I had with my back, I personally think I would definitely try it.

### Inger — 2013-12-22T11:31:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-104034>

Sean I have the same issue with my grounding rod. Not sure if it is good or not! After my latest AM radio measurements I am even more unsure.... But I know how to get the answer. We need to make a study, and that is; Buy some flowers, put half of them in a glass and the other half in another glass. Same glass same water. Put them close to another on the same place/in same room (but not touching) and put your grounding rod in one of the glasses with flowers. Then wait and see which one will last longer! If the grounding rod flower glass will last longer we know it will be a good thing

I have wanted to do this many times already but every time I bought flowers they went to something else! 1st time it was fathers day... and the yellow roses I have bought for my experiment just looked so pretty on the fathers day table so I just had to give them away! Next time I bought some red roses and they looked too pretty to just keep in the bedroom for an experiment so there it went again.... I need to get more disciplined!!! I am just too addicted to beauty in my life

### Inger — 2013-12-22T20:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-104053>

No other stuff Sean! Buy flowers! Like roses or other flowers, I bought 10 pieces for 3 €, they have those great offers once in a while. You could do 10 and 10. The roses all look the same, full grown, no seeds! You must take care the roses are close and same light, temp and same room. Easy!

### Chiquita — 2014-01-06T04:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-104857>

You guys can ground to a water faucet in your house and get the same benefit as driving a stake in the ground outside. Water lines are grounded. You also get grounded every time you take a shower. The stream from the shower head and the water you are standing in is grounded. I believe all this grounding thing is a power of suggestion thing.

### seanb4 — 2014-01-06T09:59:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-104859>

Grounding via a water faucet is good to know. Could be useful. Thanks Chiquita.

### Inger — 2014-01-09T14:17:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-105148>

Ok Sean, I did the flower + grounding rod experiment and the results are clear to me now! I put 12 white roses in two glasses of water, 6 and 6. In one I put my grounding rod in the glass/water. I had them in m y bedroom 5 cm from each other so they would not touch.
Now after two weeks or so the one with the grounding rod is fresher looking, no flowers are brown, and the leaves are way fresher too. The other non grounded glass have 2 roses that are pretty dead, brown and hanging and also the green/leaves are hanging and the other roses too look less fresh. It is pretty amazing what difference! Not huge as I do not want to wait longer (because I want to use the grounding rod myself lol), but very clear! I am pretty sure I had exactly the same amount of water in them to begin with, and the grounded glass have about 1,5 cm more water now.... like it used less water? (But both glasses do still have are more than half full with water to make sure they will not die from too little water) But I need to redo the experiment to be sure.
I will repeat the experiment (I think as it gets warmer, this spring. because it gets cold now and the roses + the water might freeze in my bedroom) and then I will measure the water too exactly, and then I will put a copper rod in the non grounded glass too, just to check that it is not the copper that preserves them. I doubt so, but that would make the experiment even better.

So from tonight I use my grounding rod again with no doubts =)

Iif you have tested yours with a similar experiment and what come out, let me know, ok!

### seanb4 — 2014-01-09T14:33:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-105149>

Sounds very promising. Good work! Will definitely look forward to the follow up experiment...take photos.

Yeah I've stopped using my ground wire the last couple days as the wire keeps braking, I need to buy a thicker one. Anyway I have a headache today, although I did drink more water yesterday, 3L, so could be to do with that or something else.

How well does your rod ground you? My current set-up only gets me to roughly 0.3V I think. I'll remeasure when I use a thicker wire.

### Inger — 2014-01-09T14:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-105152>

I do not have stuff to measure that with Sean. But I have a pretty thick rod! The thickest I got, there are many many thin copper wires in it. It goes to the river/waterfall... it is maybe 20 meters long or so. In the river I have a real grounding rod tied to the one that I use for grounding rod. It is rubber/plastic around the long rod but I have peeled it so I can tie it around my ankle. I also have a grounding strap, I sometimes use that it fells a bit more comfortable that the copper rod to the skin.

I need to get the stuff you are measuring with! Would be interesting to know.

### seanb4 — 2014-01-09T15:01:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-questions.9117/post-105154>

Sounds like a good set-up you've got there. I bought a multi meter from ebay, was like Â£20. You've got to make sure it can measure ac voltage in millivolts or something. Basic multimeter's aren't accurate enough for what we need.

### healthman — 2013-12-16T19:56:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103607>

Hi
Can anyone tell me if this Sauerkraut is okay to eat?

[http://www.tesco.com/groceries/Product/Details/?id=262299302](http://www.tesco.com/groceries/Product/Details/?id=262299302)

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-16T20:07:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103613>

kraut is always a go too food.

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-16T20:23:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103618>

It will have been pasteurised meaning all the beneficial bacteria will be dead. Ideally you should make your own.

Last year I saw what looked like fresh sauerkraut in the refrigerated section at Lidl. I've never tried it though.

### elvey90 — 2013-12-17T04:24:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103637>

How can you tell if sauerkraut is pasteurised or not when buying in the supermarket? Or is that a given?

### caroline — 2013-12-17T06:38:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103649>

I am pretty sure it is a given. - but they do have a brand in the States that is good ....bubbles - I think.

I saw some at a shop in Bondie Junction that was $20.00 for a smallish jar....I didn't read the label tho.

### Hemming — 2013-12-17T09:23:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103651>

Its pretty easy to make yourself and once you have the first batch it gets even easier. The biggest problem is storing the glasses

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-17T12:01:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103653>

> [elvey90 said:](/goto/post?id=103637)
>
> How can you tell if sauerkraut is pasteurised or not when buying in the supermarket? Or is that a given?

If it's at room temperature it would keep fermenting and the product would be way over fermented. So they heat it up to kill off the fermentation process so that it can be kept at room temperature.

I don't know if Lidl's is fresh or not but I saw them in bags in the refrigerated section which made me think it might be fresh.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sauerkraut.9119/post-103654>

>
> I don't know if Lidl's is fresh or not but I saw them in bags in the refrigerated section which made me think it might be fresh.

Lidl's - is that a supermarket chain? Do you have that in the UK? just wondering....

### Ricardo — 2013-12-16T20:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnulin-pf.9120/post-103617>

I was taking quite a bit of cinnamon then I heard that it contains substances that can accumulate in the system so I looked for a better option. Now I take much less cinnamon and I have added Cinnulin PF. It is a water soluble extract that has proven to have some interesting benefits.

Cinnulin PF will lower fasting blood glucose and systolic blood pressure. It will also help increase lean body mass.

Its helping me to get through the Leptin RX reset.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2129164/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2129164/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-learn-about-obesity-researchers-turn-to-grizzly-bears.9124/post-103655>

> But bears do know a thing or two about weight gain, and that makes them more like people than rats whose genes are manipulated to make them obese. In the weeks before hibernating, bears pack away enough apples, berries and salmon to put on 100 pounds or more under their brown fur. Their bad cholesterol jumps and blood pressure spikes. But unlike humans, their health doesn't suffer. Bears' arteries don't clog from the gorging, nor do the animals battle heart attacks or turn into diabetics.

[http://online.wsj.com/news/articles...4081632?mod=WSJ\_business\_LeftSecondHighlights](http://online.wsj.com/news/articles/SB10001424052702303330204579246003604081632?mod=WSJ_business_LeftSecondHighlights)

# Thread: Man's severed hand grafted to ankle after accident
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mans-severed-hand-grafted-to-ankle-after-accident.9130/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-12-18T12:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mans-severed-hand-grafted-to-ankle-after-accident.9130/post-103734>

[http://www.theguardian.com/world/video/2013/dec/17/severed-hand-grafted-ankle-accident-video](http://www.theguardian.com/world/video/2013/dec/17/severed-hand-grafted-ankle-accident-video)

Doctors in China have saved a man's severed hand by grafting it onto his ankle. Xiao Wei lost his right hand in an accident at work, but could not have it reattached to his arm immediately. Instead the hand was kept alive by stitching it to Xiao's left ankle, enabling him to borrowing a blood supply from arteries in the leg

### Flora — 2013-12-18T15:28:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortico-basal-degeneration.9132/post-103740>

My brother has just been diagnosed with CBD. He's gone from having slight neuropathy in his fingertips to losing the use of his left arm in the last 2 years. He also has sleep apnea and uses a CPAP machine. He's 63.

He was an Air Force (UK) pilot for 30+ years and flew every type of fighter jet -and clearly this is the end result. This Australian report on high-flying pilots is interesting [http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/265036.php](http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/265036.php)

I have sent him and his wife lots of specific blogs / threads / summaries from this site to read and I know they have already started to implement some things - turning off the wi-fi and removing the computer from the bedroom and eating more fish as a good start - and I've sent them coconut oil.
He is taking cod liver oil, B vitamins (presumably in a complex format) and an Ibuprofen tablet(!) each day - as suggested by their doctor and Neurologist.

Unfortunately I don't see him often as we live in different countries, but am desperate to get him to try CT, a ketogenic seafood diet, grounding etc. He's no longer working since a week ago-which is good because he no longer has to undergo the EMF soup of a train commute to the City each day and being attached to a computer all day.

I would just like to know if there is anything else I can suggest to them - am thinking that PQQ and COQ10 could be a good supplements? or if anyone has any experience of this neuro-degenerative disease or similar (eg Parkinson's) where they have seen improvements or a slowing of disease progress from adopting epi-paleo, as I know the prognosis is not good according to conventional medicine.

Dr Kruse - I would be so grateful for any general input from you...

Many thanks

# Thread: Salt

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-19T01:03:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/salt.9138/post-103800>

Something that I've been considering lately in my attempt to synthesize Dr. Kruse and Ray Peat is salt intake. This is something that Ray Peat speaks about extensively but Dr. Kruse hasn't seemed to address much. Common medical advice ad many paleo advocates say to limit or eliminate your salt intake. However, Ray Peat states that we should increase our salt intake substantially because it promotes energy production, reduces inflammation and adrenaline secretion, reduces activity of the sympathetic nervous system and promotes proper hydration. He also cites evidence showing that pure water causes a negative prolactin release in animals but salted water does not. Inspired by this idea, as well as research by Dr. Batmanghelidj, has inspired me to begin adding 1/2 a teaspoon of salt to all liquids I consume. Does anyone know Dr. Kruse's perspective on salt intake? Is 1/2 teaspoon to 1 teaspoon of supplemental salt negative, as proposed by most paleo-ers or is it beneficial because of it's effects on stress, hydration, etc.? Can it be susd to promote more effective hydration than just RO water? Any and all thoughts are greatly appreciated.

I second embs2001's idea. I have so many questions for you about Peat's work however I don't want to flood your forum with questions! I've been controlling myself. Back to the topic, however. Doesn't salt allow body to properly use water/potassium as shown by Dr. Batmanghelidj's work? So it seems like adding salt to water will help the body achieve proper hydration. If it truly is just a water problem and not related to salt at all, why is drinking pure water stressful eg. induces a release of prolactin and activation of sympathetic nervous system, plus it activating the aldosterone system which has a variety of negative effects. So it seems like eating supplemental salt is only beneficial. Are there negatives to it that lead to you not recommending it?

# Thread: So your supposed to be dead at 6 according to you doc?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-19T03:14:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-your-supposed-to-be-dead-at-6-according-to-you-doc.9139/post-103818>

you want to see visual success?

Meet my buddy Jeremy.

His mom told that he would not make it past 6 yrs old.............because he has a fatal disease.

He is now 31.

Here is what he does on "poor" lung function.

[http://vimeo.com/57462136](http://vimeo.com/57462136)

BOOM........

or this one: [http://vimeo.com/48052050](http://vimeo.com/48052050)

Jeremy will be doing a blog for us and a webinar soon...............

### Josh — 2013-12-19T03:51:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-your-supposed-to-be-dead-at-6-according-to-you-doc.9139/post-103821>

Death and disability are often safe calls for doctors...

I was told to go on disability 8 years ago by a friend who is a doctor, so I went out and got a job running a landscape company and switched doctors. Still going, still get up most days and work...

I am reminded of the many stories in Gerson's "50 Cases of Cancer Cured" and of Gerson's own struggle to prove healing was possible from the "incurable". In his biography by his grandson there is a great story in of how his adolescent daughters went to the NIH and said something like "my daddy cures cancer" and they said something like "yah right". The daughters asked how many cases they needed for proof so it started with 4 or so, then 10, then 25. At that point, as I recall, Gerson told his daughters to forget it because the NIH would never recognize his work no matter how many cases he sent. Dr. Gonzalez in NYC, one of the heirs to Gerson's work through Dr. Kelley, has similar stories about the studies he conducted that the NIH and FDA refused to acknowledge the results of. How Gerson discovered his "protocol" and how it evolved was amazing to me. The coffee enemas came from German field hospitals in WWI. They used enemas for pain relief in surgery because they had no narcotics and analgesics. The surgeons lived on coffee and there was a water shortage so the nurses starting putting the leftover coffee in the enemas and noticed increased pain relief....In "50 Cases", his discussions of intracellular potssium as the key to intracellular function in key tissue groups such as the liver and the brain was timely. I wonder if he knew Ling's work? The story is that the final draft of the book was stolen off of his desk and at the same time he came down with Arsenic poisoning. He rewrote the book and died a year later. A cautionary tale...

The building in the picture below in being erected next to the one I work in (company expansion). The security person showed me something he found unusual on the security monitors early the other morning. Said that it happens periodically on the night shift.

It was still dark outside, but on the monitors you could see pulses of energy(?) coming down from the sky at an angle and into the iron that is sticking up out of the ground. You cannot see it happen with the naked eye.

I assume this has something to do with grounding as these are driven deep into the ground. Would love to hear some thoughts on what it is and why it happens.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose.9143/post-103891>

I have experimented extensively up to 100 grams per day with no ill effects if I keep up the magnesium and CoQ. The GRAS (Generally Regarded As Safe) level is 20 grams per day. My wife baked Christmas cakes with it and they are wonderful. I recommend powdering it in a coffee grinder before mixing it in recipes, seems to work better. It is very hydrophilic which means it dissolves very easily in polar solvents such as water and milk. Also seems to mix well with melted chocolate if it is powdered.

### persistence — 2014-03-12T05:50:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose.9143/post-114954>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=103891)
>
> I have experimented extensively up to 100 grams per day with no ill effects if I keep up the magnesium and CoQ. The GRAS (Generally Regarded As Safe) level is 20 grams per day. My wife baked Christmas cakes with it and they are wonderful. I recommend powdering it in a coffee grinder before mixing it in recipes, seems to work better. It is very hydrophilic which means it dissolves very easily in polar solvents such as water and milk. Also seems to mix well with melted chocolate if it is powdered.

Josh, you tested out 100 grams a day on yourself against a 20 gram known safe limit? Wow, you the man.

I see the most likely negative side effect of Ribose is hypoglycemia. What kind of glucose numbers do you normally have three hours after meals during the day, and what were those numbers on 100 grams of D-Ribose? Or do you even use a glucometer?

### persistence — 2014-03-12T06:18:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose.9143/post-114957>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=103861)
>
> Has anyone tried cooking/baking with D-ribose? Is heat a problem? I know it goes well in hot coffee or tea.

nonchalant, I decided to research this the hard way. So far the only half life study for ribose I could find was this chemical analysis:

[http://www.pnas.org/content/92/18/8158.full.pdf](http://www.pnas.org/content/92/18/8158.full.pdf)

Unfortunately I am not sure if what they call "ribose" is what we buy as "D-ribose" so we should first calibrate to the right type of ribose referenced in that paper. They talk about three different forms. At roughly 212 degrees Farenheit the half life is around 73 minutes. That's not great, as most baking recipes would probably have the temperature well above that. What we really need is a study that has a fuller range of temperatures, so we can interpolate more precise values.

The other point that I saw in my reading is that Ribose creates more advanced glycation end products (AGEs) than glucose does, and that's not good news. AGEs are incredibly nasty and harmful. See for example:

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3704564/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3704564/)

Based on that, I think Ribose would be best used in liquids. If I were mixing it into a chocolate / cacao, I would probably mix it in at the very end, then quickly pour into the chocolate mold and refrigerate.

I don't have enough information yet, but I probably would not use it in baking. Higher temperatures for extended periods of time mean shorter half-life, and you increase the risk of AGEs.

But, then again, I am trying to be conservative. As the study I linked above shows, AGE data on food items containing glucose are hard enough to find. The odds anyone studied D-Ribose AGE products is somewhere between zero and zero point zero one.

### Josh — 2014-03-12T07:08:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose.9143/post-114958>

I had no glucose problems with it. I still routinely use 30grams many days. I use it alone or in chocolate mostly. The Christmas cake used almond flour with nuts and cacao and a little dried fruit.
One needs some sort of entity to buy wholesale from bioenergy, 10kg minimum.

### persistence — 2014-03-12T07:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose.9143/post-114959>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=114958)
>
> I had no glucose problems with it. I still routinely use 30grams many days. I use it alone or in chocolate mostly. The Christmas cake used almond flour with nuts and cacao and a little dried fruit.

Can you share what are your glucose numbers with and without d-ribose?

> One needs some sort of entity to buy wholesale from bioenergy, 10kg minimum.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-20T00:35:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-support-that-says-birhtcontrol-has-negative-effects-on-womens-health.9144/post-103879>

The best birth control is a rubber........not drugs.

### Josh — 2013-12-20T01:55:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-support-that-says-birhtcontrol-has-negative-effects-on-womens-health.9144/post-103884>

In the early 1980's, I worked on resettling refugees from SE Asia in New York and Philadelphia. They were giving the women implants in the refugee camps before they came here. Many of them developed health issues and became sterile. When I began practicing years later, I was shocked to learn that these drugs were now approved and regularly given to women starting as early as adolescence. Of course I have seen many of the same issues in practice that the refugee women complained of in the early 1980's.

### Hope — 2013-12-20T02:03:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-support-that-says-birhtcontrol-has-negative-effects-on-womens-health.9144/post-103885>

I have heard that some of the work Bill Gates does in Africa includes giving women vaccinations of some kind that also can make them less fertile, maybe sterile. They talk of population control. Made me want to puke. Not sure if it is true, but...

### Josh — 2013-12-20T03:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-support-that-says-birhtcontrol-has-negative-effects-on-womens-health.9144/post-103890>

I believe what they were testing on the refugees was the early versions of Depo Provera.

### cantweight — 2013-12-20T06:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-support-that-says-birhtcontrol-has-negative-effects-on-womens-health.9144/post-103897>

Man, I used nuva ring for years. Everyday I see another commercial for some drug some doc prescribed me...vioxx, phen-fen, yaz. Im amazed Im still here! This sickens me. As someone with a latex allergy condoms are not an option and the lambskin ones would have put us in the poor house! Nuva ring was the only birth control my body tolerated. I cant imagine how much of the hormone shit storm I am dealing with comes from all of these things that were govt approved and considered safe. It disgusts me!

At 12 yrs of age my daughter was telling me of girlfriends on the pill to help regulate their periods....my heart breaks for these girls and the path they are being lead down at such an early age.

### Inger — 2013-12-20T10:15:11+0200

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-20T15:51:36+0200

# Thread: Talk to me about cold  damp fresh air
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2013-12-20T00:23:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/post-103875>

We are having a patch of damp cool weather here in the UK winter and I am feeling a tremendous urge to go outside and breathe in huge lungfuls of this lovely damp fresh air - it makes me feel so refreshed and if I have a headache it disappears instantly when I go outside. I am planning to put my tent up in the garden this weekend so that I can sleep outdoors in this air - I know that it will make me sleep very deeply.

Can anyone explain why this cool damp air is so beneficial please? The dampness seems particularly important as well as the coolness. My friends don't seem to notice how wonderful it is but for me it seems like a powerful medicine.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-20T00:28:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/post-103877>

1\. Cool= Cold Thermogenesis=Hall effect= better energy production in your cells = EE10. Simple.
2\. Damp= natural water recycled by mother Earth to re structure itself in aerosols in your lungs from the Earth natural cycles. This helps with O2 production and energy see cool above.

### Hope — 2013-12-20T02:04:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/post-103886>

Another epic. This must explain why my favorite outside temp is the 40s-50s. I thought it's bc my ancestry is Northern European...

### Hemming — 2013-12-20T10:11:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/post-103898>

Perhaps somewhat related [http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/magazine-21537988](http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/magazine-21537988) . The same goes for Denmark, infants always nap outside regardless of the weather and we have the same saying - there is no bad weather only bad clothing

### Inger — 2013-12-20T10:12:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/post-103899>

Oh yes! I LOVE cool damp air too!!!!! I loved the climate in Stavanger/Norway by the ocean. It was often very damp, and pretty cool but perfect temp for me! I feel super energized  it is just that kind of wetter here today... light rain/rainy and +4 degree C Nice!

### agatha — 2013-12-20T23:27:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/post-103936>

Yes, I think my ancestry is probably Scandinavian too given my surname and skin/hair colouring. Interesting link Hemming - those lucky, lucky babies!! I wish someone would tuck me into a fur lined pram for a lovely outdoor nap.

Most English people seem to find damp rainy weather profoundly depressing but I find myself super energised and almost euphoric in mood, especially when it's cool too. It's especially good to go into a damp forest - the smell of the damp undergrowth is beyond wonderful. I would like to understand more about what the dampness does in the lungs and how it seems to lift the mood. But why do others hate this kind of weather? How are they different I wonder?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-21T06:17:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/talk-to-me-about-cold-damp-fresh-air.9147/post-103948>

Dont worry about them.........when you are wonderful.

# Thread: Do I Want Oxidized Cells?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-want-oxidized-cells.9148/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-20T02:20:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-want-oxidized-cells.9148/post-103887>

Hello everyone, I hope my constant posts aren't getting excessive. I've been trying to understand the effects of redox potential on cell physiology and function lately and it's terribly fascinating. I've been skimming the theories of Gilbert Ling and Szent-Gyorgi (both of whom saw redox potential as chief regulators of cell function). I've been considering Dr. Kruse's idea that the cell should be more reduced, but I cannot figure out his reasoning. According to this guy here, [http://pranarupa.wordpress.com/page/2/](http://pranarupa.wordpress.com/page/2/),
"the increased CO2 is known to improve muscle relaxation, this is no doubt due to its capacity to restore the cell to healthy oxidative metabolism, resulting in decreased intracellular pressure, shifting redox balance towards a more â€œoxidizedâ€￾ state, accompanied by increased ATP synthesis, increased water structure and resulting higher level of energy. This decreased intracellular pressure in Viktor Schaubergerâ€™s terms may be a result of a â€œbiological vacuumâ€™ and a resulting inflow of â€˜ethericiesâ€™ increasing cellular energy and structure (more on potential connections between Schauberger and Peat in a later post)."
Obviously this Pranarupa person thinks that an oxidized cell state is more healthy than a reduced cell state. He also states that "The Living-state for Szent-Gyorgyi (1977) was the electronically desaturated state, that allows for the development of increasingly complex life. Szent-Gyorgyi argues that cancer results from a failure in electronic desaturation, leading to loss of differentiated structure (Pohl et al. 1977)." and that cancer occurs in a more reduced state, while metabolism of normal cells can be regenerated by creating a more oxidized environment. I did a bit more research of my own and I found that NAD+ (the oxidized form) activates the Sirtuin 1 gene, which is responsible for longevity. A high NAD+/NADH (the reduced state) activates the Sirtuin 1 gene. I also found that the ratio of NAD+/NADH falls in cells as an organism ages, suggesting that cells become reduced as they age, and lose oxidizing potential. What do you guys think? What's with the conflicting theories? Am I misunderstanding something?

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-20T02:24:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-want-oxidized-cells.9148/post-103888>

Here's more info from [http://www.cell.com/abstract/S0092-8674(13)00755-1:](http://www.cell.com/abstract/S0092-8674\(13\)00755-1:) (Remember, NAD+ is oxidized)
Highlights
Enhancing NAD+ availability extends lifespan in C. elegans through sir-2.1
The NAD+/sir-2.1-dependent activation of mitochondrial UPR is crucial for longevity
Higher NAD+ bioavailability protects against ROS by enhancing daf-16 activity
UPRmt is a conserved target of the NAD+/SIRT1 axis in mammalian cells
Summary

NAD+ is an important cofactor regulating metabolic homeostasis and a rate-limiting substrate for sirtuin deacylases. We show that NAD+ levels are reduced in aged mice and Caenorhabditis elegans and that decreasing NAD+ levels results in a further reduction in worm lifespan. Conversely, genetic or pharmacological restoration of NAD+ prevents age-associated metabolic decline and promotes longevity in worms

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-20T04:25:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-want-oxidized-cells.9148/post-103892>

Oxidation has its role........it is a signal we use. The problem arises when the smoke signals are too faint or a pyroclastic flow......I like the fact you question things but I wonder if you think a bit too concretely.........biology and health are not concrete sciences.......but you seem to think they are........and I dont. Maybe that is your issue with my work.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-20T04:26:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-want-oxidized-cells.9148/post-103893>

Maybe if you read the Quilt.......you'd see what it says in the beginning. To get where I am headed you have to embrace paradox and enigma......that is where the truth is buried. Its not on the road in front of your eyes.

Until about 4 months ago, I drink a few cups of tea everyday. However, I began doing research on tea and this led me to discontinuing my consumption of tea because of it's high content of aluminum and it's extremely high content of fluoride (far more than fluoridated water, in fact). When I discovered Dr. Kruse's work it simply reinforced my thoughts on fluoride; it's terrible. However, I noticed that Dr. Kruse still recommends large consumption of green tea. That's a heck of a lot of fluoride! Does anyone know how he's able to justify this large tea intake in terms of fluoride? Do the health benefits of green tea outweigh the large dose of fluoride? This seems rather unlikely. And what about the aluminum in it?

# Thread: Newbie with Hashimoto's, MTFHR mutations and leaky gut - help!

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5

### Mikaela — 2013-12-21T06:14:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103945>
