### caroline — 2013-12-21T06:47:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103951>

Hi michaela and welcome. Congratulations on your daughter .... How wonderful!

You seem to be on top of things ..... Good job!

Have you read Dr. K.'s book? That will give you a quick overview of all of this.

Have you Been to the home page? The easy start guides are so helpful.. Have you seen all the podcasts? They also are a very quick and easy way to dial in.

If you can manage it - become a gold member and then you will have access to all the webinars and the follow up Q&A a few days later where you can ask Jack pretty much anything.

There is a free podcast by Dr. Tim Jackson "Heal your hormones" ....and he has just done a bootcamp for us that was outstanding. I think you can still purchase that.

No question - there is a lot to get thru here .... But just dive in and everyone will help! Keep reading and asking questions.

If you could transfer this to the journal section it will keep track of your journey to optimal!

For the journal section .... Just go to the top of the page and click on forum and then go down 5 categories to journals.

Have a look at a journal by Mr. Pinkies and read what she and her DH have accomplished for themselves and their children.

Where are you in Oz? There is a great thread in the cave ...Aussies and the donut hole.

We have crap water because of the donut hole ....so JK strongly suggests we drink bottled water from the Northern Hemisphere and be low carb all year .... And C/T our butts off!

Have you thought of getting a magnetico sleep pad?

Non native EMF is our biggest problem .....Jack addresses that in almost all of his podcasts at some point

### caroline — 2013-12-21T09:40:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103955>

Mikaela .... I love your name! No - there isn't an OZ supplier. There is a forum member Nick who is in Brisbane and he got one for he and his DW and son. You could pm him. It has helped his little son tremendously.

If you want .... post your labs in the lab section. Same thing - go to the top of the page and click on forum and scroll down - 7 categories I think - to labs. There are folks here who are great at reading labs.

I strongly suggest that you listen to Dr. Jackson's free community wide webinar on Heal your Hormones. He gave us an amazing amount of information. Dr. Tim is an expert in hormones and is part of a MTHFR group with Dr. Sterling Hill

For those of us who purchased the bootcamp - Dr. Tim has given us a great price on a package of three consults and can help us get testing here in OZ from a lab in Sydney ...stool testing.

He also highly recommends a book .....Why do I still have Thyroid symptoms when my labs are normal - by Dadas Karazian \[Chiropractor\] I have no idea if that spelling is correct ..... that is how it sounded ...lol

There was also a free download of a book by Ameer Rosic \[spelling?\] it might be still available - go to Amazon. I will check and get back to you. If you want pm me and give me your email and I will send you my link.

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-21T15:17:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103960>

Hi and Welcome!
I would agree with Caroline that some consults with Tim Jackson are really worth it. He can take you through a protocol to address the methylation issues. Along with all the protocols that Dr. Kruse outlines I think you are will find things will be better and better for you.

I can't answer your question about the saturated fat and snps but it doesn't make sense to me to limit these good fats.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-21T17:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-with-hashimotos-mtfhr-mutations-and-leaky-gut-help.9151/post-103966>

Hi Mikaela, welcome!! Sounds like you are doing great so far. I second Caroline's suggestion for a different water source. I also suggest you get plenty of sunshine if you have been limiting your artificial PUFAs like vegetable oil. And the magnetico is so helpful.

Remember that just because you have a gene, doesn't mean it is being expressed. For instance nuts and eggs may be a problem if some genes are expressing (because they are responding to a stress of some sort), but if the gene isn't expressing, nuts and eggs may be fine. They found that the group of people in Okinawa that regularly live to be 100 years old have the same "bad" genes as the rest of us. The 'bad' genes just aren't active. I agree with SeaHorse that saturated fats (non-trans fats) would not be a good thing to reduce. Good fats are pretty much self limiting.

Glad you are with us!

# Thread: Overwhelming CT Experience
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-21T18:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103968>

Hello,
I've recently started the CT protocol. After reading the awesome hormonal, immune and neurological benefits of CT, I was eager to begin. Very ambitious, I jumped right into the cold tub, placing ice over my abdomen and back while wearing a compression shirt. My cold tubs have been roughly 45 minutes. I have willed myself to deal with the violent shivering that ensues for about an hour after the tub. I have a few concerns because after the last few CT sessions, I've experienced welts all over my body and feel as though my immune system has been run over by a truck. I recall Dave Asprey, the Bulletproof Exec falling asleep on cold ice bags, and similarly experiencing ice burns.
Questions
1\. Did I proceed to the cold tubs to quickly? Is more CT, the longer and colder the better? At what point do I need to be weary of potential harm?
2\. After experiencing the welts and swelling, I a concerned that rather than diminishing inflammation I have induced a massive increase in cytokines and inflammation based on shocking the body? Is this the case?
3\. Would CT still be recommended while battling an illness? Would the increase in cortisol, and immune system replenishment through the cold be a quick way to eradicate the illness?
4\. Is the case that more CT at this point would be effective in reducing the inflammation induced by initially overwhelming the body?
Thanks so much Jack for your time and willingness to answer each and every one of our questions. You're optimal productivity is truly amazing.

### Josh — 2013-12-21T19:14:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103969>

In everything, gradient is important. This has been my life learning. Leap before you look works sometimes and makes for great stories, and the human body is a delicate dynamic system striving for homeostasis based on the available resources and conditions. Exceed its ability to adapt and it becomes less graceful. Consider standing before Mount Everest with no conditioning or climbing skill and saying what the hell...All this being said, I found that the gradient for CT has been very very very important. Dr. Kruse has detailed things to consider int the CT blogs, I believe 7 is a key. Also, the appendix of the book is a must read. I skipped the face, but spent a month with the abdominal ice pack. Burnt myself, worked on omega 3/6, etc.. I can now handle a tub on days when I am not tired. This is not the same thing as pushing yourself through a sports event even though you are not in shape. There also, you will hurt yourself. Gradient, the steady horse wins the race as one mentor cautioned me....

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-21T21:18:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103978>

Cold uticaria (hives, welts) can be a sign of bad 03/06 ratio...something to look at before you head back into too much CT. There was a reason that the protocol involved incremental steps

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-21T22:36:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103983>

Josh thanks for the valuable words of wisdom. Great advice and I will certainly go back and review CT-7.

### caroline — 2013-12-21T22:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103985>

Yep ..... re read CT #7 really carefully. Dr. K. is very specific with his protocols. Sometimes we don't always know the exact reasons because he hasn't laid it out yet .....

What temperature are you doing? Are you measuring your body temp?

Jack is very specific about all of this ....... all lot of us learned the hard way...

Always remember that Jack is about 10 years ahead of us on his own journey ....so he has definitely been there!

We are always in so much of a hurry - I know I am ..... but trust in the process and never stop reading and asking questions! In our great hurry we often overlook really important things - or it doesn't quite sink in ..... and our brain isn't optimal......

Have you seen the C/T webinar?

### caroline — 2013-12-21T22:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103986>

never, ever forget to trust your own instincts!

### nonchalant — 2013-12-22T03:13:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-104018>

Welcome Chris! Sounds like you have enthusiasm!

The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol blog, [http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)
is what you need. It starts with face dunking only, and progresses.

My first ice bath was only for about 7 minutes, at 55F.

### nicld — 2013-12-23T01:04:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-104088>

Welcome Chris,

I would take it slower. I started in the summer so I could rewarm outside and started out slower. I did jump into the baths right away but it was summer and we do not have central AC so the colder baths at night helped me sleep. I have never done the ice with a compression shirt but that is my N=1 and my fat is in my butt and thighs.

I think Jack has said to get all the CT one can but is had to be what you can tolerate. Today, I am trying for 2 1/2 hours total today but some days I can barely get in 30 minutes. It does take time but so worth it.

Good luck,
Nicole

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-27T22:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-104306>

Thanks Nicid,
I intend on becoming a Gold Member and viewing the webinars - specifically Deep CT - so that I can optimize my CT sessions. After my seventh cold tub, 30 minutes in a bath have filled with half-melted snow and cold water, I believe that I have reached a significant improvement in my cold adaptation. I felt a wave off exhilaration after the tub, and only shivered for around 15 minutes.
I usually engaged in some intense Qigong exercises, post-CT, to quickly turn the fire back on in the body. Boy are the tendons and ligaments supremely robust after a CT session.

# Thread: Winter Solstice - reconnect with yourself and the night
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-solstice-reconnect-with-yourself-and-the-night.9155/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-solstice-reconnect-with-yourself-and-the-night.9155/post-103975>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-22T00:36:29+0200

### caroline — 2013-12-22T00:54:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-solstice-reconnect-with-yourself-and-the-night.9155/post-104004>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-22T01:08:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-solstice-reconnect-with-yourself-and-the-night.9155/post-104007>

We are each gifted in a unique and important way. It is our privilege and our adventure to discover our own special light. Tonight, the shortest day of the year tap your light. In the depths of my personal despair in the winter of 2005, I finally learned that within me there was a hidden invincible summer that could heal me. Every night we need to get back to walking in the cold dark so each of us can learn to turn on their own light.

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-22T01:40:55+0200

### caroline — 2013-12-22T01:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-solstice-reconnect-with-yourself-and-the-night.9155/post-104012>

### Inger — 2013-12-22T11:38:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-solstice-reconnect-with-yourself-and-the-night.9155/post-104035>

So beautiful.. We need to explore the dark to find our light! I love it... the darkness takes away all the noise around and I turn inside. I went for a dip in the river the other day, it was already totally dark I could barely see the stairs into the water so I had to guess and go very slow and carefully. Here is no snow either now so very dark. It was so magic to be there naked in the river in the darkness and I could see the stars! Very beautiful indeed

This is my second winter of darkness... and I will get even more healing done. I am fascinated by the power of it and what it does... I could never have thought I could be fascinated with anything like this, not in my wildest dreams...

### Huck — 2013-12-22T13:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/winter-solstice-reconnect-with-yourself-and-the-night.9155/post-104037>

I recently downloaded this free book from Amazon/Kindle. [As a Man Thinketh](http://www.amazon.com/As-Man-Thinketh-James-Allen-ebook/dp/B0082VKK5Y/ref=sr_1_1?s=digital-text&ie=UTF8&qid=1387710864&sr=1-1&keywords=as+a+man+thinketh), James Allen. Thr entire book reinforces the idea that Gretchen and Jack have laid out in this thread.

> Mind is the Master power that moulds and makes, And Man is Mind, and evermore he takes The tool of Thought, and, shaping what he wills, Brings forth a thousand joys, a thousand ills:â€” He thinks in secret, and it comes to pass: Environment is but his looking-glass.

> THE aphorism, "As a man thinketh in his heart so is he," not only embraces the whole of a man's being, but is so comprehensive as to reach out to every condition and circumstance of his life. A man is literally what he thinks, his character being the complete sum of all his thoughts. As the plant springs from, and could not be without, the seed, so every act of a man springs from the hidden seeds of thought, and could not have appeared without them. This applies equally to those acts called "spontaneous" and "unpremeditated" as to those, which are deliberately executed. Act is the blossom of thought, and joy and suffering are its fruits; thus does a man garner in the sweet and bitter fruitage of his own husbandry.
>
>
> "Thought in the mind hath made us, What we are By thought was wrought and built. If a man's mind Hath evil thoughts, pain comes on him as comes The wheel the ox behind.... .. If one endure In purity of thought, joy follows him As his own shadowâ€” sure."​

Allen, James (2012-05-12). As a Man Thinketh. Kindle Edition.

# Thread: Your Christmas table .....

### caroline — 2013-12-21T23:30:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-christmas-table.9157/post-103992>

What wonderful things will be on your Christmas table?

It is amazing how my mindset has completely changed - all I want is seafood ... and then more seafood!

I do want cherries and peaches tho ....... and stilton cheese .... and some malbec ...

and chocolate .... but I am realising that any chocolate is dangerous territory for me. I am better off staying away from it!

but some smoked salmon and some stilton or goat cheese or brie .... and a glass of red - it doesn't get any better than that for me.......

I could easily put a straw in a bottle of port .... and go for it!

What will be on your table this festive season? what will you do about friends and family who aren't so inclined? Will you cater for them? or will you say "hell no" these are the healthy choices we have ... so suck it up buttercup!

Merry Christmas everyone!

# Thread: Preventing Toxic Effects of DHA?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-22T11:20:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104033>

So, I have to say, I'm quite sold on Dr. Kruse's ideas on the importance of DHA in the brain. However, I'm also convinced by Ray Peat's arguments that DHA is toxic to mitochondria and suppresses proper mitochondrial respiration. There are studies showing that as an organism ages, it's cardiolipin, a protein present in the cellular respiration enzyme cytochrome c oxidase, becomes more unsaturated and loses the presence of saturated palmitic acid. This deterioration reduced mitochondrial respiration as can be seen in this study [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3351630](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/3351630). Is this the choice, then? Do we have to choose between fully functional mitochondria and well structured neurons? Does anybody know of a way to prevent mitochondrial uptake of DHA and make sure it only concentrates in neurons?

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-22T12:28:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104036>

I don't know but Jack has always said that eating DHA in its evolutionary package is better than a supplement. The two are very different. Also it's better to eat raw/undercooked seafood if possible.

### Jonathin — 2013-12-22T20:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104056>

Seems like horrible research. A diet of purified casein, corn starch, sugar and corn or sardine oil . . . please! Is this science?
This is troubling but not because it indicts DHA of malfeasance.

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-22T20:58:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104057>

Good point Jonathan. However, even on such a poisonous diet, the animals' mitochondria fared better on the DHA free diet than the one with DHA. It still seems to suggest potential damage to mitochondria.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-22T21:49:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104061>

Dr. Kruse has said that when we eat seafood, the DHA is reserved for the brain. DHA isn't normally used for fuel.

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-22T22:18:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104066>

This study seems to show the DHA being incorporate into the cardiolipin, not used for energy. Maybe the iodine prevents the cardiolipin from taking up DHA? I wish we had studies on that.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-22T22:22:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104067>

This thread is really problematic for me. Citing bad science and Peat all in one invokes shades of why I had to ban BigPoppaChakra. The scent is discernible, like rancid fish oil in this diet. Jonathin is spot on. I'm wondering if we have a new name and the same old nonsense.

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-22T23:14:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104071>

Sorry Dr. Kruse, I was just trying to make sense of all the conflicting theories. Big Papa got banned? That's too bad. Peat seems to provide at least some pretty valid thoughts and I was trying to synthesize his thoughts on fish oil with yours; a quite difficult goal.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-22T23:19:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104072>

It would be difficult to coordinate Dr. Kruse's ideas with others, because others do not have his insight into our true nature. How does Peat feel about the fact that our bodies work in a quantum fashion, as well as having backup chemical energy sources?

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-22T23:21:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104073>

Based on your comment about the rancid fish oil, I assume you think that unoxidized, stable DHA would not have these suppressive effects on the cardiolipin? Would it not be taken up, or would it be taken up and just not reduce the cellular respiration. If you could point me to studies on DHA's true effect on cellular respiration, I would very much appreciate it. I was really confused when Peat stated that DHA supresses mitochondrial function considering your well supported evidence that the most metabolically active tissue, the brain, also has the most DHA in it.

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-22T23:27:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104076>

Ray Peat does support Gilbert Ling's ideas of Association Induction as opposed to the cell-membrane, using structured water and what not. He believes that sugar burning is a better way to stabilize the coherent structure of the cell, but that's a whole different discussion! xD I'm thoroughly convinced that one of the chief effectors of health is mitochondrial respiration so I'm trying to get to the bottom of what increases mitochondrial respiration and what harms it. When I read Peat's ideas on fish oil, I didn't want to believe that DHA harmed mitochondria and I figured no one could refute his DHA thoughts better than the people at this forum!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-23T00:58:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104087>

Fish oil does not and never will equal food with DHA. Peat does not understand that and never has.

### RickD — 2013-12-23T02:38:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/preventing-toxic-effects-of-dha.9160/post-104100>

To follow up on Jack's Statement above when fish oil is processed it changes from a triglyceride to an ester so it's pretty likely your body doesn't look at it the same. Now you can buy Fish oil that is changed back to a trig but it's pretty pricey stuff. Go have a look a Jack's fish oil blog and read the Science article at the end.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](http://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)

The article says in mice fish oil is less effective in lowering blood pressure likely due to the Trig vs Ester issue.

A few years back I was pumping the fish oil because it was convenient vs sourcing wild AK salmon and after about 6 months I went through a couple week period where my nose would start bleed while out running and another instance riding up a chairlift. Dumped the fish oil soon afterwards and went through the hassle to get Copper River Sockeye from AK. As a former Alaskan these are the best sockeyes out there because the Copper River which flows off the Copper glacier 300 miles inland and runs upto 12mph meaning only the stoutest fish can make the journey up to their breeding grounds.

Used to be more pricey, but in 2013 I noticed Costco stores across the Pacific Northwest and even Alberta were carrying it. US Price was $9.99/lb while in Canada it was $17.99/lb. Eating a reasonable portion 3x a week for my wife and I works out to about 120 lbs/yr. Not cheap but not ridiculous either.

### Tanya — 2013-12-23T21:09:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-woman-in-russia-who-is-not-cold.9161/post-104140>

Thank you, Optimalbound. I did not know that AK was doing this. He is a cool grandmaster indeed. He learned to play chess at age 4 and as they say never looked back. My parents, especially my father would never get tired to remind me and my brother of that.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-woman-in-russia-who-is-not-cold.9161/post-104144>

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-02-13T03:42:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-woman-in-russia-who-is-not-cold.9161/post-109900>

Love this! I do my best, and have discovered that I rarely ever bring more than my light jacket with me, and I'm rarely wearing it. But unfortunately, this has probably been our worst winter ever, so I can't say I'm being a starfish this year. The lovely thing is that my girls are very willing to embrace cold and are often out without jackets, or even long sleeves when it's pretty cold out, and I definitely make no fuss about it. The other people fussing are easy to ignore.

# Thread: Cookbook for Wisconsin?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookbook-for-wisconsin.9163/>
**Subforum:** Community Cookbook
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### meihao01 — 2013-12-23T04:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookbook-for-wisconsin.9163/post-104103>

I moved to Wisconsin for college from Asia last summer. Since I love to cook, I did a little research in order to find a cookbook. If I get an Asian cookbook, most of the ingredients would be hard to find. I guess a typical American cookbook would work, but pizzas, burgers, or sandwiches are already everywhere. Italian and Mexican food are similar and the ingredients won't be hard to find. However, the cuisines seem hard to cook. Finally, I chose a German cookbook. I thought the ingredients, like fresh cream and cheese, would be easy to find and eastern European cuisines are usually simple. The problem is that the cookbook seems to assume that I leave in a small European town with a farmer's market open every morning. I can't find fresh cream, kohlrabi, duck, rabbit, or other important ingredients. I got totally confused. These things are not supposed to be hard to find in an agricultural state like Wisconsin. I feel like everything is pre-made and because of that, some ingredients are not on the shelf for the they can't be pre-made. Any recommendation for cookbook?

### caroline — 2013-12-23T06:28:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookbook-for-wisconsin.9163/post-104109>

Dr. Kruse has a cookbook ..... go to the home page...

Welcome!

There is a good foodie site .....nom nom paleo....and lots of others. Enjoy!

### nicld — 2013-12-23T16:51:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookbook-for-wisconsin.9163/post-104119>

I agree with Caroline, check out the Kruse cookbook. If you go to Amazon and search for Wisconsin cookbooks you will find a bunch there. There is nothing really special about the foods in WI except for the cheese and cranberries.

Are you going to school at Madison?

I have lived here my whole life and have never had rabbit or squirrel but venison I get every year but you have to know someone who has some since i don't think it is sold in stores.

Nicole

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-23T18:01:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cookbook-for-wisconsin.9163/post-104126>

I do not think Wisconsin has good food at all

# Thread: What biochemical changes occur that facilitate fat breakdown?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-biochemical-changes-occur-that-facilitate-fat-breakdown.9164/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-23T11:03:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-biochemical-changes-occur-that-facilitate-fat-breakdown.9164/post-104110>

Pretty simple question that I, for some reason, cannot seem to find. I'm sure someone here will be able to help me out. What biochemical adaptation actually induces lipolysis/free fatty acid release? Free fatty acids are released into your blood and these are used as fuel for ketosis. But how exactly does the body initiate release of the free fatty acids? I know hormone-sensitive lipase is involved in there somewhere but, as the name suggests, there is a hormonal change that the lipase is sensitive to. What is it?

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-23T11:12:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-biochemical-changes-occur-that-facilitate-fat-breakdown.9164/post-104111>

I should have specified. I'm interested in how the body releases free fatty acids while in ketosis. Not just normal fat breakdown. I'm not sure if the hormonal cascade is any different.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-23T17:57:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-biochemical-changes-occur-that-facilitate-fat-breakdown.9164/post-104123>

read CT 6. it is all laid out there

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-23T17:57:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-biochemical-changes-occur-that-facilitate-fat-breakdown.9164/post-104124>

and the cascade he is quite different

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-23T20:35:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-biochemical-changes-occur-that-facilitate-fat-breakdown.9164/post-104135>

Thanks Dr. Kruse, I'll check out CT6!

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-23T22:16:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-biochemical-changes-occur-that-facilitate-fat-breakdown.9164/post-104152>

Very informative read but it didn't seem to cover one thing that I'm looking for. According to Peter over at hyperlipid [http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2009/11/brief-discussion-of-ketosis.html?m=1](http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2009/11/brief-discussion-of-ketosis.html?m=1), the lipolysis of ketosis is increased due to increased levels of adrenaline and cortisol. I thought we wanted to minimize our stress hormones but here, Peter is telling us that the hormonal changes necessary to drive lipolysis in ketosis are a rising of stress hormones like adrenaline and cortisol? Is there a way that ketotic lipolysis can be initiated without jacking up your stress hormones?

# Thread: you have health insurance; did you know that no one in your area accepts it????
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-have-health-insurance-did-you-know-that-no-one-in-your-area-accepts-it.9170/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-12-23T19:24:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-have-health-insurance-did-you-know-that-no-one-in-your-area-accepts-it.9170/post-104131>

for those who are excited about having access to health insurance for 2013 - guess what? you may not have access to doctors or hospitals who take it.

Choas had a PEd Endo apt this Thrusday but it was cancled . Now Georgetown Hospital Pediatrics/Medstar is trying to figure out how to get us in in this week or next week so Choas can been seen and still have her visit covered by our current ins provider United Health Care b/c when I told her we changed health ins and that GT/Medstar wasn't accept BCBS (or BCBS Federal) for next year she was aghast, put me on hold and verified w/ their ins section. When she came back on - she was shocked that GT/Medstar will only be accepting one BlueCross ins next year - which is Anthem BC. Welcome to the new age of health care, courtesy of the ACA. Buckle up folks it's going to get worse, as you may now have hospitals, and physicians in your area - who can't treat you. How about them apples.....

### fitness@home — 2013-12-23T20:37:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-have-health-insurance-did-you-know-that-no-one-in-your-area-accepts-it.9170/post-104136>

Those apples are rotten!

I think you are right. This is going to become the norm for us during 2014-2015...finding out that our current docs no longer on our provider list. I also forsee more going into coincerge type practices.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-23T20:49:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-have-health-insurance-did-you-know-that-no-one-in-your-area-accepts-it.9170/post-104138>

Go Obama........I told ya it was coming and people did not believe it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-have-health-insurance-did-you-know-that-no-one-in-your-area-accepts-it.9170/post-104139>

### nonchalant — 2013-12-24T02:40:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-have-health-insurance-did-you-know-that-no-one-in-your-area-accepts-it.9170/post-104166>

# Thread: I cant do 40 degree cold bath or showers no more
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### x_danny_x — 2013-12-23T20:13:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104134>

I have cold adaptive to the 50-55 degree water....I decided to go foward and cold adapt even further.....I could now hold my own for about 1 hour with 40-45 degree water...My body then suddenly could not handle it and I had to revert back to a higher temp of 48-50 degrees...I was doing 40 degree baths or showers for 3 months with no issue. The 40 degree gave the most benefit with energy, power, able to work out with heavier weights and put on some muscle.

I didnt get any shivering or problems. It just felt too cold....I couldnt go back.

### Danco3636 — 2013-12-23T21:23:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104142>

Interesting. 40 for an hour. That is crazy.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-24T02:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104164>

Danny, perhaps it is because the sun is less bright, giving you less energy each day? I know the feeling, you just can't do it anymore. You may need to find another source of energy before/during CT.

### x_danny_x — 2013-12-24T16:45:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104191>

the sun...never thought it would be an issue...its winter here in New York....so sun sets quite early.....what other energy sources do you have in mind?

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-24T16:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104193>

Danny

at the colder temps - go for shorter sessions - you don't need the same "Time" in the tank to get the same benefits. I prefer the colder temps b/c I can get the same benefits at the warmer temps where I have to sit longer (I don't always have the time for 1hr in 50 deg water) but 5-15 mins depending on temp is do able!

see if backing off the time helps. we don't all need to be Deep CTing to get positive benefits!!!

### x_danny_x — 2013-12-24T22:17:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104217>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=104193)
>
> Danny
>
> at the colder temps - go for shorter sessions - you don't need the same "Time" in the tank to get the same benefits. I prefer the colder temps b/c I can get the same benefits at the warmer temps where I have to sit longer (I don't always have the time for 1hr in 50 deg water) but 5-15 mins depending on temp is do able!
>
> see if backing off the time helps. we don't all need to be Deep CTing to get positive benefits!!!

5-15 minutes...thats all??/ god damn, wish i knew this earlier....what water temps you handle?

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-24T23:18:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104219>

# Thread: Circadian Soiftware
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-soiftware.9173/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ricardo — 2013-12-23T22:11:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-soiftware.9173/post-104150>

This software works best if you input your coordinates. The city you live in (or closest to) could suffice. Then it creates a pattern of light frequencies ( which you can adjust ) so that after dark your computer screen emits less blue light frequency.

It seems to work well enough: I get drowsy within minutes of the screen emitting the warm orange light.

This might help,

[http://justgetflux.com/](http://justgetflux.com/)

PS: Free for Windows

### sooperb — 2014-01-05T01:27:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-soiftware.9173/post-104750>

F.lux works on a mac too but only on iPads/phones if jail broken. I'm not inclined to do that just in case I blox it up.

# Thread: Nausea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cabiria — 2013-12-24T02:09:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104161>

Hi,
I've been having nausea for the past two weeks and it doesn't seem to improve. Initially it seemed to occur only after meals, but now it is throughout the day. Lately I've increased the amount of fat in my diet. However, few years ago I was diagnosed with gallbladder stones and I'm wondering whether this could have something to do with it. I don't have any abdominal pain...

### fitness@home — 2013-12-24T02:34:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104165>

Cabiria, do you use any type of digestive enzymes? Maybe your body needs Some help digesting the foods since you've increased the fat.

I don't tolerate nausea well. I'm not sure, why but a pinch of Celtic salt on my tongue always helps me when I have it.

### caroline — 2013-12-24T04:39:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104170>

Please give us some context ..... Have you seen a Doctor?

I got pancreatitis from gall stone problems. I was very sick and wound up in hospital....

I am certainly not saying that is your problem .....but wouldn't it be a good idea to be seen by a professional?

Why have you increased the fat in your diet? When I had gall bladder/gall stone issues I couldn't tolerate fat..

Please get looked at.....

I don't remember having any pain..... But I couldn't stop throwing up....

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T05:35:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104171>

Yes,I've started taking enzymes, but they don't seem to make a difference with the nausea...I've been on a gluten free diet for almost three years now and have increased the amount of saturated fat gradually. Now I'm eating mostly grain free and seasonal food, so my diet is better then ever. I've never been on a low fat diet before, but I was definitely eating a diet higher in carbs and more polyunsaturated fats...
Thank you for the tip on the Celtic salt, I will try it.

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T05:48:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104173>

Caroline, I have an appointment to see a gastroenterologist tomorrow morning. I increased the amount of fat in my diet as I was changing my diet from a high carb diet into a paleo diet. I didn't seem to have any trouble digesting the fat until now...
When I was diagnosed with the gallbladder stones, the doctor suggested removing the gallbladder, no other option was suggested...How did you solve your gall stone issues?

### Josh — 2013-12-24T07:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104177>

consider this at least for information and a perspective:

[http://www.healthbeyondhype.com/info/preparing-for-the-essential-liver-gallbladder-flush](http://www.healthbeyondhype.com/info/preparing-for-the-essential-liver-gallbladder-flush)

Doctors do not have a lot of alternatives. I have done dozens of these and passed stones 1 inch in diameter. This does not mean it is right for you, but it is worth considering before you lose an important organ.

Also, consider backing off on fatty foods until you sort it out as the fat stimulates more bile flow that may be blocked by stones.

### caroline — 2013-12-24T08:58:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104179>

I Had my gall bladder out years ago .... I was never given options. I would not do it now.

Have you seen Dr. K.'s blog post about your gallbladder? very important read before your appointment.

Dr. Kruse is teaching us to be informed about our own health, trust our instincts and to never, ever settle. If you need to get a second and third opinion .... do so.

Have you read Jack's book? ... The Epi Paleo RX .... important read for you to get a handle on all this. It is available in kindle and paper from Amazon.

My DH refused to have his gall bladder out and was bullied by lots of Docs about being so silly. He was very sick - but he cut all fat and ate lightly until he started feeling better \[and lost weight\] ..... he never did have it out!

I assume you are female? How old if I may ask? other health history? Dr. K. needs context. Have you thought of starting a journal?

If you go to the home page you will find a blog index and a search feature

The blog you want is CPC #3 Do you need a gall bladder

Please keep reading and asking questions and we will try to point you in the right direction

### caroline — 2013-12-24T09:11:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104180>

I hope you start feeling better.....

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T10:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104182>

Yes, I'm a female, 42 years old, 5'6", 120lbs. In terms of health history, I had shingles in my late 20s, 2 pregnancies (I was 36 and 39 years old), both C-section delivery. During the first one I developed gestational diabetes, which I controlled through diet only. Subsequently, I also discovered I had gallstones (no pain, but I had an ultrasound done when liver enzymes results were high at a regular checkup). At the end of my second pregnancy I developed Cholestasis of pregnancy. After I turned 40 I started having IBS symptoms, and I got interested in reading more about nutrition... From Weston Price, to "wheat belly", paleo community, Dr. JK, I started tweaking my diet. The biggest changes were removing the wheat from my diet and no longer using vegetable oils in cooking (just coconut oil, butter and lard). Removing the wheat solved both my IBS symptoms (now I only occasionally get the abdominal pain), and cleared a red rash I had on my thighs for years. Also, in the last 6 years, I've been having on and off tingling and numbness in my upper back, right side (same area where I had shingles). For the longest time I thought it was due to a pinched nerve because of my poor posture. Visits to a chiropractor seemed to solve them for a while. After reading Dr. JK blog on peripheral neuropathy, I started thinking that might be what the tingling on my back is, so I'm trying to improve further my diet by reducing the carbs more and increasing the amount of fat, but it appears I have to take care of my gallbladder stones first... However, if the nausea is due to the gallbladder, shouldn't it be only after meals, instead of throughout the day?

### caroline — 2013-12-24T11:07:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104183>

When I had Gall Bladder attacks - I would have pretty serious pain in the upper back ... I can't remember where exactly now - but it was a pretty traditional place for pain. I don't remember throwing up - but it was a very long time ago! I am pretty sure that the pain often happened in the middle of the nite as well.

But the pancreatitis that developed was awful - could not stop throwing up... and I was really sick for over a week and in the hospital.

At least tomorrow you will see a Doc and hopefully get some answers.

There is a newer book - Grain Brain by a Dr. Perlmutter that is supposed to be excellent. There was just a free Gluten summit a couple of weeks ago - Did you happen to catch that?

Have you had blood work done? Did you read Jack's gallbladder blog?

Please let us know how your appointment goes tomorrow.

Where are you located?

This is going to be a lousy Christmas for you .... so sorry ..... but at least you should get some answers tomorrow. Please try really hard to hang on to your gall bladder.

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T17:48:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104203>

Thank you for all your help! Yes, I did read Dr. JK blog on the gallbladder, and I'll just read it again now before the dr. appointment.
I'll also check out the references you mention - I haven't followed the gluten free summit.
Here's a bit more about my overall diet/environment:
0-18th - lived in a small city in Romania; food 100% seasonal, mostly home cooked from scratched (bread, deli meats, cheese were pretty much the only processed foods), very low EMF environment. However, I ate quite a lot of grains (mostly bread).
18th - 24th - moved to big city in Romania for college; food still 100% seasonal, but less good quality.
24th - 32 - moved to Virginia, US; very bad diet (mostly fast food, microwave food); stress (working and going to college in the same time); higher EMF (studying computers); fluctuating between sedentary life and bouts of stressful gym exercise. In my early 30s started to wise up about eating habits: started to eat organic, went to farmer's markets, but diet overall high in starches and wheat.
32 - 40 - moved to LA, CA; diet increasingly better: more and more home cooked from scratch; organic mostly and from farmer's markets or CSA, but didn't paid much attention to seasonal food; Still diet high in starches, moderate protein, probably high in polyunsaturated fats. High EMF (programmer, in front of the computer 8 to 10 hours a day).
40 - present - 95% non-processed food, gluten-free and organic/grass-fed. Increased the amount of fats (coconut oil, butter, lard). Recently started to pay attention to artificial light and currently taking a break from work (less EMF) and also started doing facial CT, but stopped because of current health issue.

The last blood work I had was last December, around this time. The labs were pretty much ok, except for the glucose which was on the higher end, and low for Vitamin D (31). I also did some more in depth labs at that time, and Sjongren's Syndrom marker came out positive. I did the 23andme testing to see if I have any methylation issues, but I didn't see anything on the report. I do have very dry skin. Family history of diabetes, high blood pressure, and Hashimoto's.

### Josh — 2013-12-24T19:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104209>

In my experience cleaning out a stagnant liver and gallbladder bile system is about a 2 year job. The blockage at the gallbladder is the tip of the iceberg so to speak. Once the liver and possibly pancreas become toxic and inflamed due to the blockage which may be in the common bile duct where it affects the pancreas directly, all of this needs to be addressed. There are many good practitioners in LA. I can recommend Dr. Mark Flannery DC.

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T20:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104213>

Thank you, Kosh! I checked out dr Mark Flannery and he is a chiropractor as well so he might be able to help me with my back problem as well...

I just had my dr. appt. and after the initial physical exam the doctor suspects the nausea could be due to stomach not emptying properly...he gave me dexilant to see if it makes any difference. Also, he ordered the following labs:
CBC w Diff. w Platelet
Complete Metabolic Panel -CMP
Hemoglobin (Hgb) A1c.
We'll see on Friday what comes back...

Caroline, dancing (and especially tango) was such a passion of mine, until I experienced the most brutal attack of RA. My legs suffered the most. Doctor's solution for me was knees replacement and ankle fusion, as there was nothing else that can be done for me to fix it. - To make a long story short - I proved them wrong, did some research and eventually got myself out of the wheelchair, to a walker, then used a cane. I am at the point now that I can do rebounding and walk with just a slight noticeable limp. I truly hope that dancing is still in my future once again. I am originally from the former Soviet Union, but I live now in the Northeast part of the USA.

> Caroline,I don’t know if my story is a such a successful one, but it is definitely a story of survival. The RA hit me pretty hard in so many ways. It is at times like this that you learn the truth about people around you that you thought you could depend on, but then you find that if you don’t try to persevere, that condition that you find yourself in is going to be permanent. So, it was not much of a choice for me. Caroline, yes I have tried numerous diets in the past, only to eventually go on to the next one. I am sticking with Epi-Paleo one for sure as I can see the befits of it already. I am having a much more difficult time with doing C/T though.

Please, please, please Tanya - start a journal and tell us a little more about your health history. Dr. K. likes journals and he needs context to offer any suggestions.

Your determination will empower others. Have a look at Martin's journal ... he has a hard time with C/T as well. Have a look at Mr. Pinkies journal and read what Kate and her family have accomplished in the last 18 months. There are many success stories ..... little by little, baby step by baby step ......

You never know when some thing you say touches someone else ..... or equally when they have a suggestion to offer you.

We are all on this path together .... with Dr. K shining his flashlight.....

Have you read the posts by prAna? he is a very inspiring young man. He has alot of experience with gait issues .... that is his field of expertise .......when he isn't running around like a mad man in the snow and in the forests etc. etc. and free diving and whatever else takes his fancy......
Baby steps....

### Tanya — 2013-12-27T17:55:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-not-using-your-ipad-here-is-a-unique-way-to-do-it-perhaps.9177/post-104298>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=104281)
>
> CT & RA correlate heavily with good fatty acids profile.

JanSz, I most certainly need to have some testing done - it’s been awhile. But even without it I know that I still have lots and lots of inflammation in me, even though the sedimentation rate of 110 (lab range 0-10) dropped to 16 and my CRP of 2.5 (ref. 0 -1) also went down to 0.1 now!

I had a Metametrix ION test done when my diet was quite different from my present one. I was avoiding all the fats at that time, lot of carbs and salmon and was practically overdosing myself with a lot of of Omega-3 and flax oils. It resulted in a clear abnormality in my fatty acid profile. I especially had a very low Oleic and AA, while huge ALA and EPA numbers. I presume that my situation might have changed since I started Epi-paleo, but I need to get a new test to find out for sure. Thank you very much again for all your input!

### Tanya — 2013-12-27T17:58:49+0200

I’d like to say that It is with great pleasure and interest that I read many of your posts, Caroline. You always have something kind and helpful to say not only to the newcomers like myself, but to the rest of the group here. And speaking of the group - I am very glad to be part of it now because people here are so caring and supportive of each other...

### glightner — 2013-12-25T07:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-got-labs-back-and-need-help.9181/post-104230>

I need help making out what this all means? I know there are some areas of interest, but I don't know where to start?

I have been on the Epi Paleo Rx for about 6 weeks, I have lost 20 lbs. I am using CT Protocol with about 60lbs of ice for about 50 minutes. I am 49 yrs. old and over weight at 6'5" I am 320 lbs. I started at 342lbs. I have dropped grains recently and noticed a great difference in the way that I feel. I am working with my wife's employers Onsite health clinic, they are wonderful and curious as to what I am doing. They are starting a wellness clinic after the first of the year and some of the tests that I asked them to run are in line with what they are going to do. But They didn't understand all of the tests and why I wanted them?

I know my HS CRP is very high and they are concerned about it! I would appreciate some feedback?

Glen

CBC WHITE COUNT 4.4 K/Ul 4.4-11.0
RED BLOOD CELL COUNT 5.14 M/Ul 4.3-5.7
HEMOGLOBIN 16.0 g/Ul 13.9-17.5

My Leptin Prescription

THE COLD THERMOGENESIS PROTOCOL
[http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

> Why did I choose to use protein and fat to retrain the brain?
> [http://www.jackkruse.com/how-does-the-leptin-rx-work/](http://www.jackkruse.com/how-does-the-leptin-rx-work/)
> \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> [http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-14-personal-ground-zero/](http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-14-personal-ground-zero/)
>
> > We are designed for cold environment and a marine diet and this is core foundational principle of every mammal left on this planet since the K-T event. We just have remained blind to it. Hence, this is why K-T is Factor X for all eutherian mammals left standing. Modern life has allowed us to stray very far from our real evolutionary blueprint. I am not talking about the paleo 1.0 book here either!
>
>
>
> > Food became my medicine not my entertainment. With this type of medicine, I believe, we all can regain our health. Without our health, does anything else really matter?
>
> \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> Your LUTEINIZING HORMONE (LH) is low while Total Testosterone is low.
> Your pituitary gland is not responding correctly, it should have raised your LH
> You need to do pituitary MRI.
> Either growth (usually benign) or you have been hit in the head.
>
> Eventually you will need to raise your Total Testosterone.
> With your hemoglobin=16, you will have to be careful about it, and monitor it closely when managing your testosterone. If Hgb would raise above 17, have phlebotomy, drop pint of blood.
>
> You can raise testosterone two ways.
> You have to start with HCG injections first. Say
> HCG-shot=300iu/EOD
> use
> BD Ultra-Fine II U-100 Insulin Syringes Short Needle 31g 3/10cc 5/16in
> Depending on condition of your testicles that may or may not raise your testosterone (sufficiently).
> Sufficient TotalTestosterone=>600ng/dL
> While on HCG your testicles and scrotum are protected, keep using it even when you will need additional direct testosterone supplementation.
>
> Testosterone metabolizes to DHT
> DHT is responsible for sexual side more than testosterone
>
> If with HCG use you achieve sufficient testosterone, you may leave it that way.
> If you need additional testosterone, choice of testosterone that you will use will depend on DHT.
> You want to have DHT~85 ng/dL or only little higher
> Most of testosterone can be delivered as injections, preferably use above syringe and testosterone Cypionate or Enanthate 200mg/mL
> If you need to raise DHT add small amounts of Androgel 1% 2-3x/week
> Overall you still want to have Totaltestosterone~600-1000
> Likely you will need to stay closer to 600 because of your hemoglobin being already highish.
> Actually Hgb=16 is ideal value.
>
> While supplementing testosterone you may also need to monitor
> estrogens and progesterone.
>
> Your LDL is highish.
> That may change when you adjust your DHEAs which is likely low at this time.
> You want to have DHEAs(350-490) μg/dL
> Use DHEA pills, as many as it takes, 400-500mg/day is top dose but often 100-200 is enough
>
> Your cortisol= 8 ug/di 4-22 is low, you want it at 22
>
> Following dr Jack Kruses's system should help you here, but you still may need to work on this area.
>
> You should monitor
> pregnenolone
> progesterone
> cortisol,serum(7:30AM, noon, 2:30PM)
>
> you want
> pregnenolone high within range
> progesterone>=1.4ng/mL
> cortisol(7:30AM)=22
>
> Likely your pregnenolone is low. 26 mg/mL
> Pregnenolone is metabolized from LDL with help of vitD, vitA, T3
>
> Your vit D is extremely low
> You want vitD(80-120)
>
> 2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
> 1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day
> Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles
> [http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW](http://www.amazon.com/Lugols-Iodine-Family-Pack-bottles/dp/B001RRP3FW)
>
> Rarely but some people have detox symptoms from iodine.
> If so, stop it right away, study posting of grizz how to go about it.
>
> 7/day Biotic Research Bio-D-Mulsion Forte, 1drop=2000iu Vit D
> 3/day CarlsonVitamin K2 Menatetrenone 5 mg
>
> Many if not most people are low on stomach acid.
> 8 tabs with each meal Country Life Betaine Hydrochloride with Pepsin -- 600 mg - 250 Tablets
> (Unless you feel hot in the stomach, then eat half a stick of butter and next time use less)
>
> I am not a doctor.

# Thread: Sugar vs. Fat Oxidation with Respect to Carbon Dioxide
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-vs-fat-oxidation-with-respect-to-carbon-dioxide.9184/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-26T10:12:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sugar-vs-fat-oxidation-with-respect-to-carbon-dioxide.9184/post-104249>

To start this post, I'll list some of the numerous benefits associated with carbon dioxide. It's a powerful vasodilator, it promotes energy consumption and boosts the metabolism, it stimulates mitochondria bio-genesis, it enhances body oxygenation (this is called the Bohr effect), it protects neurons, it's a muscle relaxant, and it has many other desirable effects on the body. The beneficial effects of carbon dioxide are outlined in quite a detailed way at this site, here: [http://www.normalbreathing.com/](http://www.normalbreathing.com/)

Further, Dr. Kruse has mentioned the beneficial aspects of CO2 before. Here is what he wrote in a previous thread that I started a few weeks ago:

"Under the control of insulin, glucose adsorption sites on proteins and enzymes become exposed to the environment, and glucose can be burned to replenish the ATP or shunned if the cell is made IR as mentioned above in this thread. When glucose is the fuel, at the same time, carbon dioxide is produced, which functions to stabilize the resting living state. Here is where my cosmology links come back for you to consider........CO2 in cold causes matter in galaxies to condense under the force of the electromagnetic and gravitational forces of physics. In your body the same thing happens in the ABSENCE OF CYTOKINES OR INFLAMMATION. This effect is called the Bohr effect, increases the efficiency with which oxygen is unloaded from the red blood cells, curtailing the production of lactate and protons. Protons are positively charged and consider acids and they do not like Oxygen. In this highly energized coherent state, potassium is preferentially adsorbed to the ionized carboxylate groups on intracellular proteins. This satisfactorily explains why the administration of glucose and insulin can uncomfortably lower blood potassium levels............this is has huge implications for explaining rigor mortis."

Now, I don't fully understand the complex biochemistry behind this post but I got one thing. CO2 is good stuff. My concern can be seen in this very comment by Dr. Kruse though. He specifies that this beneficial CO2 is produced "when glucose is the fuel". This concept is used by a variety of anti-ketosis gurus to justify sugar burning. Fructose and glucose oxidation produces significantly more CO2 per unit of oxygen than fat burning. They say that this boost of "coherent state" promoting CO2 is what makes sugar burning more advantageous to health than fat burning. Now, I'm quite convinced that ketosis/fat-adaptation is the preferred metabolic state, but I can't quite seem to refute the idea that sugar burning promotes healing CO2, an idea which has seemed to be validated by Dr. Kruse himself.

What do you guys think of this? Are the numerous advantages of ketosis merely more important than this one drawback? Or does ketosis have a hidden source of CO2?
Thanks.

Consider astrophysics for a minute: The only difference is the scales.....the process of what happens in space and in our mitochondria are congruent.

Plasmas are the phase of matter where atoms are ripped apart into electrons and nuclei. Plasmas are primed for strong electromagnetic force interactions with the world around them, because of all the "loose stripped charged particles" they contain. So what contains them in our mitochondria? Mitochondria allow for plasma like interactions in the quantum cell because electrons and protons from foods are stripped and charges are developed. What controls their actions once made?

Hotter plasmas often have nearly all of their atoms broken apart and ionized, whereas cooler plasmas may be only partly ionized. Cold organizes plasma’s. It turns out that magnetic fields also control plasma’s better than anything we know about in astrophysics. In nuclear reactors we can use a magnetic field to control and an out of control plasma. what would happen if the magnetic field changes around a plasma of charged particles? Change the scale from a nebula star cluster and consider the human mitochondria for a moment. The native magnetic field seems to contain action in our mitochondria. But the more charged ions you have, the more electromagnetic force interactions occur within the plasma because of all the free charge, and this is what makes plasmas behave differently from non-ionized gases. Maybe this is why all mammals effectively separate electrons from protons from foods and create free heat? It seems this is the ideal way to create negative entropy by returning entropy back to the environment huh?

### heathercstevens — 2013-12-26T16:17:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-jack.9185/post-104252>

Hello there,

What do you recommend for a recurrent Ovarian cancer patient, who does not need to lose weight. She is receiving chronomodulated chemotherapy and takes a wide assortment of nutriceuticals supplements. I am wondering if there any thing she can do to get her body to recognize the cancer cells and attack them as they normally should, but clearly are not?

Heather

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-26T19:43:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-jack.9185/post-104259>

1\. I can not treat anyone on the internet. you have to rely on your doctor for advice but there are things you can do to educate yourself to ask them better questions
2\. have a look here: [http://www.jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-to-do-with-new-cancer-diagnosis/)
3\. I can show you where to look and who on this site you may try to contact who also has a similar issue. [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?8186-Ovarian-Cancer-amp-Epi-Paleo/page2](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?8186-Ovarian-Cancer-amp-Epi-Paleo/page2)
4\. Use of curcumin is huge.....and tumeric and black pepper on everything she eats is big. Lots of 99% dark chocolate helps the immune system..
5\. CT protocol is something to look into for her too.
6\. She needs to understand that contact with the Earth should be the goal from here on out. Grounding and "eating sunlight"
7\. Strict avoidance oif high glycemic carbs is a well founded idea in the literature with epithelial cancers like ovarian cancer
8\. Epithelial cancers tend to walk hand and hand with low Vitamin D levels, Altered cortisol and melatonin levels because of artifical light. Brain Gut 11 blog post would be one you should read.
9\. The best way to help fight cancers is to augment the patients immune system...............Read the anticancer mind. The diet advocated in it is not something I call ideal but the book is loaded with gems.

# Thread: Magnetico Questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-questions.9186/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-12-26T18:20:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-questions.9186/post-104256>

I recently emailed magnetico the following but have yet to get a response. Anyone else want to chip in some answers or even ask more questions?

Hi,
I am considering buying one of your sleep pads but this will be a very big investment for me and so I need to ask a few questions first...

1\. Would sleeping on a classic pad with a 4" mattress provide more or less the equivalent gauss as using a core pad with an 8" mattress?

2\. Does the size of the positive field increase with higher strength pads?

3\. Have you had feedback from clients with digestion issues? Whatever I eat or drink, even just spring water, my stomach bloats, becomes uncomfortably full, and I can feel liquid rise up through my oesophagus. I don't expect this to directly cure it but would be interested to hear how the pads have helped others with similar problems.

4\. If I lay the pad directly on the floor is it okay to be in the same room as it for the rest of the day? I spend most of my time on my bed using my laptop when I am in my house.

5\. My laptop is grounded and 2-3 feet away from my bed. Has anybody reported equipment malfunctioning at this distance?

6\. The total cost minus duties and taxes is $1,457.66 (Â£891), I also intend to use the Jack Kruse discount, for the core pad to be delivered to England. Can you give me an estimate on what the duties and taxes would cost?

Thanks for your time, Sean.

### seanb4 — 2014-01-07T11:04:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-questions.9186/post-104963>

The answers I got if anyone is interested:

1\. Would sleeping on a classic pad with a 4" mattress provide more or less the equivalent gauss as using a core pad with an 8" mattress?
There isn't too much gauss strength increase when using the Classic 5 gauss model. For each inch less than 8 inches,you would gain about 0.1-0.2 gauss
so using the classic model underneath a 4" mattress, the gauss strength would be about 5.5-5.6 gauss
With the 10 gauss Core model, the 10 gauss strength is achieved under an 11 inch mattress then for each inch less than 11 inches you would gain approx. 1/2 gauss.
Conversely, for each inch greater than 11 inch between your body and the magnets, you would decrease the gauss strength by 1/2 gauss per inch.

.

2\. Does the size of the positive field increase with higher strength pads?
The magnetic field from the edges of all sleep pad models is about 4 feet

3\. Have you had feedback from clients with digestion issues? Whatever I eat or drink, even just spring water, my stomach bloats, becomes uncomfortably full, and I can feel liquid rise up through my oesophagus. I don't expect this to directly cure it but would be interested to hear how the pads have helped others with similar problems.

I am not aware of anyone using our sleep pads specifically for digestion issues. If you would like to discuss, I would suggest calling and speaking with Dr. Bonlie

4\. If I lay the pad directly on the floor is it okay to be in the same room as it for the rest of the day? I spend most of my time on my bed using my laptop when I am in my house.

The return positive field from the edges of the sleep pad extend approx. 4 ft from the pad so you wouldn't want to be within this distance for long periods of time.
We don't recommend using a laptop when you are on the bed or again within the 4 ft as the magnetic field may erase information stored on the laptop.

5\. My laptop is grounded and 2-3 feet away from my bed. Has anybody reported equipment malfunctioning at this distance?
We have not specifically had anyone report any issues however we do not recommend having a laptop or computer on or near the sleep pad
6\. The total cost minus duties and taxes is $1,457.66 (Â£891), I also intend to use the Jack Kruse discount, for the core pad to be delivered to England. Can you give me an estimate on what the duties and taxes would cost?
We are not able to determine duty & taxes. What we have control over is the shipping cost. We would charge your credit card the pad price& shipping& insurance. Any other charges would be your responsibility to pay at time of delivery. You would have to check with your customs dept. to determine approx cost of all additional charges ( duty,taxes and any customs fees)

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-26T19:42:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-hello-everyone.9187/post-104258>

Hello,
My name is Chris Wyllie. I'm currently running my own business in Toronto as a Performance Coach and Neurofeedback and Biofeedback Specialist. I am an avid bio-hacker and aspire to optimize my performance in all aspects of my life. I am so thankful to have come across Dr. Kruse's blog and this extremely knowledgeable community. Jack's brilliance and productivity are profoundly inspiring. I'm also intrigued by the high level of expertise and quality of discussion present in the comments and forums. I hope I can offer some valuable contributions as well!

My future plans are to begin an M.B.A /J.D program at an Ivy University in the U.S. starting next year. (I definitely will now be favouring the cooler climates, sorry Stanford!)
For me maximizing productivity, cognition and overall performance is critical. The valuable knowledge on this site can likewise make me a more astute supporter of clients in my business.

I am very interested in continuing to learn more and more about your most effective bio-hacks and how they've elevated your life! People fear what they don't understand. Rather than fearing, I am going to seek to understand as much as I can. As Jack so aptly states, I love that everyone in this community is committed to achieving not only an A, but an A +. I share your same passion.

Recommendations as to initial membership? Right now I'm thinking a Gold Membership to access all the webinars would be the best choice. Have you found them to be a crucial supplement to Jack's Blogs?

I am happy to now be on the road to OPTIMAL!

After a year and a half, I have been re-reading all of Jack's blogs from the beginning. I had saved them as word docs and slightly altered the formatting for printing. I find that reading them slowly and underlining sentences that I find outstanding to be helpful. If it is a long blog I might put it down and pick it up again over two days never in a rush to finish. Jack's writing style is not always clear on the first read through but I don't move along until a paragraph makes sense. Also spending time with some of the cites and maybe looking up terms on wikipedia or google to flesh out a bit more about sometime \[e.g. SCN\]. Fortunately, winter is the time for me to do this. Working on the EMF series now.

Right now I have about 234 page doc of copy and pasted bits from the forum including some epic threads. Mostly Jack's statements with some others for context. I was think about making this available to anybody that wanted a copy. The problem is that it will not be finished.

# Thread: Excited to Join The Optimal Community: A Summary of my first two weeks.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-27T22:28:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104305>

Hello,
My name is Chris Wyllie, I've introduced myself and provided some of my background info in the Meet and Great.

I analyze everything with an open, critical mind, and try to always think horizontally; putting the pieces of the puzzle together to provide a holistic picture from different disciplines - evolutionary bio, cognition, neuroscience etc.

Everything I do revolves around maximizing human performance. I specialize in maximizing brain function and cognition, employing many bio-hacks, including neurofeedback, bio-energetic, biaurnal brainwave entrainment, biofeedback, nootropics, nutraceuticals. Mindfulness, meditation, and Qigong, are critical to cultivating the strength of mind to make things happen in my life.

As an entrepreneur, student looking to embark on a dual M.B.A/J.D degree, and athlete looking to unleash super-human performance for myself and clients....I am completely captivated by the phenomenal knowledge and discussion in OPTIMAL community. Hopping onto the road to Optimal may be the game-changing BIO-HACK to fuel all my endeavors and aspirations.

I've spent the last two weeks, devouring the information that Dr. Kruse has provided. After many re-reads, and contemplation, I am convinced. The path to Optimal is intuitive, and I find that every piece of the puzzle is ultimately tied together. However, I want to put Jack's information to the test, and see what these principles can do for me. It is my mission to maximize every bit of my potential. If I have to deal with a little bit of cold, so be it.

Week 1, 2:
My Diet has always been OPTIMAL. Consuming Superfoods like (Brain On, an algae, which I believe, and I'm sure Jack will concur, is the best food on the planet). I'm an advocate of the Bulletproof lifestyle: IF (coffee with MCT, Pastured butter, Cacao Butter) due to the awesome cognitive enhancement provided by the Ketones and Coffee combo. In the afternoon I usually lift heavy, or perform some very intense HIIT. Followed by some Qigong and Meditation to facilitate recovery. A sizeable portion of grass-fed Offal is usually dinner. I'm going to tailor carb intake even more strictly to the seasons given Dr. Kruse's recommendation.

Overwhelming CT experience: As a young, ambitious guy, reading the CT series, especially CT 6, absolutely blew me away. Immediately after, I went outside in my boxers and started dumping piles of snow in my bath tub: I had to create my own ice packs. On went the cold water and I hopped in the tub for 45 minutes. Strength of mind enabled me grin and bear the violent shivering, but I woke up with some nasty swelling and welts all over my body. Itchiness pervaded my entire body!! During the last two weeks, I've continued with the cold tubs, reducing the duration to 30 minutes. Obviously I started way to quickly, but am happy to say that I am almost cold-adapted. I felt exhilarated after my last 30 minute tub and didn't dread it one minute.

CT appealed so much to me because not only would it facilitate recovery from chronic over-training, but it would enhance cognition and productivity by increasing the current of flow in the brain. Minimizing inflammation in the brain was another critical point for me.

Questions for the community:
1\. To accelerate cold adaptation, minimize inflammation, would a super potent DHA fish oil - say Nordic Naturals Pharma. grade - be advisable? I've self experimented with 0 to 15 grams of high DHA fish oil, and have noticed large improvements with high doses.

2\. As a Qigong practitioner, one element of CT that scared me, is that the cold would literally "dampen my fire" or "Qi / Life Energy" that I work so hard through meditation and movements to accumulate. Or does CT have the reverse effect, by improving conductivity, minimizing inflammation, it improves Qi circulation and accumulation.

3\. Please tell me your top productivity, and brain enhancing BIO-HACKS!!

### caroline — 2013-12-27T23:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104307>

So happy to have you here.....can't wait to hear more....

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-28T02:05:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104314>

Thanks Caroline. What have been the most actionable and rewarding aspects of the information Jack has provided in your life?

### caroline — 2013-12-28T03:07:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104318>

Gosh .... interesting question ...... I will have to think on my feet here ....

I think - when I first ran into JK on MDA and paleo hacks .... it all finally made sense!

I dove in head first and never looked back.

I had been a binge eater most of my life and no matter what I did I could not control it. It is amazing how one can be successful in other parts of your life ... but you can't control your weight no matter how hard you try and you can't stop stuffing your gob. The lack of control made me feel like such a failure.

Within a week of starting the leptin reset I was completely binge free and more importantly - craving free - and not hungry all the time!

I was lucky enough to have a couple of consults with Dr. K. and he helped me straighten out my hormones and I have a lovely Doc here who is willing to play along..... still a work in progress tho.....

I think if you had told me two years ago that changes would happen slowly - I might not have been too impressed!
But having said that ......I love all the changes that are happening to mind, body, spirit and I would not change one single second of this journey.

It is the quantum entanglement and quantum magic that I love the most of all. I can see the joy all around me .... I am having the time of my life ...

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-28T03:20:53+0200

You are young and enthusiastic and involved and you have a good head on your shoulders ..... just imagine how many lives you will change...

Keep reading and never stop asking questions.....

### caroline — 2013-12-28T04:29:21+0200

Jack has taught me about quantum entanglement .... and now everyday I see all the possibilities ......all the people I smile at and greet and have a small conversation with ..... totally enriches my life beyond measure.

We all owe this to Jack I think .... to pay all this forward....
