### prAna303 — 2013-12-28T11:34:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104344>

Welcome...

Just how i think and do.

> Questions for the community:
> 1\. To accelerate cold adaptation, minimize inflammation, would a super potent DHA fish oil - say Nordic Naturals Pharma. grade - be advisable? I've self experimented with 0 to 15 grams of high DHA fish oil, and have noticed large improvements with high doses.

I always go for the real thing, fatty fish that is. If i have salmon, raw is how i prefer it. When we use pills i think we perhaps can use it shortly for reversal but only for that. I belive that when we do use supplements we only get a small bit of the information food stuff should supply us. So many small sublime details of data is lost when you only want the "effect" that information should have. Think digital vs analog...

> 2\. As a Qigong practitioner, one element of CT that scared me, is that the cold would literally "dampen my fire" or "Qi / Life Energy" that I work so hard through meditation and movements to accumulate. Or does CT have the reverse effect, by improving conductivity, minimizing inflammation, it improves Qi circulation and accumulation.

That thought is because Qigong and other energy based motion often comes from a warm country, yoga for sure. The idea is quantum but many do not connect the dots on how this should work. It works but nobody knows why, i think Jack can explain this and that is why i visit this place. Remember that in a quantum world the things that may look negative in the end is positive. Like cold, think about the seebeck effect. We are quite warm inside and on the outside we should have a difference, when we are close to our own temperature we start to die in a high speed rate, maximum entropy. In our tissue and other stuff we are made of we have a lot of metals, when we get that difference we create currents (Qi)... Okey, this is the non science way i think, many others can explain it more deeply.

> 3\. Please tell me your top productivity, and brain enhancing BIO-HACKS!!

Keep it simple, i always think how i would do and act IF i did not have any modern stuff around me... Golden rule for me, be the animal i am. Always try to eat in season and what the environment have to offer. Do all my living as much in the wild, running barefeet, climbing, hiking and some freediving.

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-28T19:56:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/excited-to-join-the-optimal-community-a-summary-of-my-first-two-weeks.9192/post-104356>

Caroline, I share your same passion to make the most of every moment in life, to optimize my abilities and to bring as much good as possible to the world. I'm currently helping kids with ADHD, Autism and other learning disorders, people with Depression, Anxiety and working professionals looking to optimize performance. Neurofeedback has been a game-changer in terms of enhancing mental performance. I'm also working as a Performance Coach and Personal Trainer. I really heed the work of Tim Ferris, when it comes to maximizing learning.

In order to elevate my career ceiling and implement the type of change in our society's approach to health that is needed, I'm planning on completing an Ivy MBA next year. I've always had a passion for law as well. Ultimately, if I'm going to pay it forward as much as I would like, and achieve significant change in our world, I really do need to be in a position of economic power. Thats just the way the world works... I'm hoping to becoming one of the Jack's shining starfish and enlighten the world to some of his great work. Even if we can't get everyone doing CT - which would be awesome - we can still implement other approaches to optimize human performance. Neurofeedback, improving your neurology, and current of flow in your brain is just one such thing.

# Thread: Did you overindulge over the holidays?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-12-28T02:07:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104315>

How did you do over the holidays? did you become intimately involved with a chocolate Santa???

I did have a banana and some cherries and a couple of peaches .... but really didn't want anything apart from what I would normally have. I just had no interest at all.....
I was going to make myself some paleo banana bread but couldn't be bothered, and anyway, the only reason I am interested in it is because of the huge slabs of butter I have on it!

I really don't like how I feel when I eat out of my norm.....

How did you manage? was it tough? what was your course of action?

So many things are much more important .........

### Jude — 2013-12-28T05:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104328>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104315)
>
> How did you do over the holidays? did you become intimately involved with a chocolate Santa???
>
> I did have a banana and some cherries and a couple of peaches .... but really didn't want anything apart from what I would normally have. I just had no interest at all.....
> I was going to make myself some paleo banana bread but couldn't be bothered, and anyway, the only reason I am interested in it is because of the huge slabs of butter I have on it!
>
> I really don't like how I feel when I eat out of my norm.....
>
> How did you manage? was it tough? what was your course of action?
>
> So many things are much more important .........

I have been naughty, naughty, naughty. Went on a bender, ate everything I shouldn't ...but not in huge quantities!
Being on the lean end of the scale I decided to do an N=1.
And I have paid for it! The most noticeable thing for me was the tiredness, just didn't have my usual upbeat energy thing for those busy Christmas days..and that's the first time I've felt like that since starting 2 1/2 yrs ago. The bananas were ok, and so were all the delicious sweets but will not miss them at all.
Back on track today and already feel energised, so perhaps that's a good sign.
My dil's Boxing day spread would have made JK proud, so that's a major success.

### caroline — 2013-12-28T06:15:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104329>

I didn't even enjoy my banana! I used to love them. It is the loss of energy I hate too. Does your family eat epi paleo Jude?

### Jude — 2013-12-28T06:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104334>

Caroline we try to eat epi p.....that's the intention.

Have experienced a lovely little episode to relate to you!
Two days prior to Christmas, my dil,grandies and I were able to catch up with Nick and his family in Brisbane!!!
Was so great to meet a fellow Kruiser and his kruisers! To be actually able to talk face to face with someone who's read JK, understands heaps more and has implemented even more in their lives is like.............the best Christmas present ever!

This experience should be for all of us! Make plans now to meet up! It's exhilarating....Caroline I'm sure there must be someone near you? We're only 9 hrs away by train!

It was tremendously exciting for my dil to be able to meet other folk who are achieving such success with the JK protocols, and more specifically with regard to healing children. Nick is a great model and if iirc has a growing number of JK devotees amongst his friends and acquaintances....need a blog here Nick!

So we came away with firm intentions of going all out to get the magnetico for kids and us! Was surprised that the magnetico was much smaller in height than I imagined. Nick had lots of tips for us re use of same and was badgered with lots of questions. We are so grateful to Nick and Jacquie for hosting such a delicious epi paleo banquet, nourishing body, soul and spirit of fellow travellers with such kindness.
THE END ......
It was such a special time......................................

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:08:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104335>

How awesome Jude. Nick is such a lovely guy. How did that happen?

It is funny you mention this .... I have been thinking of doing an outback trip...Cape York or some such. Never mind that I have never camped or anything remotely close. I wouldnt have a clue how to put up a tent etc!

Do you need help shearing some sheep??

I am going to pm you

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104337>

I think we should organize our own meet and great!

Corriander, freesia, ?? Who else?

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:21:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104338>

Soooooo .....Nick puts on a big feast for people passing thru??? Good to know!

### Jude — 2013-12-28T07:27:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104339>

Yup........................................................

it's 41.4 outside now and shearing in Feb much the same...........shearing isn't all it's cracked up to be!
...........meeting up somewhere a lot colder sounds really good to me!

### Inger — 2013-12-28T09:23:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104341>

Nope.. I had some 85 - 99 % chocolate and lots of raw organic nuts (hazelnuts, macadamias, pistazios, walnuts, almonds), but it was on purpose  and it did not make me feel bad at all. I also made a raw peppermint chocolate cake/pie with just a few dates and almonds + coconut oil and spices for crust and 85 % organic chocolate, raw egg yolk, coconut oil and peppermint oil for filling, that was crazy yummy! Family liked it too (at least some of them lol)

I do ate out of my regular meal times because of eating with others and ate without hunger etc. but so far I have had no ill effects really. Going back to no carbs after the holidays for sure but the few carbs from nuts and the VLC raw choco cake did not affect me short term - or maybe the fact that I have laughed so much this Christmas made up for the damage..lol

I have had so much energy... and not tired at all even if it is so warm and dark and rainy here all days. very weird, +4 degree C
and I have not had coffee either! I have not wanted to.. that is weird too!

### nicld — 2013-12-28T19:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104355>

I have not been too bad, some caramels and a gluten free Russian Tea cake cookie every other day. Had a bit more cheese than I should have and a little more wine then I should.

Did have some risotto with roasted duck the other night and it tasted oh so good. We went out saw The Phantom of the Opera afterwards and stayed out a bit too late and yesterday was rough.

Good thing is have been doing a lot of CT. Just have to make it through New Years Eve.

### Destiny — 2013-12-29T15:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104394>

I did not have a problem to stay on the ketogenic diet. My family did the same since I am the one that cooks !

I was exposed to the CW treats from my friends but would not touch them. In addition, I was not even tempted.

### Nick — 2014-01-01T01:35:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104545>

### sooperb — 2014-01-05T15:21:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104797>

Turkish Delight, Christmas pud flavoured truffles (yes they were good!) sugar has always been my downfall. I can easily say no to bread, pasta, rice and crap food but sugar, sigh, it's a big hurdle. I eventually thru the last half down the sink ..... notice I didn't say the first half ...lol

I was surprised I didn't find it way to sweet - it was pretty delicious!

# Thread: Another wifi consideration

### lioness7 — 2013-12-28T05:51:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104327>

Just a heads up to anyone with a newer furnace or thermostat....we had a new thermostat installed in the fall. My "new " thermostat died after four years requiring a new replacement. The technician was pleased to inform me that it was wifi enabled as a factory preset. Gee golly thanks....the old one was not wifi though programmable. He thought it was great to be able to set the furnace or a/c while anywhere in the world through one's phone....I told him to disable the wifi and he did not know how and that I would have to figure it out myself which I did within a few moments. Please check any of your newer equipment. Most are pre set for wifi whether hooked up or not....fridges, freezers, washers, dryers, printers, gaming systems etc. Ridiculous! Thankfully still missed on the radar for a smart meter....they will have to deal with my dog or worse yet , me...;-) ( no opt out here yet) I am sure most of you are aware already but for those who are not ....

### caroline — 2013-12-28T06:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104330>

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:14:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104336>

How is your family? I have never forgotten that video you sent me....

### lioness7 — 2013-12-28T22:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104366>

Thank you for asking Caroline ....they are all good and his highness is thriving (lots of lip and attitude/ treatment side effect due to growing up a tad quicker, though must admit a bit of genetics as well...but otherwise, so far no txt related side effects, though i would love a sock in his mouth every so often! switched schools for the last 4 to a non emf environment...no wifi etc...no phones allowed on the property (why do i have to pay for this ?)

I had an emf scan five yrs ago....we were fine...but this spring I re did it as a result of a new tower going up. my only issue was the three yr old wifi printer I never enabled and was shocked to find out out it was factory preset. the thermostat issue really drove me over the edge...wth! we have promoted ourselves to incomptency...just because we can do something , doesn't mean we should.

### caroline — 2013-12-28T22:54:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104374>

In Jack's EMF webinar - I think .... he talked about having old appliances rebuilt instead of buying new - exactly what you are talking about.

That is so interesting about your son's new school ..... so some people are actually getting it.... You must have interesting conversations with other parents there? You are in North TO - right?

I have told this story before about a woman I met with a daughter with a very serious genetic condition and her son who has lots of problems too - very bad asthma etc. He got very much worse when they changed to a school that is wified to the hilt. I gave her all kinds of stuff to read and links etc. I saw her the other day .... she is still taking him to the same Doc and he is still continually on antbiotics and she hasn't read anything. Altho, at the time, she told me she would do anything! She is obviously not a "Lioness"

I have a lot of trouble understanding that ......

Giving you a lot of lip??? sounds pretty life affirming to me!

### lioness7 — 2013-12-30T17:45:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104448>

Not sure if people are getting it. The school's policy is more related to distraction than the health dangers of emf. Most of our friends and family think we are making a big deal out of nothing..."why don't you have cordless phones? omg , how do you talk around the house?" (duh, on a 25Ft corded one or speaker phone) "Oh but the wifi is so cool, you can start your washing machine from work with the app!" (so what, most washers have had timers for years.) "Why haven't you fixed the broken GPS on your car? " ; "If it was all bad for us, why would it be allowed?" (at which point I usually drop the topic), etc, etc.........

Having very limited internet access for the last 10 years has certainly helped my kids to not become gaming addicts, social media teen freaks and make them use their time more wisely on the computer as well as to explore better ways to spend their free time. For now, they can only tether with their phones with a pre set GB limit, making them more aware of their unnecessary usage. One day I hope to be able to have hardwire access, but for now I don't.

I will also continue to repair my appliances so that I do not have to purchase new ones or negate the warranty if I have to rip out the wifi they are pre loaded with!

BTW....99 cents on kindle today: [http://emfhelp.net/emr-survival-guide/](http://emfhelp.net/emr-survival-guide/)

# Thread: Interesting Study on Ketosis and DHA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-study-on-ketosis-and-dha.9199/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-28T09:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-study-on-ketosis-and-dha.9199/post-104340>

While doing some research on ketosis and free fatty acid metabolism, I came across this interesting study: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16125522](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16125522)
Essentially, ketosis reduces DHA and other PUFAs in the fat and blood, and increases their presence in the brain and liver (by quite substantial amounts). Now, as far as I can tell, the increased accumulation of DHA and AA in the brain is a good thing. The decreased amount of PUFA in the fatty tissue and the blood would be considered very good by Ray Peat/Danny Roddy but I'm not sure whether Dr. Kruse would see this as a good thing or a bad thing. Lastly, I'm not sure if the vastly increased amounts of PUFAs (25x increase in AA and DHA) in liver triglycerides is a good thing or a bad thing. Do they have a role in improving liver function or are they just lingering around, creating lipid peroxides and what not? I'd love to hear the thoughts of this forum's members.

# Thread: Anti Cancer Brew
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ricardo — 2013-12-28T18:15:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/post-104350>

A local scientist involved in cancer research has documented, charted, the synergy produced by a tea of simple ingredients.

He uses a 3 ingredient formula consisting of:

A green tea with a high ECGC content: Sencha is a good choice.
Turmeric spice
A tiny amount of black pepper

The ingredients are combined and added to boiling water then simmered for 10 minutes.

His chart shows that the resultant molecule is quite a bit stronger than if the ingredients were taken individually.

I don't know how to explain how this works. However, Dr Beliveau gave a seminar at the Jewish General Hospital, a well known cancer research center. A friend who is a surgeon attended and was impressed with the science behind this simple formula.

Dr Beliveau's "CV" and a word about his research:

[http://www.mcgill.ca/translational-research-cancer/researcher-biographies/beliveau](http://www.mcgill.ca/translational-research-cancer/researcher-biographies/beliveau)

It does not taste good: however, I take it as a medicine.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-28T19:10:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/post-104354>

I think this is a great cocktail for cancer.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T01:16:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/post-104412>

Turmeric tea tastes wonderful. This recipe could have green tea added to it:

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/creamy-turmeric-tea/#axzz2ouOrBANp](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/creamy-turmeric-tea/#axzz2ouOrBANp)

### Ricardo — 2013-12-30T06:15:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/post-104428>

I inquired about adding ingredients and was told that the best effect was to follow the formula without making any changes (or adding anything else no matter if its a fine ingredient. For instance, I had suggested cayenne. )

In addition, there should be a minimum of a 10 minutes of simmering as recommended. Despite that bit it tastes bitter ( the green tea tastes bitter after 3 or 4 minutes of brewing ) there are many cancer patients who take it daily.

Your recipe sounds delicious ( its all good) and I am not meaning to sound harsh. Its just that it is considered a form of medicine and there is a preferred formula that has been documented by Dr Beliveau who holds the Chair for Cancer Prevention and Treatment at the University of Quebec.

I want to make that clear in case there is someone who needs results for themselves or for someone they care about. Cancer is so widespread in these times.

I was informed that its best to follow the formula to the letter. I hope this helps.

PS: And thank you to Dr Kruse for weighing in.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:41:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/post-104453>

> [Ricardo said:](/goto/post?id=104428)
>
> I inquired about adding ingredients and was told that the best effect was to follow the formula without making any changes (or adding anything else no matter if its a fine ingredient. For instance, I had suggested cayenne. )

No worries, Ricardo. If I had cancer, I would be very careful about adding additional ingredients also, especially butter or almond milk.
Turmeric and black pepper have a history of working well together, and green tea is well known to help us control our cells that have turned cancerous for any reason. Thanks for bringing this combination to our attention.

### QuantumRadical — 2014-01-02T00:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/post-104570>

is there a recommended distribution of the three ingredients for the tea, turmeric, and pepper? In teaspoons? Or just adjust to taste?

### Ricardo — 2014-01-02T03:11:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anti-cancer-brew.9200/post-104576>

> [QuantumRadical said:](/goto/post?id=104570)
>
> is there a recommended distribution of the three ingredients for the tea, turmeric, and pepper? In teaspoons? Or just adjust to taste?

About a level teaspoon of both a green tea with a high ECGC content ( Sencha for instance) and turmeric powder. Add a tiny pinch of black pepper. It should steep a full ten minutes.

Some prefer to use turmeric root that has been crushed in a garlic press. Turmeric stains so that way can be a bit messy. Same quantity, about a teaspoon.

Here in Quebec, there are researchers doing work in neutraceuticals, especially nutritional strategies to prevent cancer. I am hoping to hear of more findings and will post any that could be helpful.

Happy New Year to you too!

Anyway, I live in the darkness of northern Europe and have been doing Leptin reset for a bunch of weeks. Started with CT recently and have just started taking cold showers with the coldest water my tap will give me (15-16Â°C / 60F). Unfortunately, I have no bathtub so the shower will have to do for now.

Due to my thyroid issues I've been feeling cold most of the time for several years, and have been wearing warm sweaters in the evening, so it's a huge change and surprise that I actually feel warm after a cold shower. If I didn't have any neighbors I'd probably walk around naked for an hour or two afterwards...

I've noticed that taking a cold shower soon after a meal tends to give me diarrhea - is that normal? If I wait for an hour or two after my meal, I'm fine.

I do my Movnat sessions outside in the cold with minimal clothing....
Sometimes during the day I will just strip down and take a cold shower or a jump in my cold pool.
During the summer months it is ice baths and or jumps in a river when I am out an about.
Basically I just try and fit it in when I can....

### Eugenia — 2013-12-29T12:08:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direction-of-sleeping.9204/post-104390>

I'm moving to a new house soon, with much less EMF around me. However, there's this still question about the direction we sleep in (because of the Earth magnetic poles etc). I searched on this forum and didn't find a similar thread. I also did a google search, and no one agrees about the directions we should be sleeping in. I think there was one study done that showed that people had more REM sleep in one of the directions slept. So, what's the wisdom here about it? Does it matter how I place the bed in the room?

### Hope — 2013-12-29T19:53:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direction-of-sleeping.9204/post-104402>

Congrats on your upcoming move! I am so jealous!

Sorry I don't know about the directions....feng shui talks about it but they don't know about EMF.

### caroline — 2013-12-29T22:33:57+0200

### Dali Dula — 2013-12-29T18:30:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/observation-on-dawning-phenomenon.9207/post-104397>

I am 53 yr old T2D/Hashi's following the Ancient pathway for two and a half years.For the past two months or so I have been observing the following:
As I wake in the am about 5:30 I have a hot flash accompanied by light perspiration over my entire body and the disappearance of morning chubby. I mean gone. I test BG upon rising and it is usually quite high (135-190) The high fasting BG is nothing new nor is losing chubby to elevated BG, but the hot flash/sweating thing is new. This has been regular. I sleep well and do not regularly sweat at night. I believe this is due to cortisol secretion. What might be changing? Any thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-30T00:07:13+0200

secondary hypogonadism from low T with elevated cortisol. Test

Me & my partner are looking at starting a family so are their any other supplements that may help.

healthman, I love maca, and how it makes me feel, but there is so much conflicting information about it regarding its use for people who are hypothyroid like I am. I'm new to ketogenesis. I've heard A LOT of differing opinions on counting carbs. I think I've dialed in my protein and I'm good with fat, but do I look at net carbs or total carbs? I'm trying to apply a keto style diet to my strength training regiment. Any insight would be greatly appreciated!

### caroline — 2013-12-30T05:12:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs.9213/post-104425>

Have you looked at the easy start guide for the Leptin RX? using the epi-paleo template?

It will give you a great overview of all of this...

### wardog0351 — 2013-12-30T13:07:10+0200

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104425)

>
> Have you read Jack's book? The Epi-Paleo RX: The prescription for Disease reversal and Optimal health

### wardog0351 — 2013-12-30T13:30:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs.9213/post-104442>

The start guide just mentioned "carbs" in reference to breakfast. I understand the premis, but "carbs" means different things to differnent people. I'm unsure if he's referring to net carb intake or total carbs. I'm having no problem keeping my net carbs below 30 but I'm finding myself struggling to keep my total carb intake that low.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-12-30T14:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs.9213/post-104444>

Ketosis is not just a black or white state, it can be further enhanced, and to reap its benefits you want it to be deep, that is measured in the ketone bodies in blood in general (but specially the beta-hydroxybutyrate ketone that fuels the brain) and you can be at a decent point limiting to 50 gr of low GI carbs according to the common low-carb community consensus, but to reach a deep ketosis state of near 3mmol that seems to be the optimal I think it will requiere a little bit less or not at all, and also an undercaloric diet and not much protein also. Some aminoacids alter ketosis in different ways also.

You can also not be in ketosis at all and still benefit from the 'lower carb' diet in comparison with the SAD or others, it depends on what you're after and your exact context.

### wardog0351 — 2014-01-04T01:40:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs.9213/post-104689>

### wardog0351 — 2014-01-05T01:38:39+0200

I've been able to consistently keep my ratios to 80% healthy fats, 15% protein, and 5% carbs. Initially I noticed some slight declines with regards to strength, but I can feel it coming back. My CT progress has been going awesome too. I figured out the best time for me is about 8:30 pm. I sleep like a baby and get my 8 hrs. I wake with no trouble at all and feel completely energized. Right now my focus is on really seeing how ketosis effects my strength training...like I said so far its coming back nicely.

so - as Jack has said ..... C/T/ oxy/sleep .... awesome combination!

you have got all this nailed - so to speak.....

I'll mix 1 cup of Coconut milk, Almond Butter and 1 T of MCT oil and then throw in in the freezer while I eat my dinner about 30 minutes and then have it as a cold treat! Thats is that last thing I eat for the day, usually at 5:30 PM then CT at 8:30PM.

### wardog0351 — 2014-02-01T08:18:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs.9213/post-107689>

### caroline — 2014-02-01T08:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs.9213/post-107690>

don't sweat the small stuff! keep grounded, hug a tree, C/T ....laugh a lot, sing, dance, hug a friend or two or three! Get lots and lots of good spring water.

in the scheme of things kerrygold is not important at all! Food is only 10% ....why are you worried about 10%?

The important thing is to have fun and connect with people you care about and interact with strangers. It is amazing what stories you hear and what you can learn - especially if you spend time listening ..... food is way, way down the list!

on your death bed you aren't going to say ..."I wish I had eaten more Kerrygold butter" I can absolutely guarantee that!

### nonchalant — 2014-02-04T19:01:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs.9213/post-108300>

Oh boy. "Better quality lighting" sounds like extra bright, stark blue fluorescent lights. And lots of them. GE is probably overstocked on them and needs to get rid of extra inventory.

> The researchers found that participants exposed to blue light consistently rated themselves as less sleepy, had quicker reaction times and fewer lapses of attention during the performance tests compared to those who were exposed to green light. They also showed changes in brain activity patterns that indicated a more alert state.
>
> "These results contribute to our understanding of how light impacts the brain and open up a new range of possibilities for using light to improve human alertness, productivity and safety," explained Steven Lockley, PhD, neuroscientist at BWH and senior investigator of the study. "While helping to improve alertness in night workers has obvious safety benefits, day shift workers may also benefit from better quality lighting that would not only help them see better but also make them more alert."

# Thread: Making new Memories....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-12-30T05:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104427>

I have been thinking about this ....Making new memories - a lot lately.

Dr. K. has said that as we age it gets harder and harder to make new memories .... so we have to work at it.

At face value - it doesn't seem like it would be that hard .... but it kinda is!

I have been trying really hard lately to do different things everyday. On the surface - it should be a piece of cake - especially if it is fun/enjoyable....... but it doesn't seem to be that simple.

I can easily revert to old habits - back to my comfort zone.

I was talking to a woman in the pool this morning about swimming all winter. She said "You just have to do - not think"

So - from now on - I am just going to get up and put my speedo on..... and then turn off my thinking machine .... and just do!

Are you making new memories? What is on your "to do" list???

Something tho - that I don't quite understand ..... If I had money I could do a ton of things - make a ton of new memories ......I wouldn't even hesitate to do the most crazy things.
But when it comes to my everyday routine ....... it is so much harder.

Some routine maybe is good for our general wellbeing? or is that just a safety net?

### Inger — 2013-12-30T08:24:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104429>

I love routines in the morning and evening... I think they do me good. I always do the same thing, wake up.. lay a bit in bed and think and enjoy the sweet feeling... raise, cuddle my cat and feed her, drink lots of good water, do face dunks, eat a good breakfast... shower/CT, look into the sky, read something that gives me good vibes, and I am good to go. I love my mornings. In the evenings I am very reduced in every way.. and I go early to bed. Those two moments of the day are sacred to me. I think I need it for my well being. Help me stay clear in my head and thoughts too.

now in the holidays the house is full of religious people that makes quite a bit of noise, so I just lock me into my apartment mornings and evenings, I do not care about their "party"... lol And it helps so much! I do not get much stressed at all. I am learning to not care about others expectations or their demands. All day I play with the children. They are magic... even if they are hurt by their parents. The more they need unconditional love. So I make those memories... for me and for them. They are beautiful. I don't know... but it always happens somethings every day and that is what will be the memories and I want them to be beautiful so I do those beautiful things. Take the kids out and cuddle with the sheep... or bake some paleo treats with them. Hold them and touch them. Listen to their stories. Talk to them. Those memories are so much worth to me and I know they are to the kids too. I remember when I was a kid and my aunts and uncles... I LOVED them. Yeah... their daddies and moms are crazy fundamentalists and hurt their kids mentally and physically - the more they need a loving aunt! I do not care about the parents. Sorry. All they do is thinking I need to be saved by God. HA! ;-)
Life is full of challenges. I try to focus on the essentials. What difference can I do in this world right now?

I think... the memories comes from all this.... when I am 100% in the moment. I have many moments in my previous life I do not remember at all.. I bet it is the times when I was not focused and sucking in life. It is kind of sad.. like wasted life/time. So I have decided not to live like that anymore. Instead I do 100% what I do. And it feels very good! Like very much alive =)

hope this does not sound too confusing...lol

### caroline — 2013-12-30T09:46:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104434>

I think you are so right Inger .... there is great comfort in morning and evening routines.

I do find tho, for me, I have to push myself out of my comfort zone or I can fritter too much time away. But having said that I think being still ... just "being" seems to be vitally important to me now ....... well - as long as it is outside.

It seems that I can sit at the ocean for hours - just sucking up all the wonderful energy .....or on my balcony listening to the birds and the ocean and the wind blowing thru the trees.

But - I do crave craziness and silliness and adventure and all the unexpected things/people that come my way when I leave my comfort zone ......

### caroline — 2013-12-30T09:51:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104435>

The thing is Inger .... If I had money - I would jump on a plane and tell you" Happy New Year" in person!
I would give you the biggest hug! xo

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:51:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104454>

Speaking of memories, not Memories, I've been a bit concerned lately about other's forgetfulness. Just about all of my relatives, even some much younger than I, are forgetting things that occurred or were said just a few days ago. Or more important things from a few weeks ago. I know I'm getting better, because I can now remember where I placed just about anything, by thinking about it for a minute. I can usually remember where I put it, as well as what else I was doing/thinking as I put it there. But I also think my relatives are getting worse.

### Inger — 2013-12-30T18:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104457>

Yes.. that would be amazing  maybe next year Audrey!
I am not sure if I will manage to stay up until midnight this year either though, last year I was at sleep at 10 PM...

### caroline — 2013-12-30T22:18:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104469>

I can't manage to stay up that late ether anymore - the other nite I was in bed a t 8:30!

This year we have a trifecta of fireworks at the Opera House/coathanger. I will probably miss all of it!

# Thread: Cold showers question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tanya — 2013-12-30T17:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104447>

Here is my dilemma. Cold showers are the only option for me to do my C/T (at the present time, anyway), but I canâ€™t stand to wash my hair in cold water. So, when I try to do it using warm water in the beginning - I have a problem switching to a cold water later. So, I start with cold and then finish it with warm water for my hair, but doing that just does not feel right to me. I am curious to know how the rest of you, who also take cold showers, do it?

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104450>

Hi Tanya. I think you will find it easier to wash your hair in the summertime, when the tap temperature is higher. I used to wash first with cold and end with slightly warm water. Perhaps you could continue through the winter and try it again in a few months?

BTW, I really like your signature.

### Inger — 2013-12-30T18:29:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104451>

I have to always wash my hair with warm water too, every second day. I use mud and with cold water only it does not get clean I think. So I shower with warm first and then after I am finished washing I turn shower to ice cold and shower my feet slowly moving the shower up on my legs.. the other foot.. then I do the same with my hands and move the shower up on my arms to the shoulders and then in the end I shower my head and face and whole bod, no issue at all. It is very nice way to do cold showers - instead of jump starting with the head... I think =)

Sometimes I wash my hair in the kitchen sink too. You could do this if you do not want warm water on your body.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:55:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104456>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=104451)
>
> So I shower with warm first and then after I am finished washing I turn shower to ice cold and shower my feet slowly moving the shower up on my legs.. the other foot.. then I do the same with my hands and move the shower up on my arms to the shoulders and then in the end I shower my head and face and whole bod, no issue at all. It is very nice way to do cold showers - instead of jump starting with the head... I think =)

I agree completely.

### caroline — 2013-12-30T22:32:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104471>

yep ..... kitchen sink for me too sometimes .....but I don't mind the cold shower either. It is hard at first - but mind over matter!. Actually I don't have a choice - I pulled the breaker on my hotwater tank! Only turn it on when my grandsons come over - and they want to have a bath. I cannot convince them that cold is good!

It is very nice way to do cold showers - instead of jump starting with the head... I think =)

Inger, thank you for these very practical tips - it might just work for me. Actually, I believe that it will definitely work for me. Anyone who can manage to get their skin as velvet has to be doing something right!!!

nonchalant, I can't wait that long for the summer to arrive. I am already late into this game. Hopefully, what Inger shared should work for me....

Thank you for noticing my signature. There so many thought-provoking and inspiring ones compiled here. Makes me appreciate even more being here!

### Tanya — 2013-12-30T23:07:46+0200

### Flora — 2013-12-31T00:46:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104479>

Yep, I'm a cold shower gal too - have come to really love it now, but I always start warm and have a wash / shampoo hair first and then turn to cold gradually. I can now do it right over my head and back (hardest part!) but mostly concentrate over abdomen, legs, face and lower back. Sometimes I tell myself that I don't have to go cold today - but I always do because I know it makes me feel so good afterwards! Water is now at around 8 C / 45 F - feeling good and definitely noticing benefits.

### Tanya — 2013-12-31T19:45:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104515>

Flora, It is good to know that you are getting benefits from taking cold showers. Interesting that just like me you also have a difficulty with having it on your back. I wonder why that is happening? At any rate, I hope that eventually we both will be able to overcome that.

### caroline — 2013-12-31T23:41:54+0200

Tanya - you misunderstand .... I pulled my breaker on purpose - so I am not tempted! I only need hot water for dishes/washing up - so I just boil the kettle. It is only me and I don't cook - hardly ever! That is why raw is great!!

When I realised how long it was taking to heat up a whole tank of water - I decided to opt out!

sounds weird I guess ......

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-01T01:25:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104540>

I love my cold showers, but also find that washing my hair is less than pleasant in the cold water. Not so much because of the temperature on my own body, but because it seems like the shampoo/conditioner doesn't like the cold temps and my hair seems to get grossly tangled up. I have found that washing my hair in the sink is a good solution. Or I'll just wash my hair quickly at the start with lukewarm water. That seems to make it easier to transition to cold cold. But I also think there is value in hot and cold, so sometimes I'll just relish the cold water for as long as is reasonable (I live in the high desert, so water is an issue here), and then I'll turn on super hot water at the end and enjoy some back and forth thermogenesis.

### caroline — 2014-01-01T22:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104563>

Tanya - I don't know if it is dedication. When we first moved to OZ I was always floored by our electric bills! when I called about it they said it was the hot water tank - it was on demand - so every time we turned the hot water tap on - it started heating.

When I moved where I live now - for some reason - my neighbour was talking about the hot water and said not to leave it on .... just turn the breaker on 15 minutes before a shower and then turn it off after.

So - as usual with me .... I went one or two steps further....

It has done wonders for my electric bill - and I get to C/T ..... multi tasking!

Now you have me really wondering about what else I have said that people may have misunderstood???? I tend to post first and think later .... maybe that better be my NewYear's resolution!

# Thread: Chrysanthemama's Journaling Journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chrysanthemama — 2013-12-30T20:59:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104463>

I woke early this morning after 8 hours of excellent sleep. After my morning affirmations and gratitudes, I suddenly had the idea that I needed to begin a journal here. I believe it will be helpful for me as I start fresh in 2014, and as a way to be held accountable. I'll provide a little background, and I hope to write here weekly throughout the next year and beyond.

\~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
How in the heck did I find this place?

Back in 2011, I found that my weight had soared despite the fact that I am incredibly active. I have always been stocky and muscular, but I was becoming just plain fat. Despite doing martial arts several times a week, lifting weights, cycling, trail running, and hiking, I was carrying around quite a load of fat on top of all that muscle. A few years prior to 2011, I ditched bread and tortillas from my diet. I substituted one meal a day with soup, took up spinning, and lost 17 pounds. That was remarkable! I was in my late 30s and had begun to feel what I assumed was a slowing of my metabolism. As time went by, I found myself adding a piece or two of garlic bread to my plate, and ended up eating the bun instead of avoiding it. And that's where I found myself in 2011. Fat. Gross. Embarrassed. I was an athlete, after all! Why was I the one who looked pudgy when all my other athlete friends looked amazing?

I have been an herbalist for over 20 years. Trained in Traditional Chinese Medicine, Ayurveda, and western herbal medicine traditions, I have long known and practiced healing through nutrition. My husband and I have always eaten fresh, whole foods, and with the exception of crackers, pasta and tortillas (We live in New Mexico!), we didn't eat many processed foods. We did, however, consume quite a lot of legumes. Traditional New Mexican food consists of a lot of beans, and we loved making dal. My biggest grain-based consumption, however, has long been beer. Micro-brewed, fresh, hoppy ale. I blame that on going to college in the Pacific Northwest at a time when brew pubs were becoming all the rage. But in general we ate good. We avoided fast food like the plague, ate a shit ton of greens and veggies, and loved to make stir fry. Because my husband and his father are bow hunters, we've long been blessed with fresh wild game, and I grew up eating home-grown eggs, chickens, and raw milk.

But by 2011, I knew that something was going on. I felt out of control. I had started experiencing swelling in my left ankle and I began to freak out that maybe I had some form of pre-diabetes, which I thought was plainly absurd! As an herbalist, I am a member of the American Herbalist's Guild, the only licensing body for herbal professionals in the country. As part of their email list, I received all kinds of information on just about anything nutrition, herbal, or health. One day, an herbalist named Karen Vaughn posted a link to Jack's website. She was responding to a post about diabetes from another member, and it piqued my interest. She was claiming he was reversing diabetes and other AI conditions. I clicked on over to Jack's site and proceeded to have my mind blown.

From my first moments browsing through the Quilt, reading about CT, and the Leptin Reset, I was hooked. But skeptical. I would read a little, think about it, wonder about it, dismiss it, and then come back for more. CT completely blew me away. "Why should losing weight be so contrived," I thought. I must've said it 50 times a day. "Grain-free? Not for me, how in the hell can I give up beer for 6 weeks?" I worked through all these questions and thoughts and emotions for months. I continued to read and wonder and have my mind blown again and again. I didn't understand everything, but I was curious to learn more.

\~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Paleo? Can I do it?

Back in April 2012, I bought a book that Jack had referred to on the Quilt, "The Monk Who Sold his Ferarri," by Robin Sharma. It is an awesome book, similar in a way to "The Alchemist," in that it is a fable meant to convey some pretty deep stuff. This book reminded me of my own intimate relationship with meditation, awareness, and power, all of which seemed to have fallen by the wayside for the past several years. During this same time (end of 2011 and beginning of 2012) I had become at risk for losing my job. In an industry that had seemed bullet-proof for years, we suddenly had no funding. A political exercise became the demise of many people's jobs. I was freaked, because I am the breadwinner for my family. With two daughters and a musician husband, a beautiful home we built ourselves, and plenty of debt, the thought of losing my job with nothing viable on the future was complete madness for me. I was stressed out.

After reading the book, I began practicing meditation again regularly. I also started journaling again--something that I did religiously for decades, until my house burned down and I lost my lifetime worth of writing--poetry, fiction, and stacks of journals. The building where I work is an old middle school, and just happens to be situated on the most beautiful canyon. I started finding myself drawn to edge of the canyon several times each week where I would meditate, write, and observe. Sometimes I found myself wracked with emotion, bawling my eyes out as I would write my thoughts down on paper. Within a week of reading the book, being drawn to writing, realizing that I need to figure out what the hell I am going to do with my life, setting a web of intention throughout my workspace that I could not be laid off, and meditation, I decided to try out paleo. I figured, if nothing else, I'd just try it out for 30 days. Because beer was such a part of my evening habit, I had to find a replacement for the end of the day unwinding. I bought some Zevia soda, and vowed to give it all a shot.

Within the first week, I'd lost 7 pounds. If it hadn't happened to me, I would NOT have believed it. I ended that week drinking a few beers on Friday, and doing the Warrior Dash on Saturday. I had started my period that morning, and I felt remarkably bad--foggy head, headache, and bloated belly. I drove over 2.5 hours to do a silly 5K that I hadn't even trained for. It was kind of fun, though, and I enjoyed the drive and solitude. After the race I never redeemed by free beer ticket, and came home and drank Zevia instead of beer. That was a first. By the end of the month I'd lost 12 pounds, and by the end of four months I'd lost 30 pounds. I was resolute and motivated.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-12-30T21:00:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104464>

And then came…
\~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
The Crash

By November 2012, I was down 35 pounds. But I started feeling awful. I was a hormonal wreck. I was eating high-fat, very low carb, and I had been feeling exceptional all summer and fall. I discovered that I no longer bonked on long bike rides and runs, and that I had gained a tremendous amount of speed during my martial arts workouts. Because I live in NM, it is not always easy for me to eat a lot of seafood, but I was trying to add in as much as possible through sardines, scallops, salmon, and shrimp. Thanksgiving came and went and I suddenly felt like I was on an emotional roller coaster. I realized that I hadn't been grounding in weeks, since early snow had seemingly shut me down from my weekly meditation/grounding sessions on the canyon. I also hadn't been doing any CT, other than being outside in the cold. I quickly changed course and made intentional efforts to ground regularly and starting CT in my bath tub at home.

Shortly before Christmas 2012 I started feeling better. I saw that Jack would be coming to New Mexico for a cold-weather MovNat workshop, and as a holiday bonus check floated through my bank account, I was excited to sign up. I began CTing in earnest in order to be prepared for the workshop. As the workshop date came closer, we just kept getting pounded by snow and storms. It was awesome. And then the magical date arrived. I'll save the story about the workshop for another entry. The most important thing I learned, though, was that nearly everything Erwan spoke about during the workshop I already knew. Many of the things we did, I've been doing forever (not the tree climbing, or certain specific MovNat skills though!!). Throughout the workshop I realized that the most important thing I was gaining was recognition of my own expertise.

That was huge.

\~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Fast-forward to the present.

For me, 2012 was the year of transition. At the start of 2013, I stated that 2013 would be the year of action.

In many way I have been correct, although sometimes it takes longer than I want for certain things to take place.

I have spent much of the year delving deeply into building a new business. I have spent countless hours learning, reading, understanding, comprehending, dreaming, idealizing, brainstorming, and thinking, thinking, thinking. As much as I wanted things in place, I am flowing through the process. Along the way, I have found myself caught up in my own bad habits, frustrations, and challenges. This all began with painting my daughters' rooms. But really, I shouldn't need an excuse to fall backwards. I just did. It started with beer while painting. A perfectly logical marriage in my own mind. Then beer on the weekends. Then beer while camping. On vacation. Even at AHS13!! Holy smokes! That was like the epitome of pure paleo blasphemy.

And then my pants started getting tighter. And I could see the puffiness in my face. And I have been struggling to come to peace with the fact that I truly should not be drinking beer. Ever. But I don't like wine, unless it's expensive. And I am burned out on soda. And tequila is good maybe once a week, if that. And my willpower sometimes sucks, and it seems like it has sucked quite a lot for the past several months. But I am aware of this penchant for good beer, and that is half the battle, right?

So, to conclude for now, I've decided to do a 30-day full epi-paleo reset beginning on January 1st. Along with it, I am vowing to meditate at least 15 minutes each day. In addition to my daily gratitudes, I am going to add in affirmations. In addition to the normal no grains, legumes, or sugar, I will be eliminating alcohol and dairy. I need to find another fat source as coconut oil does not agree with me, but I am planning to order some duck fat and some grass fed tallow. I have never gone dairy-free, but I'm willing to give it a shot, too.

It is time for me to commit to this sort of challenge. I must take the reins of my life and continue to move forward. I know that until I am where I need to be in a whole and complete manner, I will not be moving forward at the rate that I would like. So, therefore, I am hoping to be held accountable for completing the 30-day challenge, and then for not accepting beer as a habit in my life any longer.

Happy New Year! Blessings to All.

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-30T21:27:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104465>

Welcome back Kristi....

I so love your story. you are my sister in kindred spirits....

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-12-30T22:00:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104468>

Thanks, Gretchen! I have lots to tell. And surely I'm crazy to be adding a new place to write when I am already obligated in other areas. But it just seems right.

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-31T01:18:39+0200

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-31T01:20:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104482>

Great to read your story! Here's to a fabulous 2014.

### caroline — 2013-12-31T03:41:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104489>

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-01T00:58:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104534>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104489)
>
> Hi Mama! Glad to see you are back. I was worried we had lost you!
>
> Happy New Year to you and your family.

Thank you!! No, you didn't lose me. I've just been so caught up in my world of working towards transitioning away from my "career" and into my true purpose in life. I'm still working on it, but things are becoming more tangible and real. Even today, on this New Year's Eve, and on a night with a New Moon, which speaks directly to my own psyche (New Moons are my favorite moons), I ended up feeling in conflict with my employer over health insurance, which is a story all on its own. I am so in need of being fully invested in my own business, and not working for anyone else, but as the primary breadwinner for my family, I'm trying to make this happen as seamlessly as possible. Although we are working some magical gems for my Husband's business as well. I've missed being a part of the action, but I love this community!

Happy New Year to you and to Everyone!

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-01T01:08:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104535>

I have been off work since last Tuesday. My husband was in Denver performing music Thursday through yesterday. I have not taken a hike since last Sunday, and boy was I desperate for a hike and my icy cold soaking spring today. It didn't happen, however, and I am trying to not let it interfere with my positive mental attitude (PMA!). Tomorrow, I don't care if it's 6am and freezing cold, I'm taking my dog on a run to my spring, and I am going to sit in there as long as I possibly can. The grounding, the CT, the energy of the canyon is what my spirit and my body are in need of. My emotions have been on high for the past couple days. I have been having the most intense shamanic-inspired dreams for weeks, my own future seems tossed up in the cosmos, I am diligently working towards the goal of launching classes and workshops for pregnant women this coming spring, I am struggling with entrenched demons, and the New Year seems to be enhancing everything to the umpteenth degree. I need to sit in cold water, in the ground, in a canyon. But I will do it tomorrow. To ring in the New Year.

Happy New Year to All! May Blessings of good fortune, health, and prosperity enrich your life in 2014 and beyond!

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-02T02:01:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104575>

I slept poorly last night, the first time in a while. I woke at 3:20, approximately the time of the new moon. I could not go back to sleep after that, which is unusual. At 4:15 I went and got the dog from my daughter's room so I could take her on a hike first thing. I still could not go back to sleep, so I tried meditating for a while. Eventually I achieved a state of deep rest, but I never really fell back to sleep. In the morning, my resolve is super strong. I see the changes I need to do each day, and I vow to follow through. This morning, my resolve to follow through with a full 30-day commitment to no alcohol or dairy felt absolute.

At 7:30, I decided I'd better go before anyone else woke up. There was no one on the roads this morning, and I drove to one of my favorite trail heads. I did not have a time limit, so I parked at a different entrance to my soaking hole canyon. There was already a car there, but I figured they had headed up, and not down. THe lack of footprints on the trail confirmed my thoughts. I strolled leisurely along a perimeter trail, and then began the hike up into the canyon. At one point it was super dark in the canyon due to trees and the sun being so low. It felt really cool and somewhat eerie.

When I reached the entrance to the springs canyon, I stopped and looked around for a while. I hoped to see the Owl, but I didn't. Way up on the ridge I noticed movement, and realized it was the other hiker and his dogs. I'd never seen anyone on the ridge above the canyon before while I was up there, so I decided to go slowly, knowing they'd probably be heading on down the trail that winds along the ridge. I came to the big pool and it was super dark and cold. The sun wasn't anywhere near being out at this location for another couple hours. I decided to hike up to the upper pool, which was ravaged in the massive flooding we had in the late summer. THere is a huge boulder that fell into the pool, and is right where it would be perfect to sit. The sun was shining brightly up in that part of the canyon. I kept lolly-gagging around, and finally decided to strip down and get in. The temperature this morning was about 37, and with the sun shining on me, it felt pretty balmy…except for the winds that kept blowing through. The wind was really whipping up on top of the mesas, but the canyon was more protected.

I got into the pool, and I wore my vibrams to keep my feet warmer. I soaked for about 15 minutes. My pup kept coming over to me and licking my face, which was sweet. Once I got out, I let the sun warm my body. I thought I'd hike straight out the canyon, and wondered if the old trail was still in place. My pup and and I scrambled way up into the grotto. It has been completely devastated by two wildfires and the flooding. The old spring vault is completely gone. Water just starts pouring out of the side of the slopes, which is really cool! Someone should take advantage of this water and bottle it.

I skirted around the overhanging cliff walls, and thought I'd just skirt along the edge of the cliff and climb out. The higher I climbed, the more I realized that I was getting myself in a precarious position. I did not have shoes with traction on, and the snow was like sugar…it did not clump together, and much of the scree, while frozen in place, was sketchy. As I looked around I decided I should not continue up, and so I snow slid down the steep slope and ended up backtracking along the trail. It was a good adventure. I felt real fear for a moment, which is good. I hadn't felt real fear in a while…just stupid stress-based shit.

I came home and fixed a shot of espresso. I beaded a necklace today, as well. The first time I've beaded anything in several years. My creative desire to make necklaces, dreamcatchers, and other pieces of nature-based art. At least I broke the ice.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-02T17:53:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104594>

yesterday - Havoc and I went for a walk midday. Chaos was down with the flu and DH stayed home with her (he was up w/her all night - such a loving dad)

Well on our walk we crossed the foot bridge that spans the little creek that runs through our neighborhood. at the opposite bank - there's a slate bench some one built - so we sat down upon it. about 5min into sitting a squirrel bounded up to us, stopped, sat up on his hind legs and began sniffing - and kept circling around... as if to ask what were we doing sitting in his spot. then he bounded away. 5 min after that he came back put his paws upon my shoe (I had moved my feet upon to the bench) - and stood there looking at me.

I think this little fellow has been fed by humans - its the only way to explain his behavior. I now think this might be the same squirrel that I have come upon during my walks - who shows no fear when I'm walking. I also wonder if he's the same squirrel that keeps coming to our house and bangs on the windows every now and again (he sits on the window sill - and taps his front paws on the windows - like he's knocking - silly squirrel)

Havoc loved it.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-02T18:31:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-104595>

Yeah, we ran into a chipmunk in Colorado that had been similarly trained. I have a picture of him sitting with his front paws in a begging position. He was adorable, I must say. And once in the Rio Grande park, we came across a pack of javelina between us and our campsite. We just stood there, and they just stared at us. Finally I picked up some rubble, and tossed it into the bushes in a neutral place. They all ran toward the sound, apparently thinking it was food. Sheesh. Are people going to start feeding rattlesnakes next?

Most of the time, herbs support the body to recover through illness, but they usually do not result in the immediate relief-from-symptoms that most people hope for. ### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-29T00:23:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-107235>

January has been rough. I started out with great expectation and motivation, which was dashed pretty quick.
I have been doing regular CT sessions, though, and that is saving my soul and my brain. Probably even literally.
I need to start operating on quantum time, because this regular 24-hour a day thing is not working very well for me. I simply have too many things to do, and not enough time to get things done.
Lately, my biggest challenge has been fitting in regular workouts. I've always been super dedicated to my workouts, but lately it seems like if I want to CT, I have to forego my workout in order to do so. Even though I run to the spring and run back, sometimes incorporating a super steep hill climb if I take the longer route, but otherwise my schedule has been so packed to the brim, that my workouts are the first to slide. It sucks! I haven't lifted anything since October.

My homework for this week is to carve out a schedule that is going to work for me. When my stress levels start going up, as they have been because my job is still on the line, I find that I am not functioning as a Mom very well. So...I need to flow more, bend more, flex more. There is no way to balance all these things. That would be like juggling, which wouldn't work for very long. Bend, flow, flex, breathe.

I'm also doing the same with DH. its changing how the kids respond to me.

I'm struggling right now - Just flat out tired... exhausted. I'm lucky if I can make it to 930 before I pass out asleep for the night. though I'm waking at 6am w/o issue... Right now I'm taking little wins

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-07-01T06:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-132833>

Wow…it's been 6 months since I posted anything in this journal. I've been through so many different experiences since January. It is now the last day of June. As much as I've been working towards creating my business that will allow me the ability to ease out of my J.O.B., and finally start doing what I've always wanted to do (which is…), I've undergone so much stuff. Amazing, real, stuff.

Firstly, I went through a full online launch. After the launch, I realized that I had zero interest in what I had launched. Probably a good thing I realized it so quickly.

Secondly, I hired an amazing, well-established launch coach to help me launch my first online program. She is amazing, she kept me on track, but she also kept me on a topic I realized I didn't want to be on. That sucked.

Thirdly, I hired an amazing, intuitive, business mentor. She is fucking incredible. In many ways I wish I could do what she is doing. But I also realize that she is doing her thing, and I am doing my thing.

My thing means I'm not quite there yet.

The fact that both of us pulled back at the same time meant something serious was in the works in the Universe.

I tried launching myself as a birth mentor (which I will still do, btw), but I felt like I was niche-fying myself in a way that was not good.

Immediately after launching, I pulled back, I went back to my roots, literally, and things have been so much better since then.

I am an herbalist.

I've been out there looking at plants, collecting herbs, teaching people about how to identify and collect herbs. I love showing people how to identify and use herbs.

I have not been to this website in a while, but boy have I missed it.

Things are good, actually.

I've been sleeping so amazingly well. I've been non-stressed. I've been having amazing sex with my soulmate. I have been a good mom. I have not been stressed about my job. I've been trying to seamlessly move from my job to my own entrepreneurial enterprise. But overall, I've been happy. I just got a new mountain bike. I love mountain biking. It is the epitome of interval workouts. I'm just plain happy. I hope you are too. If you want to come to New Mexico and experience the amazing wonder an power of this place, contact me.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-07-30T06:27:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-136029>

Ahhh...summer sublime. This truly has been one of the best summers I've had in many years.

At the end of May, a dear friend of mine suggested that I make a commitment to myself, appointments with myself and the Divine, and allow the Divine to speak through me in whatever medium is appropriate at the time, whether writing, music, dance, poetry, song, photography, or pure and simple observation.

She made this suggestion to me after witnessing my online business launch process. As she said, "it looked forced and uncomfortable. The true you was not shining through."

Indeed.

In as kind and gentle manner as she could, she bluntly nailed my situation. If I am going to be successful with an online business, I need to be damn well sure I am doing what I'm here to do. Despite two years of diligent and concerted effort of trying to figure out in clear and concise terms what I want to do, I wasn't quite there yet. I mean, I know that I do certain things really well, like leading herb classes, martial arts, writing, and wellness consults for women, but combining all my skills together into unique and powerful programs wasn't quite working.

So, at the end of May, I made a commitment to have at least two of these appointments a week.

I stripped off all the layers of what I "should" be doing and I committed to doing only what felt right at that moment.

I am so pleased to say that this commitment has turned into something amaze-balls.

In the short span of 10 weeks, I've unearthed a hugely significant part of myself that was long buried.

Not only that, but I found a very important part of me that I left behind over 24 years ago. A part of me that was waiting to be scooped back up so that I could progress forward on this life path consciously and physically and spiritually connected to this powerful place that I live. It was kind of like finding a message in a bottle that I sent off to sea. Only the bottle was filled with the very finest essential oil distillate in its purest form.

It was the me of the age of 19. A pure, unadulterated me that was nothing but optimistic, brave, powerful, observant, connected, and spiritually inclined.

Over the course of the past couple decades, that pure distillate of me was adulterated by time, stress, life, circumstances. There was still the powerful me, the observant me, the me that would power-on no matter what. The me that achieved 4 black belts and birthed two babies naturally. The me that climbed rock and ice and mountain biked all the time. But that pure distillate of me was buried in time. Buried in the layers of experiences. Buried by stress. Buried by serious life events such a a massive wildfire that consumed my town and my beloved mountains, my husband's traumatic brain injury, getting pregnant, giving birth--twice, building a house, working full time while simultaneously owning and operating an outdoor retail store and guide business, and buried by the town I live in. Buried by my fear of appearing to be too spiritually connected to my inner wisdom (so I made sure everything was grounded in science). Buried by living in a community filled with PhDs.

Remarkably, and without any plant allies, I found that me. I found her alone, out on the mesas and within ancient caves of the cliff-dwellers. I found her within the very tendrils of the plants that I was collecting for medicine. I found her in the scent of the rain on the parched, dry earth after the rain began to fall, and lightning had energized the ground.

Ironically, at least in my mind, I feel like I've come a full circle...only the circle isn't round, it's a spiral.

I've come back to the very place I began 24 years ago.

I came back to my deep and incredibly powerful spiritual connection to place. To this place. This place where I live. On the rim of a volcanic caldera, on the solidified tuff mesas formed thousands of years ago. The canyons and mesas where the natives of this land lives for thousands of years. This place where their imprints are everywhere, and where those imprints are simultaneously being washed away through rain, wind, and time.

I came back to cave walking, moccasins, power, and reward through discomfort.

This whole process has been so incredible. The really incredible part, for me, really, is just going back to pure intuitive living.

I've had some serious experiences where listening to my intuition was critical.

This week, I elected to decline my speaking gig at AHS14. That's a pretty big fucking deal. I was accepted as a speaker, even though I didn't think they were going to accept my proposal. It was a proposal I wasn't entirely on board with. Because I wanted to speak on the spiritual shit. The shamanistic stuff I'm experiencing. I wanted to be representing a different level. But I submitted a proposal that was less than that. Less than what my true self was. And it was accepted even though I didn't think it would be.

And so for the past several weeks I've had this huge, deep, intuitive sense that I needed to not go to AHS14.

Every time I thought about preparing my presentation, I felt nothing but a massive wall of resistance.

Every time I thought about cancelling, I felt a huge wave of relief.

I had a plane ticket. I had a hotel room. They were actually going to pay for the hotel!

But on Sunday I cancelled. I felt huge relief. I cancelled my plane ticket and my hotel. I feel relived.

This is intuitive shit. This is the shit I work through. This is what I know. I know that listening to your intuition is more important than anything. At any given time.

Most people cast off their intuition with the wind. They worry too much about what others might think, or worry about obligation and what they are supposed to do. Or just simply doubt that their intuitive voice is real.

I know that while my intuition has been screaming at me to not go since the middle of June, I had to wait for a clear and distinct sign from the Universe to tell me that I was right. I have asked and waited, and asked and waited, and asked and waited for weeks. And the clarity I was seeking came to me when I was not asking and waiting. It came to me while I was sitting in a power spot and looking at the clouds, and the very distinct, very clear thought came to me that said, "you need to send that email cancelling AHS today. And cancel your plane ticket, too." Truly, while some might brush that shit off as bullshit, it couldn't have gotten more clear than that.

So, in light of cancelling my AHS14 speaking gig, I am now preparing to offer my new programs for anyone who might be interested in tuning in to their intuition, tuning in to their spirituality, and walking through places of power. We can even tailor it to include lots of high-altitude CT during the winter.

I love knowing that I am now a whole and complete me, and that I don't have to deny and ignore the facets of me that were the most interesting.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-07-31T06:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-136269>

Thank you, Ladies! You are the first to hear the reasons why I am not speaking at AHS. I cancelled and know that it's a big deal to turn down this opportunity. Curious what you think, Jack. I thought that the Paleo community was who I wanted to target, but now I realize that the Paleo community is too limited. Sure, some of them might be interested in the spiritual, shamanistic connections to the Earth, as well as the herbal and plant foraging/food knowledge that I can share. But the fact of the matter is that I am more interested in a broad audience. I'm not even sure I'm going to miss the symposium. I had fun last year, but I was surprised by the lack of connection to the actual outdoors (3 full days in a hotel, up and down the elevators!), and the cliquish nature. Granted, I met some amazing and lovely people that I fell in love with instantly and who I am still friends with now, and I know the networking is valuable. Anyway, it's all interesting.

I am so happy to read about all that you have been up to, as well. I love this place.
