### Chrysanthemama — 2014-08-29T21:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-139274>

I have continued to go through really profound stuff recently.

We went to the mountains of CO the second week of August and spent a week doing lots of thermogenesis. Hot springs, super cold mountain water. Often together. Total, pure bliss. I still want to create my in-ground rock hot tub and cold plunge in my yard. A week of hot springing made that desire even more strong.

I wrote a blog post about my experience, as I truly went through a mystical transformation. [You can read about that here.](http://www.kristibeguin.com/magic-in-the-mountains/)

I didn't delve into all the weirdness that I was experiencing in the blog post, however. I was supposed to be presenting at AHS the first week of August. My presentation was to have been the very last one on Saturday the 9th. Beginning in July, I started feeling a profoundly deep knowing that I shouldn't go to CA. I couldn't explain this, and I set out to be fully determined to know whether this resistance was simply fear on my part, or whether the Universe was really telling me I shouldn't go. I've had some pretty intense intuitive feelings like this before, and when I had denied these strong intuitive feelings before, really bad things had happened. So, I knew that I needed to be mindful of this feeling, and I had several weeks to get clear on this before feeling like I would be up against a rock and a hard place when it came to either completing my presentation or canceling. Every single day I would ask, is this for real? Should I not go because of some specific reason, or am I just feeling flaky? For weeks the answer was not clear. Although, in retrospect, it really was, because my heart was telling me it was so.

Out on the trails, I'd ask the question, and signs were provided without clear direction. One day, in particular, I was riding my bike back to work after having taken a gorgeous ride for lunch. I'd already been feeling very present and in-tune with my life and the environment. I asked this question for the umpteenth time. Out loud. As I rolled over a ravine I saw a small blue-black raven feather. Then I saw another, and another. I thought, I need to collect those, so I turned my bike around, fell into a tree, scraping up my arm pretty good. I parked my bike, saw a fourth, large raven feather, and walked back to the first feather. I collected them, one, two, three, and then the fourth was gone. Gone! This huge feather I had just seen was gone. No wind, no nothing. Only 20 feet of distance from where I had seen it to where it no longer was. I zig-zagged over the whole area and it was gone. I hadn't imagined it. It had been there. I left feeling confused.

But now I know what the answer was in that 4th large feather that went missing.

It wasn't for several more weeks that the answer came to me clear as a bell. I was at this amazing power place. It is only a short hike from my front door. I was writing in my journal, being present, observing, and finally I rolled back and lay down on the rocks and looked up at the clouds. I asked the question again. Clearly, as if someone were talking to me, I heard, you need to write the email canceling today. Well. That was clear enough.

So I did. Never once did I feel a moment of regret. Not once. Not one ounce. I didn't know if I was averting some disaster, or if no disaster would occur since I wasn't going. It didn't matter. I followed the direction of my heart, and I knew it was right.

Two weeks later, the week I should have been gone, my youngest daughter was attending horse camp. The first day, I explored this area where I had grown up, but where I'd never been. The camp was held at this amazing, gorgeous NM ranch along a river. I grew up mere miles from there, but had never been to that part of the valley. I drove around, explored, journaled, took photos, and then eventually ended up at the river. I found some beautiful rocks, walked up and down the river barefoot, and shooed away flies in the heat. I decided to eat my lunch and read a book. As I sat there, I started feeling like I was in danger. I quickly got up, looked around, shuttled my stuff back to the car and got in. I actually was filled with adrenaline. It reminded me of being a kid knowing that people got knifed in the river. I used to be terrified of being knifed in the river. I got in the car and drove back to the camp where I immediately felt safe.

The next day, I dropped off my girl and decided to go to Santa Fe. I didn't tell anyone I was going. I got to SF and went to my favorite book store and esoteric shop. I browsed for hours and ended up buying a Tibetan black quartz crystal, a purple flourite crystal, and a piece of Amazonite. I bought two books. I asked if they had any Shungite, but they had sold their last piece the day before and were waiting on their order. I left and felt compelled to put the Tibetan crystal inside my bra, immediately beneath my heart and left breast. I then headed to the herb store to get some supplies. While driving to the herb store, I noticed that the car in front of me was filled with some very strange creatures. I looked in their rear-view mirror, and I swear to god they did not look human. As I looked, the driver was looking right back at me. He looked like a spirit, or like a caricature of a spirit. He did not look human. His passenger did not look human. I freaked the fuck out and suddenly was filled with dread. Pure dread. I hadn't felt dread like that in years. Literally. It was pure energy from another plane of existence. I willed the stop light to change and then immediately turned right into the herb store parking lot. I was freaked out. My heart was racing. I suddenly felt like I needed to get out of Santa Fe soon. In fact, I probably would have left right after that, but I was starving, and needed to get food. The herb store instantly grounded and calmed me. I regained my bearings.

There, I purchased a bracelet of Aventurine and put it on.

Oddly, I hadn't felt compelled to work with stones or crystals in years. But for that past week, I had been drawn back to the rocks and crystals in my own life. While waiting for a client one morning, I sat on a hill above the trail head and wrote in my journal. While sitting there, I noticed hundreds of pieces of flaked obsidian. I started walking around picking up obsidian flakes and putting them in my pocket. Later that week, I started feeling like weird malevolent energy was coming at my house. We have a circular glass block wall in our house that is immediately opposite our front door. Over the course of that week, I began creating a design with the obsidian flakes, crystals, and other stones that I had around this wall. It felt protective, somehow. Somehow this process of protecting the front of my home stirred deeper connections in me, and so while in Santa Fe on this day, I found myself buying up all these different stones.

I went to Whole Foods to get lunch. When I got back in my car I was once again filled with dread. I felt like I needed to leave Santa Fe RIGHT THE FUCK NOW. It was the weirdest, most malevolent feeling I'd had since 1999, when I experienced the foreboding of the wildfire that was to come destroy my home and town. I got in my car, and decided to leave through the main entry, and not the rear like I usually do. As I waited for the heavy traffic to clear so that I could make a right-hand turn, the person in the vehicle behind me started honking. I was like, what?? Why are you honking at me, there's hundreds of cars? He kept honking, honking, honking. I looked in the rear-view mirror and my heart stopped. I was instantly flush with adrenaline. My face turned hot-red. I felt completely in danger. At the first opportunity I had, I pulled out. I made a swift left to get on the road out of town. This person was following me. He kept honking at me. I worried that my roof box was open, or I had a flat tire. I was freaking out. I kept breathing...in....out....in...out. Willing my heart to calm down and my lungs to keep carrying oxygen throughout my blood.

Finally, I got to an intersection and he took another turn. I felt the danger subside. I got on the highway, north out of town. I felt like I was being carried by the wind. Huge cumulonimbus clouds were building up over the mountains and the valley where my wee one was. My heart was still racing. I hadn't told anyone I was going to Santa Fe. At that moment, I felt the Tibetan crystal hot against my ribs. Protecting me. Carrying me with the wind back to safety.

One week later, I was sitting by myself next to the fire at my campsite. The river was gurgling behind me. I'd had seriously lucid dreams of spirits, stones, and animals. I had felt the vibration of the owl's call. I had walked into the woods earlier that day, holding each stone in my hand, thinking to myself, why have I been going through this weird stuff? Why am I feeling like I am feeling? Which was as if a barricade had been built inside my heart. I had shut off my husband. I felt like I had buried myself within the depths of my soul. I was locked out of the normal world. We were having our 20th wedding anniversary, and I didn't want even one mention of it. I was lost in the la-la-land of my brain. Deep, buried, walled-up. It was SO FREAKING WEIRD.

However, that day, I walked into the woods, and the trees spoke to me. You are about to know what your true purpose is. The aspens quaked. The spruce towered over me.

That felt mystical. Magical. Angelic.

I opened up some of the stones I was hiding beneath. I felt some of the wall falling down.

Part II below.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-08-29T21:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-139275>

Continued:

That evening, I was in a symphonic communication with the mountain. Sitting below the north flank of the 14-er Mount Princeton, the entire mountain and I were in chorus. I was being flushed with information. All the hard work I have been doing was being rewarded with the music of the ancients and song of the true wild. I was being validated. Everywhere I looked, I was being filled with sign after sign after sign that I am on the right path. That I have finally realized what I needed to realize. That all events and time and places had conspired to bring me to that place at that very moment so that I could be there to hear what I was being told with majestic harmony and goodness and wonder. The smoke swirled around me in blessing. The thunder clapped and acknowledged my thoughts. The wind spiraled around with the smoke and reminded me to the very depths of my bones of why I am here. I worried that I would forget, that I would lose the message I was being given, but the thought, If it is true, you will know, kept repeating over and over and over.

I recognized the power inherent in all everything. The Tibetan quartz was buzzing, the Amazonite was tingling my other hand. My face was being graced with the wind and the smoke, and my eyes were filled with the vast power and beauty of that place. My heart was flush with awareness that all my feelings of ambivalence were necessary for me to be filled with this deep, tremendous fount of empathy. I felt split wide open.

At one point, in need of acknowledging my mentors, I sent my energy and intention all the way to NOLA in thanks. Gratitude for what I've learned through Jack. And all the way up to San Francisco, to another mentor, to let her know that I had found it. It had found me.

Immediately after this orchestral symphonic mystical experience, I placed a huge puffball mushroom my daughters and I had found on the signpost designating our camp site number. A sentinel. A guardian. A keeper.

One week later, last Saturday August 23, I was preparing some mushrooms for our dinner. I was at the stove stirring in the butter, and I thought of that puffball on the signpost. Immediately, I was instantly tranported as if to the very spot. I could see the mountains. I could hear the river. I was casting my view over the steep walls of the mountain. And then I was told, California is about to have the biggest earthquake they've experienced since 1989. Literally. It sounded as if someone had spoken it to me.

When I woke up Sunday morning, and saw that there had been an earthquake in Napa, I just about shit my pants. As a result of a delicious dinner, and delicious sleep, I'd forgotten the message I had so clearly heard until I saw the news on google. Things were feeling pretty weird at this point.

This week, I've been feeling pretty odd. Fall is beginning to show signs. I'm still bursting with ideas that I need to fine-tune and prepare into a program that I want to lead. My husband is about to leave to go bow hunting for Elk, tomorrow. I still feel like there are malevolent energies busting about in my personal space. The impending change of season is making me feel like grieving. Summer has been SO incredibly powerful. But I am looking forward to cold and winter. And outdoor winter cold-spring soaking.

I am now certain that had I gone to CA something tragic would have happened.

The lesson in the feathers? The 4th feather could have been me.

I chose to listen to my intuition. As a result, my intuitive strength has been increasing by orders of magnitude, as has my spiritual connection to everything around me.

Blessings. xoxo

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-09-21T03:02:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-141485>

Since my odd episode of malevolent energies and feelings of danger that took place in August, I've been devoting an extensive amount of time interacting with stones and plants. I have given myself permission to simply just allow myself to observe my interactions without emotion or bias. Additionally, I've basically realized that I cannot stand shoes, period. I wear my moccasins, and my leather sandals when I "have" to have shoes, but this is proving tiresome. I need to come up with a better solution, as my easily kicked off leather flip-flops have broken and become unwearable. My Chacos flip flops, which used to be my go-to sandal, cause me pain when I wear them. I feel like I've reached a new level in barefootness. It's awesome!

I've been reading this fantastic book, [Stones of the New Consciousness, by Robert Simmons](http://heavenandearthjewelry.com/Stones-of-the-New-Consciousness.aspx). On the surface, this might look like the typical New Age woo woo book, but there are so many Jack-isms in this book, and I'm being continually blown away with nearly every page I read. He goes into great detail about the liquid crystal body matrix, semi-conduction within cells, discussions about collagen fibers, water, and quantum entanglement. Two controversial scientists he constantly refers to are Mae Wan Ho and Lian Sidirov, along with tons of references to Heart Math.

I bought this book the same day I felt like I was literally swept out of Santa Fe on the wind, thanks to the protection that was granted to me from a Tibetan quartz crystal. You can't get much more woo than that. But it was so flipping real, that I cannot discount that awareness.

While in CO, I read about the stone [Moldavite](http://www.metaguides.net/pbos205.jpg). I knew I wanted some, but then forgot. Then I saw two pieces of jewelery with Moldavite, while working our birth worker booth at a local Wellness Fair. Then I couldn't get them out of my mind. I began to feel almost desperate to get some pieces, which are becoming more and more rare. I also want some [Shungite](http://www.metaguides.net/47shungite.jpg), which I've still not been able to obtain, as of yet.

Ironically, way back when, when I was a late late teen and in my early 20s, I was completely intuitively using stones in my bead work. Every piece I made was intuitively and thoughtfully designed based on the energy I wanted the piece to emit, or the protection I wanted it to convey.

Thankfully, once again, I've fallen back in love with stones and crystals, and I'm so curious how they affect us at the cellular level, the energetic level.

The other interesting thing is that immediately during this malevolent energy thing, I put every piece of obsidian I had on this little circular ledge that is the first thing you see upon entering my house. I felt like placing the obsidian there was protective. This was before I had re-read anything about any stone. But then, [in reading about obsidian](http://www.metaguides.net/pbos214.jpg), it's clear why that was my first go-to stone. "It is a strong stone of spiritual protection." On Wednesday, I took an awesome hike up to this incredible meadow. All along the way, I kept finding obsidian pieces, many of which appear to be crafted. In fact several appear to be one big spear tip, or large arrowhead, that all broke apart over time. This is not an archeological area, but clearly, people were hunting there hundreds of years ago. Given the rough terrain, there is no indication that it would have been a place where people lived...only hunted.

Yesterday I received my Reiki 1 certification and bought an amazing piece of Moldavite that is wrapped in gold. I am at this moment wearing it around my head, and over my 3rd eye. The 3rd eye is pulsating, and my entire head feels like it is pulsing with energy.

Today, I did my first meditation with this Moldavite piece. I sat at my usual spot on the edge of a beautiful cliff. I'd worn the piece all night as a necklace, and I had the most bizarre dreams throughout the night. But, until I did the meditation, I had not felt the actual stone in my hands intentionally. I immediately began to feel a pulsating in my hand, which extended to my other hand, that was holding the hand holding the stone. The pulsation went up my right arm. It was a very strong, and very real pulsation.

I was compelled to open my eyes, and when I did, my eyes landed immediately on the top of an oak shrub tree in front of me. In the top of that oak, there was what looked like a perfect, large feather. I stared at the feather for quite some time, not knowing what it was, as it was a black shadow within the beautiful green leaves, and I couldn't really discern what it was. It felt like it was a gift. I immediately took off my shoes, and scrambled down the rock, down through all the thick bushes, down and around to where I could get to that particular oak, climbed back up a big rock, pulled on the middle branches, and finally carefully plucked the perfect Raven feather out of the top of that shrub. As I did, all of the canyon erupted in chirping and song and harmony. The wind blew, all the birds were singing their songs, and a lizard ran right up to my foot and looked at me.

I scrambled out another cliff, and just knew that whatever that was, was right.

I have many Raven feathers, since they are everywhere, here, but I've never seen on in the top of a huge oak scrub tree, nor have I ever done what I did to get this one.

Pretty cool.

The gifts of nature are everywhere. It is up to us to be aware enough to see them.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-11-26T02:01:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-148509>

So much has continued to evolve, grow, shift, transform in my life.
For those of you who do not know, I am hosting a 30-day challenge on Facebook to encourage people to get outside, get cold, be aware, get grounded. It's called the [Waken Your Winter Wild Journey](http://www.kristibeguin.com/winter-wild), and it's a countdown to the Winter Solstice and December New Moon. I am sending daily prompts for people to consider each day, as a means to step away from the stress of the Holidays, and to find a grounded, connected place from which to experience these last, beautiful, colder, darker days of the year (at least in this hemisphere!!) If you want to sign up, I'll keep registration open until December 1.

I interviewed our very own Dr. Jack, and I'll be posting the link to the interview on Thanksgiving Day. In addition to the interview with Jack, I also have other bonus interviews with Erwan and Jessika Le Corre of MovNat fame, as well as high priestesses, shamans, crystal gurus, and others. It's awesome! It's free! There are prizes!

[Here is the link!](http://www.kristibeguin.com/winter-wild)

All this came to me after I made a spiritual commitment on Halloween. Within hours, this Winter Wilding idea began flooding my consciousness. It all feels like this is coming through me, not from me. And already, there are people out there pushing their limits, stretching outside their comfort zones, and embracing this challenge. I'm astonished! FOr those of you here in this community who have joined, YAY!! It's so exciting to be a part of this process together. And if you are interested in joining, sign up! It's fun, and you might win something cool! Plus you get another bonus Jack interview!

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-12-05T06:14:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-149263>

Just checking in to say, Wow! I've been so amazed at the flow that has been flowing through me. It is not from me, it is coming through me.

I worry that I jinxed us here in NM with my cold journey...I've always been one of those that does think that the weather is controlled by singular people, as completely ridiculous as it seems. I do a cold weather challenge and it's been balmy, warm, and sunny here in our mountains, when it should be cold and snowy. It has been raining for over 24 hours, however, so the ice climber in me knows that we will have some incredible ice forming up regardless; it still gets really cold in the shaded canyons.

Jack's Kruse. Whoa. I want to go more than anything. My oldest daughter is terrified of cruise ships, thanks to her love for the movie _The Titanic_. I want us all to go. We will drive to NOLA and then board the ship. I'm going to make this happen. I love this sort of planning for something, and I know it is right and true and timely. The energy shows it to be so.

So much has been changing, shifting, transforming, and moving. The Holidays feel light, lovely, and wonderful. We just need snow and cold.

Send that Maunder Minimum stuff this way, will ya!

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-10-13T03:03:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-179259>

I'm back! Its only been almost a year. Time flies.

Jack, thank you for that link. I will explore those more fully. This is right in line with what I've been doing for the past year. I just completed a 9-month intensive apprenticeship with a shaman and energy worker. Talk about quantum entanglement. Working with energy at the cellular level and beyond. It's been incredible, mind blowing, eye opening and so satisfying to see how real this shit is.

In perfect timing, I recently went through a massive health crisis right when this apprenticeship was coming to completion.

There is no doubt this timing was right in line with the energetic and physical changes that needed to happen for me to step through the portal to my own becoming.

I am going to document this experience here, however, in a series of short posts (I tend to write novels, so in the essence of time, I thought maybe I should do a set of shorter posts for you to comment on, if you feel so compelled). This has been a very intense situation that I'm still recovering from.

In any case, I'm checking in here in the forum, and I think my activity here must correspond to colder weather! Lol! We went to the lake today, which was a cool 55-degrees. The temps outside were 80. It was awesome to swim for hours in the cold water and then sun on the sandstone rocks. It's getting colder, and I'm gearing up for another Waken Your Winter experience to count down to the Winter Solstice.

Looking forward to catching up here!

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-10-28T05:08:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-180604>

Alright. I'm taking the time to sit down and write about all the shit I went through recently.

But to back it up a bit, I'll start with where I was.

Beginning of 2012. Athlete. Lives at 7500 ft above sea level. Eating home-cooked, mostly organic, whole foods. No "junk" really. But lots of beer.

Ahh....beer. Microbrewed beers have been the bane of my existence.

Gretchen posted this yesterday: [http://wilwheaton.net/2015/10/seven-things-i-did-to-reboot-my-life/](http://wilwheaton.net/2015/10/seven-things-i-did-to-reboot-my-life/)

And he writes about beer about how I feel about beer. It sucks.

Anyway...

In March of 2012, I saw a picture of me with two of my dearest friends and I looked really bloated. Not so much fat, but puffed-up. It was gross and made me feel bad. I'd been reading Jack's blogs since the middle of 2011, and I was having my mind blown. All of sounded interesting, contrived, possible, miraculous, and something I'd have to give up beer for.

But in April of 2012, I gave it a shot. Sort of by accident I also ended up going low-carb, although it's not anything I intentionally set out to do, but once I did, I really, really liked it. Until I didn't like it. Because I wasn't actually following anyone's protocols, but was just sort of winging it and going at it alone.

So, in November 2012, I felt really bad. Irritable, hormonally unstable, and stressed because my job had been totally on the line for a while. Working a lot, busy Mom of 2, husband who didn't bring in income, and really feeling like I'd hit my wall. But Thanksgiving happened. I was still grain-free, but I I fixed some kick ass t-day dinner with carbs. I ended up feeling a bit better. (Yeah, I've read about this. I've been learning how I fucked things up back then. Totally out of sync with the season, even though I was still doing a lot of grounding and CT, almost no sun, yeah...)

I'm fortunate. I work in an old single story cinder block building with a concrete floor, with no wi-fi, and you can't even get a wi-fi signal where I'm at. I am right next to an amazing canyon and beautiful trails right out my office door. I work 4-days a week. We homeschool (Unschool for those of you familiar). I have long been an outdoor freak. I mountain bike, rock climb, ice climb, hike, trail run, do martial arts, swim, and generally just enjoy my life.

In 2012, I was really, really stressed. I went low-carb in the spring, and didn't get enough morning sun. I ate very little seafood.

That November, I started feeling better with some carbs. I haven't been in ketosis since then. I am working on getting back to ketosis this winter, however. I feel much more informed and in control.

2013--lots to tell...some of it is back before this post. Met Jack. Met Erwan, met Joe, did MovNat. Presented at AHS13 and met lots of paleo-folks. Started drinking beer again. (Painting my kids' rooms began that habit again...then camping...aye-yi-yi!) Experienced a profound shamanic journey.

2014\. More of the same.

2015\. Began shamanic and energy healing apprenticeship. Basically all else faded into the background during this experience.

September 11, 2015. Ate eggs, green chile, and hash browns that my mother-in-law fixed for breakfast. About 1pm I began experiencing severe stomach cramps. By 6pm I was experiencing severe stomach cramps, chills, and just needed to lie down. I had experienced similar symptoms to eggs before, just never to this extreme. I had to lie down, where I stayed. The next day, no change in my condition. The next night I experienced the worst stomach pain I'd ever felt in my life. Piercing, radiating pain. I thought perhaps it was a gall-bladder thing, or maybe pancreatitis. It was horrific. Focused mostly around my duodenum.

Sunday morning, the pain had subsided, and then came and went in waves that were 1 to sometimes 2 hours apart. I could sort of function. At times. Still mostly slept, lay in my bed. Didn't even have the energy to watch a movie or read. Mostly had to just be with my eyes closed. Could not turn on any lights when darkness fell. Just had to be on my bed, in the dark. Sunday night, around 5pm, a horrific headache began to develop. At this time, I felt like I'd been poisoned with something. Arsenic maybe...that's what I imagined arsenic might feel like. My muscles hurt. My everything hurt. The stomach pain came and went in massive painful waves. Then this headache started. I tried two advil. Nothing. Four hours later I tried 4 aspirin. Nothing. It seemed to be worse. The headache grew in intensity from 5pm until about 6 am the next morning, getting worse and worse and worse with every passing hour. I just simply endured. I drank a shit-ton of water. I did do some massage on my neck and head which helped while I did it. I was up that entire night. Couldn't sleep at all. It was brutal--the combo of the excruciating stomach pain combined with the worst headache I'd ever had in my life.

Monday I went in to the Urgent Care clinic. After waiting nearly 2 hours in extreme discomfort (granted, I hadn't been able to get out of bed before this), I saw a PA. My blood pressure was high (160/100) as was my pulse. I knew I'd made a mistake when the PA began furrowing her brows at me and looking at me like I was crazy or confused. She palpated my gall bladder and pancreas and there was no pain. That gave me more reason to suspect this was more related to the eggs I'd eaten. She said, you probably have an ulcer, and prescribed Prevacid. Okay. SHe also put in an order for an ultrasound and bloodwork (which I wanted, btw, since I've had no bloodwork done for many years).

I went in the next day to schedule the b/w and u/s. I have a high-deductible plan that wouldn't pay any of it and it was going to cost over $1000 for the blood work and $500 for the us. I said thanks, but no thanks.

I endured the rest of that day and the next. No more headache, just constant waves of stomach pain. I couldn't get on the computer for more than 10 minutes without feeling entirely exhausted and would have to lie down. I slept a lot.

Wednesday night I practitioner friend of mine ran me through an alternative testing protocol. Without knowing the details, her conclusion led to eggs being the cause with effects being acute over-production of stomach acid leading to an ulceration, auto-toxemia ( my body simply couldn't process all the by-products of the inflammation and whatever else was going on), among other things.

Thursday was far less painful. Friday, I started feeling way better. Saturday super much better. Went to Japanese spa for detox.

Sunday, felt good enough to take a walk but was totally startled by how much my fitness had been affected. I felt breathless on the smallest hill, and my heart was racing.

Monday, I woke up and my limbs and muscles felt like someone had poured cement into them. I took a shower and almost fainted. I got out, and had to sit down to towel off (NEVER BEFORE IN MY LIFE!), I was getting dressed and literally had to hold on to the shelf in my closet when I almost fainted again.
I could barely move. Had to lie down all day. At some point, I was brought food--fajitas. I did not know there was any avocado in the food, and scarfed it down. I was hungry after nearly 11 days without eating hardly anything...applesauce, of all things, was the only thing I could eat.

I am highly allergic to avocados. Even though I was feeling the fainting spells and cement limbs, my stomach had not hurt since the previous Thursday. Monday afternoon, however, I started feeling the slow decline towards the horrific pain I'd felt most of the previous week. Later, I realized that I'd eaten avocado and wanted to cry. Thus began the second week of horrific pain.

Thursday of that week, I spoke with our pediatrician who is also an allergist, although she does testing and treatment considered to be "garbage" by most medical professionals. She didn't bat an eye at what I described and said she saw this sort of reaction quite frequently. SHe suggested I drink Magic Brew--oh and MAGIC it is!! 1 tsp. sea salt, 1 tsp baking soda, to 1 L water. Oh My god. I wish I'd talked to her sooner. It soothed the stomach pain within minutes.

At this point, while I seemed to be getting the stomach pain under control, it was obvious that my blood pressure had been wacked out. The fainting spells, a pounding in my head everytime I stood up, and then my ankles swelled up like balloons with pitting edema. Ugh. I HATE PITTING EDEMA.

Kind of seen the same things around here, very much in sync with your dates.

Some friends of mine has been in and out of care and they can not find a thing. Fainting on and off, pain in neck, double visions... List is long.

During this period of time i was out in the wild for the whole time and saw outstanding effects on the sky, day and night... Had an almost electric feeling in my body, like an urge to constantly move and change spot for rest.

Back to reactions... I did a check at that time how the sun has been active or not... Okey, it was quite sleepy but did send some serious hits toward earth.

My guess would be not to seek food for errors but look up...

It started some days before.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-10-28T20:12:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-180641>

I hadn't had a chance to finish, since I went to bed.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-10-29T00:26:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-180659>

I hadn't actually considered the sun, so thanks for that idea. While, I've not been able to be out in the wilderness, I'm definitely outside and grounding regularly. Lots of barefoot walking, lying on rocks in the sun, biking, and hiking. Even leading up to this episode.

Since you brought up not looking at food and looking up, I'll bring up the other thing that was going on.

The 9-month apprenticeship I experienced involved ever-intense layers of learning, expansion, and personal evolution each and every week. There was an incredible amount of internal work that was involved was mind blowing, sometimes quite literally. Working at the quantum level began on day one.

So, the work had nearly reached the peak the week this episode began, and for the two previous weeks, I'd been feeling as if I was on the cusp of something huge. Magnanimous. At the cellular level, I could feel as if my entire system was on poised on the abyss and I was about to leap. On the Sunday before that Friday, I had made this deep commitment to myself. I'd agreed to commit to letting go of these deep-seated habits that I'd been continually holding on to, and often making excused for, even when I knew that only through the release could I step across a threshold to another level of being.

Additionally, I've been feeling an intense urgency about doing whatever it takes to actually transition out of my environmental science career and in to doing the energy work and shamanic work that I need to be doing. On the day this began, I had helped lead a blessing ceremony for a dear friend who has also been one of my colleagues for these past 22 years and who was finally making her own transition out of environmental consulting and into her own massage therapy and energy healing practice. Surely there is no coincidence in this timing.

Throughout this entire experience, the one thing that stood out more than anything, was just Being With. Being with myself in my agony. Being with without DOING anything. Just being. It's difficult to explain, really, but the themes for the previous two weeks had been Being With. There was no doubt that my entire system was participating in this exercise at a greatly enhanced level, most likely because that is what was needed for it to really take root.

Additionally, my entire being was completely shaken up, stirred, and dumped out. For nearly two full weeks I couldn't eat anything other than tea, water, and applesauce. My entire gut was rearranged. The brain-gut connection became hugely apparent when I finally expelled the last of whatever it was that had attempted to stay rooted in my bowels. Literally, when I pooped out the contents of my system on day 9 or 10 or 11, it was like these microbes were making their last attempt at brain connection and control. I wish I could describe it as vividly as I felt it. It was like they did not want to go. I would nearly pass out on the toilet as they made their final transition to the outerworld. It was surreal and probably sounds whacked, but it was so incredibly real as I was experiencing it.

For more than two weeks my bowels were nearly black. Like meconium. And totally locked up. Despite the tremendous amount of water I was drinking, along with tea, I was more constipated than I'd ever been in my life. In fact, only in the past week have my bowels returned to "normal".

To say that my entire system was rearranged would be an understatement.

The other weird factor, that the pediatrician said happens in events like these that she's seen, is that I overnight had teeth sensitivities, which I've never had before, and I'm guessing was because my stomach was overproducing stomach acid. How this affected my teeth is unknown to me, but it happened on day 9 or so, right in the midst of it all.

So, in any case, there definitely seemed to be things going on that cannot all be attributed to food.

When I started feeling better, I began CTing as much as I could. At first I was going up to the river in the caldera and sitting in the cold water and getting grounded. I got my CT tub set up, and so now I've been trying to CT as much as I can at home, and warming up in the sun afterwards. Two weeks ago, I had nearly 6 days in a row of CTing, and last week I had 4. This week, so far, 2. I've been trying to get morning sun every day, and grounding as much as possible.

Last week I was able to take my first real hike, and yesterday I did an awesome run/hike/grounding/sun session. Finally feeling like I was coming back to myself. Saw a pair of hawks spiraling overhead, too, which is always a treat.

Anyway, this has been quite a ride.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-12T04:19:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chrysanthemamas-journaling-journey.9218/post-181725>

Monday November 2 I went to Ojo Caliente, a natural mineral springs and spa in northern NM. It's lovely, and I hadn't been there since my birthday in July.
I was alone, and taking advantage of a rare Mom's day off. When I left my house, a pair of red-tailed hawks were spiraling right above me. A good sign, I thought.

Another 10 miles away I saw a trio of red tails...flying with several ravens. That was 5 in less than 10 minutes!

Hawk medicine is all about having a big-picture perspective, and being able to snatch opportunity when it presents itself. All right in alignment with what's been going in my life, and where I want things to go.

I got to Ojo after the 1-hour drive on an insanely gorgeous day. The cottonwoods that line the rivers of NM were in full golden spectacular! So incredibly beautiful. It was a Monday, and so there were almost no cars on the highways at all. Just a beautiful blue-bird day with insane golden light emanating from the autumn leaves. I changed into my swimsuit and headed to the first mineral spring, the Iron Spring. Despite the empty roads, the springs were pretty crowded. I aimed for a vacant spot in the spring. As I entered the water, I recognized a woman who was tucked in between two rocks, clearly in bliss. I did a double take, and mouthed, "Jessika?" She looked at me like, who the fuck are you, and then realized who I was and blew me a kiss. And right then Erwan smiled broadly and waved a huge hello. There are many reasons why I love NM, and this small-state experience is one of them. I hadn't seen them since our Jack Kruse Mov Nat experience in January 2013, but the timing seemed auspicious.

Later that day, heading home, I saw another trio of hawks. Eight red tails in one day. I've never seen that many hawks in one day.

More hawk medicine: when a superfluous number of hawks appear it means all your wishes are coming true. All the intentions you've set are reaping the harvest you've sown.

Nice!

This was the first week I really began to feel good. Like GOOD good. I realize now, in looking at my experience, that while eggs might have been the trigger, redox was definitely the source.
Low redox to be exact.

Of course, this baffles me, because I always feel certain that I'm doing things "right" and for the most part I'm not in a really bad environment...at least that I know of. I do live in a highly complex government research facility, and I do live at the highest point on the north side of town. I'm at elevation, 7500 ft above sea-level. But thankfully I work in an old single-story building with no wi-fi (not even a signal!), and I am in a room with real windows and concrete floors, with no moisture barrier. I get outside a lot. A lot a lot. I ground a lot.

I do the bulk of my movement outside--trail running, hiking, mountain biking, rock climbing.

I've been eating a lot more seafood. I've been averaging 1 DHA meal per day, usually salmon, shrimp, or sardines.

But still, I know that I'm not optimal. This whole experience showed me that I was not optimal.

But I am healing. My redox must be getting better.

I dream like a crazy Spielberg movie every night, and I'm super entangled in my dreams--dreaming of people who I'll be encountering or working with every single night. I've been sleeping so deeply, it's been amazing.

I have been CTing on average 6 out of every 10 days. I'm SOOOOO glad I got this CT tank. Wow! What an amazing thing. I relied ont he spring, or the long drive to the rivers for the past several years. It is so nice to be able to walk out my door and into my tank. Regardless of the weather.

In any case, I'm planning to get labs run. I've never done bloodwork the entire time I've been a member of this community, and learning about all this stuff. But now is the time. I'm 44. I need to know what the fuck is going on bloodwise, so that I can continue to get a better perspective of my own whole picture.

I loved the most recent blog, post, btw. I wrote some comments in, and actually feel compelled to write my own blog post on the chakras and the light spectra as they are so closely aligned. I think there is more there to be explored.

It is new years eve here in OZ. it is a gorgeous day at the beach. So many people! Everyone has their sunshade and coolers and waiting for the fireworks tonite.

I hope we all make wonderful new memories this year.

Happy New Year Dr. Kruse .....and everyone that Makes all this happen.

# Thread: The Sideritis (Greek Mountain Tea) question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sideritis-greek-mountain-tea-question.9222/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eugenia — 2013-12-31T04:57:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sideritis-greek-mountain-tea-question.9222/post-104493>

_Update: Could an admin delete this thread, it's duplicate. Thx!_

In the whole of Balkan, Turkey and Italy they always considered the mountain herbal tea from the Sideritis family one of the most healthful things one could have. I'm actually pretty surprised that the Paleo community hasn't tapped on to the specific herbal tea family, because research is abundant about it, and in my opinion, it shows higher protective results than that of green tea. For one, except the fact that it doesn't have caffeine in it (for some of us, caffeine sends us to the toilet), but most importantly, it doesn't accumulate fluoride. All modern caffeinated teas these days have more and more fluoride in them, as the environment becomes dirtier by the minute by the industry. Especially true for tea that comes from China. Sideritis tea has no fluoride in it. Here's the research, way too much of it to give specific links: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=sideritis](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=sideritis)

I personally buy Greek Mountain Tea from my local Mediterranean store, but Amazon also carries it. Since it's a tissane-type tea (it's not minced as in industrial teas, but it's the whole, unprocessed tea plant that you buy) it has a more specific way to prepare it. I put up a tutorial with pictures here: [http://eugenia.queru.com/2007/04/21/greek-mountain-tea/](http://eugenia.queru.com/2007/04/21/greek-mountain-tea/)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

A few years ago, I couldn't remember what was being talked about in a conversation, was extremely tired and anything would make me cry. As I'm not a crier, it was bizarre. Spent weeks in bed recently, was soooo tired, so back to the Dr. for another round of blood tests, see above, and now on T again at a higher dose .75 - 1gm and 200 mg progesterone.

Was still feeling tired, so tried 19 mcg T4 4.5 mcg T3 supps which literally turned me into a raving maniac. Became aggressive and nasty, felt like I was doing amphetamines including the crash. Never a fav high for me and stopped after 2 days.

Thoughts, brilliant ideas, concerns, suggestions?

# Thread: Just for fun....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-for-fun.9230/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-01-01T03:07:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-for-fun.9230/post-104552>

I wonder how many bottles of really, really good wine does it take for JK .... to celebrate a really off the charts great New year's Eve in gay paree with his lovely bride?????

What kind of wine do you think they are drinking? and what are they eating?

I guess you can tell I am getting bored ..... better start reading QM again.........

### Flora — 2014-01-01T14:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-for-fun.9230/post-104556>

Caroline, I'm pretty sure he will have found some really great bottles of French Malbec! I live right in the middle of the Malbec wine growing region which is the AOC Cahors region of France. The Malbec grape has been grown here since Roman times and was only taken to Chile/ Argentina in the mid-19th century - so the French know a thing or two about getting it right!

Obviously we drink it all the time (well. not ALL the time - occasionally have water!!) and we buy it directly from the chateau. It's quite interesting as it's a very small wine region in comparison to most and many of the vineyards are family affairs, producing grapes on quite a small scale, so they nearly all use organic methods and it is excellent quality.

Also, because it's not a particularly well-known wine (in comparison to the South American Malbecs) it is ridiculously cheap for its quality - we buy excellent 100% Malbecs for just 12 euros a bottle from our favourite chateau - and that's for special occasions - it's called _La Passion_ too - very appropriate! The standard stuff (called _Tradition_)is still delicious, though and is just 3 or 4 euros (or even less when you buy it in a 5 litre box!)

I imagine he's in heaven with the food too, because _Le_ _Reveillon_ (NYE) always involves TONS of fresh oysters!

I had my fill of both yesterday but today I've decided I'm going alcohol-free for a while as I enjoyed the wine a little too much over the holidays and I want to get back on track again. I think there are a few oysters left though so that will be a good start!

Bonne Annee!

### caroline — 2014-01-01T22:32:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-for-fun.9230/post-104567>

How wonderful Flora! Happy New year! Thanks for sharing all that about the wine ... sounds like heaven

So oysters are very available to you? and not too expensive?

I wonder what french Malbec we have available here in OZ?

Your area sounds like a great spot for a meet up ..... what fun

Please tell us more about your area.....

How are you doing?

The new **BioMat 7000mx controller** has an advanced I.C. chip that controls electromagnetic waves, protecting you from the harmful effects of electromagnetic radiation.

**The Biomat EMF Inteceptor**

If I feel a cold coming on I take it off the massage table and sleep on it at 104 and the cold stops progressing and goes away after one or two nights sleeping on the mat. Before I had the mat I would get sick from my clients or on my own if a cold was coming on without it coming from a client.

When the health issues I have kick into high gear and my health starts to plummet, like it has been since Nov 2013, I sleep on it nightly at 104. I started sleeping on it again nightly about a 1 month ago... If I don't I can't function. The Biomat is my most favorite technology I own that constantly gives me consistent results I can count on.

### Hope — 2014-01-01T16:07:57+0200

I recently got an EMF poncho for $4.95 at lessemf.com. Yesterday I started driving with it on my lap, up to near below my neck. And for some reason, it made me feel calmer. But I don't know if it's my imagination, or if it could be real?

# Thread: Ways to Rectify Problems with Tea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-02T01:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104572>

So, a while ago I started a thread hoping to get to the bottom of the fluoride/aluminum issue with green tea. I've been doing tons of research on this topic and I feel like I've found some ways to significantly reduce the amount of fluoride from tea. The aluminum is a harder issue to solve.
Tea has numerous beneficial properties but it is, unfortunately, very rich in fluoride. Brick tea seems to be the highest, and easily contains toxic amounts. Black tea is next, with one study revealing 7.8 mg/l of fluoride in it. According to this study [http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S027869150700511X](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S027869150700511X) black tea has between 0.32 and 4.54 mg/l fluoride. To put this in context, tap water normally has 1 ppm fluoride. Additionally, according to the Linus Pauling Institute here, [http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/infocenter/phytochemicals/tea/](http://lpi.oregonstate.edu/infocenter/phytochemicals/tea/), black tea has 1.0-1.9 mg/l fluoride. It also states that green tea has 1.2-1.7 mg/l fluoride. However, the study on sciencedirect.com found that white tea only contains 0.37â€“0.54 mg/l fluoride. Still a bit of a high amount, but clearly much better than either black or green tea. In order to further combat this lower amount of fluoride, I'll be supplementing with a bit of egg shell calcium with my tea and adding a pinch of borax to it. Calcium carbonate has been shown to considerably reduce the absorption of fluoride by turning into the less harmful calcium fluoride. The boron in the borax that I'll be adding to it has been shown to protect against fluoride toxicity and enhance the body's ability to excrete fluoride (as well as other benefits like balancing calcium metabolism and boosting sex hormones/reducing estrogen) Source: [http://www.health-science-spirit.com/borax.htm](http://www.health-science-spirit.com/borax.htm)
Aluminum is posing more of a problem. Firstly, never add lemon or citrus to tea as that boosts aluminum absorption from it by up to 11x. Theoretically, white tea should have less aluminum than green or black as it is made from younger tea leaves and is usually higher quality, however I have no research to back this up. Additionally, drinking it on an empty stomach may reduce the absorption because there is less stomach acid and less aluminum will get chelated, but again, no research backing up this idea. I'll be drinking a cup of bamboo tea with every cup of white tea that I drink because bamboo tea is very rich in silica which has been shown, in some studies, to promote the excretion of silica (by quite a lot, actually) and reduce it's absorption. Unfortunately, the research is conflicting in this regard as some studies show no effect form silica while others say only oligomeric silica has any effect, and monomeric silica is useless in this regard. It's very confusing.
Well, that's all I've got. If anyone has any other info, I'd love to hear it. Hope I was able to bring some new information to the table.

### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-02T01:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104574>

Also, the concentration of these compounds seem to be highly environmentally influenced, as the tea plant is a fluoride/aluminum magnet. People suggest the tap-water used to water the plants and the fertilizers have something, however organic teas don't seem to make much difference and one study actually found more fluoride in organic tea. Perhaps if someone could find tea cultivated in pristine, mineral rich, fertilizer free soil high up on a mountain and watered exclusively with spring water, we could find an ideal solution, but that doesn't seem to be an option currently.

### Tanya — 2014-01-02T16:31:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104590>

QiGuy1997, very important research! Thank you for doing it and sharing with us. Interesting idea about the bamboo tea -0 I have never even heard about it before. Any specific brand that you recommend? I also wonder if you have any information regarding the rooibos tea. I know that it does contain a significant amount of fluoride and I try ti mitigate it by making sure that I drink fluoride-free water and supplementing with extra boron, but aluminum problem still is a huge factor for me.

### Jonathin — 2014-01-02T21:10:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104599>

Mineral sources of phosphate - either superphosphate or rock phosphate - contain very significant amounts of fluoride. Excess soil acidity - i.e. low calcium- creates soluble aluminum from the massive amounts in found in all soil. Most agriculture is done using the equivalent of the SAD diet.

I am curious what the fluoride content is of the green tea extract powders. Might it concentrate the fluoride as well as the EGCGs?

### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-02T22:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104610>

I'm glad someone is finding this information helpful! The bamboo tea that I drink is from this small, organic farm in Florida. Here's the link if you're interested: bambooleaftea.net Rooibos tea has fluoride levels comparable to that of regular tea but, as far as I can tell, it has far lower levels of aluminum. Something to look into though, is the phytoestrogens contained in Rooibos tea. The verdict is still out on phytoestrogens as some people consider them estrogenic while others think that they block true estrogen from the receptors, thereby becoming anti-estrogenic. That's just something you might want to look into/consider. Even so, the supplemental boron probably goes a long way into balancing these hormonal effects.
Interesting comments on the soil conditions Jonathin. I wish I could find tea produced in actual nutrient rich soils. That's probably what ancient healers and qigong masters described to be so healing, not the tainted kind we have today :/ I believe green tea extracts tend to remove the fluoride from the actual extract, but it may depend on the brand. For powders, I'm not entirely sure. Powders like Matcha and Sencha, however, would contain as much, if not more, fluoride because they are just ground up tea leaves.

### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-02T22:54:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104611>

Here is a very promising green tea extract: [http://www.ilovegreentea.com/index.html](http://www.ilovegreentea.com/index.html)
It seems to be highly concentrated with EGCG and has no fluoride at all. As an added bonus, they add in some goji berry and astragalus extracts, both powerful adaptogens. The only downside that I can see with it is it has it's caffeine removed and, contrary to what most people think, I think caffeine is quite beneficial.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104663>

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-03T18:41:04+0200

I admit I haven't researched this subject much but I remember seeing this last year...

**Could cheap tea bags make you ill? Study reveals they contain high fluoride levels that could damage teeth, bones and muscles**

Fluoride is essential for bone and dental health but over 4mg per day can cause pain, muscle problems and joint and tooth disease in the long-term
Study analysed fluoride levels in supermarket and branded tea bags
Found that supermarket value brands from Tesco, Sainsbury's and Asda and Morrisons provided 75% to 120% of your daily intake of substance
Experts are now calling for fluoride levels to be stated on food packaging

Read the full article here:

### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-04T01:05:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ways-to-rectify-problems-with-tea.9234/post-104687>

The link I posted concerning borax/boron is extremely informative and disusses how it should be used in depth.

# Thread: Lipids and Cortisol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 112
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ricardo — 2014-01-02T20:02:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-104596>

After about two years of low carbs it became clear to me that I had become 100% adapted to using fats as fuel. I could have a couple of Tablespoons of Olive Oil in the morning, walk for 4 miles, have a heavy workout at the gym at 11 AM and then eat lunch. I did not experience hunger until noon.

Fat was fueling my energy expenditure and I could work out as heavy as I wanted to without any undue fatigue. It initially took about two years for my system to adapt to fat as my main fuel. I will never go back to carbs as my main fuel. Its not even close to the energy and mental clarity I get from fat as fuel.

I began to experiment with different fats.

For instance, first thing in the morning: I found that if I took 2 Tbsp of MCT oil I seemed too hyper. Its as if I was making too much cortisol. I had trouble focusing mentally.

Olive oil, on the other hand provided a much steadier form of energy. As my cortisol is highest in the morning, I don't want to make it any higher. MCT seemed to do that. I seemed to be running "too hot" with MCT as my sole source of energy at that time of day.

Coconut oil seems to make me less hyper than MCT does: even if taken first thing in the morning. I consume a lot of coconut oil with food since reading about Dr Kruse's Epi-Paleo diet.

I take MCT only after eating at least one meal. Its great fuel, but maybe a bit "over" first thing in the morning.

My question is.. is there a connection between lipids and cortisol ?

### Jonathin — 2014-01-02T20:59:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-104598>

Could you redefine your question?

Jack has said that dis-regulation of cortisol is a brain and receptor issue. What supplies energy to those cells and how well do those mitochondria work and with which fuels? Seems like you have figured that out.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-02T21:35:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-104600>

> [Jonathin said:](/goto/post?id=104598)
>
> Could you redefine your question?

Hello Jonathin,

I believe that fats can raise cortisol levels: especially if they are the sole or main source of fuel for a period of time.

I also think that the type of fat may determine how much cortisol is affected.

My experience is that olive oil is somewhat neutral in terms of its effect on cortisol. Whereas MCT oil seems to raise cortisol levels.

This is all hypothetical based on how my organism reacts to fats.

So to rephrase: do various fat configurations affect cortisol in ways that are measurable ?

PS: I don't limit my breakfast to 2 Tbsp of Olive oil since following ( as much as my current knowledge permits) Dr Kruse's Epi-Paleo diet.

My exercise-induced inflammation has been reduced considerably since I have been following concepts put forth by Dr Kruse. In addition, I recover much faster and can workout more often.

### caroline — 2014-01-02T22:30:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-104608>

I have no idea about the answer to your question - but I was wondering .... are you using regular MCT oil or the newer one that Dave Asprey is selling? I think it is called Brain Octane ......

Do you use GF butter? if so - what do you notice?

I have been using a lot of butter lately and am noticing more fat on my body ...

### Ricardo — 2014-01-03T01:34:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-104615>

Hey Caroline,

I don't use much butter. I went for years w/o it while I was following Dr Budwig's concepts on lipid selection and absorption. She got excellent results with some very sick patients by combining cold pressed flax oil with a sulpher protein ( cottage cheese).

Here is something interesting: she used to get the nurses to roll the patients beds outside during the day. At the end of the afternoon the patients beds were rolled back into the dorms: subdued light until lights out at the same time every day. Does this sound like a circadian reset ? Her motivation as she stated it in one of her letters was so that they would be exposed to sunlight and fresh air.

Attacked by the establishment, nevertheless she persevered and as she peered into her microscope she observed how green sick damaged cells had become round and healthy. She went to court to face her opponents. The judge ruled in her favor. Going against the established mainstream is never easy.

I am using another brand of MCT although I am sure Dave's is quite fine.

### caroline — 2014-01-03T02:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-104620>

One of our acquaintances just underwent surgery for uterine cancer. It was a late diagnosis: her cancer spread to the lymph system and so she is battling metastasis and is undergoing chemo. She is taking it daily with hopes that it will help.

Here in Quebec a lot of people take it because Dr Beliveau is considered a local hero and people follow his work. He had a weekly TV program called Kampai in which he teamed up with a popular chef and they did healthy recipes.

That show was very unique because he would explain in layman's terms what he was trying to do about such diseases as brain tumors. ### Ricardo — 2014-01-04T02:43:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-104694>

Caroline,

I never really got into eating butter since Dr Budwig preferred other fats such as coconut oil and cold pressed flax oil. If you were sick she would not allow you butter until you got well and even then... She apparently felt there were better choices. She based her decisions on criteria such as which lipids slow or halt the progression of cancerous lesions etc. It might be ok to eat it if you are well. I prefer not to consume it.

Yes, I prefer MCT oil because it generates a better supply of ketones than does coconut oil. Something to keep in mind: coconut oil will generate ketones for 8 hours whereas MCT oil will generate ketones for 4 hours.

Despite what my mind tells me, my body seems to prefer coconut oil. I think that's because I now metabolize fats a lot faster and MCT is already high octane. I don't want to run too "hot". I am looking for homeostasis. Coconut oil is slower to digest and therefore it seems less thermogenic to me.

For Alzheimer's patients, apparently, a ratio of 4:3 coconut oil to MCT may be a good mix. One company is already producing a mix with those proportions as suggested by Dr Mary Newport MD.

Green tea: I know its a minefield to buy anything. So true. Dr Beliveau has charted the ECGC content of various teas. I prefer to consult his chart before I answer that for you. I will be able to find that out for you sometime in the next month. I prefer to give you a science based answer rather than a marketing based one.

Labs: The Neurologists treating my auto-immune issue schedule me for bloods about every 6 months. I'll wait till after the next one so we can get a "before" and "after" comparison of inflammation markers.

Whats the weather like in Australia this time of year ?

\------------------
Cinnamon,

Its minus 30 here too. ( Quebec province). I eat lamb in the winter only. It heats the body more than beef or horsemeat. This is "lamb" weather.

> [Ricardo said:](/goto/post?id=104596)
>
> After about two years of low carbs it became clear to me that I had become 100% adapted to using fats as fuel. I could have a couple of Tablespoons of Olive Oil in the morning, walk for 4 miles, have a heavy workout at the gym at 11 AM and then eat lunch. I did not experience hunger until noon.
>
> Fat was fueling my energy expenditure and I could work out as heavy as I wanted to without any undue fatigue. It initially took about two years for my system to adapt to fat as my main fuel. I will never go back to carbs as my main fuel. Its not even close to the energy and mental clarity I get from fat as fuel.
>
> I began to experiment with different fats.
>
> For instance, first thing in the morning: I found that if I took 2 Tbsp of MCT oil I seemed too hyper. Its as if I was making too much cortisol. I had trouble focusing mentally.
>
> Olive oil, on the other hand provided a much steadier form of energy. As my cortisol is highest in the morning, I don't want to make it any higher. MCT seemed to do that. I seemed to be running "too hot" with MCT as my sole source of energy at that time of day.
>
> Coconut oil seems to make me less hyper than MCT does: even if taken first thing in the morning. I consume a lot of coconut oil with food since reading about Dr Kruse's Epi-Paleo diet.
>
> I take MCT only after eating at least one meal. Its great fuel, but maybe a bit "over" first thing in the morning.
>
> My question is.. **is there a connection between lipids and cortisol** ?

yes within the hormone cascade - read Hormones 101 & 102. I'm currently putting together a vblog w/an info graphic to explain this easier.

but here goes Lipids, specifically LDL is converted into pregnenolone with the assistance of Vitamin A and free T3. Pregnenolone has three pathways within the steroid hormones where it is converted into DHEA, Progesterone, and Cortisol. when things are working optimally Pregneonlone is converted into DHEA and Progesterone. when you're stressed for any reason Pregnenolone will be converted into Cortisol - hence cortisol steal syndrome. If you have Low levels of Vitamin A, which is indicated by low pregnenolone levels, you will have pregnenolone steal occurring. this provides a feed back loop to your adrenal glands, pitituatary and hypothalmus which tells your liver to make more LDL so it can make up for the loss of steroid hormones due to the pregnenolone steal; this then becomes a do-loop and will continue until the underlying issue is resolved. the underlying issue maybe entirely related to your vitamin A cycle (see hormone 102) or it could also be related to your thyroid production of Free T3 (or Reverse T3) in your gut. If your gut health is off - you won't be able to make the active Thyroid hormone.

look at the quantum health model watch the video I put together to explain the infographic here [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rwVgK8cmgd4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rwVgK8cmgd4) you'll see how everything is connected.

The fat you does not directly influence cortisol. heart palpitations after consuming MCT oil - can be an indication of magnesium deficiency as well as an imbalance of Mag and potassium at the cellular level as you move toward ketosis - both of which point to dehydration at the cellular level. I'd say you need to increase your water consumption, embrace the cold, and continue to follow the protocols.

consider your environment and figure out a way to decrease what's causing your dehydration....

Hello Caroline,

Dr Beliveau charted various teas:
\---------------
Concentration of ECGC as a Percentage of Leaf Weight:

Sencha Uchiyama............5.8
Goyo Kuro......................4.9
Matcha (Japanese)..........2.7

He charted a few Senchas and they were the highest in ECGC content.

The Chinese teas were lower in ECGC content than the Japanese teas.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-08T04:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105046>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104695)
>
> I don't quite understand what you said about coconut oil vs. MCT oil and ketones .... I thought we would want to burn ketones longer?

Caroline,

Sorry I missed this one.

MCT and coconut oil generate ketones for us to burn. MCT generates more ketones per volume. However, MCT generates ketones for 4 hours as opposed to coconut oil which generated ketones for 8 hours. This is useful to know because ideally you want to have ketones to burn 24/7. My body craves some coconut oil before sleep. The coconut oil will generate ketones all night long.

You can get the ketones from MCT even if you are not following a ketogenic diet.

I am eating low carb plus a heavy emphasis on lipids, primarily coconut oil and MCT oil. I had, for instance, 4 TBsp of coconut oil with breakfast. My brain thrives on this level of ketones.

Some parts of the brain need glucose as fuel. No biggie, there is enough protein in my diet for gluconeogenesis to occur. If need be, my body will convert protein into glucose to meet the needs of my brain for glucose.

If I get up at night to urinate and the odor is inordinately strong that doesn't worry me at all...it just means that my body is in full ketosis mode. The healthy kind. And thats what I want: better more efficient fuel.

I worked up to this state over a period of two years.

I find that if I overdo it with MCT then I start to feel uncomfortable: I am tipping in favor of too many ketones. I believe this should be done slowly and homeoostasis can be achieved at various stages of the process.

My choice of lipids is simple and could be considered restrictive by some. I live on coconut oil, MCT oil, EVOO, a little krill oil, selected meats ( in small amounts) and lots of salmon: thats it.

btw, I noticed that Dr Kruse suggested no nut or seed oils during the LeptinRx Reset. I think this is wise.

In addition, if I had any cancer at all, I would restrict my selection even further to Budwig compliant lipids only. Doctors found that her selection of lipids slowed or halted cancer lesions. Other lipids, although considered healthy, did not.

Lipid selection and timing is hugely important.

### caroline — 2014-01-08T07:10:39+0200

### Ricardo — 2014-01-10T06:44:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105263>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105049)
>
> The part I don't understand is ....MCT generates ketones for only 4 hours .... whereas coconut oil for 8 hours..

Hello Caroline,

Both MCT oil and coconut oil are processed by the liver and both are converted to a certain amount of ketones. MCT oil will yield a higher percentage of ketones than coconut oil will. However, coconut oil will be converted by the liver over a period of 8 hours as opposed to mct oil which will be converted over a period of 4 hours. The timing.. the way that plays out is not something I have looked into. So rather than give you a hypothesis of why one takes longer than the other I will find out and get back to you.

The ketones are tremendously important to an Alzheimer patient or even to prevent the disease. It has been determined that Alzheimer may be caused by insuline resistance. Interestingly, even if the patient does not have Type 1 or Type 2 diabetes, there may be a serious problem of glucose uptake resulting from insuline resistance. ( I've heard Alzheimer referred to as Type 3 diabetes). So here is where the ketones, which easily cross the blood brain barrier, become an alternative fuel for the brain. My own experience points to ketones as being a better, more efficient source of fuel for the brain than glucose. I do well in a state of mild to medium ketosis.

Ketones can be generated from a ketogenic diet.
and/or from MCT and/or coconut oil.
and/or from ketone esters which will soon be available.

Ketone esters have the advantage of furnishing the same amount of ketones as a state of mild ketosis without either a ketogenic diet or the consumption of either MCT or coconut oil. My own preference is a ketogenic diet high in MCTs.

In addition, although it is not part of the scientific body of knowledge at this time, there are some who think that ketones serve as fuel for neurons. Studies will prove or disprove this.

As a person with an auto-immune condition, I function best on a regular and steady regime of MCTs... year round.
That said, I agree with Dr Kruse that coconut oil should be considered as a seasonal choice ( for someone who is well ).

The take-away infos from all the research is that MCTs offer some protection against Alzheimer and can restore some cognitive function to even advanced cases.

Alzheimer is becoming a widespread disease: the annual budget in Quebec is over 4 billion in health care required per year. I wonder, though, why can't we have the same cognitive function at 90 as we have at 40 ? Lets stay optimal and fuel our brains and organs with a good supply of ketones.

PS. I normally express myself in French. You alluded to that in another post ? Is my English ok ? I don't get to practice it as often.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-10T23:21:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105318>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105266)
>
> So - you make sure you have MCT oil every four hours? Have you had a chance to look into the High Octane one yet?
>
> What would happen if you were to do a fat fast using MCT? more beneficial? or just not neccesssary?
>
> How many carbs and what kind would you have in a day?

"Luuuuucy ... I'm hoome...."

Its not necessary to time the consumption so precisely if you are already on a ketogenic diet. I listen to the feedback from my body and generally I average about 5 TBsp of coconut oil and MCT oil combined. I never did MCT oil without at least some coconut oil in my diet. Coconut oil has health producing benefits. It is better to consume them throughout your day to get ketones to your brain and organs on as steady a basis as possible. The ketogenic diet is will ensure at least a minimum is fueling your brain 24/7.

Magnesium before sleep is a good idea: magnesium levels drop during the night. Magnesium is part of the process of energy production and we are often deficient due to agricultural conditions.

During the course of a day I consume an average of 70 grams of carbs. Keep in mind that I am 6 ft tall,weight is 175 lbs and I exercise daily with kettle-bells. I do lots of intervals with weights.. never to muscular failure.. always keeping fresh...preferring more intervals and shorter sets to longer sets and exhaustion. I do 2 heavier workouts a week with intervals.

I get carbs from non starch vegetables daily and fruits when they are in season. I occasionally eat a little sweet potato.

I don't fast. I'm already in ketosis 24/7.

Just to take the idea of ketones as fuel a little further:

I believe that anyone who has a condition that is a result of impaired glucose uptake into cells due to insuline resistance or insuline deficiency should try a ketogenic diet and add some MCT and coconut oil to foods. Of course, your physician should be appraised of any changes in your dietary habits.

I believe this would be helpful for Multiple Sclerosis, Parkinson's, Huntington's and, of course, diabetes.

The Epi-Paleo diet would be a good solution. In addition, I would include regular consumption of MCT/coconut oil.

I have encouraged some friends to try this over the years and generally its been a good move. The problem seems to be that if people try to transition too fast that can cause a hiccup or two. Take for instance brain chemistry, when carbs are diminished serotonin production can be affected. There are things that one can do to help make the transition easier. DLPA can help keep endorphins at a good level and hence keep one's mood and morale up. D-phen does the same thing and its less stimulating. 5 htp is useful while a person adjusts to lower carbs: it helps keep serotonin up. A neurotransmitter, l-taurine can be helpful if your transition becomes too stressful or anxiety producing.

Meditation is helpful. I read a book by a doctor many years ago: Herbert Benson MD, his book was called the Relaxation Response. I have been meditating ever since and have never looked back. These last few years I have been doing what is termed as Mindfulness Meditation. Jon Kabat-Zinn has written the swiss army knife of mindfulness meditation guides: Full Catastrophe Living. I think we all need some meditation to detach ourselves from the mind chatter that plagues many of us.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-12T06:35:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105422>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105385)
>
> Did something change in your environment when you were 35? home, work? \[other than the candles on your birthday cake of course! introduction of non native EMF? moved/new house? mould? lyme disease?
>
> Is this common with auto-immune issues ... they just seemingly come out of nowhere?
>
