I can't recall anything out of the ordinary that happened at the time when my auto-immune issues started. The hypothesis is that it could have started by the residual effects of a bacterial or viral infection. Yes, as a matter of fact little is known about the true causes of many neurological problems. There are a lot of variables.

I, personally have found that ketones from MCT and Coconut fats are very helpful for my auto-immune disorder. Further, it makes sense to me that any condition that has impaired uptake of glucose to cells due to insulin resistance could benefit from ketones as fuel.
Ketones:
First, they lower inflammation.
Second, they have anti-oxydent effects.
Third, the human brain and organs, except for the liver, all perform very well on ketones.
Fourth... this is a hypothesis.. I believe they create less metabolic by-products. (not sure about that)

Our survival as a species may be largely due to the fact that we do well on ketones.

Consider this.

Grokky Grocardo gets hungry. He leaves the cave and goes hunting. His physical efforts to find prey and his mental alertness are fueled by ketones. He gets hungrier ( ketosis) and his mind and senses get sharper: "gotta find food to eat or my kids and wife will starve." ( I believe we are at our best while fueled by ketones ).
This quick and easy switch from glucose as fuel to ketones as fuel starts at Grokky's birth. As a newborn baby he won't get any milk from his mom for at least 36 hours. Being fat, babies are fat...he lives off that fat... his body is in ketosis until his mom has milk to feed him.

I believe we are made to be mentally sharper and to do better physically when we are fueled by ketones. Ketones made us better more effective hunters.

Then back at the cave after he feeds his family and himself a feast from his hunt and gatherings: his blood sugar goes up a bit and so does the available glucose. He begins to fuel with glucose again. His senses and reflexes are not as sharp after a big dinner. So he relaxes and thinks about tossing his boomerang.

Did I didjeridu ? hmm I think I dijerididn't. But thank you for reminding me.

PS: Is it true that women who move to Australia from Toronto dijeridu a lot ?

I was up late last night.

A dear friend's mother is getting symptoms of Alzheimer... not the best news. My friend remembered what I had mentioned to her about coconut fat and ketones. However, her mother refuses to get tested since she feels that the meds available are not very effective anyway. And when my friend asked her to try coconut oil she said she doesn't like it. So I was just figuring out a strategy. btw.. I think this is where ketone esters would be a potentially good solution.

Alzheimer is epidemic and the curve is on the upswing. 500,000 Canadians have Alzheimer. In the states over 5 million people have Alzheimer.

The best solution so far, I believe, is ketones. Small tests have shown it could be promising.

So getting back to Grokky. After hours, perhaps even days of pursuit, he sprints toward his prey and musters the strength to throw a spear with enough force and accuracy to kill his target of choice and thereby secure food for his family. All this is ketone powered. ( unless of course Lucy packed him a lunch ). The point is if his biology relied exclusively on glucose as fuel, then when glucose petered out it would have been "curtains". Instead, ketones made it possible for him to survive.

I mentioned that once I became efficient at fat as fuel I became determined never to go back to carbs ( glucose) as my main fuel. Fuggedaboudit ! For my biology..ketones are a superior fuel...by a country mile.

Wanna hear something exciting ? There is currently a hypothesis that a ketone fueled body may be more resistant to the big C. Thats not to say a person fueled by ketones 24/7 would absolutely not get cancer, it means that the increased resistance would be there. The reasoning seems sound enough. Research and tests need to be done.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-16T06:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105865>

Caroline,

How have you been ?

Just a quick post.

I know I have been posting a lot about ketones: I believe their potential for providing healing, wellness and quality of life is extraordinary.

Because they have the potential to aide in healing diseases that are treated with expensive pharmaceuticals, I somehow doubt that there will be many, if any, large clinical trials. Funding for large trials may be a problem. However the anecdotal evidence will, I think, attain critical mass at some point and it will become known on a widespread basis as an potential alternative to Alzheimer meds for instance. I hope it does.

So to continue..Consider this:

When our friend Grokky is born, his mother's body is producing milk which will contain medium chain triglicerides (MCT). Its the only time our human bodies will produce MCT. Now that in itself tells me something. Nature, in its wisdom, provides newborns with MCTs to start life on the right foot. It gives them ketones to thrive on.

While Grokky is pursuing a food source he is getting exercise which also adds to the ketones he is getting from hunger.

In fact, ketone levels for exercise are in the same range as ketones levels from mcts or coconut oil.

Ketone levels
Exercise ............................0.3-0.5 mmol/l
Coconut oil / MCT oil ..........0.3-0.5 mmol/l

Both starvation and a ketogenic diet are about the same in terms of producing ketones .......... 2.5-5 mmol/l

As a matter of fact premmies (premature infants) were given drops of MCT by neonatologists in the 70's. They did this until the companies that produce baby formulas decided to include it in their products.

In addition, I know of at least one pediatric neurosurgeon who would insist that mct and coconut oil be included in the diets of hospitalized children with brain and/or neurological issues.. ( especially children having brain injuries resulting from cranial impact.. car accidents, for instance).

I left a message for my friend whose mom is starting to have symptoms of Alzheimers. I left her a bottle of MCT oil along with some suggestions. I believe coconut oil has its place, even with the addition to the diet of MCT oil. So since her mother does not like coconut oil, she could just rub it on her arms as she would a moisturizer. Coconut oil absorbs into the skin very easily. It gets to work quickly. Its considered a beauty aid in some cultures.

Her mother is 84. Statistics reveal that at age 85 we have a 50% chance of having Alzheimer.

Be well,

PS: I am not forgetting any topics you brought up: I will converse with you about them next week.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-20T02:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-106252>

Hello Caroline ( <--- correct spelling  )

How are you ?

So a typical day ?.. I'll post in separate sections so its not too long.

I get up at 7 am.

I take probiotics of which I have a selection. I look to start the day with
probiotics then fats and electron rich foods for reasons previously mentioned.

Breakfast is 5 tbs of a sulfered protein such as quark cheese into which I
thoroughly blend
2 Tbs of cold pressed organic coconut oil
1 Tbs of extra virgin olive oil
1 heaping Tbs of freshly ground flax seeds.

I normally avoid dairy however the blending of these two substances (fat and
sulfered protein) creates a third substance which I can easily tolerate.

While blending, I add coconut milk until it is the consistancy of thick yogourt.
After the mix is thoroughly blended.. no sign of oil at all... I flavor it in one of
two ways. It can be flavored a number of ways: these two are my favorite.

The juice of a half of freshly squeezed lemon or lime plus a bit of stevia.

or

Finely ground green tea and a bit of stevia.

I am working toward the Epi-paleoRx recommended amount of protein and
carbs so now I will add a lettuce-wrap filled with salmon. I still need to consume
more carbs to attain to the recommended amount for breakfast... working on it.

This combination of fats is highly active.
In addition to being a nutritional powerhouse, it will counteract the accumulation of toxins in all tissues.
It will support the absorption of oxygen by the organism.
In addition, it will help regulate and optimize the
entire metabolic process.

Be well,

R. R.

PS: If I'm in a real hurry I will just take 2 Tbs of EVOO. I am comfortable with
this, and can function well until noon w/o hunger. However I am aware it is
not balanced as choices go.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-21T01:38:02+0200

" Lucy, how come you are so quiet ? "

Caroline thank you for the infos. I am interested in joining the Optimal Reset.

I'm kinda swamped with work these days and will come by as time permits. We have, among my friends, more or less divided the follow up on some important subjects such as food additives and the reliability of our food sources. At least this way we can get better information with less time invested.

Here in Quebec we often rent church basements at Christmas time for family reunions. Its been unsettling to hear in recent years who has cancer and who has Alzheimer or other diseases. It seems like more and more people are getting sick, more-so than in previous years. The statistics are not promising, especially for some diseases. We are looking for answers.

I like that the approach of people like Dr Beliveau and Dr Kruse is upbeat. After all, it should be fun and interesting to look after one's health and enjoy intelligent food and lifestyle choices.

Unfortunately, there is a lot of erroneous and questionable information even within the health movement. Personally, I look for some scientific basis when that is possible. In addition, what may make sense for one person, may not for another because of biological diversity.

At the end of the day, each of us has to determine what works best, what moves us toward homeostasis and what level of ketosis is optimal at any given time.

I think its best not to jolt the body, not to go too quickly. Steady progress is preferable and trumps occasional train wrecks from trying to go too fast.

I have a friend who works in medical equipment who told me "We are all athletes." Interesting thought, isn't it ?

Be well,

PS Please tell me what you did that was so fanatical: I'm trying to get up to speed.

PPS About protein powders.. I don't use them. Some amino acids can be converted to glucose. I prioritize ketones as fuel and less glucose is better for what my objectives are. I know its a trend to consume protein shakes: I see guys in gyms walking around with their protein shakes. I also know that some of them are taking protein shakes every 3 hours to promote anabolism. The kidneys are not meant to function that way. I suspect they are doing themselves more harm than good.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-23T15:40:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-106753>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106732)
>
> The Canadian young woman lost to China btw ....in case you are interested?

Hello Caroline,

Interested yes.. but I just don't have time to follow the news.

I wondered if its all that important to follow the news: I asked a friend who teaches at a local university and he said he only reads the "Toronto Globe". So I only get a little (at best) international and national news from the newspaper.

I am always amazed at how the presence of ketones manifests itself in the human body.
They work at the cellular level and so a lot of good things can happen.

A women who is the wife of a friend tried some MCT oil: 2 Tbs a day as a "preventative".
After only 2 weeks the dark circles under her eyes ( she has had them for at least the 30 years I have known her ) disappeared !
Well, needless to say she is thrilled ! And I am thrilled for her.

I find it interesting to observe how electron rich fats can accelerate healing. I have seen people try this and that to resolve various issues. Things really start to turn around when they go with electron rich fats and eliminate bad fats.

Maintaining a negatively charged outer membrane is the basis of health for our cells. Nothing achieves this better than electron rich fats.

What I am very interested in these days is how the various good fats "play out " in my biology.

Do you remember the Canadian lipid specialist Udo ?
He wrote "Fats that Heal and Fats that Kill". He also has tried various good fats and noted how they "play out" in his biology. I'm keeping notes.

I am looking into grounding. It makes sense: the earth is a tremendous electron donor. As a kid I would observe how farm livestock would lay down on muddy areas to get better when they were sick or injured. Their instinct guided them. I thought it was the mineral content of the mud, however the mud might have acted as a better electron conductor.

If you didn't get to hear the 3 hour presentation by Dr Kruse it could be because of a shockwave flash problem on your PC. I'm not up on PC tech however it is explained on the net. It was a worthwhile presentation. I look forward to hearing more.

Be well,

### Ricardo — 2014-01-24T02:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-106846>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106796)
>
> I have a dear friend whose DH has just been diagnosed with Dementia .... he hasn't been well for years - prostrate cancer etc.
> .... I suggested making him a smoothie using coconut cream and MCT oil and a banana and a bit of frozen raspberries .... any other suggestions?

Super suggestion !

MCT may be able to produce sufficient ketones to improve cognitive function in the Alzheimer brain. That insight has been around since the year 2000.

The knowledge that ketones could reduce the cerebral infarction in brain injury was already part of the body of knowledge at that time. Many other benefits have since been attributed to ketones.

Consider this : ketones, even at low levels, can increase cerebral blood flow by approximately 39% !

Ketones drive the production of ATP better than other alternative sources of fuels.

So, yes, coconut oil and mct oil should be added to your friend's husband's diet. In addition, getting at least 2 dosages a day would be better.

As we age, there is a shift in the types of fuel used by the brain.

When we are young it is 100:1 glucose to alternative fuels.
As we get older it becomes.... 29:1.
Those numbers radically change in the Alzheimer brain.... 2:1.

From these statistics it becomes clear that as we age we should access more ketones from dietary sources and strategies.

I think its great that you are suggesting quality ketogenic agents for your friend's husband ! <----Lucy knows best !

Be well,

R.R.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106862)
>
> Is there a book you would recommend that I can suggest to my friend?

Sure.

" Alzheimer's Disease: What If There Was a Cure ? " By Mary Newport MD. 2nd Edition. Available at Amazon. In addition, it can be downloaded as a kindle book.

I read the entire first edition. A very compelling story: a triumph of the human spirit. They say love finds a way.

The author is a neonatologist whose husband had severe symptoms of Alzheimer. After research, she gave her husband some coconut oil in his cereal one morning. After just 4 days "we knew our lives had changed".

Details of her husband's recovery are encouraging and inspiring.

She is a delight to read. There are scientific explanations and illustrations as well as a complete lipid strategy.

Caroline, your energy is noticeable. You must be making some good choices.

Be well,

R.R.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-25T02:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-106947>

So to complete what I mentioned earlier..

As we age our brains are designed.. at this point in our evolution ...to begin to use alternate sources of fuel to a greater extent than when we are younger. It behooves us to choose the right fuel.

In addition, the human body is designed to switch to alternate sources of fuel after glucose stores become depleted.
Alternate sources could also be aminos (by way of gluconeogenesis), lactate or fatty acids. I say fuggedaboudit: Go with a clean burning fuel and forget the metabolic byproducts and potential problems associated with other alternate fuel sources.

Ketones are far superior as an alternate energy source... by a country mile.

My best academic sucesses in University took place in the year that I went to the swimming pool for lunch instead of eating. So as we saw earlier, exercise potentially can provide as high a level of ketones as MCT. In addition, since I was depleting glucose from an hour of swimming with no compensation from carbs, I probably was becoming ketogenic. I believe for most of that year I was in a state of ketosis. My mind was sharp.. in a good place.

I wanted to understand and duplicate this sharpness and resistance to fatigue. However, it wasn't until the late 90's that I started to understand how ketones could be advantageous as a fuel source. I have been following the research ever since.

Y'know at the IVIG unit, I get to meet with people who have MS. What an insidious disease ! I guess we all have dreams.. one of mine is that we live in a world where information is coherent and readily available in order to address diseases with treatments that work.

I believe MS can be resolved or, at the very least, stabilized, with dietary intervention. And I believe the basis of such a dietary intervention would be ketones.

Well, I will be away "cocooning" to get some work done.

Lucy, be well, and keep on "doin it the way you're doin it ".

### Ricardo — 2014-02-10T01:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-109350>

Hello Lucykins,

Howya been ?

Just been going through some data on ketones and came across something I wanted to take a minute to get out there. Dr. Mary Newport's husband Steve is apparently retaining his progress in overcoming Alzheimer's. She mentioned something that is important to know. Twice he missed his morning oil ( MCT + coconut oil ) due to fasting bloodwork or travel:

" He became “dazed and confused” with a full-blown tremor. He returned to normal shortly after receiving the oil. "
Mary Newport MD

My "take" from that is once on a program of recovery for AD patients it is therefore important to respect the dosages and timing.

I also want to add a quick word about some feedback I got from friends starting to consume MCT and coconut oil in appreciable amounts. I hear about periods of "euphoria". Well considering the comments coming from AD patients such as "its like someone turned on the lights" and " I feel like I got my life back" its probably just a normal reaction to the enhanced fuel source (ketones). In that case, I suggest just staying grounded: even some light physical activity should help.
The brain will soon get accustomed to the increased amounts of ketones. The euphoria is temporary, however, the increased mental clarity is usually experienced on an ongoing basis.

Be well,
R.R.

PS The Optimal Reset 2014 is great ! Lots of useful information !

Turn into a polar bear and eat like a great white shark ? You know, sometimes I do really well with resistance to cold. When my ketone levels are high I can take out the garbage in my t-shirt even if its minus 30. I believe that ketones build resistance to a lot of things.. to disease , for instance.

There was another death due to exposure to a "super bug " here in Canada a couple of weeks ago: I keep my ketone levels high as a precautionary measure, (since I frequent hospitals. ) Also, if someone needs surgery it might be a good idea to ask about the presence of strains of bugs known to be difficult to control with antibiotics. Sometimes you can choose among hospitals where you or someone you love will undergo surgery.

Sitting in warm water wearing an ice vest sounds a bit radical until I think of how some Europeans take saunas and then go walking outdoors in the snow. If there is science behind it, then why not ?

Cape Breton Island !....home of the Capers ! What a beautiful place it is ! I went all around the island following the shore....very scenic ! And the people are so friendly !

Since you mentioned it, I started looking into didgeridoos. Interesting ! The sounds can be very therapeutic. I downloaded an instrumental with didgeridoo and tibetan bells...very nice.. relaxing and grounding. Have you ever tried to play one ? Apparently there is a special "circular breathing" technique.

Be well,

R.R.

### caroline — 2014-02-10T05:13:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-109405>

You always make me laugh! ......and I can't believe that you aren't used to writing English.
You are exactly right about the didgeridoo .... you are way too smart for your own britches! ... no - I have not.
But when you make your first recording .....can you imagine Ricky Ricardo playing the didgeridoo? .... I will line up to buy the first copy - second career perhaps???

I met some Canadian friends who were in Sydney last week ...and so I said hi to the didgeridoo guy again for you - you are best buds now.

Could you post and link that in the music thread? ..... the one where everyone is trying to help me get a life!
someone posted "Walltzing Matilda" for me but it got lost in the melt down.

Cape Breton is spectacular - Did you go to Rita McNeill's tea room? We drove around the island too - about a year before we came here and it is probably the best holiday we ever had. I have wonderful memories. We stayed in my DH"s home town of Sydney Mines - in a B&B that was spectacular and with great food.

Have you seen the movie ... Margaret's museum?

Better get back to serious stuff - lest people think we are fooling around .....

Please read about the hot/cold thing .... might be a great thing for you. I think it is called the Fournier affect. Jack did spell it but I didn't quite get it. Yes, I think you should get an ice vest .... and I have a great idea! I will pm you.

Have you used resistant starch? Did you see the webinar?

I am always well Ricky ... I hope you are too

### kovita — 2014-03-06T21:30:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-113825>

guys, i do not know about you but in my case ketosis elevated my fasting glucose to 95+. I did some home tests with carb loading over a few days and i seem to respond well, giving I do not have 50g of sugar without few days of slow upping. in all the cases i had to leave my GP, she laughed at me and my article about physiological insulin resistance on ketogenic diet when I refused glucose load test and received prediabetic label. She as well said, that macronutrient ratios play NO role in your fasting glucise levels and your ability to do glucose load test. Will this be a steady feature of longterm high fat low carb? I have done it only Since November, but 2 years gives youa. different perspective. By the way, I tend to have lower glucose after BAB breakfast than before.

She as well said, that macronutrient ratios play NO role in your fasting glucise levels and your ability to do glucose load test. Will this be a steady feature of longterm high fat low carb? I have done it only Since November, but 2 years gives youa. different perspective. By the way, I tend to have lower glucose after BAB breakfast than before.

Kovita...had a similar experience this fall with my cardiologist...he is even an anti-aging guy.....promotes ketotic eating etc. etc. But when it came to the glucose tolerance test he was adamant that my CT, ketosis etc. would have no bearing on the outcome. I just refused to take it (did confirm with an ask Jack question too) on the grounds that I wasn't going to blast my body full of sugar after working so hard to get it out of my system (serious brain/addiction issues). I even brought articles to show him...oh well. We still work together in a kind of gingerly fashion.

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-06T22:03:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-113829>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=113157)

>

>
> Are you worried about not getting enough nutrients?

Sean....have you read Dr. Kruse's material on the water/fat diet? Electrons not nutrients are what is operating here. You have to be in the right environment and have a good redox potential to take advantage of this. I know you are having a terrible time with your bloating, but asking what other people do can steer you in the wrong direction as much as it might actually help. Do you know what I mean?? I wish I could give you the answer, but I can't...sounds like working on your environment with the magnetico etc. is a good way to go for now.

### caroline — 2014-03-06T22:18:45+0200

### seanb4 — 2014-03-07T13:25:37+0200

@[SeaHorse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/562/) Yea I've read that post on 90% water diet, thats what got me thinking. I'm just trying to find out if other people can handle more fat and I just need to adapt or if I'm actually having too much and that's why I can't really handle it.
I think it's more of an adaption thing.
Yea my main plan is to save up for a stronger magnetico, and get more sun/artificial light electrons. The more electrons I can get in my body without going through my stomach the better.

# Thread: Weston Price Project Gutenberg

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-01-02T21:36:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-project-gutenberg.9237/post-104601>

[http://gutenberg.net.au/ebooks02/0200251h.html](http://gutenberg.net.au/ebooks02/0200251h.html)

An Epic Classic of Scientific adventure and great powers of observation.

Remember, no EMF's at that time....

### nonchalant — 2014-01-03T04:16:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-project-gutenberg.9237/post-104641>

Thanks for the link, Josh. I have wanted to buy this book for a while now, but it never seems to go down to a price I can jump on.

> The nutrition of the people of the Loetschental Valley, particularly that of the growing boys and girls, consists largely of a slice of whole rye bread and a piece of the summer-made cheese (about as large as the slice of bread), which are eaten with fresh milk of goats or cows. Meat is eaten about once a week. In the light of our newer knowledge of activating substances, including vitamins, and the relative values of food for supplying minerals for body building, it is clear why they have healthy bodies and sound teeth. The average total fat-soluble activator and mineral intake of calcium and phosphorus of these children would far exceed that of the daily intake of the average American child. **The sturdiness of the child life permits children to play and frolic bareheaded and barefooted even in water running down from the glacier in the late evening's chilly breezes, in weather that made us wear our overcoats and gloves and button our collars.**

> One immediately wonders if there is not something in the life-giving vitamins and minerals of the food that builds not only great physical structures within which their souls reside, but builds minds and hearts capable of a higher type of manhood in which the material values of life are made secondary to individual character.

### Lahelada — 2014-01-20T16:25:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-project-gutenberg.9237/post-106292>

Thank you for this find. Weston A price excerpts made me take note years ago but I never read his book.

### sooperb — 2014-01-20T19:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-project-gutenberg.9237/post-106299>

Is there anyway of downloading it? I'll bet it's fascinating but I'm not sure I can sit and read it on the computer lol.

### Josh — 2014-01-20T21:03:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/weston-price-project-gutenberg.9237/post-106310>

# Thread: New eCourse:  Biohacking 101
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ecourse-biohacking-101.9241/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 29

### tellmisty — 2014-01-03T02:54:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ecourse-biohacking-101.9241/post-104631>

Hey everyone,

We've just launched a new FREE eCourse on the site called BioHacking 101. We've had a couple hundred of you sign up so far ... you can sign up using the opt-in on the blog, or go here: [http://www.jackkruse.com/biohacking-101/](http://www.jackkruse.com/biohacking-101/)

With Dr. Kruse's help, I wrote this course for us to help navigate the world of biohacking ... this course is a work in progress, and I really would like us all to come together around the pathway that's emerging and share our ideas, success stories, and strategies for navigating the world of labwork and the path to optimal. Special thanks to Gretchen and Dr. Tim for their invaluable contributions to this course! I mean ... you all know it already, but those two are bah-rilliant and we are really lucky that they're part of the JK.com community.

I'll try to be better about stopping by and participating in this forum in particular ... I am really committed to growing the course and making it better for ALL of us! Please share your thoughts liberally ... and if you're really committed to becoming a good biohacker, I highly recommend setting up an Optimal Journal: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/forumdisplay.php?17-My-Optimal-Journal](http://forum.jackkruse.com/forumdisplay.php?17-My-Optimal-Journal). This community is FULL of brilliant members who've got lots of experience on this journey; we all lift the boats together!

Love you guys! Keep your eyes peeled for the 2014 Optimal Reset ... OMG, it's going to be BANANAS!!! We're gonna get optimal together!

xo
Misty

Thank you, I've found it. I don't think there's any point my signing up, lab tests in the UK are either at the discretion of your GP or you can pay a lab to do them but they are very expensive, I had a Homocysteine test and that was Â£149! I'm beginning to think that after 60 years of less than optimal living and diet and reading just how much can go wrong that I'm never likely to be able to turn back the clock and put absolutely everything right. I'll settle for carrying less weight (if I can ever achieve that) and hope that by following a Paleo lifestyle as much as I can that anything else out of whack sorts itself along the way.

### caroline — 2014-01-08T23:42:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ecourse-biohacking-101.9241/post-105110>

There is always high value in reading and following along - even if you can't do the tests for now. You never know when something will click for you.

This is a journey to optimal for me .... and I wouldn't dream of settling ... but then - I love everything about it. I love all the things I am learning and I love all the interaction with everyone here. I just love trying to figure all this out.

I would never have dreamt that I would love reading books about Einstein .... and reading Dr. Becker and Dr. Merino and Richard Feynman etc.

Every time I go to the pool at the ocean .... I always wind up talking about epi-paleo and C/T .... and I have been wondering - why does that keep happening? I have finally come to realise that people always eventually ask me what I am doing and have lots of questions and then we get into great discussions.

This journey is so individual .... we are all so different. At the end of the day - We all definitely need to do what is right for us.

# Thread: Dogs and the magnetic field:
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dogs-and-the-magnetic-field.9242/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Medichecks in the UK are doing a special offer this month where a liver check is Â£48 (normal price Â£99). It's a finger prick test so can be done at home and sent in by post.

I did this last year with a similar test from them but for some reason my blood sample kept clotting so they sent me to a local private hospital instead.

[http://www.medichecks.com/index.cfm?s=2&d=21&test=72mc](http://www.medichecks.com/index.cfm?s=2&d=21&test=72mc)

This is what the test covers:-

Sodium
Potassium
Chloride
Bicarbonate
Urea
Creatinine
Bilirubin
Alkaline phosphatase
AST
ALT
CK
LDH
Gamma-GT
Protein (Total)
Albumin
Globulin
Calcium
Phosphate
Uric Acid
Glucose
Cholesterol
Triglycerides
Iron
TIBC

### HoneyChild — 2014-05-01T13:08:00+0300

# Thread: Just thinking out loud....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-thinking-out-loud.9246/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tanya — 2014-01-03T18:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-thinking-out-loud.9246/post-104665>

As I am putting on my blue-blocker eyeglasses,
so that I can chat with one of my FB friends,
I think just how lucky we all are now that
We can keep in touch with our friends and family
without paper, envelopes, and stamps;
We download and read the best books on the Internet
We look for and find all kinds of information:
articles, music and movies ;
We can buy most of all that we need
without even stepping out of the house.
We meet on the Internet and acquire new friends.

But

I still remember the time when
We were just walking in the parks;
Libraries and bookstores were our favorite places to go;
we composed our poetry and read other peopleâ€™s.
Got together with your friends and family and
Played sports, danced and went to theater together.
We sat talking in the kitchen,
Played guitar and sang songs
We wrote letters to them by hand
and sent them in the envelopes
and most important one of them all:
We simply looked them in the eyes -
We did not need blu-blockers then....

### fitness@home — 2014-01-03T21:42:35+0200

### prAna303 — 2014-01-06T12:04:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-thinking-out-loud.9246/post-104862>

> How could you go to a dance club without a partner? As you can probably tell ..... I am a novice at practically everything - sad to say...

Many forms of dance do not require your own partner, when you join a workshop you would be teamed up with someone that can match you.

For a starter i would look into not just the "classical" versions but into some that will expand you, stress you to find your own body language.

> Its roots trace back to 1972. Contact Improvisation, usually done in duets, pulls elements from martial arts, social dance, sports, and childâ€™s play.\[2\] Upon entering a Contact Improv structure, two bodies must come together to create a point of contact (i.e., back to wrist, shoulder to thigh, head to foot, back to back, the options are endless), give weight equally to each other, and then create a movement dialog that can last for an undetermined amount of time, as long as both participants are fully engaged.

I do this form called Contact Improvisation and it really is a special form of dance, everyone can do it because it is a challenge to everyone to adapt to your partner for that specific moment...

\[video=youtube;a82Qy62bUTc\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a82Qy62bUTc\[/video\]

### prAna303 — 2014-01-06T23:31:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-thinking-out-loud.9246/post-104924>

> How did you end up int the corporate world?

Every part of life is a "must try" for me... If i have not tried it, how would i know if it kills me.

I applied my thoughts to sales and it worked, not following the rules that others dictate but perhaps at a higher level, beyond what others find okey. That is why i got banned from that kind of world.

Movement is a must for me and that also makes me a lonely person, have not found anyone that is willing to not get locked into modern thoughts about life... All of my girlfriends have come and gone but i have never felt alone, knowing that i must keep moving makes every meeting special, i can only make them feel good about themself and make them aim high with their lifes.

# Thread: Nuthin but a hot mess! (with low body temp!)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-01-04T02:29:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104692>

Hi all, from New Zealand!

Im new to posting on this blog, but discovered JK about two months ago, Im gutted that I never found him sooner, and it surprises the crap outta me, given my constant 'health trolling' about adrenal exhaustion etc..

Im almost stuck at where to start, such is the 'quilt' of my ill health..

Heres where Im at now, iv just ordered some labs - sex hormones, melatonin and a cortisol profile (all saliva) and i will try to get vit D and HS CRP, though i recently read on the inet, that its basically not an option in NZ (like lots of things)

Over the past 5 years mission of trying to lose weight (but just keep getting bigger) iv had lots of hormone panels done. My cortisol is always showing its shot to bits - really low by day, and uber high at night. I first got relief from my nightmare when i met the most amazing american doc working at a place i stayed at in thailand - and he took me from a vegan diet straight to paleo, and off coffee (detoxing from that was a b\*tch!) no change for weeks, then after like 3 months, i basically woke up thin! as weird as that sounds, i wouldnt believe it myself if i hadnt see it with my own eyes! ( i was taking adrenal cortex and some herbs because hed run cortisol labs on me and showed adrenal burnout)

And its just been a mare since then. Just before i met that doctor, Id been back to antarctica for work (summer) and that year was when i stopped sleeping well. We were on night shifts for most of the 4 months, and though the rooms are pitch black, it was still 24 hour daylight outside, but not for the whole 4 months. It got to the point where you get up in the dark, and go to bed in daylight - and im a chef, so i was working inside under fluro lights - NONE of that rang any bells but its thru JK that iv been able to string bits together and see a pattern.. SO -

i went back for two more consecutive summers. and Iv also seen with my own eyes, i just got bigger, and bigger and bigger - and i hadnt changed my diet or exercise (except id stopped the chronic cardio) but i was under a lot of stress, and back to 24 hours light, and mixed up shifts...

I used to leave the ice an go and hang out in asia, literally lie about in the air con and just while away HOURS on youtube watching crap - that was my 'rest'! Id go to the gym but other than that.. i was happy laying low - getting massages, taking a wander and watching all that fab junk that i could on my laptop. It was my second time doing that that i REALLY started to have sleep issues - im talking taking sleeping pills and still lying awake all night, bashing heart (that used to start at 10.30 on the dot, some nights so badly that i couldnt lie down..) thank god i found a chinese doctor who gave me some herbal sleepers that worked amazingly, im not sure how i would have survived the last few years otherwise!

A year ago, i self medicated with thyroid extract. Im so sick of low body temp (average around 35.) but continued to swell up and and up and up, low energy, very unstable moods.. basically a hot mess! (back in nz at this point) then i met a naturopath who tested my iodine, saliva and (now i cant remember, blood or urine?) and i barely scarped through, it was DISMALLY low! wow, what a shock! so iv been taking iodine ever since. and she helped me see how the dessicated thyroid wasnt really helping, so i came off it. (and i lost the feeling of being too big for my skin)

the other job i do is as a soigneur - i work with bike teams doing massage / food etc.. which takes me all over the world. this year, in europe for 5 weeks, i didnt take a laptop, just ipad and iphone. hmm i never got a sim for my iphone, i was given a brick phone, i couldnt be bothered with european tv, so never watched it, and basically was kinda busy at races, and naturally following light cycles. ( i was doing some cold blasts in the shower - but i hadnt discovered JK at this stage) but low and behold, i came home 5-7kg lighter! and i got back to miserable nz winter, and spent two weeks in bed recovering jet lag n all, HOURS of blue light.. and HELLO, the weight came back! hmmm

then i eventually discovered JK. so here i am. Its not been the easiest for me to do strict epi paleo fx, but i def do ok cos i dont go for lots of carbage etc, i do the BAB daily, no snacking etc. I did get changes in my sleep for sure. this can be a bit up and down ,depending on whats going on in my life.
what id like to do is get my lab results back and then become a gold member and discuss this all with JK. some days i feel SO desparate and hopeless, it really sucks. (like now! lol) Iv been to hell and not come back yet, like so many others on here.. its funny how ppl still comment on how big i am, or how i should eat or exercise.. i say 'honey! youre so 10 years ago when eating less actually worked for me! if it was that easy, i wouldnt be like this!'

i do the grounding when i fly. My next race is to LA and then colombia, so im going to try CoQ10 for those flights. I listen to all JKs podcasts, and really feel this is the last place i will come to for help, that this will actually help me!

I know therell be things i missed out, and i tried to make this as condensed as i could but yeah.. thats me in a hot-mess nutshell!

oh yeah, i was bulimic for about 8 years when i was younger, and i dabbled in anorexia as well, but found bulimia more fun. and i raced bikes myself, for about 15 years, and talk about chronic cardio! fully low fat, lots of grains etc..

I wanna thank JK again, for what you do, learn and then share. freaking amazing!

HMM - Hormonal Mess Margo! lol

### caroline — 2014-01-04T03:47:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104698>

Welcome Margo! what an amazing story you have shared with us!

Could you please start a journal in the journal section ..... Dr. K. needs context to offer suggestions when he can.
Journals really help everyone .... you never know when someone will say something to help you .... or something you say really resonates with someone else! We all help each other on this amazing journey.
A journal really helps with commitment and it is fun and helpful to go back and see your progress.

Did you see the free webinar with Dr. Tim Jackson?

When you become gold member you will have access to all the webinars at any time and even more important, the live Q&A with Dr. Kruse where you can ask him pretty much anything.

Please don't feel desperate .... altho I guess most of us have been there. You can and will turn your titanic around! Lots here have done it.

Just take baby steps - you didn't get broken overnite - so it is going to take awhile! we are very lucky to have JK shining his flashlight for us.

Have you got Jack's book? The Ei-Paeo RX ... it is a great overview of all of this.

How are you doing with the BAB? it worked wonders for me .... I had been a binge eater for most of my life.

You seem to get the non native EMF connection - hugely important ......especially for you with all your travel.

Do you have blue blocker glasses?

Please just keep reading and asking questions...... you have so much to share with all of us .... can't wait!

### Josh — 2014-01-04T03:51:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104699>

Welcome! I would recommend reading the blog posts in chronological order. The early ones are good, but it really gets good when you read through the topical ones in order. The concepts emerge and it gets you thinking with him. It is empowering which I feel is his mission, the N=1 thing. The key from your story looks like what we might call keeping your energy or electrons topped up. The system tends to run well when that is in order and otherwise it gets really inefficient and unpleasant things happen, many of which you have already identified. Its simple, but not easy....He lays it all out brilliantly through the blogs, especially EMF into Quantum Biology. Beware of too steep of a gradient as this gets one into trouble, only took me 50 years or so to learn.

### margor_j — 2014-01-04T05:17:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104705>

HI guys,
thanks for your positive replies.

Iv been wading through JKs blogs since i discovered them, i have his book and i have downloaded every podcast i can find where JK is interviewed

Caroline - yes, i have blue blocker glasses. I did have my room blacked out, which was pretty mega, but the foils been removed (not my doing  But im not working at the moment, before I was getting up at 4.30 so i could do CT in the cold lake right outside, before I started my shift (that has been reallllyy eye opening for me, because i found i got a huge reduction in body pain / back pain - working as a chef can be really hard on your body! plus, it made a huge difference in how hot the kitchen felt when i got to work each day! )
I also noticed that i have started to sweat again, not hugely, but definitely noticeably. Ever since i can remember, iv been the person who can sit in a sauna and not sweat..

The last race i was away at, i would have the small tv on in the background, but i didnt turn lights on, i could massage in that semi light (natural) plus the little the tv put out, and some from the hallway cos i kept my door open.. anyway, it was amazing how knacked i was by bed time and i really think it was the fact i made the most of natural light... (huge windows, curtains open, naturally getting darker...)

Yeah josh, i wonder about my emf damage also.. i actually have a pendant i wear - not every day, but regularly, to help with emfs. whether it does or not, i dont know, but i figure if it could help, its worth it. I also try to earth every day cos, while i havent been going barefoot alot before hand, the 3 summers in a row down south also coincided with my first iphone, ipad, fancy ipod and spending loads of time on my computer (what else do you do on a blizz day when you cant go outside!)

and caroline yes, i sometimes 'fear' that reality that i may be looking at something that will take a long time to correct because its been a good few years building up..

any thoughts, ideas on raw milk? i have access to it, get it weekly, and here in NZ were lucky to have A2 beta-casein.. i feel like a calf sometimes i just love it so much!
also, coffees SUCH a thorn for me.. but i know i feel so much better if i can go months without having it, its just very hard for me to seem to be able to do.. altho, italy this year did change the way i drink it. and interestingly, in italy i never got the shakes from a coffee!! they do ristretto shots (shorter, so less caffeine) plus, they dont do the big milky mega cup size like over here (how i used to drink it). its more like 2-3 sips and its done, and its not really strong.. but its hard to find ppl in nz who understand how to make that!

thanks for the advice to start a journal, il endeavor to do so!

### caroline — 2014-01-04T06:31:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nuthin-but-a-hot-mess-with-low-body-temp.9248/post-104706>

You are a chef too??? That is my background .... Nothing like working 16 hours in a hot kitchen!

Do you know about BPC? Bullet proof coffee. Dave Asprey has a website and he talks a lot about the mould in coffee.
We can get his coffee here.... Optimoz.com

What else can I tell you?

Honestly. - if you had told me 2 years ago I would still be working a way at this - I might not have been too impressed! But it is amazing how things change over time and often you don't even notice. I know that sounds strange but I think everyone here will tell you the same thing. We are all working on our own masterpiece... Our own n=1.

Also remember that Dr. Kruse is 8 - 10 years ahead of all of us.

We all love bio hacking ourself ...seeing what kind of a difference we can make for ourself.

hi hi

oh youre a chef? classic.. yeah, its a hard life! i did put that in my original post i thought.. i was cooking in antarctica.

wow two years youve been going?! i do notice a lot of folk talkng about the months theyre 'doing it ' - yet JK talks about 6-8 weeks (which id like to nail!) so what are ppl doing after 6-8 weeks?! im thinking that once you heal, you still have to watch the same old same olds - light, carb cycle, emfs, bab...

hmm well, i get frustrated to cos EVERY dam thing iv tried, every pill, exercise, etc etc - where normal people will shed kgs, i get zero change. every single time, its a bit of a joke actually! (but im not laughing!)

# Thread: Chronic Eczema hacking for years & no cure! EMF or Methylation?

### LaurenR — 2014-01-04T14:58:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104719>

Long post...
I have been hacking my chronic eczema all over for 3 years, tried many things...

Diagnosed as infant with eczema, asthma etc. Flare ups age 17yrs through to mid twenties, also IBS, allergies, chornic sinusitis (all fixed now).

Aug 2010- Chronic eczema on inside of forearms- dermatologist UV B treatment, helps a little.

Sept 2011- Still eczema, saw naturopath- started herbal meds & herbal cleanse, followed by probiotics (standard weed, seed stuff) .
Also stopped asthma meds and pill at same time = MASSIVE flare up! Face, neck, arms, upper body- lasts for months!
CDSA-4 stool- says no parasites, extreme dysbiosis, little to no good bacteria in gut.

Mar 2012- Series of 4 colonics & stop anti-depressant meds results in complete body flare up, EXTREME odema- legs,ankles, Extreme flaking of skin all over, in car, in bed, all day and night.

Next 3 months- extreme itching and burning sensation- like having acid poured on me, also little to no sleep at night- fall to sleep usually around 3am-5:30am, impacted life, ability to work etc.
Jul 2012- start a cortisone cream so I can sleep, function, work etc, helps greatly, continue use of cream.

Jul 2012- Jan 2014- continued chronic eczema, rash. Better, but not healed, some flare ups. Eczema forearm/elbow, behind knees-weeps, bleeds.
But also a general all over rash on back, outside of arms.

I have been Paleo (a little dairy) for 3 years, Dairy-free for 2 years, STRICT epi-paleo for >1 year.
My diet is tight: seafood/fish daily, grass fed/ free range red meat, some pork belly, coconut oil, coconut milk, limited veg (broc, cauli, brussels etc ), herbs, turmeric, ginger, garlic, onion, some 90% chocolate. I don't eat any of my IgG intolerance foods.

Starting again and simplifying: Currently redoing on a gut healing protocol- glutamine, aloe vera. Also bottled spring water as only water I drink now and reducing EMF where in my control (no sleep pad yet).
I still have "standard" eczema symptoms- sun makes it better. Showers, wool clothes make it worse.

Why am I posting in here? I AM GOING TO HEAL THIS IN 2014! But it's time to look DIG DEEPER!
I'm wondering about the impact of EMF (emf 7). I'm wondering about methylation.
It is more than a leaky gut!!!

I'm am wondering where to from here, as I begin an new year, try new labs etc.

I thank you for your time taken to read this. I appreciate your help to finally overcoming this issue.

### GoldFishie — 2014-01-05T16:40:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104801>

Lauren honey, I am SO sorry for the misery you are going through! I had horrible eczema up until the age of 35 and was on steroids for 10 years as a child until they pulled me off of them at the age of 17. Then I went from the proverbial frying pan into the fire.

I certainly don't have 'the' answer for you but I will tell you this - get off of all pork period. Oh if I would have known this as a child growing up on a farm! I am clear now, and seldom eat out, but one time I ate some chicken that had been cooked on the same grill they cooked pork on and I broke out for 6 weeks. This piece should be huge for you.

Secondly, make sure that you get a good balance on your essential fatty acids. You need more than the average person. I know that you are in Oz and don't know if you have access to Genova labs but they have a blood spot test for this.

Lastly, the sun makes a tremendous difference for my skin. I live in the southern US and am outside butt naked as much as possible and use a tanning bed during the winter.

Hope this helps~

I can actually attest to this. When my eczema was really bad (when I was younger) I visited one doctor who told me to take like 6 capsules of evening primrose oil a day. It really did help to improve my skin then. I have no idea how or why it works though. But I did read somewhere that dry flaky skin was due to an Omega 6 deficiency. I don't understand the pathophysiology of eczema......so there is something wrong with the Vit A cycle in the skin.....affecting the immune response...but what does that have to do with Omega 6? Maybe the connection has something to do with Omega 6 -> inflammatory prostaglandins?

I would really like to know more about eczema......I know so many people who have it and although I can tell them what I did to get rid of it.....what worked for me might not necessarily work for them. But I think people with eczema should really focus on light cycles more than anything(including diet). Adhering to light cycles would be a high yield area..... Lots of sunbathing, the more skin exposed the better, during the day and strict avoidance of light(especially blue light at night) would benefit your skin more than any diet could.

# Thread: Sleep patterns  and the brain....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-01-04T23:26:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104736>

sleep patterns and more specifically - my brain! Which could be a whole different kettle of fish I suppose.

I have been noticing for the longest while now .... in the evening,when I have my blue blockers on, and watching TV a little......I start getting really drowsy and struggle to stay awake. I know that is kind of normal - but - I think I start to go into that semi coma state where our brain is getting ready for sleep - the vortex changes direction. Because the TV is on I am still vaguely listening to the noise - but my brain is much less active - but my auditory sense is still tuned in.

When I stat getting this drowsy I immediately turn off the TV and get into bed - but what the heck happens?
all of a sudden - my brain is active again and my mind is flooded with the events of the day or QM or whatever. How can I go from this lovely, very drowsy state in minutes to a state of being totally awake and starting to process all kinds of stuff all over again. I try really hard not to ....I try really hard to maintain that drowsiness. It is not that I am worried about anything .... it just seems like my brain reactivates.... Would this mean that the vortex then changes direction?

Long time ago - I asked Jack about this and he said I should have just stayed asleep on the couch - but that doesn't seem to be an option. I sometimes fall asleep for 15 minutes and then wake up - but still drowsy. It would be lovely to wake up 8 hours later!

Does it have something to do with our auditory sense? I think I remember JK saying something about how important our auditory sense is but he hasn't got into it yet? I could be totally wrong about that.

Does this happen to anyone else?

I am going to go back and listen to the sleep webinar later today ... and also re read the thread on it.

Sleep is so hugely important ... I really want to get this right. How can I go from a state of bliss to ....not?

tonite I am going to put the tV on a timer and then go to bed - I should be able to hear it vaguely ... and see what happens.

If we start to get drowsy - that must mean that serotonin/ melatonin is kicking in properly .... doesn't it?

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-05T00:06:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104742>

Audrey....how is your cortisol at night? And what about no tv...just reading in bed and be ready to stay in bed. ### Josh — 2014-01-05T04:45:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104765>

Try meditation or some sort of relaxation before bed and compare and contrast with straight TV, whatever you watch. Even watching people talking about "meditation" such as Echart Tolle, J. Krishnamurti, Ram Das, whoever gets you interested may have an effect. It is a quantum thing and it can be contagious. I find a huge difference between watching videos of discourses by people oriented towards becoming more conscious, etc. vs general information and entertainment. Another option is to just listen to audio by such people and see how it affects you. If you want to try someone who has never failed to put me to sleep, download a discourse of Osho at [www.oshoworld.com](http://www.oshoworld.com). Hell I can fall asleep listening to Dr. Kruse. When I accept someone as an ally on this planet, I relax and their voice helps me let go for a while.

but Josh - the thing is ... I think there is some big auditory connection there that Jack hasn't told us .... but then - I might be making that up! The hopes of finding where and if he actually said that are probably slim .....

I really, really find i go to sleep much easier listening to someone... but that implies to me that the brain is slowing down getting ready for sleep.....so once we take away the noise - whatever it is .....our brain re engages for some reason.

My cortisol isn't too far off where it should be .....I am not worried about anything....It is like a switch is flipped..........

### Andrea — 2014-01-05T07:31:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104786>

I've noticed the same thing since I started to wear blue blockers in the evening. TV or no TV. After perhaps half an hour with them on, I feel drowsy. So I only wear them 1-2 hours before I want to go to bed (it's pitch black at 4pm here this time of the year...).
If I do watch TV, I easily fall asleep on the couch, but just for half an hour-hour, then I wake up, still drowsy.

Anyway, when I get myself to bed and try to sleep, I feel totally awake. Obviously, I fall asleep at some point, but always wake up at 3-4am.

Without the blue blockers, I'm not drowsy in the evening, just "worn out" (probably from decreasing level of T3 due to my med's wearing off), but I fall asleep very soon after going to bed. Easily. And I sleep the whole night until I want to get up.

### Gracelind — 2014-01-05T09:07:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104787>

Caroline, this just happened to me, though not with blueblockers on. I was watching a You Tube about Evernote organization with classical music from the TV. Started drifting off and then actually fell asleep for awhile sitting up on the sofa, with feet on ottoman.

One of the times I regained consciousness I thought that if I gave in and truly fell asleep it would be too early and I'd pop awake in another couple of hours, around midnight. So I purposely woke up. Still feeling drowsy but all the routine of prepping for bed will surely wake me up even more.

Big sigh . . . You aren't alone.

> [Rerun said:](/goto/post?id=104786)
>
> I've noticed the same thing since I started to wear blue blockers in the evening. TV or no TV. After perhaps half an hour with them on, I feel drowsy. So I only wear them 1-2 hours before I want to go to bed (it's pitch black at 4pm here this time of the year...).
> If I do watch TV, I easily fall asleep on the couch, but just for half an hour-hour, then I wake up, still drowsy.
>
> Anyway, when I get myself to bed and try to sleep, I feel totally awake. Obviously, I fall asleep at some point, but always wake up at 3-4am.
>
>
> Without the blue blockers, I'm not drowsy in the evening, just "worn out" (probably from decreasing level of T3 due to my med's wearing off), but I fall asleep very soon after going to bed. Easily. And I sleep the whole night until I want to get up.

When you don't wear your blueblockers - you fall asleep faster .... do you wake up rested?
I don't get why if we fall asleep on the couch - why we don't stay asleep?

### sooperb — 2014-01-05T14:46:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104793>

Ah the age old problem, you're sitting on the sofa, your TV is on, you may be reading or using a device too, then you start nodding, drifting, it's a very enticing state, you start letting yourself go with the feeling and soon you're in just the state you describe Audrey, not entirely awake but you can still hear the TV. What to do, no one really sleeps well on the sofa, they're not designed for it so neck cricks and backache are likely to be with you when you wake and drag yourself to bed.

I read quite a while ago that it's more to do with Circadian rythmns and that there tend to be two windows in the evening and you're likely to find yourself drifting off in yours. These were noted as being 9pm ish and 11pm ish. I'm definitely in the 9pm ish bracket. I either try to fight it off but find myself mostly asleep but bringing myself round or I go completely for an hour or so and then wake up usually by some trigger such as the music to the news which tells me subconsciously that's it's 10pm. I struggle awake and take it from there.

Thinking about going with the flow, well, for me, 9pm feels too early, if I decided to go to bed I would be either woken later when OH comes up or he would think he was on a winner and go to bed too, more wakefulness would follow. If I wait until 11pm, I am drowsy again but ................ my bedtime routine is to let the dog out, feed the cats who assure me they would starve overnight if I didn't, to the bathroom where I thoroughly clean my teeth and apply a few unctions to my body, then finally to the bedroom and into bed. I'm not very sleepy by then lol.

I suppose I should be more "selfish" and expect OH to let the dog out, fill the dishwasher, feed the cats and then creep into bed so he doesn't wake me if I'm already there. It might work except he often goes up to bed before me, it's not a plan then really is it.

### Tanya — 2014-01-05T17:22:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104807>

This is how I do it now and my sleep is sooooo much better.....My TV and all my numerous technological devices are all turned off after 6PM. The last hour before going to bed I spend reading or doing some paperwork in a room with the amber-colored lights. When I start getting to feel sleepy, which is around 9PM, I and my book move to the bedroom which also has the same kind of lights. Once in bed, I use my Bionaise device while my DH massages my feet. I am asleep within the first 10-15 min of that happening. That book never gets a chance.... But .... I am sound asleep until I wake up automatically at 5 AM.

### Andrea — 2014-01-05T20:30:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104814>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104791)
>
> When you don't wear your blueblockers - you fall asleep faster .... do you wake up rested?
> I don't get why if we fall asleep on the couch - why we don't stay asleep?

Yup, I wake up rested and I feel good in the morning after not wearing the blue blockers the previous evening. For now I sort of hope that I will get used to them and sleep better soon.

I'm thinking of giving Dr. Jeffrey Thompson's [Delta Sleep System](http://www.amazon.com/Delta-Sleep-System-Jeffrey-Thompson/dp/B00005BIY7) a shot. Just not sure I like the idea of having speakers and my cell phone close to my bed...

As for sleeping on the couch, I suppose mine isn't comfortable enough to sleep and turn around on.

### caroline — 2014-01-05T23:15:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104817>

The last hour before going to bed I spend reading or doing some paperwork in a room with the amber-colored lights. When I start getting to feel sleepy, which is around 9PM, I and my book move to the bedroom which also has the same kind of lights. Once in bed, I use my Bionaise device while my DH massages my feet. I am asleep within the first 10-15 min of that happening. That book never gets a chance.... But .... I am sound asleep until I wake up automatically at 5 AM.

Tanya - you are a rock star! That sounds like a great plan. I am going to try turning everything off after 6:00pm. I was thinking that was the problem but I only put the TV on for company.

Now to find some one to massage my feet ......

You don't get up for the bathroom in the middle of the night?

### caroline — 2014-01-05T23:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104819>

sooperb ... you always make me laugh! It is all the unctions that need applying that will get you every time!

What price beauty ... heh?

I went to the movies late yesterday afternoon - I thought at least I would be out of the house. I didn't turn the TV on at all....and I had a horrible sleep last night!

The movie - Osage something or other - with Meryl Streep was such a downer...... I was going to leave half way thru and then thought it might get better .... wrong, wrong, wrong!

### Josh — 2014-01-06T02:13:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104846>

As a note, I only watch my TV through a computer with n.flux so it does not matter as much whether I wear the glasses. One small thing I noticed is that my eyes hurt with the blue-blockers sometimes and they get better when I take a little vitamin B.

### sooperb — 2014-01-06T16:30:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-patterns-and-the-brain.9251/post-104873>

The unctions aren't for beauty's sake Audrey (with my visage that would be a waste of time and money  ), not exactly lol. I rub oil into a pre-varicose area on my ankle, I started in the hope that I could somehow get rid of it, it hasn't gone but it is less purple and slightly smaller. I also rub oil into the back of my hands to reduce the chance of age spots. I know, I know, not exactly scientific but I started with Rosehip oil, which I like a lot and hang the expense lol, and I don't have more than a few small, pale freckly things. So it might be working.

Audrey I think when you get more quantum coherent ;-) you get more sensible in a way, the dullness subsides. And that is why you cannot tolerate being at the movies at night or watching TV. ### QuantumRadical — 2014-01-05T01:18:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104747>

The "tourists" stuck in the ice in Antarctica are actually a scientific expedition in Antarctica to study global warming. **_How ironic._** Yet, the media continues to ignore this part of the story and continues to describe the lot as just tourists. Now, with at least two icebreakers stuck in the ice, and the coldest air mass in twenty years descending from the Arctic into mid-America, you've got to wonder about computer models and theories. I put my money on a coming ice-age rather than a balmy beach vacation at the poles.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-05T01:22:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104748>

I think you're right with the low sunspot activity - and the increased cold temps as well as increased Arctic and Antarctic ice coverage ... we may be looking at something similar to the maunder minimum.... better get your land investment in trees to make the next awesome violins.....

### nicld — 2014-01-05T04:03:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104759>

Yep, not really believing in global warming right now either since we have been so freeking cold this winter so far. I really don't think that man had anything to do with the last ice age either.

When we visited the Denver museum they said that the Rockies were once under water and I don't think man did that either. I know that we are doing crap that is horrible to the environment but how arrogant are we to think that we can effect how the earth takes care of it self. We did not make the dinosaurs disappear.

nicid, The sun is by far the biggest determinant of our climate--especially its dynamics over time. Although I have not fully researched this, but from a sampling of the real (non-politicized--if that is possible nowadays) research, the sun is going through a cooling period of its own, which will ultimately lead to the earth cooling, and then we won't have to buy anymore Ice to CT with! I think Shijin13 is right about the possibility of a mini-ice age. One of those kicked Napoleon's Army's butt in the long Russian Winter back in those days.

### Josh — 2014-01-05T04:51:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104766>

I think the main point is that whatever we are doing is not helping and we know there are local effects. A wise man once said that the inhabitants of this planet had a peculiar tendency to periodically destroy everything in sight. Let us see if a strong sun cycle brings out some special behavior from all camps with extreme positions and agendas.

### caroline — 2014-01-05T05:01:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104770>

I guess I better buy a snow suit......

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-05T10:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104788>

Energy is a zero sum game......when we create excessive man made surface layer EMF the sun can sense it via the solar wind. It can also react in kind.......sounds crazy until you realize how everything is coupled.......

### sooperb — 2014-01-05T14:53:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104795>

I don't buy into global warming either, there's no real evidence. Anyone remember the hole in the ozone layer? Hasn't it gone quiet about that now. How they could determine that it was manmade I don't know, they didn't know how long it's been there, what caused it, if anything, whether it's benign or dangerous, if it's bigger or not, all speculation with very little to back it up.

There's always been weather, weather changes, it has cycles, it moves stuff about, but all in all the world goes on despite it or even because of it. Here in the UK we were losing a lot of trees and in Scandinavia too, it was acid rain created by too much industrial pollution, oh my god we were killing the planet. It turned out to be a virus that was killing them. QED.

I'd bet the entire DOD budget that they saw the solar cycle slowing down - based upon their mathematical calculations and knew that we were due a serious "cold snap". It would be interesting to see if the Mayans astrological information actually showed a complete stall on the solar cycle..... Whats interesting about this whole "Stuck in the Ice" boondoggle - no one's really talked about the US's main icebreaker just returned from its voyage through the Arctic almost a month late - and is due for serous repairs due the the thickness of the ice that they encountered before it was even the high point of ice.....

### Ricardo — 2014-01-05T02:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ricardos.9253/post-104752>

"Lucy are you sure this shampoo works ?"

I have a multi-focal blockage of nerve conduction. I have been on IV Gamma Globulin ever since they began to use it for people like myself as well as MS patients. Basically my immune system is confused.. or abnormally stupid.. depends on how you look at it. I am fortunate for having had great neurologists to help me with my disorder. Currently, how this condition begins is still just a matter of hypothesis. I do better if my inflammation markers are lower.

I think Dr Kruse has a lot of information about lowering inflammation and, in addition, resetting one's biology. I am impressed by the amount and quality of knowledge available here and at Dr Kruse's blog.

I have done a fair amount of research on my own to try to better understand health related matters, so maybe some of what I have discovered or hypothesize might be useful to others.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-05T05:39:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ricardos.9253/post-104779>

At this point I have begun to integrate some practices suggested by Dr Kruse's blog.

Perhaps I should begin by the big picture as I see it, then go through what, at this time, is for me an optimal day.

So big picture first:

Let's consider the body as a work-site, a work in progress. I believe there are 2 main types of workers: probiotics and enzymes ( by that I am referring to all the enzymes in the body.. not just those involved in digestion). So you need sufficient members of both trade unions.. the probiotics and the enzymes to do the work. If you don't have enough of both types of workers your raw materials just sit.. That can cause problems.

I start every day with a good strong array of probiotics. Since I go to hospitals regularly, its important to have the right good guys (probiotics) inside my system to defend my body against the "super bugs" that populate certain hospitals. And btw, these super bugs have killed and will do so again at every opportunity. These "super bugs" are powerful strains that have been created by over consumption of anti-biotics.

So take your cue from the japanese who have their white miso soup in the morning. Same idea.. starting the day with probiotics.

There are probiotics and there are super probiotics. Here is an example of an advanced probiotic: Plantarum OM.

The US patent states.. and I quote...
"The L. plantarum, OM strain has proteolytic, anti-viral, anti-retroviral, anti-bacterial, anti-microbial, and anti-tumoral characteristics and uses."

This is a warrior strain.. one bad-ass probiotic.

[http://patft.uspto.gov/netacgi/nph-...50&s1=5895758.PN.&OS=PN/5895758&RS=PN/5895758](http://patft.uspto.gov/netacgi/nph-Parser?Sect1=PTO1&Sect2=HITOFF&d=PALL&p=1&u=%2Fnetahtml%2FPTO%2Fsrchnum.htm&r=1&f=G&l=50&s1=5895758.PN.&OS=PN/5895758&RS=PN/5895758)

I know of a woman who had a tumor the size of an orange..medication wasn't working so she took this probiotic and in 2 months kapow ! The tumor disappeared.. gone...kaputsky !

There are others. So. Probiotics. Workers. I make sure they show up on the worksite at the beginning of the day to do the work. And I make sure there are plenty of them to do what has to be done.

As for the other crew..the enzymes... that could take a little longer and its getting late.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-13T22:17:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ricardos.9253/post-105578>

Super.. and I send my regards to the didgeridoo guy. The more good vibes we send each other the better the world we live in.

While I'm here in this thread, I should continue with the descrip of a logical, I think, perception of how our biology functions:

So if we consider the body as a worksite, I believe there are two main types of workers: probiotics and enzymes.

Probiotics were thought of as bacteria useful in the GI tract. They are way beyond that now. Thanks to microbial engineers there are now probiotics that are veritable "super beasts" that perform work systemically and can eradicate pathogens that were previously not accessible to probiotics.

There are nutritional strategies to support the work of enzymes in the body.

Fats, more than any other nutrient, support the production of energy, of new cells, and regulate the entire metabolic process. Making the right choices in fats is perhaps the number one key to building health and eliminating disease.

Ingest electron-rich foods. Unprocessed foods: the closer to nature the better. Simply stated, to support the work of enzymes, give your body the right components to work with.

Combining molecules to produce therapeutic and optimal results:

Dr Beliveau is director of the molecular medicine lab here in the city. He was featured as the last speaker in a day long seminar at the Jewish General Hospital. By the end of the afternoon most everybody is starting to yawn and feel sleepy. Well his talk about research into the powerful properties of food compounds got everyone excited and wide awake.

Based on the findings of the MM lab, it would be advantageous to combine foods, herbs and spices that are known to have medicinal properties, in order to form more powerful molecules. Their findings are being documented.

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
