### Carr Carr — 2014-01-06T00:06:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this-dont-know-where-to-start-on-leptin-reset.9257/post-104823>

Want to do the Leptin Reset but don't know where to start there is so much info on forums I am a little confused on what I should eat and what I am not allowed to eat. Can someone point me in the right direction as excited to start this. Want to start feeling better

### caroline — 2014-01-06T00:35:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this-dont-know-where-to-start-on-leptin-reset.9257/post-104828>

Welcome Carr!

Please go to the home page - there are great easy start guides for the leptin RX and C/T. Just dive in with those first. There is a lot of info here and it can be overwhelming - so just get those down pat first.

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi-Paleo RX : The prescription for Disease reversal and Optimal health. It is available on Kindle or paper from Amazon. His book will give you a great overview of all of this.

Have you seen Dr. K's podcasts? they are an easy and fast way to get info .... on the home page.

Consider becoming a gold member \[if you can manage that of course\] and you will have access to all the webinars at any time - and the live Q&A that follows a few days later where you can ask Jack anything.

Please start a journal in the journal section .... go to the top of this page and click on forum - and then go down six categories. Dr. Kruse likes to have some context ... so tell him a bit about yourself - do you live in a big city? what is your health history?

Welcome to this amazing journey to optimal health!
Always ask questions if you don't understand something .... we are all in the same boat!

Do you have labs? you can post them in the lab section and someone will help you understand them if you want.

# Thread: Stomach issues thread... again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 76
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2014-01-06T13:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-104867>

Hi guys,

If you've read my previous posts you'll know I'm the guy constantly whining about stomach problems. I am not much further in discovering the cause but I thought I'd come at it from an new angle in hopes that it sparks off some new insight from someone that could help me.

Whenever I eat or drink anything (including spring water) my stomach bloats out proportional to the volume of food/liquid, my heart rate increases and starts beating HARD, I can clearly see my entire abdomen pulsating in time with my pulse and it is very uncomfortable, and I feel the food rising back up my oesophagus if I eat/drink too much. The long term effects seem to be massive fatigue, hypothyroid, constipation, and other undesirable things.

The thing that is concerning me most is the heart palpitations. It feels like my heart is working on overdrive 24/7 and even more so at meal times. I feel like this is cutting years off my life, it is also very uncomfortable and makes me slightly worried that I might have a heart attack. However since this is directly linked with my stomach after eating I feel like the clue is in there somewhere. It's like my stomach requires much more blood than a normal persons after a meal, yet my stomach seems to be working worse even with this extra blood; it feels like the food is still sitting there and returning up my oesophagus even though tests suggest it's working fine.

This problem started roughly around the time I took too many NSAIDs. Before I was eating according to the leptin reset and was the healthiest I have been in my entire life. This made me realise how far ahead Jack was over almost every one else and has convinced me if I am going to find answers it is going to be on this site using this information.

Recently I have increased my water intake to ~2 Litres, started grounding via a rod outside, and moved away/grounded EMF sources near my bedroom. Since increasing the water I have noticed an increase in energy and hair/skin quality, however it means more stuff going through my stomach so more bloating, palpitations, etc.

I have almost read through the EMF series and am currently trying to save up for a magnetico sleep pad. Hopefully this will allow me to get more energy from sources that don't have to go through the stomach. This extra energy would hopefully allow my body to make some repairs, maybe the protection from EMF will stop my gut from being leaky. I am sceptical however as my EMF situation did not change when my problems started, it was not, to my knowledge, EMF that caused it so I'm not sure blocking it will fix it.

After trying the magnetico and getting as many free electrons as I can I am fresh out of ideas, so if this post has given you any idea's or clues please post them and I will be very grateful. Any information at all will be a massive help.

Thanks guys, Sean.

Sean

### seanb4 — 2014-01-07T10:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-104960>

@Jude

I have seen doctors but they haven't been much help, I've had various tests such as h.pylori, upper endoscopy, stomach emptying test and they all came back normal.

My weight has remained pretty stable. I recently have been eating ~2000 kcals with 2L of water. I can eat more than that but my stomach symptoms get worse the more I eat. Even though I can feel the food coming back up through my throat I have never really been sick from this, I always manage to keep it down.

It is good advice to try GAPs as I have heard it works well however I have tried this for several months with no changes in my symptoms whatsoever, even whilst doing the introduction diet. I have also tried FODMAPs diet, SIBO diet + supplements, coconut oil fast, 30 day water fast, 7 day dry fast, and many more. None of them help. What convinced me that food intolerances wasn't the main issue was when I did the 30 day fast. When I was drinking just spring water (hot, cold or room temp) I would still get the exact same symptoms, my stomach would bloat, heart palps increase, stomach would feel heavy/full, and I would feel the water returning up my throat. Since it is very unlikely I suddenly have become allergic to water I reasoned it was a mechanical problem in my stomach.

Yeah I'm going to go through the emf threads now and look for a cheap way to check non native EMFs in my room.

### seanb4 — 2014-01-09T14:41:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-105151>

Some additional information.

The heart palpitations seem to be worst about an hour after food. Also certain foods, dark chocolate, seem to also make the palpitations worse.

I belch not much but for a long time after eating and it smells/tastes strongly of the food I ate. Like 2 or 3 burps per hour or something. My stomach feels a tiny bit of relief after belching, slightly less heaviness/pressure/discomfort. Drinking water seems to help induce this...

### Grizz — 2014-01-10T17:34:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-105285>

Sean,

Here is the likely solution to your problems. Someone else reports very similar problems:

> I am now a true iodine believer. I suffered for over 30 years with PCOS, debilitating IBS, chronic allergies and upper respiratory infections,(including six bouts of pneumonia and many more of bronchitis and sinusitis), widespread eczema, fibrocystic breast disease, chronic fatigue and other hypothyroid-like symptoms such as weight gain and depression, asthma, heart arrythmias, and many other minor and chronic issues, finally culminating in breast cancer five years ago, and the removal of one ovary due to severe cysts. At one point, a doctor explained to me that we would be looking for one cause for all the problems, rather than many problems in the same body, which made sense. So needless to say, I went from doctor-to-doctor for years, searching for the underlying cause of all these many problems, with not much more than a pat on the hand and a lecture to watch my diet and get more exercise. In the meantime, my marriage, family and entire life were seriously impacted. I finally stumbled across the concept of iodine supplementation on the web â€” a simple pop-up ad did for me what all those doctors and their fancy degrees could not. Looking at the list of possible symptoms of iodine deficiency was like looking at my own medical history, and I couldnâ€™t start taking it fast enough. After one month on 50mg of iodine, my IBS disappeared, after over 30 years of daily misery. And I mean DISAPPEARED. It was like a miracle for me. My energy picked up significantly. My skin stopped flaking, my hair became shiny again, and I havenâ€™t broken a nail in three months â€” Iâ€™ve never experienced that in my whole life. I have to file them down weekly! My eczema is greatly improved. My one remaining breast is smoother, and less painful around my period. My allergies are also significantly improved, and getting better. My heartbeat is much more regular now. In short, I have seen improvement or complete resolution of nearly ALL of my symptoms, and the improvements continue, after four months now. It truly is amazing. The impact that this small amount of iodine has had on my health cannot be overlooked or overstated. My one continuing hope is that Iâ€™ll lose some weight, and I believe that itâ€™s only a matter of time.
>
> Itâ€™s really a tragedy that most doctors still refuse to believe that iodine can help with these kinds of problems, and, indeed, are afraid to supplement for fear of poisoning their patients. Iâ€™ve never felt better in my whole life, taking the kind of dose that many warn will cause a toxic overdose! Ignorance abounds, and I see so many women who clearly suffer from iodine deficiency, and will likely suffer for the rest of their lives, because of this ignorance. Iâ€™m so glad I found my own answers, and I hope this will help others to do the same!
> [http://www.paleoplan.com/2012/09-10/do-you-need-to-supplement-iodine/](http://www.paleoplan.com/2012/09-10/do-you-need-to-supplement-iodine/)

Note that nearly all of her symptoms are classic iodine deficiency symptoms, as detailed here:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency)

Doctors will never cure iodine deficiency symptoms because they are not trained on iodine which was THE main medicine for over 100 years, and is now censored by the drug companies.

Grizz

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-29T21:44:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-107340>

The whole issue with methylation is very complex. I don't see these genes are being a problem as such, they're just slow and therefore means you need to be eating foods with folate and b12 in them such as liver, kidney and shellfish.

Jack recently said in the free Q&A that the MTHFR gene means you have a poor redox. I would assume this also applies to MTRR as they work on the same pathway.

In terms of supplements, there are differences of opinion as to the most effective brands. Jarrow Formulas used to be very popular however some people found their symptoms returned so had to find alternative brands. So far Enzymatic Therapy and Country Life seem to be pretty good, based on what I've read.

I don't think it's a good idea to take individual supplements in isolation because this can put increased demands on other nutrients. For instance there is a woman on another forum who found her son had the MTHFR defect so he started taking a lot of methylfolate with good results. But then he started getting depression and she thought this was because the folate was increasing the demand for magnesium.

[http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/magnesium.27758/](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/magnesium.27758/)

I am much better during the day now but used to suddenly just get feelings of panic or similar that stirred up my gut. I think the most constant symptom is the globus hystericus, lump in the throat. It doesn't usually work though, it sticks there till it just suddenly goes.

### Penny — 2014-02-18T06:22:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-110954>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=110627)
>
> I forgot to mention this symptom, when I pee it smells VERY strongly of the meat I have just eaten.
>
> I know you need to pee out ammonia (???) after protein but I never remembered it smelling so strong.
>
> Any idea's what this might be?

Hi Sean - you probably have the lovely mthfr 1298 and/or cbs mutation -however, since you mentioned NSAIDS and since one needs zinc and vitamin D to have a non-leaky gut, I would start ramping up

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=110627)
>
> I forgot to mention this symptom, when I pee it smells VERY strongly of the meat I have just eaten.
>
> I know you need to pee out ammonia (???) after protein but I never remembered it smelling so strong.
>
> Any idea's what this might be?

Hi Sean you have ammonia because you don't have enough BH4 to break down the ammonia generated from eating meat - I would eat less meat and more fat - this means you might have have MTHFR 1298 - get a 23andme.com genetics test - it'll open your eyes - in the meantime, to heal your gut take zinc orotate, vitamin D (5000IU) with fat and rosemary - you can also take glutamine and colostrum on an empty stomach when you first wake up - eat chicken liver which has boatloads of folate - take digestive enzymes and bile salts with each meal - whenever you eat meat, chase it with an alkaline veggie - when you get the results of your 23andme test ($99.00), run the raw data through genetic genie.com or mthfrsupport.com and see what enzymes aren't working - you also might want to start taking a trace mineral pill like the one from lef.org or pure encapsulations - you can also eat oysters for the zinc, but orotate is very bioavailable - you can also join the american gut project and for $99.00 they will run the DNA on your gut flora - the guys at american gut advocate lots of plants to feed the happy actinobacteria... I take digestive enzymes and no more GERD... you can't get nutrition from your food if you can't digest it - another happy thing to do is just ingest good flora (probiotics) with the meal and let them digest it... sauerkraut juice - oh, BH4 is dependent upon methylated folate to work (BH2+methyl folate=BH4), so Metafolin by Solgar might be a happy thing for you - you must take b12 first, then take the metafolin - titrate up slowly - the cool thing is, these are cheap fixes - especially the zinc - if zinc orotate hurts your stomach, switch to zinc picolinate - the only place I read how zinc is required for vitamin d to bind is the Linus Pauling site at Oregon State - seems obscure - think of your gut wall like a basket weave - without zinc and vitamin d and methyl groups, the wall doesn't seal... good luck and let us know how you fare Oh, one more thing, BH4 is required for dopamine and serotonin - so if your focus is in the toilet as well as your sleep, that is a giant clue - also, you need serotonin to signal your pituitary to make TSH - another silly reason to just trust TSH for a measurement of thyroid output... To read some wonderful papers on methylation - the best I've read so far, go here: [http://nancymullanmd.com/resources/](http://nancymullanmd.com/resources/) - I hope this helps you - I took ibuprophren and aspirin for years, it'll do a number on your gut - take care Penny

### Penny — 2014-02-18T06:40:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-110955>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=107442)
>
> Sean, you mentioned blood vessels in your stomach, elevated homocysteine is associated with damage to blood vessels too.
>
> [http://circ.ahajournals.org/content/111/19/e289.full](http://circ.ahajournals.org/content/111/19/e289.full)
>
> I've been looking at this a little more as my BF burst a blood vessel in his finger which made it numb, also his mum burst a blood vessel last year and blood spurted everywhere like something out of a horror movie.

Elevated homocysteine is definitely methylation - the quickest way to bring it down is by eating beets or taking TMG (trimethyl glycine) - this at least gets methylation moving - it is also recommended to take phosphatidyl serine/ethalomine/choline complex for the lovely methylation shortcut called BHMT... you probably have a block in those enzymes too - another thing no one seems to chat about is in order to transport b12/folate, you must have enough lithium to do that - b12 eats up lithium - so unless you're supplementing or eating kelp/blue corn tortilla chips, you will seriously crash... because you run out of lithium - the bummer is that lithium competes with iodine so you must take iodine on your skin (lugol's) and ingest the lithium orotate (Concentrace mineral drops also have some lithium in them) - start slow at 1/4 capsule - you can also test the lithium level in your blood or hair - depending on who you believe... this is not the crappy lithium carbonate from Big Pharma in high doses because it doesn't get past the blood brain barrier, this is lithium orotate and the dose is 4.6 mg of elemental lithium per tablet - the stuff is amazing - if you have the COMT gene, it will help that enzyme break down the excitatory neurotransmitters - great for people with bipolar - I think it breaks down estrogen too, but not sure about that - blood vessels have something to do with nitric oxide - I think - which is another place BH4 is used - the guy at heartfixer talks about it at length: [http://www.heartfixer.com/AMRI-Nutrigenomics.htm](http://www.heartfixer.com/AMRI-Nutrigenomics.htm) - don't freak out at the picture, look at it like a giant dominoes game, when one domino falls, so do the rest in line... have fun with all that, I sure have been...  Penny You need the 23andme test for sure

### Penny — 2014-02-18T06:50:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-110957>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=107340)

Jarrow Formulas used to be very popular however some people found their symptoms returned so had to find alternative brands. So far Enzymatic Therapy and Country Life seem to be pretty good, based on what I've read.
>

>
> Have been reading more on it and I definitely fit some of the symptoms such as the pulsating in my stomach, and feeling full off little food... The mortality rate is really high for this one but luckly I am seeing a doctor fairly soon anyway so will mention it to her.

Sean - methyl groups are needed to build the gut wall, and if you don't convert/recycle folate, the wall becomes more susceptible to breach - like a celiac, someone with an autoimmune issue might have - racing heart could be glutamates - bizarrely, folic acid is a string of glutamates, so if you don't convert folic acid, you end up with glutamates -an excitotoxin - this is why folic acid is outlawed in Britain - some people don't convert it so it's tied to dementia - this could cause tachardia -

Magnesium is also great for racing heart beats - you might want to soak your feet/take a hot bath in Epsom Salts - magnesium is used in 300 different reactions in the body - you could also be low in ubiquinol - usually people with methylation issues are low in carnitine/coq 10 - I actually have no idea how to post stuff in this forum, so excuse me if this is the wrong place... Penny

### seanb4 — 2014-02-18T11:13:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stomach-issues-thread-again.9260/post-110966>

Hi Penny, thanks a lot for the detailed responses.

I will maybe get a 23andme test in the future but there are other tests I will get first.

I have tried many things for leaky gut but I don't think it's my main issue as even when I was taking high dose vit D3, Zinc, Eating GAPS diet, taking cholostrum, and probiotics, it made no difference.

I had a vit D test done recently and my levels where good but I know I don't get enough sun light. Am going to post a thread soon about sun lamps. See how good they are.

I have also taken many digestive enzymes and betaine hcl which didn't help. Also interestingly I never experienced burning in my stomach with even the really high doses...

I also had a b12 test done and my levels where at the top of the ref range...

Also for a while I drank beet kravas, and saw no improvement. If elevated homocystine was the issue I should have noticed some change.

I have took many a magnesium tablet and was recently eating 100g of dark chocolate per day, although I had to stop that because it made the heart palpitations worse, probably something in the chocolate and not the magnesium.

I might look into soaking my feet in epsom salts from magnesium and hydration. Anything that circumvents putting things in my stomach is good for me!

# Thread: Allergic Reaction - Help Needed
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2014-01-06T15:09:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104869>

Yesterday I made broiled cod and shrimp for dinner. Shortly after eating, my eyes starting itching and I rubbed them without thinking. Within minutes my eyes were swollen almost shut.

I had a similar reaction a few years ago that I traced back to shrimp. Now shrimp is one of my favorites foods and I have eaten it hundreds of times, only having this reaction twice.

Since I quickly realized what was going on, I knew what to do based on my previous experience. I took 2 benedryl and put an ice pack on my eyes. Last time I hadn't experienced any breathing problems, but to be safe I made sure my wallet, house keys, coat, cell phone were all where I could easily find them if it became necessary. I figured even if I could barely see, I could make it over to my neighbors or call 911.

I laid in bed with the ice pack on my eyes and kept checking to see if the swelling was going down. After about 2 hours, it seemed the swelling wasn't getting any worse. I slept about like usual. When I got up this morning, my left eye was still swollen shut. The right eye was close to normal. I have been putting an ice pack on my left eye and the swelling is slowly going down.

So my question is why did I have this allergic reaction? Why do I have a less than 1% allergic reaction to shrimp? I do take glucosamine/chondroitin daily and don't have a problem with it.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-06T16:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104870>

That is troublesome, Huck. Seafood allergies do seem to be more common in people who take glucosamine/chondroitin, though.

### sooperb — 2014-01-06T16:14:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104871>

Maybe it was something you smeared into your eye without noticing? The fact that one eye has recovered and the other hasn't and maybe the shrimp was just coincidental. As for the glucosamine, hasn't research proven that it's ineffective for joint pain etc? [http://www.nhs.uk/news/2010/09September/Pages/osteoarthritis-supplements-of-no-benefit.aspx](http://www.nhs.uk/news/2010/09September/Pages/osteoarthritis-supplements-of-no-benefit.aspx)

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-06T17:34:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104877>

Just a thought - it might not be the shrimp but the preservatives in the shrimp that you had the reaction too.... did you buy a different brand of shrimp?

### Huck — 2014-01-06T17:47:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104878>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=104877)
>
> Just a thought - it might not be the shrimp but the preservatives in the shrimp that you had the reaction too.... did you buy a different brand of shrimp?

I always worry when I buy frozen shrimp from overseas that it may have thawed and been refrozen. This was Walmart frozen shrimp (Thailand), which I buy occasionally. Usually I buy gulf shrimp. Does frozen shrimp use preservatives?

### Huck — 2014-01-06T17:50:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104880>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=104871)
>
> Maybe it was something you smeared into your eye without noticing? The fact that one eye has recovered and the other hasn't and maybe the shrimp was just coincidental.

That's possible, but my gut says it's the shrimp. I used spices that I use almost daily.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-06T18:00:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104881>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=104878)
>
> I always worry when I buy frozen shrimp from overseas that it may have thawed and been refrozen. This was Walmart frozen shrimp (Thailand), which I buy occasionally. Usually I buy gulf shrimp. Does frozen shrimp use preservatives?

yes sometimes frozen seafood will have preservatives. IIRC there was an Ask Jack question about this - where Jack Replied that the reaction was not to the seafood itself but to the preservatives used in the flash freezing....

add to the fact you don't seem to have issues with gulf shrimp - would indicate to me - its preservatives.

### sooperb — 2014-01-06T18:14:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104882>

Spices? Chilli? If you get that in your eye you know about it, it can transfer even after you've washed your hands.

### Huck — 2014-01-06T18:27:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104883>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=104882)
>
> Spices? Chilli? If you get that in your eye you know about it, it can transfer even after you've washed your hands.

This isn't a burn like you get with chiles. Imagine the worst picture you have ever seen of a boxer's swollen eyes and mine were worse. Last time my left eye ended up black and blue for a few days afterwards and I bet it happens again this time. At least my left eye is about halfway open now,but still hurts a little. I imaginethe swelling damaged some blood vessels.

### Huck — 2014-01-06T18:28:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104884>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=104881)
>
> yes sometimes frozen seafood will have preservatives. IIRC there was an Ask Jack question about this - where Jack Replied that the reaction was not to the seafood itself but to the preservatives used in the flash freezing....
>
> add to the fact you don't seem to have issues with gulf shrimp - would indicate to me - its preservatives.

I threw the bag away, so I will check next time I go to Walmart. I'm also going to email them.

### sooperb — 2014-01-06T19:15:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104886>

Sounds grim Huck, you have enough to contend with!

### Huck — 2014-01-06T19:26:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104887>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=104886)
>
> Sounds grim Huck, you have enough to contend with!

It's just inconvenient. I was reading a good book and want to get back to it, but eyes aren't willing yet.

### caroline — 2014-01-06T22:44:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104904>

I react to shrimp that I buy already cooked from a seafood shop ..... I think they use something to preserve colour and freshness etc. they tell me they don't use anything.......but I am reacting to something for sure. I don't have a problem with frozen shrimp but I always buy the same brand.

good luck sorting it out Huck.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-07T02:09:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104937>

I would guess that it is something sprayed on the shrimp too...quercitin if you have it is useful to help with histamine overload. Hope things are feeling better soon.

### Eugenia — 2014-01-07T04:39:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/allergic-reaction-help-needed.9261/post-104944>

Yeah, I'd say it's something added to the shrimp too. In my case, I get asthma from chicken. But only chicken in Greece, and only chicken that was roasted in a restaurant, and only about 5-10% of the times. It never happened in any other of the 4 countries I lived in, or from bought-chicken cooked at home. So in my case, it's probably a spice mix that some of the Greek restaurateurs are using.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-07T18:41:20+0200

# Thread: The truth about sugar/fructose
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-truth-about-sugar-fructose.9262/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sooperb — 2014-01-06T16:17:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-truth-about-sugar-fructose.9262/post-104872>

I have just watched this, it's made me angry and it's made me cry to think what I have handed to my children  I think watching it should be compulsory for every parent and anyone concerned about their health. Robert Lustig, you're awesome.

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dBnniua6-oM](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dBnniua6-oM)

### cinnamon — 2014-01-06T21:51:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-truth-about-sugar-fructose.9262/post-104898>

Thanks for the link, sooperb!
I was astonished to find that I could somewhat follow those metabolism charts he used.

Having a better understanding of how our bodies metabolize fructose got me thinking about the seasonality of eating. It's actually pretty amazing how it would allow us to eat large quantities of fruit in season (late summer / early autumn) and quickly store fat to help us get through the coming winter. In that context, it's a really good thing! Do you suppose the fructose metabolic processes are even more efficient at the natural time of fruit harvest? I mean, if you have been paying attention to light cycles so your body actually knows what time of year it is...

Of course, for most folks, their bodies have no idea what time of year it is - endless summer in our warm homes and cars. Blue light at all hours, and orange juice all year round. No wonder so many have become so efficient at creating fat!

I know Dr. Kruse has talked about this before and has even said you would be crazy to eat bananas in January (in the Northern Hemisphere). Still, the more times and ways we see it said, the clearer it becomes. At least, it is that way for me.

### caroline — 2014-01-06T23:42:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-truth-about-sugar-fructose.9262/post-104927>

I know I have been craving fruit now ..... It is very explicit - like I should be eating it...

### nicld — 2014-01-07T04:59:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-truth-about-sugar-fructose.9262/post-104945>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104927)
>
> I know I have been craving fruit now ..... It is very explicit - like I should be eating it...

Is it not summer for you now? If it is in season might be ok to have some. Body is probably telling you something.

### sooperb — 2014-01-07T10:14:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-truth-about-sugar-fructose.9262/post-104956>

Dr. Lustig says that fibre is very important, maybe fruit, in season of course, fulfills some of our bodies needs for fibre. Apples and pears have quite tough skins, oranges seem quite high in fibre, berries often have a lot of seeds, raspberries and strawberries for example, I suspect that they would be good for the digestion etc. so maybe that's why we should eat them. If we used to consume 300gs of the stuff and now we don't ............ maybe our bodies are trying to tell us something. I make smoothies from time to time, I often put a whole orange in skin and all and the only fruits I peel are bananas and avocados though I suspect my Vitamix would make very short work of reducing them to an edible state

### caroline — 2014-01-07T10:42:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-truth-about-sugar-fructose.9262/post-104959>

Yes Nicole - it is summer here......but - Dr. K. has suggested we would be better off in OZ being low carb all year because of the donut hole.

# Thread: Hi from the UK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sue-UK — 2014-01-06T19:38:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-104889>

Hi everyone

Just saying hi before I start posting â€¦â€¦ I live in the UK and found the website and forum after searching for more information on leptin â€¦.. Iâ€™ve become addicted to the blogs, and have the book and cookbook.

Health wise I was at rock bottom after the birth of my third child 16 years ago, but have studied complementary therapies and nutrition since then and at 56 am healthier than I was at 40, but I'm aiming for optimal.

Have had a quite a few light bulb moments from the blogs â€¦.

Sue

### sooperb — 2014-01-06T21:01:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-104895>

Hi Sue Uk from another Sue, also in the UK  I found this place somehow, can't remember quite how. It's a mine of information and sometimes I feel as though I'll never get to read it all. One things for sure, it will be making a difference to me and mine, bit by bit.

### caroline — 2014-01-06T22:27:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-104903>

Hi Sue ... and Welcome!

Please think about starting a journal in the journal section so we can follow your journey to optimal!

What have you done so far in your journey?

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-07T10:35:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-104957>

Thanks Sooperb and Caroline

Caroline Iâ€™ve been doing the BAB and not eating after 7 p.m, and can usually skip lunch. Keep some tinned fish and the makings of a BPC at work so am never tempted to go and buy a sandwich or cake if I do get hungry.

Doing cold showers, and packs a few times, did a few ice baths but they didnâ€™t feel right (right idea at the wrong time IYKWIM), so will work back up to them.

We live a couple of miles away from town, EMF situation not too bad, but how did I let the wifi slip past me?  (Studied Beckerâ€™s work and EMFs generally 15 years ago as part of a subtle energy course, the microwave disappeared immediately). Have tightened up on wifi use and darkness/temperature of bedroom at night.

Sleep is much better, I used to kid myself that if I couldnâ€™t sleep it was OK to go and study things I love, which was better for my stress levels than listening to DH snoring (he could snore for Britain,  and he was developing sleep apnea). It wasn't his snoring that woke me up, but it didn't help me trying to get back to sleep. Iâ€™m not sure what change has done what, but heâ€™s also doing the BAB, 7 p.m. cut off for eating (especially carbs), swapped sweet potato for potato and cut down on the grains, and in the last couple of months has lost 30 lb without trying, and the snoring and apnea is soooooooo much better. And so is my sleep

### caroline — 2014-01-07T10:39:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-104958>

awesome Sue ..... tell us more - we all need to hear success stories.....

### sooperb — 2014-01-07T16:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-104982>

30 lb, I am envious!

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-07T20:42:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-104997>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=104982)
>
> 30 lb, I am envious!

Tell me about it â€“ Iâ€™m the one doing the cold packs and showers and heâ€™s the one losing the weight

But I think my body shape is changing a bit, skin is warmer, I cope with the cold better, and my hair seems to be growing out less grey. Iâ€™m getting lanulae on my fingernails, before I only had them on my thumbs, but they are now on my index and middle fingers, particularly on the right hand. I havenâ€™t weighed myself for a while, not tempted to at the moment as I donâ€™t want to be put off if the scale hasnâ€™t shifted. Think what my body is doing is putting first what matters mostâ€¦and itâ€™s not yet weight loss â€¦.

### sooperb — 2014-01-08T00:40:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-105030>

I'm interested in your lunula observations, I only have them visible on my thumbs, what does this suggest? When hubby and I first cut carbs he lost over a stone and I lost almost a stone. Sadly this hasn't stopped off and I feel as though I'm swelling round my middle instead of shrinking

### caroline — 2014-01-08T02:04:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-105033>

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-08T10:06:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-the-uk.9265/post-105050>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=105030)
>
> I'm interested in your lunula observations, I only have them visible on my thumbs, what does this suggest? When hubby and I first cut carbs he lost over a stone and I lost almost a stone. Sadly this hasn't stopped off and I feel as though I'm swelling round my middle instead of shrinking

It apparently points to possible malnutrition and/or anaemia. I had a ridge on my right ring finger that also pointed to it which has now gone too. I was interested in the why. Seems mad because Iâ€™ve been eating better for several years, but weâ€™re not what we eat we are what we assimilate.  Iodine has been my light bulb moment, because of its role in making stomach acid. In my case not enough iodine intake and a lot of tea drinking causing losses! Hence why my previous grain free tries werenâ€™t successful , it was less about inflammation (although that is a factor), and more about iodine deficiency and fluoride toxicity.

Iâ€™ve also seen lack of lanula related to low thyroid, but Iâ€™m not convinced yet as Iâ€™ve not found that on a referenced or medical source (and if someone is malnourished, then low thyroid could be the expected effect not a cause?).

# Thread: Not sure where to post
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-post.9266/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-01-06T20:46:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-where-to-post.9266/post-104894>

....but this might be a good spot since it is about trying to Heal the Hormones (if not right, let me know and I can move it  .

Feeling a bit sad and frustrated today.

I am still trying to make sense of my last set of labs and in reading some more stuff, I KNOW that I had adrenal fatigue and a cortisol crash a number of years ago. I was reading an article called Steroidal Hormone Balance “The 11th FHC” from William L Wolcott, Winthrop, January 2009 and it said that “Commonly, will recognize that they have a lot of stress in their lives, and they’re juggling a lot of balls in the air, but they feel great and think they’re handling the stress well – plus they have high energy and a sense of well-being )all thanks to the high output of the adrenal hormones).” They also state it is kinda like pressing the gas pedal down all the way and never letting up. That was me, I was going back to college for an engineering degree due to a stressful work situation, working full time at night, intense workouts to lose weight, diagnosed with celiac and major stress with the youngest DD. I knew it when it crashed yet no doctor recognized it and I did not have the knowledge to help myself.

I will be sending my hormone doc my new tests results and will wait to see what he says. I need to relisten to the Hormone Boot camp this week too and hope that Dr Tim rolls out his new plan for the new year. Insurance year just restarted so I have funds that I need to use on me soon and really need help figuring out how all these labs are related cause it’s making my head hurt.

**My question is**, if all of my other hormones are off to keep cortisol all happy and balanced (which tests results indicate cortisol levels are happy right now) then how do I fix the cortisol issue so the other hormones don’t suffer and can get balanced again? I am already eating epi-paleo, reduced EMF's, wearing blue blockers and getting TONS of cold CT.

I am sure feeling this way is not helped by the fact that I sat out in the freezing cold yesterday (which was not too bad) and watch my football team lose in the playoffs, got home way past bedtime so a bit tired, ate a bit suboptimal yesterday and am still frustrated that I have not been able to lose any weight. The HCG I have is calling me but with the hormones still being so far whacked, any loss would not stick and would be a waste of my time.

Plus the news said this morning that today is the most depressing day of the year and so far, between the car not starting, the #stupidcold temps and work issues it turning out that way.

My New Year’s resolution will be to figure out these hormones and to try and keep the negative body image thought OUT of the head.

Speaking of steroid hormones, in healthy person, in the drop of blood, if you measure the most significant hormones,
by weight,
DHEAs participation is 95%

Your first order of business is to make sure that you have good DHEAs levels.
All other come latter.

....
Women 275-400 μg/dL

Keep popping DHEA pills until you get there.

Use this test at LabCorp to do the measurement;
Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

..
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio)
........

### nicld — 2014-01-07T05:06:46+0200

That is actually one level that showed very good levels, 197 mcg/dL . I do supplement with a little cream, no pills. The pills for me turned into testosterone and had left me with horrible cystic acne. The cream has worked much better and no more acne.

# Thread: Want to help your kids........teach them about harmonics in everything.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 52
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-06T22:45:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-104905>

I am not going to elaborate but lets just say something clicked at the Louvre.............

Most people associate harmonics with music........

I dont.

It is about life. All of it.

After I saw DaVinci's St. John the Baptiste live for my first time it struck me.

Listen to the key four notes in Pink Floyd's Part of Shine on your crazy diamond. 4 notes struck together........just do something you did not expect.

This was the last of DaVinci's works I had seen for myself and it completed my bucket list I wrote when I was 15.

On the flight home I wrote a new list................It's a list of melodies of harmonics. They are everywhere and we just do not see them. I saw them in ceilings. buildings, walls, and stone sculpture. I now see it in my own field. Blind once, but now I have new vision where the darkness roamed before.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-06T22:47:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-104906>

same four notes in PF song above.........pattern the human heart beat. I did the acoustics check today.........amazing. Different octaves but spot on. Never saw it until today.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-06T22:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-104908>

from any religion............

For kids today i think this is a clue to make them more living, making art is not done today. I see all of my friends feed them with digital copies of the world, not the wonders of art, music and other great things we have done. School in sweden seems to stop giving free classes in music and that is sad for me.

We have been with frequencies from day one i think, perhaps the piano is the quantum computer we try so hard to create today, just because we once started to think in binary...

### Inger — 2014-01-07T09:29:14+0200

Hypnotic is the word Sally!!! I love it!!! I always get hypnotized from great music with great harmony.. I just listened to the PF song Jack mentions above, and it is very beautiful... I did not know it.

I have one brother that is amazing in creating music with great harmony.. when he let the spirit in (lately he has been very weird with his music as he have wanted to take the soul away from it as it is unchristian to make music with soul so that was almost a pain listening to him singing and playing. Like my mom and dad, they are so fundamentally converted to their believes that soul is sinful so their music is not enjoyable. But as my dad was away and he was with us my bro got the courage and played with all his soul again) -> once he played a solo song on piano and I got... kind of paralyzed! We also played instruments together, violin, mandolin, guitar, piano... we just changed them up when we got tired of playing one instrument and everyone improvised, I had never played mandolin before but it was so much fun! And I promise, I am not good at it at all! But it was not about it being perfect... it was about creating harmonies even if playing only a few different tones

I loved this post of Jacks. Now I need to contemplate on this further.....

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-07T11:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-104968>

Jack's posts reminded me of when I learned that some places of worship are designed specifically for frequencies of mind, light and sound, from the dimensions of the building, to the flutings, stained glass windows and columns and carvings â€¦.. Goethe apparently described architecture as â€œfrozen music.â€￾

When I was at my most stressed a few years ago, one of my favourite things on holiday was to find a quiet church, no music, as little noise as possible, and let the architecture sing to me â€¦.

I love how I can find clues about it in vocabulary about church architecture â€“ I can be in-spire-d, en-trance, altar (altered states of consciousness?) by frozen music!!

It's interesting you should recognize such a harmonious pattern in the song and painting, as the person for whom it was written was probably the most mentally disharmonious, unfortunately.

I love the Louvre. I've been a few times and it just never gets old. I also like the Musee D'Orsay. Not a huge Picasso fan but his museum was still a cool experience.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T04:23:23+0200

So the painting of John the Baptist is a depiction of the heart's sinus rhythm? Shoulder, head, elbow, & finger?

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-02-17T01:50:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-110683>

Pink Floyd has always been one of my most loved bands. I came to love and appreciate them while exploring facets of reality as a young teen. As I type this, I have the song playing while my daughter uses her ear to find the notes on the piano. When I first learned to play music, my band instructor didn't take the time to ensure that each of us was learning how to read music properly. I learned to play by ear for my first year of music, and created my own way of reading music that was definitely not the correct way! I learned that the hard way when we moved to Vegas my 7th grade year and I had to audition for chair position. Out of 24 flautists, I was dead last because I could not read the instructor's hand-written music since I'd learned to read in such an ass-backwards fashion. Eventually, I passed each one of those flute players and advanced to the higher band, once I learned to read properly. But playing by ear has always been the way I play music best…along with improv. Harmonics have always been one of my favorite things to play around with. I can see how the visual harmonics along with the musical harmonics create a perfect symphony. Love this!

### Jason Lutan — 2015-07-03T21:34:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-170594>

Classical music in general is suppose to be on point with heart beats, hence why its so powerful for healing. People love rocking in rocking chairs, espically when feeding babies, it flows with our heart beat.

Study: Group put 3 plants in different rooms...One listened to classical music...other one was rock music...other one was no music.

Rock music, plant died.
No music...plant grew normal
Classical...plant doubled their growth rate..

)))

### optimizedprimate — 2015-07-06T01:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-171030>

A while back there was a lot about the use for baroque music 60-70bpm for accelerated learning. Although I never really got much out of that type of music outside of enjoyment. Gregorian chants and Ayahuasca ceremony chants always transported me to different brain states.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-06T03:25:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-171045>

**From FB tying music to magnetism:** "A teaching lesson about numbers tied to magnetism: Numbers explain physics better via colors to scientists, at least that is their idea now. Colors in all things are all about frequency of light they reflect and absorb. Colors for me, and hopefully for you, is not about what we see, it is about what they are fundamentally. they are unique packets of vibrational energy in the form of waves/particles. Consider red light: has a frequency of 436,803,079,680,000 oscillations per second. That equates to the same energy in a high C note in music. That note has an oscillation frequency of 4,096 oscillations. per second. Xrays, microwaves, cosmic and gamma rays all have these same type of numbers and colors tied to them but when you read these numbers they make no sense to you. However, the color has a qualia you do understand fully. You know what red and purple means to you. You know what frequency/color/numbers means to me? Magnetism, one part of the electromagnetic wave of light, has the HIGHEST oscillatory rate we know of in nature. Few people know this fact. It is 18,446,744,073,709,551,616 oscillations per second.........It is hard for the mind to grasp that many things can be simultaneouslyvibrating that many times in a second, but guess what.......last week on the cruise while everyone else did there own thing in Playa Del Carmen I made sure I took a person who needed to feel that vibration to a cent. I went into the belly of the crater where those oscillations have existed and been echoing in rain water for 65 million yrs.........Patrick asked me how I expended all the energy I did every day and night on the cruise.......and not get tired. I remained ketotic and at once and day and drank a ton of water........why? Because that water assimilated all those oscillations from the crater to renew me.........I knew I could give a lot because I was going to get a lot more in my time in the crater. For those of you bad at math: That number above is 18 quintillion, 446 quadrillion, 744 trillion, 73 billion, 709 million, 551 thousand, 6 hundred, and 16 oscillations per second of vibrational energy. That my dear is physics 101 of water. Remember water is a magnetic dipole that reacts to those magnetic moments in life. Get some.

### drezy — 2017-03-27T19:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-211230>

Don't forget that kids need to be educated in "bad" harmonics as well.

Jack's warning of nnEMF extends all the way to anything digital. Digital signals, on and off square waves, don't really exists outside of theory.

I spent a decade and a half working with high speed signals for computer ports and cell tower chips. It was a favorite pastime to pick on fresh engineers and ask them to explain why their "square" wave was broken on the scope when it wasn't broken but reality just doesn't match textbook theory.

Jack ain't shitting about nnEMF. What this shows is what actual "digital" signals always looks like. Your 1 Ghz port/chip isn't just spewing out 1GHz signals. It's got harmonics at 3x,5x,7x,9x,... frequencies.
For math heads:

For music heads:

When you just want C2 also force yourself to play G3 (1/3 the volume) E4-ish(1/5 volume) A#4-ish(1/7 volume) D5ish (1/9 volume)... Yuck right?

Bottom line for me:
Think you and your little darlings are just exposed to 2.4Ghz with wifi? Nope, with harmonics it's 2.4G, 7.2G, 12G.... It's like expecting silk when you reach out but instead putting your hand into 25 year old shag carpet from the back of a weirdo's van. Total hand sanitizer moment.

Consequently, one big challenge in producing high speed digital semiconductors is getting(designing in) enough electron flow and getting enough grounding... Gee, Sound familiar ? It sure does to me. I've tracked down this electron & ground issue on processor memory interfaces and high speed graphics ports.

The power grid also has to account for digital devices

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I'm glad you're out there pushing. I'm only able to influence my own family's journey at this point and have noticed benefits across the boards. Momma, Poppa, and baby bear now all CT!

FWIW I spent 7+ years working under "flickering" light and 6 feet from a large pice of equipment that could supply high end network semiconductors with large amounts of "instantaneous" power. There sure were harmonics and numerous other effects there.

About 10 years later, after having left, I attended a party with the old crew and was told "You haven't aged at all in 10 years, what are you doing?" I didn't have all the pieces then but I'm filling in the picture more now thanks to this site.

**All** basic electronics relies on storing(or inadvertently leaking) energy into capacitance(chemical storage) and inductance (magnetic field storage or leakage \*read nnEMF\*). Fiber optics can be raised as an exception, but the optic line always terminates at an electrical transceiver so same issues.

The wave form in the picture above is the key.......that picture is generated by a semiconductor that does not use true optical signaling. It is digital electronic and makes very unusally shaped wave forms. Those shapes mean that electron flow is inconsistent and lead to energy (electric and magnetic leak) leakage..........when energy is leached this effects the wave form and the optical power that is possible.

Biologic semiconduction is all about photoelectric semiconduction. The sun has to exicte those electrons and because the sun's light is so chaotic (see the Fraunhoffer Lines) electrons excitation is the first way to make the optical information in them less chaotic. The most important part of the Rosetta stone cipher of life is found in the DNA code. Why is it that DNA only codes for proteins and very little else?

My belief is simple........the DNA code is all about our semiconductor fab plant. Our nucleic acids specialize in in making non linear optical chips to make more sense of light using non linear optics.

[In 1961, Franken](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?title=Generation%20of%20optical%20harmonics&author=Franken,+P.A.&author=Weinreich,+G.&author=Peters,+C.W.&author=Hill,+A.E.&publication_year=1961&journal=Phys.+Rev.+Lett.&volume=7&pages=118&doi=10.1103/PhysRevLett.7.118) and his co-workers observed the phenomenon of second-harmonic generation (SHG) in quartz. This was seminal to understanding how optics differs from electronics. Quartz also has a very special ability: It allows passage of UV light. And it turns that UV light into very special wave forms to make sense of the information in light. Quartz is a crystal that participates in non linear optics because of its own crystalline lattice. I think biologic proteins all have NLO factors that make them highly conserved by evolution in DNA by magnetic photolithography. Second-harmonic generation is but one aspect of non linear optics and I did a webinar several months ago. Our brain is made out of liquid crystaline NLO materials that are all carbon based and hydrated that also participate in non linear optical changes. Today nonlinear optics has attracted much attention in electric engineering and has developed into one of the key branches of modern optics.

Without exception, nonlinear optical (NLO) crystals, have become the most important optoelectronic functional material types in semiconductor designs that human engineers know about. Those same himans have no idea how to really use these characteristics. If you observe thigns life cand do as I do you see it clearly. In electronics, NLO's are now widely used in laser wavelength-converting technology.

Our brain has been doing this to wake life up now for 600 million years. **Non linear optical crystals allow for a physicochemical phenomenon when they interact with light.** These phenomena manifests often by leeching onto the non linear crystal materials as the carrier. This is why DNA codes for them. They decipher the signals in sunlight further photo-electrically. This means light programs the NLO to do things we can hardly believe............ In turn, evolution has usually found a practical application of NLO's via evolutionary history. Life, cells, and tissues have continued to benefiting from the attached property that the sun has given our biologic NLO's. This is critical to a quantum evolution. That attached property in biology is pretty varied..........across tissues. This is how animals evolved wakefulness from the unconscious sleep state. Once awake, more energy was made from our hydrogen fuel cells called mitochondria, and that phontonic light energy was used with more evolved NLO crystals cells innovated to provide us the ability to create how sensations acquire characteristics, such as colors, pitch, and tastes.

# Thread: Light vs. Temperature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2014-01-06T23:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104930>

We talk a lot here about the importance of light mismatches. But I am wondering about temperature mismatches as well.

We all know the role of cold. But could it be that in order to really benefit from it in a true mismatch-avoiding way, one should move to where it is cold rather than recreate cold?

The reason I ask is that right about now, there are places in and around the 37th parallel that are balmy and producing tons of citrus. In fact, in California, winter is the season for citrus. And if you were a premodern human who stumbled upon a citrus tree, you'd eat your fill.

And we know that the earth has been much warmer in past ages, so much so that vineyards thrived in England at one time. And had you lived back then, I'm assuming it would not have been a mismatch to eat grapes although it is now. And it's also seems the only reason it is a mismatch to eat grapes in England now is that we have a changed climate not changed seasonal light periods.

I live near Turkey and right now Turkey is spitting out mandarins and all sorts of citrus. Spain has seaaonal pomegranates. I'm seeing seasonal kiwis as well. Lots of vitamin C for the taking.

So I am wondering if we need to be more mindful about what temperature is signaling to us while still minding light cycles. And if you live in a warmer, northern, non-tropical place where climate would allow you to eat fruit during shorter light periods, should you?

### prAna303 — 2014-01-07T00:00:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104931>

> And if you live in a warmer, northern, non-tropical place where climate would allow you to eat fruit during shorter light periods, should you?

I live in Sweden... From my point of view, nothing would grow even if the winters would be warm... The sun do not provide enough power to make that kind of life explode during the period from october to june. We have a short span here with plants that gives us fruits, three months i think and after that they get rotten.

So i would say no, and because how the world look like today, NO!

Just my way of thinking.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-07T00:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104932>

Great question Jason... Love it. I think the answer is going to depend on your particular health issues. If you're healthy no AI issues etc, and you had access to seasonal fruits that grow in your region - why not?
but I think for me the answer is no, at least given my current situation. That doesn't mean once I've fixed myself the answer won't become yes.

I associate ruby red grapefruits with Jan/Feb. and when I was sick this weekend I asked DH to get pick one up for me, b/c it was about the only thing other than gelatin, and sweet potatoes that I could eat. so I ate it. I really want another now. I've been very good at eating only fruit in season... I will say that when "pomegranates" were in season at the store I ate the greadliy - b/c to me they are a winter fruit - we'd get them all the time when I lived in CA. probably not ideal....

### TheKid — 2014-01-07T00:32:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104934>

I think it's important to think in both directions. We tend to give a lot of consideration to the consequences of eating XYZ out of season.

But maybe we should be also as careful about the consequences of NOT eating PDQ when IN season.

And we should remember that the ability for flora to thrive is more a function of temperature than light. Just look at all the micro climates created by the Gulf Stream from France to even north of England

### nicld — 2014-01-07T04:34:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104943>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=104931)
>
> I live in Sweden... From my point of view, nothing would grow even if the winters would be warm... The sun do not provide enough power to make that kind of life explode during the period from october to june. We have a short span here with plants that gives us fruits, three months i think and after that they get rotten.
>
> So i would say no, and because how the world look like today, NO!
>
> Just my way of thinking.

Nothing would grow here in the winter either. I have been reading a book on the history of man and I am wondering if when man was hunter gatherers how far they would migrate to warmer climate where food would be more available. Since we no longer migrate for food, I am still trying to figure out this whole seasonal eating and how far my range is. Pineapple, kiwis and bananas would NEVER grow where I live so I am thinking that they should never be part of my diet in the winter and maybe not even in the summer.

### Inger — 2014-01-07T08:48:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104949>

I live close to Peter and I think... he nails it. The world we live in today - impossible to escape the man made EMF, carbs are not our first choice. At least not if we are not healthy. I am skeptical to cultivated carbs anyways. Yes, we have apples in the garden. Do I eat them in season? No.. because they do me not good. Berries I can eat as much as I want, in season. I have had wild frozen local berries in winter too and I did not felt any bad effects.. but they are very concentrated and very little carbs. Almost all fruits we can buy are heavily cultivated for sweetness and to be bigger... and are having way more fructose than the wild fruits. I am a fan of wild food. They make me feel the best. They make me THRIVE

So for me, even cultivated fruits when in season are practically out. My own N=1

I do not even eat much cultivated veggies. I prefer wild greens and herbs. They have a different effect on me. They too makes me thrive

### Inger — 2014-01-07T08:57:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104950>

I think it is kind of dangerous to "cheat" with fruits and carbs because you easily get "addicted" to them. I was so addicted to fruits I might never have been able to quit them if I had not run out of money. It is like I did not realize they hurt me.. I killed two of my teeth too because of that... but it did not make me stop. The thing is, everyone says they are so healthy.. and they taste, oh so delicious. Yeah. But oh so dangerous in today's world. ;-)

If you run naked all day long in the tropics, I guess they might not hurt you

I guess it is about context too. Only the fact that we use internet makes us pretty un suited for fruit consumption ;-) IMHO

### Destiny — 2014-01-07T12:54:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104973>

I don't eat fruit ... period. When I stopped, it was my turning point. One piece here and there ... not worth it for me.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T15:54:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104977>

Your question shows your hand. What do I mean by this?......you are showing us your perspective by asking this because this is how you are perceiving the environment you're in. ### TheKid — 2014-01-07T16:53:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104986>

I brought this up more from a theoretical point than from a practical one. I personally am not touching fruit. I got problems, big ones, that I am only beginning to unwind. So really this question is more me just looking out at the world and wondering about why we see some of the things we do. And having lived in California, I remember what came in season there and when; and it got me thinking about the various aspects of our human biology that are still not well understood by the masses.

What I was hoping Jack, or someone else, might clarify in a little more depth, is the relative importance of temperature (all temps, not just cold), in signalling to humans vs. light; and how they work together or, possibly work against one another. Because as I noted, we have the Medieval Warm Period, when grapes apparently flourished in Britain. So what do we make of the changes in climate that come and go versus the changes in light periods, which remain consistent?

We have talked before about how the light period leads to literal energetic differences in fruits/veggies that grow in certain seasons. And Jack has used the example of eating a banana in Canada being a complete energetic mismatch. I assume using the same reasoning, an orange in Louisville, KY would be a mismatch as well, because it's now winter. But hold on a second. Louisville is essentially at the same latitude as Palo Alto, CA, where, right now, oranges are fully in season.

Or you might think about New Orleans. I remember being there and seeing banana trees. And apparently, bananas can be in season into like December. I wouldn't eat on in Ohio. But do you eat one if you live in New Orleans and are otherwise healthy?

So like I said, I just want to put the light/temp picture together.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T22:08:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-105008>

Life is all about charge separation. In photosynthesis we use sunlight to separated charges. In animals we use hydrophillic semiconductors to do the same thing. What is the fluid of charge separation? Water. Water next to a hydrophilic semiconductor acts like a N type semiconductor. This makes the water adjacent next to the semiconductor negative. The further you go away from it the charge is positive. What helps energize this simple battery? More sunlight.........its energizes can alter that battery function positively or negatively. In the zone next to the semiconductor Becker found something called the DC current. This is a negatively charged current. He had no idea where it came from. Pauling, Ling, and Szent Georgyi did. It came from the separation of water into hydrogen oxygen and electrons. In the exclusion zone of water physicists like Mae Wan Ho have showed electrons carry the charge. It also turns out these electrons reside in oxygen molecules in the water. They are usually distributed throughout the water lattice adjacent to the hydrophilic semiconductor. The number of oxygen atoms dictates the DC current. The larger oxygen content the larger the electron density. This is why cold water water carries more Oxygen. It is more electron dense because cold water has more dissolved o2 in it........This is at the core of why CT is powerful. It allows for more electrons to be dissolved in the major fluid of your cellular matrix to deliver more energy by liberation of these electrons when charge separation happens....................This is where temperature sculpts. Physics.......and physical chemistry showing you why chemistry and biology left quite a few lessons out of their bags of tricks. Water chemistry is the biggy they forgot.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T22:11:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-105009>

axons are gels who are extremely hydrophilic. It should be no surprise why Becker's DC current is found above it and below the myelin layer in neurons if you paying attention to the painting I am painting for you slowly...........Out of this tube.........comes protonated water jets.......that can be used to do many many things.......that biology is clueless too.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T22:12:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-105010>

protonicity is a function of the DC current. The DC current is used in regeneration of all mammalian tissues. Protonicity is big inside mitochondria.

### Marie — 2014-01-07T05:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs-yah-they-work.9273/post-104947>

For the last year I've been on a Fish Egg and Oyster bender. I admit it, I LOVE them and was eating about 2-3 TBS a day of fish eggs and at least a LARGE oyster a day.

I just got some labs back and have an O6:O3 of 1.7:1 and DHA of 6.8% and EPA of 3.2%.

So if you are looking to boost your O3s, Ranch99 is the BOMB!

### Inger — 2014-01-07T09:01:40+0200

### Linz — 2014-01-09T21:21:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-eggs-yah-they-work.9273/post-105208>

Healthman,

Were you looking at the fresh ones (usually frozen) on the fish counter? The little jars of fish eggs are usually full of additives but I don't think the fresh herring roe are unless they perhaps spray them with sulphur dioxide (I think) like prawns so they don't discolour. I have never asked about this.

### JanSz — 2014-01-21T02:10:33+0200

> [Marie said:](/goto/post?id=106367)
>
> My understanding is that 4:1 to 1:1 (O6:O3) is healthy with 2:1 being "likely" for paleolithic peoples.

My understanding is that (O6:O3) ratio is not fully defined and as consequence one is able to manipulate it with supplements.
Actually, most doctors do not have understanding of this topic, advice their patients to eat fatty acids supplements but do not follow that advice with proper testing.
Oil supplements industry make money on this misunderstanding and is not about to clear things out.
Testing industry developed tests that check the O6/O3 ratios only (without going into further details)(so as not to rock the money making boat).

Everybody make money on sick people, it is much harder making money on healthy people.

O6/O3 ratios are likely to be acceptable when person eats only natural food
AND
does not use any Omega3 or Omega6 supplementation (krill, fish oils, cod liver oil) or oils containing O3 and/or O6, such as sunflower, grapefruit seeds, hemp seeds, flax seeds or similar.

\-----------------------
Omega3 is a sum total of ALA + EPA + DPA + DHA
Omega6 is a sum total of LA + GLA + DGLA + AA + DTA

Not only O6/O3 ratio should be at the right level but also the other 9 individual fats should be at proper level.

Overall the problem is to align properly all 10 variables, not only the one (O6/O3).

////

# Thread: How cold is it?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2014-01-07T10:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-104961>

How are all you guys in the U.S. doing? How cold is it?

Are you more cold adapted? Are you even slightly enjoying it? apart from the inconvenience....

It looks frightful on our news casts here ......

Are you using this extreme cold to explore your inner polar bear? How are folks coping who aren't cold adapted?

Are you sleeping more? better? deeper?

Are you eating slightly different? do you crave more fat?

What changes in yourself are you noticing?

### caroline — 2014-01-07T10:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-104962>

pictures anyone? you know how I love pictures.........

### Destiny — 2014-01-07T12:47:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-104972>

It is -15F with a windchill here and 5F without. For some reason, I don't mind. I used to hate this kind of weather back in Canada. Maybe it is that I had a long break from it ?

Also I am more cold adapted than ever for sure. Since my thyroid improved, I don't feel as cold as I used to. I feel like a little furnace that is humming along!

### caroline — 2014-01-08T02:29:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-105037>

awesome Destiny! Do you find yourself needing more food?

### Destiny — 2014-01-08T14:09:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-105061>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105037)
>
> awesome Destiny! Do you find yourself needing more food?

Interestingly enough, I don't. I used to eat much much more compare to now. I never felt satisfied and was quite hungry all the time. I guess my body was not getting enough quality in nutrition so was asking for more in quantities of food.

Now, I am eating much less and I am not hungry. I also feel really good. I think it is really the timing and composition of the food that keeps me satisfied and makes a difference for me.

### Inger — 2014-01-08T14:14:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-105062>

I am not in the USA but I like the cold too  we have above freezing here now since weeks! This is Finland in January. crazy! But water in the river is around + 3 degree C so it sure is refreshing. I do 2-3 minute dips and I love it. I do wait for the snow though! But I am not complaining, I like to see the earth and grass too
and I feel great! (just my mind took a dive around the holidays :-o but on the surface again and I can see the sunlight even if rainy days lately)

### nicld — 2014-01-09T03:48:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-105127>

Uhmmm -50F windchill on Monday. I like the cold but that was too much, not enjoying it, I have had to wear a jacket the past week  Today it was -9 F and a bunch of us thought it was not too bad out but that was with Monday still etched in our face. This weekend we are getting a heat wave 35F. I will be out in shorts getting some awesome grounding cause man do I need it.

I am handling it much better this year thanks to CT, I just know it.

For me, the one thing the cold has done was kill the appetite. Most days when it has been cold I have been eating just 2 meals a day.

I will try to post some pictures from the football game where the cheerleaders were wearing pants.

### caroline — 2014-01-09T05:18:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-105129>

That can't be good .... Cheerleaders wearing pants ... Lol!

### caroline — 2014-01-09T05:20:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-cold-is-it.9274/post-105130>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=105061)
>
> Interestingly enough, I don't. I used to eat much much more compare to now. I never felt satisfied and was quite hungry all the time. I guess my body was not getting enough quality in nutrition so was asking for more in quantities of food.
>
> Now, I am eating much less and I am not hungry. I also feel really good. I think it is really the timing and composition of the food that keeps me satisfied and makes a difference for me.
