# Thread: Pregnancy nausea!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CoolSwede — 2014-01-07T12:05:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-104969>

I talked to my GF the other day about pregnancies (we do not have any children yet, but plan to in the future) and she told me about a friend of hers who were very nauseated during the first 3 months of her pregnancy. That got me thinking:

\-Are humans the only mammals to get pregnancy nausea?
\-Why do we get it?
\-What are the causes? Remedies?
\-Isn't it counterintuitive that a mother throws up food when she is in most need of it?!

What are your experiences, ladies? Did you manage to eat high-fat/paleo/epi-paleo during your pregnancies of did you fall of the wagon due to nausea?

Thanks!

### fitness@home — 2014-01-07T15:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-104976>

Three preganacies.

1 - Tiny bit of nausea the first couple of months. Excitement over first preganacy helped me overlook it (Gained 30 lbs)

2 - OMG...very sick for 6.5 months with extreme nausea. Couldn't stand the smell of coffee or even something a simple as hearing/seeing bacon sizzle. (Gained 30 lbs the last 2 months). three years later during procedure for tubal ligation, surgeon told my Mom that I had "significant scarring" on my gall bladder...always wondered if this preganancy's horrible nausea was from gall bladder issues. BTW, this child came quickly, latched right on to my breast after birth, and was _extremely_ healthy!

3 - No nausea but did have "oofs" (tiny burps) the entire pregancy. After pregancy #2 this one was a piece of cake (gained 30 lbs).

Eating crackers and light things like soup helped. Both of these have a saltiness to thiem and for some reason, salt helps my tummy settle down when upset. I stayed away from any prescriptions and just toughed it out.

Another thing I noted was it was better for me to take the prenatal vitamins at midday, with a meal. Mornings I could not handle them.

### CoolSwede — 2014-01-08T13:44:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105057>

Thanks Fitness for your reply. It still puzzles me why many (most?) pregnant women suffer from nausea. I just seems so...counterintuitive...

Edit: You mention salt. Could salt deficiency be one piece of the puzzle?!

### fitness@home — 2014-01-08T15:49:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105066>

Increases or fluctuations in hormones? They are certainly all over the place during pregnancy.

I remember salivating much more, which for me is always related to nausea. There was also increased sensitivity to smells of foods. Things I used to love were no longer appealing.

The morning sickness likely led to dehydration in my case. Maybe that's why I craved the salt.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-08T16:30:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105069>

I remember Jack saying it was something to do with an upside down PG:E2 ratio. I've also read it's associated with elevated leptin. [http://beyondmorningsickness.blogspot.co.uk/2011/03/hyperemesis-gravidarum-related-to.html](http://beyondmorningsickness.blogspot.co.uk/2011/03/hyperemesis-gravidarum-related-to.html)

### JanSz — 2014-01-08T16:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105070>

[http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/...oung-male-I-call-bullshit&p=218649#post218649](http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/showthread.php?23765-What-s-a-healthy-normal-testosterone-level-for-a-young-male-I-call-bullshit&p=218649#post218649)

> [Hugh\_E said:](/goto/post?id=0)
>
> Regarding low testosterone levels and unusual body shapes for people in the first world, I've been trying to draw people's attention to something that probably goes a long way towards explaining it: the medical use of artificial hormones during pregnancy. Many hormone-based treatments currently and historically being given to pregnant women would shut down testosterone production in an adult man if he were to be given the same. Most of these treatments tend to be given during "problem" pregnancies, where the mother's thought to be at risk of having a miscarriage or going into premature labour. This practice started shortly after WW2 with an artificial estrogen called DES. DES was pulled from the market in the 1970s, however it was replaced with progestins, which are a second type of female hormone (and, as is the case with estrogens, can suppress testosterone production in an adult man at doses below those commonly used for medical treatment in women). In high enough doses, corticosteroids can suppress testosterone production too.
>
> You might think, why is testosterone production important in an unborn baby?
>
> Although we have a sex-determining chromosome (the Y chromosome), it only has a few dozen functional genes on it, and basically all it does is instruct your undifferentiated gonads to turn into testicles (without it they'll turn into ovaries instead), plus providing some of the genes needed for spermatogenesis. That's all it does. All the genes that control sexual development are held elsewhere on your genome, so everyone has the full set of instructions for both sexes. By default you develop following the instructions for female development, but if there's testosterone (and its derivative DHT) present, then you'll develop as male instead. Going on what happens in a condition called 5-alpha reductase deficiency, DHT seems to be the more important of the two as far as genital development is concerned, whereas testosterone itself seems to be what drives masculinisation of the brain.
>
> So what happens when you expose a male fetus to testosterone blocking drugs? In theory at least, you'd end up with a baby who developed fully as male up until the time the drug started being administered, but (assuming the dose is high enough to shut down testosterone production) developing fully as female during the time the drug is being administered.
>
> I couldn't find anything in the publicly accessible medical literature dealing with this potential side effect of hormone treatment in pregnancy, however I did discover that there was this drug DES which had been widely used as a treatment for miscarriage and was also used to chemically castrate adult men with prostate cancer. The doses used for miscarriage prevention were considerably higher than those used for prostate cancer treatment (under the standard "Smith and Smith" dosing schedule, over 40 times higher during the later stages of the pregnancy). That made it virtually inevitable that it would have shut down fetal testosterone production for at least part of the pregnancy, making "DES sons" the ideal candidate for finding out what effect testosterone blocking drugs have on male development.
>
> For the last couple of years I've been trying to find out as much as I can about DES sons, both by joining a DES sons support group (that was originally set up as part of a research project into DES sons), and also by using a search engine to find discussions about DES in online forums. Just recently I've been given a link to some research conducted by the organisation Hhorages France, which basically confirms what I saw in the DES sons support group. In both groups, around a third of those exposed seem to have intersex-related genital abnormalities (several of the ones I've talked to personally were born quite badly intersexed and needed multiple surgeries during their childhood as a result). They also seem to have very high rates of endocrine problems and signs of having undergone "eunuchoid" development, indicating lifelong lower than normal male testosterone. The biggest gripe in the DES Sons support group seemed to be gender dysphoria though, about a third to a half of them seem to have ended up identifying as women. With Hhorages France, the focus is on severe mental illnesses and very high suicide rates. I guess France is one of these countries with a very macho culture that classifies gender dysphoria as a serious mental illness. It's not though, based on what I've seen, if you expose a male fetus to testosterone-blocking drugs during the second and third trimester (which is what often happens with these miscarriage treatments), then you'll end up with a person who looks male but has a brain that's predominantly developed as female, right down to having a need for female-typical hormone levels rather than male-typical ones.
>
> I'm wondering what to do next. The situation with DES is so bad that there must be people who knew at the time it was pulled from the US market in 1971 that something had gone terribly wrong there. Nonetheless, the public's been kept completely in the dark about, it and very few of those affected even know they were DES-exposed (after all, do you know what medication your mother was given during her pregnancy with you?).
>
> I get the impression that everyone looking at the DES disaster has assumed that, just because it's such a powerful estrogen, all the harm it caused was through estrogenic effects. The fact that it's also highly effective at blocking testosterone production seems to have been completely overlooked. All use of estrogens in pregnancy was ended shortly after DES was withdrawn, but progestins and other hormones with the ability to block testosterone production continue to be used in medicine to the present day. I think this is the reason why we're suddenly seeing so many stories in the news about men wanting to become women (and, based on what I've been told, DES appears to have sometimes created people in the opposite situation too - born female but identifying as male).
>
> What do people here think? Until a couple of years ago I was testing computer software, and now suddenly I've stumbled across what looks to be a colossal medical disaster that nobody wants to own up to. What's the correct procedure in such a situation? I was planning to petition the FDA, and here's what I've written so far:
>
> [https://www.change.org/en-GB/petiti...-pregnancy-can-cause-intersex-and-transgender](https://www.change.org/en-GB/petitions/the-us-food-and-drug-administration-fda-acknowledge-that-hormone-treatment-during-pregnancy-can-cause-intersex-and-transgender)
>
> It should have a covering letter though, and I'm not sure who at the FDA to address it to. What do people here suggest?

### CoolSwede — 2014-01-09T10:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105137>

Interesting links, JanSz and HoneyChild. If it is caused by elevated leptin, then I assume that women with leptin resistance and/or obesity should be at greater risk. Sounds like the fenomenon might be neolithic. Are there any H/G societies with this issue?!

### Linz — 2014-01-09T11:47:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105140>

I had moderate nausea, quite extreme tiredness and felt cold every evening working in a city office (with lots of natural light and before computer screens were introduced). During a week's skiing holiday nausea and tiredness disappeared completely from the first day and were much less when I returned to work. I had expected not to be able to ski everyday or to keep up with my party but I hardly knew I was pregnant. Leptin must have been doing the right thing up that cold snowy mountain.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-09T16:58:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105168>

Interesting. I had no nausea, but I know my hormones weren't optimal because I had extreme tiredness and sleepiness also. I certainly had no "healthy glow" that everyone expected.
I had a slightly better pregnancy 17 months later, however, possibly because I didn't try to mall walk so much for exercise, and because I really pushed the (fluoridated) water. I suppose a lot of polluted water is better than a little polluted water. ;-) No nausea the second time either, but still tired and sleepy. I gained less weight, though.

### CoolSwede — 2014-01-09T17:07:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105170>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=105140)
>
> I had moderate nausea, quite extreme tiredness and felt cold every evening working in a city office (with lots of natural light and before computer screens were introduced). During a week's skiing holiday nausea and tiredness disappeared completely from the first day and were much less when I returned to work. I had expected not to be able to ski everyday or to keep up with my party but I hardly knew I was pregnant. **Leptin must have been doing the right thing up that cold snowy mountain**.

CT and recreation = Hasta la vista, Leptin!

# Thread: How do you protect yourself from all of this?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-protect-yourself-from-all-of-this.9276/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tanya — 2014-01-07T16:42:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-protect-yourself-from-all-of-this.9276/post-104985>

2014 consumer electronics show is here now with the emphasis on â€œinternet of things - any object that is connected or run via the Internet.

I must admit that I used to look so much forward to seeing all these new gadgets and some of them even made into my wish list, but this time I have a quite different view of that thanks to this blog.... You can read the whole article, but this is what sent chills down my spine:

By 2050, analysts project, there will be 50 billion Internet-connected devices, or five gadgets for every man, woman and child.
"It transforms things in pretty significant ways," said Scott McGregor, president and chief executive of Broadcom, an Irvine company that hopes its semiconductors will power this trend. "You're going to see an explosion of devices."

Thanks to smaller, cheaper sensors, speedier wireless connections and the explosion of smartphones and tablets, it's becoming easier and more cost-effective to link just about any object to the Internet.
Gadget by gadget, people are coming to expect that even the most common things will be more useful when they are connected. Pets. Livestock. Sports equipment. Watches. Power meters. Light bulbs. Washing machines. Thermostats. If you can think of it, someone has probably stuck a sensor on it and connected it to the Internet.
"I think a lot of this is going to start with the smart home," said Kelly Davis-Felner, a representative of the Wi-Fi Alliance, whose members have played a key role in enabling this connected world. "The smart home is going to be built brick by brick until 10 years from now we're going to be looking back and saying, 'Wow, I can't believe there used to be a time when everything wasn't connected.'"

[http://www.latimes.com/business/la-fi-ces-internet-things-20140105,0,3796601.story#ixzz2pittZDwW](http://www.latimes.com/business/la-fi-ces-internet-things-20140105,0,3796601.story#ixzz2pittZDwW)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T18:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-protect-yourself-from-all-of-this.9276/post-104991>

### Tanya — 2014-01-07T22:49:31+0200

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=104991)

For some of us being optimal is just an impossible dream that will never come true, and all you want is to prevent that horrible scenario that you once found yourself in from ever happening again. So much suffering could have been avoided if people only knew what is causing it in the first place....

**Subforum:** The Cave

# Thread: Sumatran lamb curry
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Linz — 2014-01-07T21:00:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/post-105000>

A really special curry for a cold winter day.

Blend 5oz onions, 4 cloves garlic, 2 inch pice of ginger,(peeled and chopped), i red pepper (seeded, chopped) and 3 tbs water. Add 2tsp paprika, 1 tsp cayenne, 4 tsp ground coriander seeds, 2 tsp ground cumin, 1/2 tsp turmeric, black pepper, 1/2 tsp ground cardamom and 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon. Blend briefly. Add enough water to 14 oz thick coconut milk/cream to make 1 1/4 pints.

Heat 6 tbs of suitable oil - coconut, olive, duck etc and stir in blended paste. Stir for a few minutes until the paste is rich reddish brown and add 2 lbs cubed lamb shoulder or casserole lamb. Stir for a minute and then cover and cook gently for 10 mins, stirring occasionally. Add the coconut milk, 2 tbs thick tamarind paste and salt to taste. Stir and simmer slowly for about 45 minutes or until tender.

Serve with cauliflower or dark green veg - spinach or kale - and substitute the nightshades for something different if you need to.

### fitness@home — 2014-01-07T22:53:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/post-105016>

This sounds really good. I have everything on hand except the tamarind and lamb. Going to try this one - thanks for posting!

### Tanya — 2014-01-07T23:12:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/post-105018>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=105000)
>
> Serve with cauliflower or dark green veg - spinach or kale - and substitute the nightshades for something different if you need to.

I wish I could have this., but eve if I substitute the nightshades, the curry itself may present a problem for me. I can't imagine making this dish without it though. Thank you for sharing your recipe.

# Thread: Carbs and Vitamin D - no problem if there is no inflamation and according to seasons?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-and-vitamin-d-no-problem-if-there-is-no-inflamation-and-according-to-seasons.9279/>

### marcus — 2014-01-07T21:38:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-and-vitamin-d-no-problem-if-there-is-no-inflamation-and-according-to-seasons.9279/post-105006>

I am trying to get the picture regarding vitamin D. After reading several blog posts here (science behind leptin reset, hormones 101, hormones 102, Vitamin D: The Sunshine of Your Life, Quantum Biology 7: Vitamin D) vitamin D levels in humans seem to be influenced by several factors:

If there is inflammation present, vitamin d levels fall because of 'prenenolone steal syndrome'.

Another issue is VDR polymorphisms. It's an issue especially of people suffering from autoimmune diseases.

In QuantumBiology blog post series Vitamin D is the connection between ('binds') the brain and the skin.

So far so good.

But how is this quote meant: "The circadian rhythm of vitamin D levels is also accounted for to judge light levels as well. Those closer to the equator would have much higher levels of Vitamin D and would be able to account for a higher carb level they face. Many current day leptin resistant folks find out their vitamin D levels are low when they finally test for it."? ('science behind leptin reset' blog post).

How are vitamin D and carbs in humans yoked metabolically? Why do I need high levels of vit D to stay healthy while eating carbs?

It can be deduced that eating no carbs in winter (when sun levels are low) is no problem to have a low level of vitamin D?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T21:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-and-vitamin-d-no-problem-if-there-is-no-inflamation-and-according-to-seasons.9279/post-105007>

How are Vitamin D and carbs yoked? Via the photoelectric effect. Vitamin D is made below the power of natural light of 520 nm. 200-380 nm range. Carbs grow in longer light cycles where photons and electrons always carry higher energies from the sun. They are naturally absent from places on this planet when these metrics are absent. Vitamin D is also provided for at the same time because UV light has a lower wavelength than the visible spectrum therefore UV light will always be present when the 520 nm light is p\[resent to grow carbs. Vitamin D is made with this light source in skin. This is how two parts of the spectrum are used by two different sets of Maxwell demons to catch photons and electrons of different powers. The difference in energies creates an "opportunity for the brain's receptors". If you carefully read CT 6 what did I say about Vitamin D in winter time? We have a metabolic trap door.......we dont need it if we can generate NOS. Normally we can when our redox potential is good........but when we cant..........shit hits the fan.

### SeaHorse — 2014-05-12T15:31:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-and-vitamin-d-no-problem-if-there-is-no-inflamation-and-according-to-seasons.9279/post-126438>

I think with all snps their expression is the key and remember there are over 98 VDR snps…..I looked into this a bit over at the Phoenix Rising Forum: this exchange (I hope I'm allowed to copy) was interesting both for the info. on VDR and also the whole premiss of Yasko's work with autism.. it's not that she doesn't have success…it's that it may be for reasons other than she thinks.

Anyway this was an interesting perspective:

1.

2.  @dbkita

    Thanks for doing all this research on VDR SNPs. There has been a long-standing confusion between the results from Yasko and other labs. I'm afraid I don't know enough science to understand all that you have written, though I think I get the gist of it.

    I had my VDR SNPs tested at another lab. I'd be grateful if you could tell me how you would read them.

    Here is what it says:

    FOK1 polymorphism (ff)

    BSM1 polymorphism (BB)

    Thanks,
    Sushi
    [Sushi](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?members/sushi.47/), [Apr 14, 2013
    #21](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/vdr-alleles.22270/page-2#post-347982)

3.  [](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?members/dbkita.2835/)
    **[dbkita](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?members/dbkita.2835/)Senior Member**
    Messages:
    [655](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?search/member&user_id=2835)
    Likes:
    267
    [↑](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?goto/post&id=347920#post-347920)

    Dbkita,
    thank you for airing these suspicions and discussing the validity og SNP interpretation by Yasko. Very interesting.

    I've been around for half a century. I have no background from informatics or genetics (linguistics and anthropology) as the informatics came too late for me, but I'm a systems thinker by nature. I've suffered series of collapses throughout life and recently rehabilitated myself from dysautonomia. I came into this forum backwards; only after finding help through supplements that worked, did I start to question the genetic underpinnings of DNA methylation.

    Mine is a long and complex story. I am self diagnosed and self treated for the past 16 years. I came to this forum through recently discovering Richard van Konynenburg's methylation hypothesis on Dr. Sarah Myhill's website. I only learned of Amy Yasko through this forum, and of Genetic Genie through a rare haplotype group I share with 18 people that may or may not cause lowered tetrahydrobiopterin. It's been a very steep learning curve!

    While I in general consider Amy Yasko a credible pioneer, trying to help primarily children with autism and their parents, I wonder if she has taken too much upon herself, and therefore simplified the whole approach, contrary to her proclaimed intentions of individualised interpretation. She does state that the genetic profile is just one of the many factors in multifactorial disease. However, her methylation and detox profile seem to contradict that, as one predictive value seems to be given to all SNPs. I don't know if that's her fault, or if the people using her data misinterpret.

    I read your posts here on the topic of VDR Taq with great interest, as I share your confusion and suspicion (without intending to diminish her work). Based on what little I have grasped about polymorphism variation, I find it simplicistic to assign a +/- value to variation that may be differently distributed in different populations, gender, age or in correlation with other SNPs. Both the COMT 158 Val/ Met and the VDR Taq seem to be population specific, according to scientific studies. However, even the scientific studies on genetic polymorphisms are not a measure of anything. The work on the COMT 158 Val/Met polymorphism reads like a horoscope (you're either 'a warrior' or 'a worrier'), but that SNP itself is significant. I prefer to focus on the biochemistry of its function, rather than on the personality traits.

    I cringe when I read "being positive for a mutation". Genotyping is not like testing positive for a viral or a bacterial infection, but in most peoples' minds it may sound like that! Although the genetic variation technically is a mutation - a mutation that many people carry - it is not a mutation in the sense of the gene defect. From the established genetics point of view, ie current knowledge about the inborn errors of metabolism, only the proven defects are caused by gene _mutation_. What a single polynucleotide variation does in any individual, is to be discovered, not simply charted.

    The SNP variation does not cause anything directly, but indirectly, by affecting a synthesis of an important vitamin or coenzyme, or by inability to metabolise common drugs and environmental toxins. Here I'm in agreement with Yasko.

    The only thing I can think of, in her defence, is that autism may be a differently organised system altogether. I am on the opposite side of that spectrum (gifted and not a trace of developmental problems). I too am a fast oxidiser and despite having genotypes that enable me to deal with stress more successfully than most people, my HPA axis broke down. I've experienced first hand dysautonomia (a total neuro-endocrine-immune derangement) so I know how a deranged metabolism is not like a normal metabolism. That's why I wonder if maybe Yasko's interpretation of methylation in autists is based on her clinical experience that won't be matched by the usual genetic studies.

    Just thinking out loud. If you'd care to comment, I'd appreciate it very much.

    If you do reply, could you also point me to the posts you wrote about immune dysfunction, as I find that the most difficult area of all I had to deal with. Thanks in advance.

    Sonja

    I would agree with you except in the case of VDR, Dr Yasko was labeling the baT haplotype as "defective". While in her practice autistic children with baT may be able to tolerate more methyl donors the idea that the baT haplotype in the general population is defective and means less dopamine is plain wrong. It is the dominant haplotype. Especially in certain ethnic groups.

    Sorry but I have made so many posts. Try the search feature with "dbkita" and "immunne". Adreno has been on a lot of those threads as well. Good luck!​

### NeilBB — 2018-03-01T21:17:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-out-with-dave-asprey.9281/post-236090>

One of the major advantages of an establishment with an "official dogma" is its ubiquity, united front, and strength in numbers. This is a powerful socializing force and gives propaganda its juice.

Conversely, the reason most anti-establishment movements ultimately fail historically is because the various opponents of the establishment rarely agree with each other and schisms invariably multiply over details, spoils, and mechanics of implementation. It's always been true across all disciplines.

I personally think that opponents of the establishment should band together over the "bigger picture outlook" to defeat the mutual common enemy and wait to battle out the details later, once the establishment is fully discredited. As the US, Europe and Russia came together in WW2 to defeat the Nazi's and Facsist's. But that's just my view and I know it's a pipedream.

# Thread: Margos leptin reset - Highway from hell!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/margos-leptin-reset-highway-from-hell.9283/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-01-08T10:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/margos-leptin-reset-highway-from-hell.9283/post-105051>

Hi hi,

This is going to be very long, this i know! So im gonna try condense it as much as possible, and use the key points.
History = endurance athlete for 14 years.
Bulimic 18 - 25 years
Tried the contraceptive pill around 26 yrs old - had to come off it, got to the point where i could barely leave the house, so exhausted, very reactive to wheat and was never the same on the bike again. (not as strong as pre the pill)
Migraines started age 27. Debilitating pain, always stemmed from left side @ C1. If they were bad then I would vomit, and would continue violent dry retching in excess of 8 x per day (and im talking really violent!) resulting in me on the floor from the pain in my head. Longest one 72 hours. They have reduced by about 98% since going paleo, and the severity has reduced drastically
Was vegetarian / fat phobic for years. Used to get severe abdominal upset some days and thought nothing of the regular 5 bowel movements pday.

@33 years old - started 'losing it'. Very emotional, out of control - crying, sullen, withdrawn and very stressed.
@35 years old - lost the plot. I was at work one day and literally 'felt' a cape of darkness being draped over me. It was all i could do to get through my shift at work (chef). GOt prescribed anti depressants. Did counselling, flicked the loser guy, took some time out.
@37 years - went to thailand for a detox. Ended up going to a second health farm and met the doctor that changed my life - took me from vegan to paleo, tested adrenal function (very poor), and i detoxed from coffee.
After 3 months of paleo / adrenal extract i literally woke up thin! Was amazing, felt amazing, didnt even know i was getting a period, life was easy!
\- back to antarctica to work. (sleep issues had started down there the previous summer, but i was ok when i left). No change to diet etc but weight started creeping up. Stress?
\- I had 3 years of summer in antarctica, and then 4-5 months in asia. Lots of blue light - hours on the computer, lots of flying, weight still going up..
39 yrs - Breast reduction, breasts doubled in size in 2 years. for all those who joke and say they want big ones / what a waste etc - try living with constant back and neck pain, being paranoid, being harrassed.. it sucks.
\- Weight still going up. Ability to get off an 8 hour flight and not be able to get my jacket closed - i swell up that much  that sucks, because its very slow to go down again.
\- Sleep issues at their worst meant i could take a pharma sleeper and lie awake all night. I felt, all along, that it was cortisol related - heart would start thrashing by 10.30pm on the dot.
\- after the breast surgery, self medicated with thyroid extract.
\- Moved back to NZ, continued swelling up, felt some days, that i was too big for my skin. Lost all definition in legs, gained all over body cellulite, including lower back & chest (yeah, effed up, right?!), severe pain in back exacerbated at work (standing on my feet) , plantar fasciaitis.. Not very happy!
\- 40 yrs - started seeing a naturopath here in NZ. onto wilsons adrenal protocol, iodine tested - barely any showed. started iodine supplement.
\- Came off thyroid. (dessicated pig thyroid).

\- Last six months - blacked out windows in room. noticed a change in sleep on night two!
\- body pain drastically reduced.
\- have occasional coffee, love it!
\- have a 'ring' around my neck (like a crease) at the bottom that can flare up at times - not sure what makes it stand out more, but i think its thyroid related?
\- took body temp this week, was 36C - this has come up from the 34-low 35C that i used to sit at constantly

Always had a six pack, was always 'lean' - am now around 25kg heavier.
\- Get bad headaches with period, not always
\- still eat paleo including raw milk
\- current, been doing JK protocol for about two months. not always to the letter, but as best as i can. I do get frustrated still, and worried it wont work (more so before my period). I can get quite down around period time, which seems to have gotten worse lately.

Just waiting on next test kit to arrive. am going to do salivary cortisol, melatonin and DHEA and sex hormones. Then Id like to join this site so i can have a consult with JK and see whats really up.

Im wearing blue blocking glasses at night, look into rising sun in the morning and supplement with oxytocin (when i remember to!) to try help that melatonin reset (looking at the sun)
Swim in the lake over the road for my CT. Its a constant 11-13C. can do up to 28 minutes.

### caroline — 2014-06-20T11:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/margos-leptin-reset-highway-from-hell.9283/post-131491>

Hey Margo! you got a heck of a story there girl! where are you now? I can't keep track of you ....lol

> Do you currently have time to train much, I imagine your job is taking most of your time.
> I will add you to my follow list if you don't mind

hi hi!
sure you can add me! im back in thailand now. some quiet time! i dont ride anymore. infact, i really watch that old 'chronic cardio'! iv had a few months out of the gym to. just been walking, yoga and now doing some T Tapp again. im thinking of gymming again but im on the fence. im finding now, my periods just knock me back ten steps and it takes like a week to get over it :-/ so im not sure. im interested to hear what your story is, with its similarities!

# Thread: Lauren's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 71
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LaurenR — 2014-01-08T14:18:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-105063>

I thought I might join in the fun and start a journal!

Righto so here is a brief history, of diagnosis, medications, progress:
Age 2- Asthma - put on steroid inhalers serotide and ventolin- took age 2 to 29
Age 5- Eczema - begin steroid creams on & off
Age 12- Digestive issues begin, cramping, diahorrea
Age 13- Elimination diets for digestive
Age 15- Severe allergy and hayfever symptoms
Age 16- Chronic sinusitis begins
Age 16- De-sensitization Injections for garden mold, following skin prick tests
Age 17- Acne and increased hair growth- put on the Pill
Age 18- Sinusitis continues
Age 19- Surgery on sinuses FESS & Septoplasty
Age 20- Blood tests and investigation into fatigue-nothing found
Age 20- Colonoscopy & Endoscopy for chronic cramping & diahorrea- IBS diagnosis
Age 20- Anxiety & Panic attacks developed following car crash
Age 21- Acne put on antibiotics for 3 months at a time...
IBS continues to plague daily life
Eczema continues on and off in patches
Age 24- Mild depression
IBS continues
Eczema continues on and off in patches
Age 27- 10months backpacking Sth America alone.
Both amazing and traumatic experiences.

Aug 2010- Return from overseas and diagnosed with Depression, PTSD ( trauma in Sth America) & exhaustion. Most likely adrenal fatigue too.
Eczema returns (from constant summer to winter )
Begin grain free, dairy free- and finally digestive relief and heal IBS and hayfever!

Sept 2011- 5 day skin allergy test
Oct 2011 - Say goodbye to GP's and visit Naturopath- poop test-high dysbiosis, low good bactieria;
Stop contraceptive pill & asthma inhalers.
Begin parasite cleanse, liver detox, mag, vit c, herbal meds, chlorella.
Experience major detox, vomiting a few days & rash extreme flare up face, neck, arms, chest.

Mar 2012- Stop anti-depressants (after 1 year) and do 4 colonics and a clay colon cleanse
Extreme eczema flare up, oedema, detox, anti-depressan detox?
Spend the next 3 months in burning, itching agony, don't sleep, painful to dress, move.
Bleed everywhere, ruin many sheets, weeping and shed more skin than humanly possible!!!
Try acupuncture.
Cycle regulates

Vit D= low, begin supp; B12= low
ASI Test:
7am- 22nmol/L (target 25)
12pm- 10 nmol/L (target 15)
6pm- 7 nmol/L (target 5-10)
10pm- 6 nmol/L (target<5)

DHEA-S= 7AM 9.8nmol/L (ref range 0.7-8.7)

Liver Function Test= fast p1, slow p2
Hair metals Test= multiple toxicities- i realise not useful.
IgG t=Test- cow, sheep, goat milk, wheat, egg white, kidney beans, soy, almonds, brazil nuts, peanut

June 2012- Start using a cream for rash, skin. Supps.
Sept 2012- Infrared saunas, start kinestiology (combo of muscle testing, EFT, craniosacral)
Dec 2012- Begin Leptin Rx, epi-paleo. Supps.
Jan 2013: Adrenal fatigue RX, Leaky gut RX. Supps.
March 2013: Begin CT (stop in May due to move). more supps...
July 2013: Move O.S

So where does that leave me...
Well I have just moved from Australia to London...that puts me out of the donut hole frying pan and into the EMF fire of London

Food Goals:
My diet is tight and seasonal, but food is not as good here.
I want to start eating seafood/oily fish twice a day not just once.
I also want to eat more types of shellfish. I accidently poisoned myself on mussels, so haven't been eating them. Not a huge fan of scallops or oysters, but will get back into them.
Also eating more raw protein is a goal too, when I feel comfortable on the source and process etc.

Body Composition Goals:
I don't weigh myself, haven't for a couple of years.
So I weigh around 52kgs = 114lbs and I am 165cm = 5'3".
I have not done a DEXA scan, so could be around 20% at a very rough guess.

I want to lose 3-4kgs (um what is that 5-7lbs) of body fat and a couple of %'s.
Mostly stored on my love handles, belly fat, my adductors (inner thigh) and back.
I am not overweight, but I am not lean or optimal in the midsection!
I also want to increase muscles mass and squat some booty into my butt

Optimal Goals:
Get optimal...obviously!  Not just health, but in mind, career, relationships, connection.
This is about an optimal life not just health!
Heal skin, leaky gut, adrenals, brain, clock

RX Goals:
Do my best with EMF in this place!
Get magnetico sleep pad
Tighten on bluelight and circadian cycle, I have blue blockers and recently got amber bulbs too and candles.
CT again! (I think I packed too many shoes not enough cold packs). I found the ice packs much much easier than a bath of cold water.

Retest usual and Test thyroid, sex hormones, ASI, 23andme

Activity Goals:
Increase muscle mass through lifting; increase PB's for deadlift, front and back squat, chin, pull ups; dance more; more fun!

Continue daily meditation, EFT, kinesiology.

### Hope — 2014-01-08T16:26:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-105068>

Lauren, welcome and wow, what a story you have to share!! I find it fascinating that you didn't notice any digestive issues till you were 12. I wonder if they were brewing all along, and if your mom's health didn't contribute. What was/is her health like?

Also, I wonder if you have read at all about mold/biotoxin issues? Reading your list it popped right out at me. I don't know where you live but is it possible you were ever exposed to a water damaged building? Or anywhere for more than 10 minutes where it smelled musty? I just wonder.

Here is the life-changing blog that I had to come across twice before I realized, mold/biotoxins are likely part of my story:

[http://www.jackkruse.com/hey-lyme-disease-meet-leptin/](http://www.jackkruse.com/hey-lyme-disease-meet-leptin/)

There is also a great online test you can do (though it costs $15) to see if you might be affected:

[http://www.survivingmold.com/store1/online-screening-test](http://www.survivingmold.com/store1/online-screening-test)

Congratulations on finding this place and doing so much to get optimal!!!!!

### LaurenR — 2014-01-19T13:59:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-106193>

I am about 10 days in with NO steroid cream for eczema, as I have run out.
This happens every 4-6 months. I run out of steroid cream and I test to see what happens etc....inevitably the same thing!!!

My arms-inside & out, neck, chest, breasts and parts of back and rib cage are COVERED in eczema. Red, hot, itchy, acid burn feeling and very very dry.
It's flaking a lot this time.
I haven't slept properly for days. And this is ultimately what happens each time I have done this.
I end up using the cortisone cream again so I can start functioning properly, quality of life etc.

This time it has really got to me. I have been using this cream since July 2012, (I went for 9 months prior without a prescription cream, bad idea!).
I have a feeling if I left it longer it would continue to creep down my torso, thighs, calves, ankles...

I am no better than I was before I started using it!!! I am a strong woman, but years of itchy, hot burning, sometimes takes it's toll on my mental & emotional wellbeing....

I need to fix brain, photoperiod reader, vit a cycle. I want to understand this completely.
The Rx: circadian cycle & sleep, along with the other kruse rx's.

The catch: I face and have faced for years now- the eczema, the heat, the itchiness impacts my ability to fall asleep.
EVEN when using a prescription cream, I still have trouble falling asleep. And here in lies a hurdle.
I meditate but that does not help with this, other things but not this.
Anyway that is where I'm at.

### sooperb — 2014-01-19T17:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-106197>

I think my daughter would sympathise Lauren. She has had asthma since she was 2.5 and at 41 is still a sufferer. She thrives physically when she takes her medication but it is steroid based and she finds her mood sinks and her temper frays alarmingly. She has tried to live without the meds many times but while she may get some months symptom free (usually in the summer) she always seems to relapse. At present she has been trying to do without the meds but is then needing to use a nebuliser in order to be breathe. It's no fun and she hates it. She is an allergic asthmatic so there can be many things that spark it off and of course she doesn't know until she hits an allergen by which time it's too late.

Have you ever bio-hacked yourself to see if there are any particular irritants that set your eczema off or is it something more internal that causes it? According to what I've read about paleo and via some of Dr. Kruse's posts, eczema and asthma are classed as neolithic diseases that one should be able to heal by living optimally. I'd love to believe this is true.

Sooperb, not to hijack, but this sounds like you were talking about me, the way I used to be. I also had some relief from asthma during summer. And I was allergic to a host of things, including some foods. All of it went away, including the various allergies (even to poison ivy) when I dropped the wheat. I think the wheat got my system all riled up so that the smallest irritant, like cedar pollen or nuts or a cat or whatever, would set off my asthma. I dropped wheat and after a while I would get only a very mild reaction to things that used to set me off. Later, I stopped having the mild reactions with the addition of CT. It blows my mind how one thing messed up my system so badly. No more ear infections, sinus infections, asthma.

### LaurenR — 2014-03-07T16:24:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-113997>

I pulled three little black things out of the same spot in my arm, which I took to the infectious disease clinic. I did a series of parasitology and microbiology tests which cam back negative. They said the things in my arm were not parasites, but I have had another in my ankle!!!

So update...I was then tested to for strongaloydis, which I have not heard back, which I think will mean I do NOT have it.
Wanted to be clear of this, as I have had this rash since returning from 10 months in South America.

I am also doing a parasite cleanse with tincture drops, in case there are parasites that were not detectable by their testing.
Have not really had any symptoms. Keep checking poop, but hard to tell if I am seeing parasites lol.
(I remember being told in my Invertebrates class at University that if you removed most matter from the world but left nematodes, that you would still be able to "see" most things or something along the lines. Gross! Never forgotten that!).

My new idea/thought...
I have definite patches of Eczema on my body, scaley, weeping, little sores.
BUT, this rash over my body, perhaps it is not eczema. It is red, itchy, rough, but I am not scaly and weepy all over.

I am thinking it could be a different kind of skin issue.

I am also wondering if it could be related to low thryoid. I do not have all the hypothryoid symptoms, but I do have a couple.
I plan to test my thyroid in next few weeks.
I wonder if any other thyroid people here have skin rashes?

I take out headphones, remove phone from bedroom, come back to bed, my body will heat up within a few mins, get itchy, scratch, wriggle, get hotter, itch more, get hotter, get itchier, fluff the doona to let cold air in, scratch, get hotter etc.
Then I run into late night cortisol spike, mind starts going, lot's of creative ideas flow (frequently at night).
More hotness and itchy and awakeness. The room is always cold- no heating in the bedroom.

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-13T15:21:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-115175>

Yikes....you might ask Josh on the forum for some insights...he's been dealing with immune/allergy issues in some very creative ways.
The histamine is going to be an ongoing issue if you have mastocytosis because you have more cells to produce more histamine than the average person..worth checking out.
Some supplements that can help all over allergy issues (you may know of them already) are PRP (NuMedica) and NeuroProtek which has luteolin, quercitin and rutin in it. You have probably already read lots about the different histamine receptors...I found that I had to take something that targeted more than one to get any relief. I too used steroid cream for years and years to deal with rashes that arrived every month before my period....no body ever bothered to check my hormones....I used to get contact hives from hot water , stress...on and on. You know how it is. Rashes and thyroid can be connected too. Remember skin health is a reflection of brain health so the function of the brain rather than filling in holes with hormones is the ultimate thing to focus on. Sounds like you are doing well with light cycles etc. It will take awhile.

I would get your thyroid tested a.s.a.p! magnetico and 23 and me (really worth it to know about methylation) I know these are on your list...just giving you a pep talk
Interesting about PTSD....do you know about Somatic Experiencing work? Another very useful tool to have in your toolbox.

### LaurenR — 2014-06-05T18:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-129804>

For sure, I have looked like this for 2.5 years and still no closer to an answer, except maybe now...

I will definitely give that cream a go!

Although I do not have all these

[As per Dr Theoharides](http://www.mastcellmaster.com)

Mast cell activation syndrome is mainly diagnosed on the basis of symptoms: (From a recent presentation)

(1) Dermatologic: flushing, pruritus, urticaria pigmentosa, angioedema, dermatographism (sometimes)

(2) Respiratory: wheezing, sore throat

(3) Cardiovascular: chest pain, hypotension, tachycardia

(4) Gastrointestinal: abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, bloating, malabsorption, esophagitis

(5) Naso-ocular: nasal stuffiness, pruritus

(6) Neurologic: headache, memory and concentration difficulties/brain fog, paresthesia, peripheral neuropathy

(7) Muscoskeletal: bone/muscle pain, degenerative disc disease, osteoporosis/osteopenia

(8) Systemic: anaphylaxis, fatigue

So maybe it is histamine.

### LaurenR — 2014-06-05T18:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-129807>

For histamine the question is.. why mast cells release too much histamine?
Some searching found this:

\--An elevation in histamine would generally be associated with classic IgE allergies. I have only tested IgG.

\--mast cell disorders respond to a wide range of triggers, which may include environmental chemicals, foods, temperature changes, medicine, smells, stress, viruses and fungi.

\--Mast Cell Disorders are not the same as Multiple Chemical Sensitivity. But some people with MCS may be incorrectly diagnosed and actually have a mast cell disorder.
I have have 5 day skin chemical tests and came up positive to numerous chemicals. This was back in 2011!

\--The long list of symptoms associated with mast cell disorders can be found at the **The Mastocytosis Society**. Some people are called “shockers” because they go into anaphylactic shock when triggered, whereas others are called “leakers” because their mast cells slowly leak their contents causing chronic symptoms. The symptoms can range from mild to severe. There are specific medical treatments that help many people reduce the symptoms of mast cell disorders.

\--genetic polymorphisms that can cause an upregulation of specific **CBS genes**, which may impact mast cell degranulation
I have the SNP.

I feel like i am the closest to solving this in 3 years.

### LaurenR — 2014-06-13T11:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-130731>

I have had another new discovery. I was researching MCAD and all things mast cells and talking with sufferers in various online groups, when I was pointed in the direction of Topical Steroid Addiction/ Withdrawal.

My heart sank a little yesterday. I read the ITSAN site, [http://www.itsan.org/eczema\_questions\_answered.html](http://www.itsan.org/eczema_questions_answered.html)
The symptoms. This is me. This was me in 2012 9 months into withdrawal and then I started using steroid creams almost daily...to now.

I felt a bit sick reading it all. The pain, the burn, the sleepless nights, the feeling of dying from the inside out.

I feel really sad. And scared. Healing is slow, really really slow. And I am scared to be in that amount of physical and emotional pain again...and for so much longer.

They recommend no sun during the initial flare.

I would really love to find a method of making this easier on my body. Maybe the magnetico would help. I am not sure if this would help with the nerve and blood vessel healing, I'm not sure.

They mention use of antihistamine tablets and sleeping pills- both of which I don't want to use.

I am worried about trashing my cortisol again from the itch/burn and histamine release and ruining my sleep.

Tough day.

Here is a clip from one of the leading Dr's in this field.

It is summer now here and it is advised NOT to withdraw during summer, as you are also advised to keep sunlight to a minimum because the skin is so damaged during flares.

I have a few trips planned around Europe, so I'm thinking I will need to wait until they are done and withdraw in October. The thought of another few months on the creams makes me unhappy.

I think that my partner and I will have to talk about when will I do this. We might have to consider moving back home and even moving in to my parents depending on how I go. I'm not sure we have the support we need here in UK with just a few friends and couples and no family.

Thanks Jude. I have worked for a while at fixing circadian rhythm and I eat fish or shellfish everyday sometimes twice a day.

A side effect of withdrawal and I also experienced this in my 9-month withdrawal a couple of years ago is insomnia.
You feel so hot and itchy and like you are being burned by acid that you just can not fall asleep. My usual time to fall asleep was 3am-5am in the bad flare.
Obviously this impacts the next day greatly.
I ended up taking a natural sleeping pill and st johns wort to help. But this is where it can be difficult to get circadian rhythm in line. And I would say that, that 9months with poor sleep is one reason I headed deep into adrenal fatigue and feeling pretty down.

I definitely will plan this carefully.
My diet is dialed in except for not enough raw protein or organ meat (would like to make pate). Seafood 1-2 everyday.
Been on bottled water for 8 months now.
I have cut all my nails off to reduce damage from scratching.
Daily grounding

Will purchase:
Natural sleeping herbs
St johns wort if required
Adrenal support supplement
Licorice tea
Sago palm vit c (high dose) - I can't take quercetin due to a SNP
Curcumin
Special long sleeve clothing to prevent itching

We plan to get a magnetico, but if we are moving back to Oz not sure it's worth it getting a "northern hemisphere" one as we will need a "southern hemisphere" one there.

I really do believe that with all I have discovered on this site, that I can combine it with the info there and heal easier and quicker.

I read someone did a cosmetic acid peel on their face then used vaseline for 3 days and then did not have any more issues with their face.

I am really intrigued to comprehensively understand about the nerve ending repair and the vasodilation that causes the pain.
There has to be something in that to work with...

### LaurenR — 2014-06-28T18:39:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-132520>

I think i have just developed a duck egg IgE allergy in 5 meals and a few weeks! (i posted this in monster thread).

In order to try something new I have been eating duck eggs occasionally.
I have had them 5 times now.
1st- nothing
2nd- nothing
3rd- felt a bit off unwell
4th- 4 hours later nausea, followed be diarrhea
5th- today this happened...

warning below TMI!

Within a few minutes of eating the eggs (scrambeled with spinach) my throat starting to feel itchy.
Then my tongue feel tingly. The bigger tastebuds at the back of my tongue started to swell.
I stopped eating then took a shower as I felt a bit anxious about what was happening.

Went to lie down, started to feel nausea, 10-20minutes later I developed cramps and a very strong need of vomit.

I didn't vomit but I did have to go to the toilet, 2 x BM- normal.
Went back to bed, and feel asleep for ??15mins maybe.

Woke up more cramps, painful, nausea.
Felt Hot and had tingly hands.
2 x diarrhea

Then extremely intense vomiting! ABout 10 waves/retches all in a row.
Back to bed, more painful cramps, one more diarrhea.
Then back to sleep.

All of that within in 3 hours.

So...is it possible to develop an IgE allergy out of nowhere and with eating duck eggs 5 times?

I have no other IgE allergies (nettle tea gives me itchy throat immediately).

I found it all quite scary as I knew what was going on as soon as felt itchy and my tongue tingly. I wonder where that leaves me with hen eggs...

### Sean Waters — 2018-05-28T11:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-242185>

Hi Lauren,

I had eczema since 2 and Crohn's at 16 years old.

Beat Crohn's but developed rash on wrists and hands. After moving to London it spread to arms and legs and nearly everywhere. I also developed fatigue and depression, an inability to go Ketogenic aswell ( insulin resistance / metabolic issue). Hereditary anemia too.

I went to Yucatan Mexico for 2 months to heal but nothing changed. Histamine was identified as an issue.

I took 3-5 TSP of Sea Salt a day and I stopped itching. Came to UK and still fine. The rash on body cleared total. Arms still feint outline of area but unnoticeable. Hands still red and raised but very rare do I scratch.... First time in a month was today! Which is why I'm searching for info....

Plot thickens... My hand rash disappear when I take Fish Oil at extremely high dose (12g).... Jack said early on, skin issues usually tied to an Omega 6 to 3 ratio being out of whack.

Taking 12g o3 is not good idea, I get twitches and feel a bit weird. It has to be done slowly.... Jack said 2-4 years, sane time for adrenal fatigue too, another illness requiring DHA. HEAT and CT and Sun and Saunas and London EMF will all remove water from your cells. You need even more Salt to mitigate post Heat/EMF itching... Its about "salinity", sodium holds water in cells like sea water holds water even in presence of extreme sun the ocean remains. You are losing water and not replacing. Get some sodium. Remove all 06. Including Duck eggs!! It's not IGE it's 06. Pure seafood diet plus la guarande salt. Wild mackerel is cheap. £2 for 2 at tesco now lol

# Thread: How do you brew your BPC?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-brew-your-bpc.9286/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2014-01-08T18:01:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-brew-your-bpc.9286/post-105078>

So I am thinking I need to try to get my DH off his Starbucks coffee habit and onto BPC. So I wonder, do any of you brew your coffee without a coffee machine but with some other device? If so, which?

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-08T19:50:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-brew-your-bpc.9286/post-105087>

I switched from a French press to a regular brewing machine when I have time and I don't need to make a large pot I use pour over with filter. I always use a filter now....

here's what the pour overs look like. [https://shoponline.melitta.com/items/COFFMKRSPROVER](https://shoponline.melitta.com/items/COFFMKRSPROVER)

I've got a travel one that made of silicone and collapsed - it goes in my luggage along w/preground beans, ghee, Coconut oil and braun hand blender

If we had the space I'd get the porcelain 6 cup coffee maker but since we don't I've got a regular drip brewer...

>

And wow, you travel with it too?! What kind of Braun hand blender do you use?

Thanks a million....

\-----------------------------------------------------
[http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/)
Off hand, from the list

1\. DHA/EPA or any at all fish or krill oils.
you should not use them, eat sea food.
\-------------------------------

2\. Minerals Iodine, Iron, and Selenium

About iodine/iodide, rather than supplement with Lugol's dr Kruse encourages eating sea food, (oysters are #1) and on his recent podcast with Dave Asprey he specifically mention seaweed Arame.

At this time I am waiting for clarification on Arame:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthre...th-Dave-Asprey&p=105067&viewfull=1#post105067](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?9281-New-podcast-out-with-Dave-Asprey&p=105067&viewfull=1#post105067)

Arame grows naturally only around Japan, and is also farmed around South Korea.
I need clarification about radiation from Fukushima.

\----------------------------
10\. DHEA/MELATONIN

No to melatonin, unless flying more than 3 hrs and changing time zones.

\----------------------------

8\. Multivitamin

Personally I do not use any multivitamins.
Instead I do Spectracell Micronutrient Analysis and follow my test results with targeted supplementation.

\---------------------------

Overall, the first priority is to modify and align life style:
CT
Food (Epi-Paleo-Rx),
Circadian Rhythm, BAB, (exposure to blue light after sunrise, no exposure to blue light after sundown)
minimize EMF exposure, control it (Magnetico)
Micronutrient supplementation

If the above does not make you happy,
hire medical professional (who's priorities are aligned with dr Jack Kruse's)

Gut analysis/optimization
Optimize micronutrients
Optimize Fatty Acids and Phospholipids
Optimize Steroid Hormone Panel
Optimize Thyroid Hormone Panel
All else

//
Depending on dedication, many of the above are good concepts for do it your self project.
Dr Kruse is a fantastic teacher plus he make available to us best expertise.
You should buy and study recent seminar with dr Tim Jackson.
Likely other are in the pipeline.
If you are at first reluctant to experiment on your own body, (n-1), at least that would give you a background to evaluate your doctor.

/////////////////////////

> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthre...th-Dave-Asprey&p=105067&viewfull=1#post105067](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?9281-New-podcast-out-with-Dave-Asprey&p=105067&viewfull=1#post105067)

>
> No to melatonin, unless flying more than 3 hrs and changing time zones.
>
> \----------------------------
>
> 8\. Multivitamin
>
> Personally I do not use any multivitamins.
> Instead I do Spectracell Micronutrient Analysis and follow my test results with targeted supplementation.
>
> \---------------------------
>
> Overall, the first priority is to modify and align life style:
> CT
> Food (Epi-Paleo-Rx),
> Circadian Rhythm, BAB, (exposure to blue light after sunrise, no exposure to blue light after sundown)
> minimize EMF exposure, control it (Magnetico)
> Micronutrient supplementation
>
> If the above does not make you happy,
> hire medical professional (who's priorities are aligned with dr Jack Kruse's)
>
> Gut analysis/optimization
> Optimize micronutrients
> Optimize Fatty Acids and Phospholipids
> Optimize Steroid Hormone Panel
> Optimize Thyroid Hormone Panel
> All else
>
>
> //
> Depending on dedication, many of the above are good concepts for do it your self project.
> Dr Kruse is a fantastic teacher plus he make available to us best expertise.
> You should buy and study recent seminar with dr Tim Jackson.
> Likely other are in the pipeline.
> If you are at first reluctant to experiment on your own body, (n-1), at least that would give you a background to evaluate your doctor.
>
>
>
>
> /////////////////////////
>
> ..............

Hello,

I appreciate the quick replies to my question about supplement timing. Looking in the comments area after Leptin Rx, I found some responses from Dr. Kruse about vitamins. He said he split his dosages of Resveratrol, K2, NAC, into I think 4 times per day. I will also check the Life Extension brand recommended supplements directions to see if some need to be ingested on an empty stomach or with food. Now I am learning the benefit of giving the body a more constant dosage several times per day. I liked the few replies that said that the food is the nutrition but I thought during the 6-8 week reset I would benefit from the recommended supplements. I will start a beginner log like Caroline recommended.

Thank you,

John

### LaurenR — 2014-01-15T20:12:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-post-finasteride-syndorme-experience-info-in-the-forum.9290/post-105779>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=105155)
>
> Describe how do you know that you have Post-Finasteride Syndrome.
>
> Have you been eating finasteride or applying it to skin or hair?
>
> Write essay about your self.
>
>
> It is my partner, male now aged 28.
> His story:
>
> Age 23, Weight 178lbs, very strong muscular male, started to notice hair falling out on pillow.
> Age 23 started taking finasteride pills- no test results for pre-medication.
>
> Took daily age 23 to week before 27th bday, around 4 years.
> Symptoms while on medication: unusual "ache" in one testicle (scanned showed nothing), itchy and occasional oozing from one nipple.
> Libido, thoughts about sex, erections all remained the same as before.
> TEST RESULTS while TAKING finasteride: BLOOD ONLY
> Free = 764 (225-725)
> Total = 28.3 (8.3-30.2)
> DHA-S= 5.0 (3-10.5)
> SHBG = 24 (13-71)
> FSH: 2 (1-10)
> LH: 4 (1-10)
> Oestradiol: 117 (50-150)
>
>
> Stopped Finasteride due to increased education, understanding.
> Symptoms since stopping:
> Reduced libido, reduced thoughts about sex, reduced "noticing" women, reduced number of morning erections, but still get's erections.
> Pain in testicle has gone as has nipple sensation.
> TEST RESULTS after medication: ALL BLOOD TESTS only:
> Free: 203 (225-725) (was 764!!!)
> Total: 9.8 (8.3-30.2) (was 28.3)
> DHEAS: 5.2 (3-10.5)
> SHBG:30 (13-71)
>
> FSH: 1 (1-10)
> LH: 1 (1-10)
> PROG: 0.6 (1.2-4.8)
> Oestradiol: 51 (50-150)
> low neutrophils, low platelets, low wbc
>
> Now weighs 167lbs (lost 11lbs), noticable decrease in muscle mass (not strength), DEXA scan 6% BF.
> Sleeps fine, asleep in 5-10mins. Hair not falling out.
>
> Thank you so much for you help and assistance. Really appreciate it.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-15T21:06:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-post-finasteride-syndorme-experience-info-in-the-forum.9290/post-105784>

Comfortably Numb helped me though labor with my second child and enabled me to do a drug free delivery. I am thinking tomorrow's CT will be in candle light listening to Pink Floyd. Sorrow is a kick ass song too.

### Inger — 2014-01-09T09:39:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oh-the-stupid-stop-just-stop-with-your-stupid-smart-phone.9294/post-105135>

The reason I am still in this house.. is mainly because my parents think technology is, kind of... Antichrist (among other things.. they have many rules..lol) and they happily listen to me when I talk about its dangers - for me this is a blessing! Every time I look for somewhere else to live I get freaked out about all the wifis and stuff surrounding any apartment or home that is close to neighbors. I have to wait for having enough money or the opportunity to live in a place those things are far from me! I feel very lucky I have this place and other "issues" feel rather small.. because I see what is coming
sure here are towers (a few km away) and smart meters too (have installed a filter on the house but IDK if it helps much), but no wifi's and not much technology either. Still, I feel a big difference being out in the wild / here
I am working from home now too. It is not a risk less thing I have jumped into but, it might be worth it. Means a little more time on the computer but I still think it is a better choice than working in the big city as I did before.... I feel this weird urge to not go in there (into the city) if I can avoid it. I just have to balance increased computer time with more time outside etc.

it is like everyone just HAVE to have those smart phones and toys

if being bombarded with radiation impacts our thinking and screws it.. it is pretty serious what is going on

This was my favorite Pink Floyd album ...The Dark Side Of The Moon

[http://www.amazon.com/The-Dark-Side-Of-Moon/dp/B004ZN9RWK](http://www.amazon.com/The-Dark-Side-Of-Moon/dp/B004ZN9RWK)

### nicld — 2014-01-09T16:30:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105161>

Ok, ANY are awesome. Start with you tube and see which ones you might like. Yes, there are many versions of some of the most popular songs but most of them are live recordings.

One of my favorites is Delicate Sound of Thunder, a double live album and then Pulse, another live double album. Pulse has the whole album The Dark Side of the Moon played live. The Wall is pretty good. I am a big fan of the David Gilmour stuff and the the real early stuff it a bit trippy for me.

Maybe I can try to send you a song or two since I have pretty much ALL of them. Just let me know what you might want.

One of my fantasies is to be a background singer for Pink Floyd. They don't need to turn the mic on, just let me be on stage with them

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-10T16:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105280>

I love their entire anthology.

Dark side of the moon is epic, as is Wish you were here.

For me personally - Momentary Lapse of Reason was a profound album I bought the tape at a local record store on the release date. I wore that damn thing out. IT was a pivotal album in my teen years.

As I was beginning my career in the Air Force they Released Division Bell. That Album has stayed with me. the lyrics on that album resonated as I was blooming as an adult.

And of course if you get Dark Side of the Moon - you can sync it up and play it while you watch the Wizard of Oz muted. Cool synchronicity totally worth watching just to watch it. I was shocked that DH never did that...

"What do you want from me"....................................... good song

I have done a lot of dumb stuff in my life and have learned from everything so I try to say that I have no regrets but NOT buying tickets to see Pink Floyd on their Pulse tour when they were in Madison WI and Camp Randall is my one regret in life. My hubby lived one block from the stadium and we partook in some adult beverages before the concert since a bunch of his friends had tickets. We started to walk around the stadium seeing if there were tickets available and we found some but I would have had to walk a ways to get some cash and was getting tired from drinking all day so we passed (STUPID, STUPID, STUPID ).

Now, my only hope to seeing them is to either see Roger Waters (which I heard is really good) or David Gilmour if they ever tour again.

> Big Giant Leering Head

Poland 1973
I was already in USA, picking off gold nuggets lying freely on the streets.
At the time my DW was my DGF and still in Poland.
She was professional classically train ballerina. #3 in the troop, right after white and black swans.

On my request she visited my parents (for the first time).
My parents lived on a small farm, not much different from Afghanistan caves, minus head gear.
She went there in thigh high, red leather boots with fat high heels.
Between miniskirt and top of boots you could see fish net pantyhose.
She was able to charm not only my parents but also our guard dog.

......
Today, the air is 66 Â°F and fog is dense on beach of Myrtle Beach, sea temperature is 69 Â°F.
Wet sand and sea water fills out my body with electrons.
[http://weather.yahoo.com/united-states/south-carolina/myrtle-beach-12770270/](http://weather.yahoo.com/united-states/south-carolina/myrtle-beach-12770270/)
[http://www.surf-forecast.com/breaks/Myrtle-Beach/seatemp](http://www.surf-forecast.com/breaks/Myrtle-Beach/seatemp)
..

### freesia — 2014-01-11T21:00:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105376>

I spent my some of my formative years (late teens to mid twenties) in London and in the Middle East. I saw Eric Clapton at the Sultan's pool in Jerusalem...then again at The Deed Sea. Outdoor concerts in beautiful weather are my favourite.
I saw the Floyd at Wembley (Momentary Lapse of Reason Tour) when I was 18.
Lotsa others...but just the one for Pink Floyd and it wasn't quite the 'same' without Roger Waters.

### Josh — 2014-01-12T04:23:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105419>

Consider most rock kills plants, I still like it but it makes me tired after a while. Classical through Stravinsky has transcendent beauty, after him things have gone a bit odd with the exception of Copeland and Gershwin who was more of a jazz classicist. I have been partial to the Grateful Dead, Country and Western and classic jazz. I play all of it poorly. The music that made the plants grow best that is palatable to most humans is Indian Classical...The Sonic Bloom stuff that is now used commercially is not really for humans, but the plants seem to love it. Sacred music of all types works when one is available to the sacred and I have found the sacred in rock and roll in the right state. I will confess that I listened to a Bossa Nova version of Guns and Roses greatest hits daily for a year while doing coffee enemas, do with it what you may. I wore purple overalls to the Met and sat in the 6th row center for 2 years with my grandfather's subscription after he died, I do not think I ever got why I was there. Years later, I saw the Ring Cycle from way up high and loved it. The funny thing is that as I have continued to meditate, all music sounds new, no matter how many times I have heard it. One of those little blessings and paradoxes....

### prAna303 — 2014-01-12T19:27:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105464>

Since we are into the subject of music, resonance is as i said in others posts very important for me. These waves makes me work but then i also wonder and feel like this.

How do you listen to music?

What kind of transporter and recreator of the music do you use?

Most new stuff is made with modern technology, digital ways of sampling and creating new tunes. This would in my way of thinking be very wrong. When you sample something you cut off the small details that would take space in digital era. Everything odd and analog is simply removed and made simple.

For me, this makes me feel ill, almost angry. Not just in my way of thinking but also physical, hate to listen to music the modern way. The headphone world, no good for this boy.

The only way to me then is, live with living humans and with good HiFi and vinyl.

When i dance i can tolerate more of the digital stuff because i use it as a tool, a way to connect motion into sound but at home, with a glass of bordeaux wine, candles, vinyl is the way.

I mentioned this somewhere, digital sampling and digital presentation is like a virus for me, a computer virus that makes me feel odd.

### Hope — 2014-01-13T01:27:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105507>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105501)
>
> I am trying to process all this...... I think I am worried. It seems I like more mellow stuff - does that mean I have less energy? I don't think I do ..... I feel like I have all this energy bubbling under the surface.
>
> When I go to the Opera - it feels to me like I am greatly enriched and energised in every pore of my being .... I can't contain myself .... like I want to jump out of my skin.
>
> Like being in New York City ......I love every single second of just walking the streets and absorbing it all.
>
> I have to think more...

Audrey, for me....when I am tired, I cannot handle fast music much, or even any (I am talking super tired). I do wonder about a correlation - it's like my brain does not want it then. My acupuncturist says, silence is very healing for the kidneys. I also think about the opposite - some people listen to super fast music to be able to do things, have more energy. When I used to run, fast songs would inspire me to keep going. One of my favorite MDs, Bernie Siegel, who has helped a lot of cancer patients, uses a lot of classical music in his practice to help people calm down or something. I can't remember exactly what he said. My DH on the other hand, who is actually a pro musician (or could be, he has his masters in performance from an excellent school though he does it only for fun now), LOVES his fusion jazz and anything fast. When dating, I was OK listening to that music....now that we have been married and I got sicker over the years (especially since we moved to DC in an equally moldy home), I could not hear his music mostly...it is getting better now. Only the classical stuff which thankfully he loves too bc he grew up on it.

That you love being in NYC....is great. I wouldn't. Been there many times, but now I would always rather choose nature. I must be old. LOL.

### freesia — 2014-01-13T02:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105509>

Has anyone noticed they can find the right note and pitch much more effortlessly (with their voice) since starting CT?
I have, and at least one other person I know doing CT has mentioned finding the same thing.

My CT embracing youngest child (just 9) made stunning progress playing a classical stringed instrument last year. He's been playing 8 months, and he's playing the same as kids who've ben playing that many years. Also, he taught himself simultaneous translation between clefs (give him music for one instrument and he'll play another) and understanding of how to work harmonies. I think he's likely extra tuned in and I'm thinking CT could be playing a helpful role here...maybe also his natural instinct for what heals (he has some neuro-physical challenges as part of his 'package'). We have never asked him to practise, he's just drawn to it - the way he was to cold.
Maybe I have it backwards yet - the 'seeking what heals' bit - but he's been fascinating for us, and his teachers, to watch.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105542>

I know there are apps to convert existing music to the A=432 frequency, but I wish there was something I could use on my stereo. The A=432 music I can find are mostly meditation CDs or whale songs. I was looking for people songs.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-13T16:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105545>

You might like to go to RikBarron.com....his last three cds are in 432..one is an instrumental and the other is trad/folk (plus his last children's folk cd). No whales or chants but great music all the same...just remember he's my husband, so if you don't like it....keep it to yourself  Actually, I wouldn't mind at all...music is all about what makes you sing inside and we are all different.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-13T17:09:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105547>

Thanks, SeaHorse! So "Speechless" and "Over in the Meadow" are in 432? I am interested in the instrumental. I see he has a hymn in there. I liked what I heard of the samples.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-13T17:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105550>

"Speechless" "Never so Far" and "Over in the Meadow" are all in 432...he won't record in anything else now, but when he plays with other people he still has to tune up. We joke that that 432 is the secret key to the universe and he's out to take over the world one note at a time...but there is no doubt that it changes the experience of sound for both the listener and the player...subtle energies that have a lot of depth. The difference between listening with your head and listening with your heart.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-13T17:44:58+0200
