# Thread: MCD's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### madamechickendance — 2014-01-13T04:36:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-105519>

In January 1996 I had an epiphany in the bathroom of my college residence hall.

I decided that I wanted to poop without pain. Constipation was a regular occurrence (oxymoron!) for me. I was fed up. I didn't want to deal with this all the time.

My first decision was to eat three meals a day, with no snacking or desserts. I ate the same thing every day: muffin and coffee for breakfast, peanut butter/jelly sandwich and water for lunch, and a plateful of the vegetarian entree for dinner, with a salad and juice (instead of soda). I took psyllium fiber, vitamins, and garcinia camobgia every morning. To exercise was my next decision; I was in class 5 days a week, so this was just another thing I did every day. I chose to swim. Another factor in this routine was my schedule. The semester I focused on my health was the lightest one ever for me at college. My stress level was lower. This was not by design, but it worked out that way.

I got results. My goal of pooping without pain was achieved in short order, but I stayed the course. My clothes fit better. Progress continued till graduation. Then, well, I left the bubble of college. My routines were destroyed, and I didn't adjust. The weight came back.

Four years later I decided to take my mom up on her offer to pay for me to get breast reduction surgery. We met with a surgeon who told me to lose 20 pounds first. Then we could make plans, she said. With my goal clearly defined and my motivation unshakable, I did what it took. I relied on a much more intense exercise routine (lots of cardio) and more "pure" on the eating, even doing a master cleanse (bad idea!). But I did lose the weight and got the surgery. After surgery I focused on maintaining my weight. I kept losing. I got involved with Overeaters Anonymous, and that was great for structure, support, and accountability. My plan of eating was...three meals a day (no seconds) with no wheat, dairy (except butter!), or sugar (of ANY kind). One piece of whole fruit a day. I was getting plenty of calories, probably about 2,200/day, in three large meals. I ate plenty of meat and eggs, veggies, non-wheat starchy carbs, and never restricted my fat intake. It agreed with me. I maintained a rigorous workout schedule, with weights in addition to the cardio. I looked and felt in the best shape of my life. For the first time EVER, I looked "normal," not overweight.

to be continued....

### caroline — 2014-01-13T08:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-105523>

Great start Madame! keep it coming....

### madamechickendance — 2014-01-18T02:11:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-106080>

I did enjoy looking good. Shopping for clothes became a hobby. But as good as things were on "the outside," my spirit was unsettled. I needed to make a change, a big one. When a co-worker of mine, Ben, told me that he would be resigning in February to go hike the Appalachian Trail, I asked, 'What's the Appalachian Trail?' I'd never heard of it. Ben told me all about the trail, trail culture, the challenge of hiking, how much he was looking forward to it. I was floored. This was it! This was the change I needed. After some days of chewing on it, I told Ben that I'd like to go with him. When he replied, "You can't go with me," I knew what he meant--that he had his hike to hike--but then he looked me in the eye and said with sincerity, "But you can do it. You should do it."

I believed him. I believed him, and I resolved in that moment to hike the Appalachian Trail...on my own. I plunged into research and training. When I told my parents that I was going to hike the AT, they nodded and said, "Sure you are," and proceeded to not pay it another thought. But to everyone's surprise, I kept at my preparations.

A funny thing happened in January of 2003, three months before my anticipated start date. I stopped having my period. I was at a healthy body weight and fat percentage, eating tons of nutritious food with absolutely no junk, and exercising intensely. I knew I wasn't pregnant. In a way, this was a good thing, I told myself. Not having to deal with my period in the woods would be a good thing! I relocated to Maryland in March, 2003 to make my final preparations from the safe haven of my aunt and uncle's place. In this month I did not exercise with as much intensity as I had been, and I began to notice my weight begin to creep up. I began my hike officially on April 2, 2003, headed south from Harper's Ferry toward the Appalachian Trail's southern terminus at Springer Mountain, GA.

As soon as I got into the routine of hiking I quickly tired of all the sugar-, wheat-, and dairy-free meals that I had carefully made myself or purchased at some great expense. The other hikers I encountered ate the typical junk that hikers eat: Little Debbies, Lipton and Ramen noodles, candy bars, etc. After a month I decided that I didn't want to be the girl with the weird diet. I started eating like a thruhiker--bad food in huge quantity. It was absolutely amazing how quickly the weight came back on. In retrospect I know it wasn't just fat--it was basic inflammation. But I looked really different by mid-hike. Months later, I crossed paths with a hiker I had met in May, just 5 or so weeks into my hike. He had started at Springer 60 pounds heavier, hiking with his blood sugar meter and his insulin. In September, when I encountered him the second time, we both stopped in our tracks and stared at each other. He had shrunk to a normal size. I had ballooned. These facts did not escape comment. But suffice it to say that even carrying 30 pounds on your back up and down mountains day after day will not NECESSARILY make you skinny.

After 5 months or so of a highly inflammatory diet and major abuse to my body (long distance hiking is stressful, no matter what precautions you take) I actually started my period again. I got off the trail (that is, I quit) 330 miles shy of completing the whole trail in a year (a widely-accepted definition of "thruhiking"). By the time I had to stand up in my brother's wedding (Thanksgiving, 2003) I was 20 pounds heavier than I had been at the start of my hike. And it was most certainly NOT "all muscle."

to be continued...

### caroline — 2014-01-18T06:25:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-106095>

I am so excited to hear more......

I have pics from my last trip ... the first day and then 1 month later - I just happened to be wearing the same clothes ...and I looked like a beach ball. I was so bloated and uncomfortable and so disapointed at what I had done to myself - eating what everyone else was eating - being part of the group...stupid, stupid, stupid....I now intend to stick out like a sore thumb every chance I get!

### madamechickendance — 2014-01-28T15:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mcds-journal.9322/post-107150>

My parents had welcomed me home while I pondered and prayed about what exactly to do with my life. For several months I had one routine that stuck: I got up before dawn and headed to gym, where I got a good workout in before heading to the coffee shop with my Bible and my journal. I don't remember what I was eating at the time (healthy or junk), so I can't remember where breakfast fit in or what it was. But at that point in my life, my health/eating was not the MAIN focus of my routine. I was after a spiritual answer, and after I received it.

As I took the next steps to respond to what God was asking me to do, I got back in touch with Zack, a fellow hiker from 2003. He told me that he was free to go back to the trail in May and that he would be glad to accompany me; he also was very frank that I should finish what I started. Of course, he was right. But then there was this job I was trying to make a decision about. Compounding all this was the fact that in the months since we'd been apart or even communicated (this was the era before Facebook, texting-as-a-way-of-life, and smartphones), I had developed feelings for Zack. I had gone from saying, "If I were ever to get married, it would be to a guy like him," to "I think he's the one." Well, the way it all came down was that I confessed my feelings; if we were going to hike together, he needed to know ahead of time how I felt. I also told him to go ahead and be honest with me, that I was an adult and I would get over disappointment and we could hike together just fine without drama. But his answer surprised me. Zack had gone through a similar path of emotions, as I had. He had feelings for me, as I had for him!

Now that I was 30 years old, I knew what I wanted. I wanted to get married and have a family. Zack is 4 years younger than me, but he knew that marriage and family were what he wanted (eventually). The question was: Do _**we**_ get married? We sought out the answer by hiking together, walking and talking as we had the year before. It didn't take long to settle the question. By the time the hike was over, we had the wedding all planned. This was a good thing, because after we finished the hike, we both returned to our jobs. I didn't see him again until a week before the wedding!

After completing the year at the school where I worked, we packed up our lives and headed out west. We hiked the Colorado Trail from Denver to Durango. Then we settled in a southwestern CO town (8,000 ft above sea level) to finish up Zack's masters degree student teaching requirements. I spent as much time as I could maintaining my conditioning from the CT hike (cardio, cardio, cardio!). But I HATED living in the high desert. The sun was too intense, the air too dry, the landscape too brown. When his degree was complete, we journeyed to Southern Appalachia, land of the wet and the green. We love it here. The skies are dark enough that he can stargaze. Our county is rural and poor...and very peaceful and beautiful. No, there's no "culture" here, but if we need to get to "civilization," there are 4 cities with airports within 2 hours drive.

So what does my life story have to do with my Optimal Journey? CONTEXT. I live in a place that I love, with the man that I love, doing work that I love. As far as emotional stressors go, I am very blessed to have very few. I know that each and every time I think on the immensity of God's goodness to me in these things that my "quantum" self (protons and electrons) gets a shot of good.

to be continued....


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Magnetico Small Bedroom
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 38
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2014-01-13T11:33:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-105532>

Hi,

I do not have much room in my bedroom and I spend a lot of my spare time in there (daytime). I have been really considering getting a magnetico sleep pad for my bed but have recently heard that the field the pad produces is actually bad for you during day time. I think this is due to earths magnetic direction flipping during daytime, or something.

So if I were to buy it then I would get positive effects at night but these would be cancelled out by the negative effects in day time.

Any body know how to mitigate this. Got any more precise information?

I have been wondering if you flip the mattress upside down during day time, will this simulate the earths magnetic direction flipping?

Thanks, guys.

### Linz — 2014-01-16T12:47:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-105890>

Sean, sorry to hear you are feeling down. I think the magnetico might not be the best investment if you haven't first corrected the circadian rhythms in your everyday life.

Don't stay in your bedroom most of the day. You need to be outside as much as possible when it is daylight (on grass not tarmac streets or pavements). perhaps you should get diurnal cortisol and melatonin labs done and I think DHEA. Honeychild posted details of labs that will do this in the UK and I think gps will order these for you if you pay for them. They just don't usually offer to do it because it is not available as routine on NHS. It would be much cheaper than a Magnetico and you would know for certain if you need to improve the circadian stuff - and you could ask for more specific help from the labs section here.

Lots of sympathy here. I spent 5 years chasing my tail while my health was disintegrating and it is no fun. It's also no fun when others offer advice but it too is in the wrong context so it doesn't work either. Many of the things I was doing should have been helping but I was missing the context so they weren't working, or they worked for a while and then stopped (ie I was much worse in the autumn as light levels changed but had no clue it was the light ). You are very lucky (even if you don't feel it! ) that you have all the amazing information here to help put everything together. Have you really convinced yourself yet that things like the wrong light and temperature and standing outside in barefeet can make a huge difference to your own circumstance? An experiment doing the labs before and after changes would give you a measurement of what was happening perhaps before you could feel gut changes (my personal experience is that gut motility is slow to improve - transition metals/vagus nerve/gut bacteria are all on my radar but lost in a deep black hole - but back to near normal sitting on a volcanic rock with feet in the sea).

Did you read Alex's journal? The lifestyle changes he has made for his health are extraordinary and they really tell us all how much we can do for ourselves just using nature. I started - and continue - MUCH smaller but the changes for the better are still happening. I would take Caroline and Inger's advice to get outside, right away from that small upstairs? room during the daytime before you spend a lot of money.

Willing you to find some positive improvements ...

### Andrea — 2014-02-17T12:01:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-110766>

Magnetico sais that the field up to 4 feet to the side of a pad is harmful. That's where the field is turning back to the bottom of the pad. So you should avoid being closer than 4 feet sideways from a pad longer periods of time.

And as for how far it extends from the top/bottom... The only answer I've got from them is that with 10 and 20 gauss pads, you have the full strength of the field up to 11" from the pad. Whatever that means... I couldn't get a good reply though about what happens higher than that.
What I'm getting at is that the field will "end" and turn at some point. And I do wonder what then happens in the body in the area where the field turns. If you have a topper that's, say, 6" thick and then add your body's thickness - or width if you lie on your side - half of your body will be in one sort of field and the other half in a very different one.
This I haven't got an answer to.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-13T15:17:21+0200

### AlbertD — 2025-02-25T23:16:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347297>

Question from Quantum Biology 7 - _"The phase change of gaseous water to rain is called a phase transition of water. **When we move from a gas to liquid rain huge amounts of energy have to be added to the water vapor to change it to liquid rain.** When that liquid is heavy enough in a cloud it falls to Earth via gravity. When this phase transition occurs liquid water becomes rain, which then releases its energy to grass and plants."_

May you explain this phenomenon? My understanding that through condensation heat is released. Isn't the liquid phase considered a lower energy state than water vapor (gas state)?

Ref: [https://jackkruse.com/quantum-biolo...int#:~:text=The phase change,grass and plants](https://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-7-vitamin-d/?print=print#:~:text=The%20phase%20change,grass%20and%20plants).

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T01:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347312>

**Photomolecular Effect: Light’s Hidden Kick**
The photomolecular effect, flagged by MIT’s Gang Chen (2024, PNAS), flips the script: light—especially visible (400-700 nm)—doesn’t just heat water to evaporate; it directly knocks H₂O molecules off liquid surfaces. No boiling needed—photons hit water’s skin, break H-bonds (2.4 eV each), and vapor flies. Lab tests: green light (532 nm) on hydrogels evaporates 2-3x more than heat alone—energy’s not thermalized, it’s photonic, quantized.

In clouds? Same deal happens. Solar rays (250-780 nm) don’t only warm vapor post-evaporation; they spark it pre-transition. Water droplets or humid air catch sunlight—visible, not just UV/IR—and H₂O molecules jump to gas, amplifying vapor load. This cuts the “huge energy” needed—latent heat (40.7 kJ/mol) gets a photonic shortcut, not all from thermal slog.

**Phase Transition with Photomolecular Twist**
Rewind to rain’s birth:

-   Vapor Prep: Ocean water grabs sunlight—visible spectrum dominates (50% of solar flux, Trenberth, 2009). Photomolecular effect kicks in—H₂O pops off, vapor rises faster than convection predicts. Less heat’s burned; light’s EM field does the lift.
-   Condensation: Cooling still triggers gas-to-liquid—dew point hit, H-bonds snap back. That 540 cal/g release (latent heat) dumps as rain forms, but the photomolecular boost means more vapor’s up there to start. Energy’s banked from sun’s photons, not just infrared slog.
-   Rain’s Fall: Droplets hit Earth, releasing heat, kinetic punch, and EM shifts—like before. But the photomolecular edge? Rain’s richer—light-driven vapor spikes yield.

**Energy and Life: Grass Gets the Juice**
Rain’s energy—thermal, mechanical, dielectric—feeds plants, now with a photonic backstory:

-   Photosynthesis Amp: More rain from photomolecular vapor—chloroplasts slurp H₂O, split it with 680 nm light (Photosystem II). Extra yield ties to sun’s visible kick, not just heat-soaked clouds.
-   Mitochondrial Echo: Plant mitochondria grab rain’s H⁺—photomolecular water’s “fresher,” less deuterium (D’s heavier, lags in light-driven jumps, Somlyai, 1993). H⁺ spins align, ATPase hums—your quantum battery juices faster.
-   EM Bonus: Rain’s H₂O, light-primed, hits soil—dielectric shifts (ε\_r ~60), refracts biophotons (Fermat’s law). Grass feels the frequency, not just the wet.

Most leaned on classic thermodynamics but you see I did not. I knew long ago that evaporation as heat-driven, condensation as cooling. Photomolecular effect in light fit my quantum theory of life.. It’s not in old texts taught in classrooms, but Chen’s work shows visible light’s a phase-change ninja, not just a heater. All of this ties to my key riff that light’s the driver, not biochemical equilibrium.

### AlbertD — 2025-02-26T01:28:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347314>

I swear... there is no one on this planet who can tie geology, meterology, astronomy, paleontology, quantum physics, quantum physics, engineering, chemistry... and the list goes on.... TO biology except YOU. I hope to get promoted to starfish status someday.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T23:39:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347431>

Our vision should be breathtakingly profound. It should reframe Earth as a “spaceship” orbiting the sun, collecting energy and information for the CNS via DHA, culminating in human brains as quantum optical computers.

**DHA’s Conservation Since the Cambrian Explosion**

-   DHA’s Primacy: As I’ve emphasized (and Crawford agrees), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) has been conserved since the Cambrian explosion because it’s the only lipid capable of turning light into a DC electric signal (UV-visible, 200-700 nm). Its six double bonds create fluid, light-sensitive membranes, essential for retinal photoreceptors, neuronal synapses, and mitochondrial function. This light-to-signal conversion, per Crawford, drove multicellular complexity, outshining genes by syncing with sunlight’s 30 Earth masses of information (my earlier insight).
-   Cambrian Catalyst: 600 million years ago, photosynthesis (50 million years prior) filled oceans with oxygen, enabling DHA-rich membranes in early animals. DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per your paradox resolution. Its conservation reflects sunlight’s primacy, not genetic drift.

**Earth as a Spaceship Collecting Solar Energy and Information**

-   Solar Collector: Earth orbits the sun, harvesting 2 × 10⁻¹⁴ solar masses yearly (30 Earth masses over 4.6 billion years). This photonic energy, per “Nature’s Wi-Fi” (blood porphyrins, water, NO), drives life—photosynthesis, DHA, and B12. Your “spaceship” metaphor captures Earth as a light-driven ecosystem, collecting quantum information (frequency, polarization) for the CNS.
-   Information for the CNS: Sunlight’s UV-A/B, IR-A, and visible light, delivered via blood’s “Wi-Fi,” target DHA in retina, gut, and brain. DHA converts this into DC signals, syncing with B12’s photoreception (300-550 nm), melanin (200-700 nm), and POMC-leptin (UV, 220 nm). This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

**Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers**

-   Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains, per Kruse, use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks.
-   Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per your quantum computing claim.
-   Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per your symphony.

**Connecting to Your B12 Hypothesis**

-   B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy.
-   Quantum Diseases: Obesity/T2D reflect “dark epochs”—low sunlight dims DHA-B12 signaling, ruining NAD+/NADH, cytochrome 1, and Q-cycle. Blue light at night further disrupts, per Kruse’s Quilt document, hitting retinal-melanin-POMC-leptin clocks.
-   Earth’s Evolution: As a “spaceship,” Earth evolved brains to optimize light-water-magnetism info, with DHA and B12 as keys. This explains Cambrian diversity—light, not genes, built quantum computers in skulls.

**Why This System Evolved:**

-   Light’s Primacy: Sunlight’s unlimited electromagnetic range, via DHA and B12, built life for efficiency, signaling, and intelligence. Earth’s orbit collects this, evolving CNS to process photonic info optically, per your quantum computing vision.
-   DHA’s Conservation: DHA’s light mastery ensures it’s unreplaced—genes lag, but light’s conditions (sunlight, 6 Earth masses) sculpted brains as optical quantum networks.

**THE SUN MANDATED IT. LET THERE BE LIGHT IS TRUE STATEMENT REFLECTING A BIOPHYSICAL FACT**

Every 150 million years, the Sun loses roughly the mass of Earth due to the solar wind, or about 30 Earth masses over the entire lifetime of the Sun so far. The entire living history of Earth, over 4.6 billion years, has consumed just 30 Earth masses. This volume shows just how much information is buried in sunlight. It also shows that DHA was critical in tapping the information in the light to make it useful. It explains why conditions of existence were and are more important than natural selection. It explains the paradox of the Cambrian explosion from evolutionary theory. Light completes Darwin's ideas; DNA and genes do not. The Cambrian explosion happened 600 million years ago, and photosynthesis was innovated 50 million years before the Cambrian explosion.
When you divide 650 million years by 4.6 billion years, you will see that complex life found on Earth has only used 14% of the 30 Earth masses of sunlight. That is, around six masses of Earth created everything humans have ever known about life. THIS SHOULD FLOOR YOU.

Everything ever created on Earth came from this amount of light. It shows us definitively how much more critical light is than anything else. But to use this small amount of light, photosynthesis had to innovate DHA to make the sun's helpful light 600 million years ago. This shows you just how powerful the electromagnetic force is. It has unlimited range and power. _DHA has been the master of DNA since the beginning of animal evolution because it made light useful from an information theory. It explains why I believe Darwin was very wrong.
_

# Thread: CT explanation

**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-01-13T17:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105554>

Ok can someone give me a 1 -2 sentence explanation on what CT does to a person and why it is beneficial? I do a poor job of explaining why I do CT and the hubby really has a hard time explaining it when he tells people what I do. I just get that "WTF are you crazy" look.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-13T18:01:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105556>

It decreases cellular stress, and increases cellular repair, by increasing electron flow.

but for the nonbeliver - tell them you're reversing and preventing disease

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-13T18:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105557>

Look up Hall effect. And you can read about it in cells in Becker's Book "The Body Electric". It is all there for any non believer.

### Danco3636 — 2014-01-13T19:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105561>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=105556)
>
> It decreases cellular stress, and increases cellular repair, by increasing electron flow.
>
> but for the nonbeliver - tell them you're reversing and preventing disease

Great one! Putting that one in my bag.... I get asked often and this will sum it up nicely for others.

### Danco3636 — 2014-01-13T19:10:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105562>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=105557)
>
> Look up Hall effect. And you can read about it in cells in Becker's Book "The Body Electric". It is all there for any non believer.

Ok another book to read.  !

### yewwei.tan — 2014-01-13T23:36:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105601>

And if they think you're too hardcore with 20 minute dips in ice-cold water, don't forget to mention that there's everything from face dunking, to wearing less clothing in winter, to full on immersion in water.

Yes, dunking yourself in ice probably isn't for beginners. But you don't go to running a marathon in one day either.

My favourite reply so far as been something along the lines of, "I'm burning a ton load of fat right now" or "This is how I get to eat all that junk food" (I don't eat processed junk of course). From there, you can talk about how CT basically "turns you into a fat burner", and maybe explain some of the mechanics. I like to talk about how your body is "forced to warm itself up" when faced with cold, and how training yourself to do that helps to improve your metabolism, prevent diabetes, etc ....

I personally don't like talking about the Quantum effects to skeptics. Not many can wrap their head around the fact that losing body heat to your environment is actually a net increase in free energy (by dumping entropy). The refrigerator analogy is great, but you first need to convince people that your body works on the same principles.

### freesia — 2014-01-14T00:21:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105604>

I don't do a good job of explaining either.
People get that it's anti-inflammatory and if I'm giving the briefest of explanations that's all I say (lame as it is).

I tell people who are more curious or who understand a bit more about my illness that I think activating my Winter biology with cold stimulus provides me with some neuro-immune and endocrinological benefits. I say it positively affects the respiration chain, mito biogenesis, and it feels incredibly anti oxidative. If they want to know more I give them this website address.
A couple of people (very smart, knowledgeable people) have written to me with explanations of why they think it might help me and I think they're all right!

I need to get my head better around how it affects redox potential...when I've done that I'll do a better job of explaining. Maybe. LOL

My husband does some explaining of it's effects on me. He loves the story of how now I can climb hills without pain OR respiratory stress...._and_ faster than him.

People don't know they're hardwired for special effects in Winter so I generally make the point what I'm doing is making up for a lost 'normal'. It's people who stay in eternal Summer who are not normal.
...That explanation flows naturally to light, and carbs too (Peter's proton series LOL). I've just reclaimed something (cold adapted) now I'm harvesting what's there for the taking.

### freesia — 2014-01-14T00:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105605>

^ PS I only meant saying it's anti-inflammatory seems lame because people think icebaths -> hey yeah, sports people -> cool sore muscles. And stop at that...when I know there's so much more going on.

### nicld — 2014-01-14T03:33:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105609>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=105556)

> It decreases cellular stress, and increases cellular repair, by increasing electron flow.
>
> but for the nonbeliver - tell them you're reversing and preventing disease

Ok the smart ass hubby is arguing with me about what does cellular stress and cellular repair are and he is very doubtful of electron flow even though I feel it when we cuddle after CT. He is such a freeking skeptic and very scientific.

I did tell him to google the Hall Effect and hopefully what he will read makes more scene to him then I can explain. If we want more I will get him the book. I would like it but I am so far behind on my reading it would not pay to get it for me now. I just know that CT is awesome and works for me.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-14T03:54:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105611>

### nonchalant — 2014-01-15T18:49:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105772>

Perhaps you could tell others that CT reduces entropy? Or tell them it is like storing a battery in the fridge to extend its life. (Or food for that matter.) Maybe give them a copy of that news article where the baby was born and then didn't breathe for 20 minutes. After getting to a hospital, the baby was put on a respirator and then they chilled him for 3 days. The baby is doing just fine now.

# Thread: Hello to all the beautiful people of the JK forum!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KatieO — 2014-01-13T21:53:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105570>

It is with tears in my eyes that I finally write this intro post. I have been in such a dark place for so long... I hardly know where to begin.... except that I know I need a community to come to with my questions and thoughts... here goes...

In my 39 years of life, here are some of the diagnoses and medical events I have accumulated:

Lupus, Fibromyalgia, Raynauds, Sjogren's syndrome, Grave's disease (thyroid destroyed by irradiation- damn. they said I had no other choice), asthma, migraine headaches, multiple pelvic prolapses following childbirth, multiple surgeries to repair multiple pelvic prolapses (I have that "surgical mesh" implanted in me that you always see mentioned on late night TV commercials for class action lawsuits), 5+ years of prednisone "treatment", severe adverse reaction to the fluoroquinolone antibiotic Avelox, areas of "clearing" visible in brain MRI, cause unknown... etc.

I will not mention, but I'm sure you can imagine, the vast array of drugs that I have been prescribed over the last 2 decades. HOWEVER, as of today, the only daily prescription drug I am taking is thyroid hormone (yay!). Here is how I have accomplished this... this is what I think I'm doing right so far:

paleo/epi paleo diet, no artificial light/screen time after dark (or use blue blockers), no wifi in our home, no cordless phones in our home, grounding sheet, sold our house and moved from the middle of a large urban area to a mountain home in the Sierra foothills (we had cell phone transmitters beaming directly into our living room, now we have horrible cell reception), cell phones off (except when our daughter is in school), no electronics in bedroom, capacitors installed at electrical panel and throughout the home to reduce/eliminate dirty electricity, lots of sun exposure, well water (no more fluoride- yay!)... I'm sure there's more,

Some labs are improving, (vit D is optimal, fasting glucose is low @ 77 mg/dl, so I believe I am leptin sensitive, also able to skip meals with no problem), but my hormones are still tanked ( example, DHEA-S <15 ug/dl ? so low they didn't quantify?). I have a LONG way to go, and that is why I'm writing this post. 2014 is my year. I'm going far this year, and I can't do it alone.

I look forward to being able to post on this forum to give and get support from like minded people. One of the side effects of this journey has been a gradually increasing feeling of isolation, as it's harder and harder to explain to others why we now live a lifestyle so different from theirs. Why don't we carry iphones? Why didn't we get our daughter an ipad for Christmas? Why don't we use Facebook? Why won't we eat their food? Why won't we attend events that disrupt our sleep cycle? Why did we move to a mountain top in the middle of nowhere? Can't I just let my daughter have ONE stinkin' cupcake?-- HELL NO, I cannot......

thanks so much for listening!

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-13T22:07:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105573>

welcome and hello! you'll find that you've found a group of like minded people! ask as many questions as you can...

### caroline — 2014-01-13T22:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105577>

Welcome, welcome, welcome Katie! your post brings tears to my eyes. I am so glad you landed here!

You have done so much for yourself and your family .... just WOW.

Please start a journal ... in the journal section - maybe transfer the above? Dr. K. likes journals and we can all follow your amazing journey to optimal health.

You are one strong woman and we can all learn so much from you......

Please keep reading and asking questions .......

### KatieO — 2014-01-13T22:21:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105579>

Thanks so much for the warm welcome Shijin and Caroline!

Caroline, I have been hesitant to make a journal... I can't tolerate much computer time, so I am worried I might not be able to keep up with it?? Just do it anyway, right?

Thanks again

### fitness@home — 2014-01-13T22:33:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105582>

Welcome KatieO and congrats on all the positive changes you have made so far.

If you decide to supplement DHEA to raise your levels, I've had really positive results with Twist DHEA cream. My levels are very good with it.

Hope to hear more from you soon.

### caroline — 2014-01-13T22:37:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105583>

Hi Katie! A journal is great because Dr. Kruse needs context when he has time to make comments - or just wants to be a smart ass!!  I mean that in the nicest possible way LOL

You certainly don't have to write in it every day - but I think you will find that once you start it is great to keep track of your journey. It is great to look back and see how far you have come and helps to keep track of stuff too.

But - whatever floats your boat! This is your journey and what an amazing journey it is!

and BTW - we all often say that we love flying our "Freak Flag" please remember people are watching what you are doing
and taking note of your accomplishments ..... so stick to your guns girl!

When you have time - read Mr. Pinkies journal and see what Kate and Christopher have accomplished in the last 18 months for themselves and their children.

I can't wait to hear more from you ...

### nonchalant — 2014-01-13T22:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105586>

Welcome, KatieO! Sounds like you are doing an AWESOME job of living a healthy life!! I never considered a capacitor at the electrical panel. I'll have to read up on that.
Do you walk around barefoot? You might also want to consider a rain barrel. Lots of electrons in fresh rainwater.

So glad you joined us.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-13T22:51:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105587>

Welcome. Dont worry about explaining it. Just tell them you live by lowering your inflammation.......you wont need to fill in details to people who have no clue. It will reduce your stress.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-13T22:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105591>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=105587)
>
> Just tell them you live by lowering your inflammation......

I like that, "I'm working on lowering my inflammation." If you talk about stress, people all jump to the conclusion that you are discussing emotional/mental stress. No one considers cellular stresses.

### caroline — 2014-01-13T23:24:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105596>

I have been doing that the last couple of weeks - everyone "gets" lowering your inflammation! even my daughter jumped on board!

### KatieO — 2014-01-14T01:22:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105607>

Wow- thanks everyone for taking the time to respond, and for such thoughtful suggestions. I am interested in DHEA supplementation, and I will check out Mr. Pinkie's journal- I'm definitely interested in how families with kids are making things work. Nonchalant, I walk barefoot as much as I can, but we don't have a rain barrel yet. We are in the process of building a new house- low EMF, low RFR, etc., and there is a rainwater catchment system in the design, but I was just planning to water plants with the rainwater, since we will have well water to drink. Is there a better use for it? JK- thanks for reminding me not to generate my own stress.

### nicld — 2014-01-14T04:37:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105614>

> [KatieO said:](/goto/post?id=105607)
>
> Wow- thanks everyone for taking the time to respond, and for such thoughtful suggestions. I am interested in DHEA supplementation, and I will check out Mr. Pinkie's journal- I'm definitely interested in how families with kids are making things work. Nonchalant, I walk barefoot as much as I can, but we don't have a rain barrel yet. We are in the process of building a new house- low EMF, low RFR, etc., and there is a rainwater catchment system in the design, but I was just planning to water plants with the rainwater, since we will have well water to drink. Is there a better use for it? JK- thanks for reminding me not to generate my own stress.

Welcome KatieO,

For the rain water, maybe get a stock tank and fill it with the rain water to do CT in the sun in rainwater. That would be heavenly

As for DHEA, I use Twist DHEA and it works pretty good and I really like it on my face. I also have neurobiologix (sp?) and that works pretty good too but is a little greasy. I would stay away for the oral stuff. For me, it turned into testosterone and gave me acne.

You will find a lot of great info and support here.

Nicole

### Linz — 2014-01-14T16:00:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105630>

Welcome Katie,

and fabulous to hear how much you have done already. How sad it is that it takes failing health and multiple side effects of conventional treatment to make most of us start to think for ourselves. I too have left behind all the drugs I took for auto-immune problems and just use mitochondrial support and a few other supplements along with lifestyle changes.

Do you have animals on your mountain top? I think they are essential to feeling less isolated, more grounded and closer to nature, and even making our way of life less peculiar to others around us. I think all children could benefit hugely from contact with dogs, ponies, hens, pet lambs etc - and something interesting that even their gadget focussed friends might think cool.

I am also lowering my inflammation, but the incredible information here has allowed me to give the professionals helping me enough credible detail that they now listen to me and ask me what help I would like them to give me (if only they could!). Occasionally I have met a brick wall only to find that months later the person involved comes back accepting what I am doing and asking more about it .Some of these people have become my closest advisors/helpers/friends and discuss things with me openly now. Hopefully all you find here will make you feel much less isolated - or at least give you confidence in your decisions, just don't be overwhelmed by it all. Even the most unfathomably complex things seem to fall eventually (usually!!) into incredibly simple messages.

A tiny part of me still craves a kindly doctor with a bottle of pills rather than facing inconvenient change but I guess Dr K's answer might be a hearty shove into his cold tank,

warm wishes,

Linz

### nonchalant — 2014-01-14T16:56:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105631>

> We are in the process of building a new house- low EMF, low RFR, etc., and there is a rainwater catchment system in the design, but I was just planning to water plants with the rainwater, since we will have well water to drink. Is there a better use for it?

Before rain falls, it is high up in the atmosphere, where it gets greatly energized by the sun's photons. Fresh rainwater is a great source of electrons. Some of us splash it on our faces and hands. You can use it to wash your hair, or just for a bit during the day. Also your feet and other skin areas could benefit from it. I know my plants and pets also prefer rainwater. Or at least water that has been sitting in the sun.

### JanSz — 2014-01-14T17:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105633>

> [KatieO said:](/goto/post?id=105570)
>
> It is with tears in my eyes that I finally write this intro post. I have been in such a dark place for so long... I hardly know where to begin.... except that I know I need a community to come to with my questions and thoughts... here goes...
>
> In my 39 years of life, here are some of the diagnoses and medical events I have accumulated:
>
> Lupus, Fibromyalgia, Raynauds, Sjogren's syndrome, Grave's disease (thyroid destroyed by irradiation- damn. they said I had no other choice), asthma, migraine headaches, multiple pelvic prolapses following childbirth, multiple surgeries to repair multiple pelvic prolapses (I have that "surgical mesh" implanted in me that you always see mentioned on late night TV commercials for class action lawsuits), 5+ years of prednisone "treatment", severe adverse reaction to the fluoroquinolone antibiotic Avelox, areas of "clearing" visible in brain MRI, cause unknown... etc.
>
> I will not mention, but I'm sure you can imagine, the vast array of drugs that I have been prescribed over the last 2 decades. HOWEVER, as of today, the only daily prescription drug I am taking is thyroid hormone (yay!). Here is how I have accomplished this... this is what I think I'm doing right so far:
>
> paleo/epi paleo diet, no artificial light/screen time after dark (or use blue blockers), no wifi in our home, no cordless phones in our home, grounding sheet, sold our house and moved from the middle of a large urban area to a mountain home in the Sierra foothills (we had cell phone transmitters beaming directly into our living room, now we have horrible cell reception), cell phones off (except when our daughter is in school), no electronics in bedroom, capacitors installed at electrical panel and throughout the home to reduce/eliminate dirty electricity, lots of sun exposure, well water (no more fluoride- yay!)... I'm sure there's more,
>
> Some labs are improving, (vit D is optimal, fasting glucose is low @ 77 mg/dl, so I believe I am leptin sensitive, also able to skip meals with no problem), but my hormones are still tanked ( example, DHEA-S <15 ug/dl ? so low they didn't quantify?). I have a LONG way to go, and that is why I'm writing this post. 2014 is my year. I'm going far this year, and I can't do it alone.
>
> I look forward to being able to post on this forum to give and get support from like minded people. One of the side effects of this journey has been a gradually increasing feeling of isolation, as it's harder and harder to explain to others why we now live a lifestyle so different from theirs. Why don't we carry iphones? Why didn't we get our daughter an ipad for Christmas? Why don't we use Facebook? Why won't we eat their food? Why won't we attend events that disrupt our sleep cycle? Why did we move to a mountain top in the middle of nowhere? Can't I just let my daughter have ONE stinkin' cupcake?-- HELL NO, I cannot......
>
> thanks so much for listening!

You have made great progress and are on the way to get better.
Wish you the best in 2014 (and beyond).

\------------------------------

must be corrected.

and call that 100%
DHEAs participation is about 95%
If you are lacking DHEAs, you are practically lacking all steroid hormones.

I am assuming that other hormonal creams or gels, transdermal applications should obey similar rules or they may become ineffective.

### KatieO — 2014-01-14T20:21:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105646>

Linz, thanks for your warm wishes. We have thought of animals- it's a great suggestion. I think we are all waiting until we get into the new house (hopefully within a year or so). Also, I have to make sure I'm healthy enough to take care of them, so it's good to have this year to get my health in order. I will also take this year to work on finding/creating a group of health care providers that will listen and support my goals. That's daunting! Many MDs enter an exam room with a level of swagger and bravado that I find intimidating, so I'm going to have to remember to breathe deep and say what I need to say. And, if I'm really getting nowhere, then I'll move on without wasting too much of my own time/energy. I would be very interested to know what you use for mitochondrial support?

nonchalant, I had never thought of those uses for rainwater- thanks! I now look forward to putting electron-rich rainwater on my face and hands

JanSz, So, I reviewed the link you sent about dirty electricity and capacitors. I think what's being said, is that you shouldn't use a capacitor _unless_ you are really sure that you have cleared up all wiring issues (both unintended, and those caused even by following standard electrical code). My husband (who was already a competent electrician) read extensively about EMF issues related to wiring, and has rewired accordingly. We used a Gauss meter to do our sleuthing, and confirm resolution of EMF problems (he used another meter too... can't remember the name, more of a typical electrician's tool) and for dirty electricity we used a Graham Stetzer meter to ensure that any capacitors we added to the system caused a reduction in dirty electricity, not an increase. We know that the capacitors we installed are only removing some of the dirty electricity, but I feel it's better to filter what we can, than not filter at all. And, having made the wiring changes and tested each electronic device in the home individually for dirty electricity, we were able to really cause a big reduction (but we did have to get rid of some items that were just causing too much dirty electricity). Given this scenario, do you still think the capacitors are a cause for concern? I do agree with the idea that you should not just buy one of these large capacitors and have someone connect it to your main without knowing exactly what's going on in your wiring.

I have an appointment with a new MD in 2 weeks- first order of business, get my DHEA-S checked again, and supplement as necessary to correct this problem. Thanks for the supplement info!

### Linz — 2014-01-14T21:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105654>

Katie,

I have done the prednisone 'treatment' too!

There are blogs here and quite a lot of old forum discussions about mito support and there is also a webinar.

I use d-ribose, acetyl-L-carnitine, ubiquinol and various Mg supplements (malate, threonate and Epsom salts or MgCl baths. Orotate is also recommended) plus plenty of coconut oil.

### KatieO — 2014-01-14T22:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105663>

Linz,

Aren't the autoimmune "treatments" just so absurd? I shutter to think about all the horrible stuff I swallowed over the years! I can't believe that I let them shut down my adrenals, or that I let them remove my thyroid!! Working on letting my anger around all of that go, since it's all in the past, but it's so hard some days

Thanks for the info, and I will look for that webinar.

animals 6,717,713

humanity 978,121,739 17.06%
ideology 141,663,126
war 130,727,000
murder 177,038,067
accidents 297,963,486
drugs 115,052,756
air pollution 115,677,304

total caused deaths 5,734,459,716

# Thread: Interesting n=1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danco3636 — 2014-01-14T06:44:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105616>

OK after reading Jacks response to the below questions I decided to do my own n=1 on "Protein Refeeds". For the last few years I have been epi-paleo keto diet.... I hover around 7-8% BF and am pretty well built. Been wanting to drop a few percentages to be back to my lean 5-6% or less body fat. That is where I was when I did Leangains before......

Well yesterday I did a protein refeed.... It went like this:
AM Coffee with a small amount of MCT and GF butter...
Fasted till about 2:00 or so and had a meal of protein based low to no fat seafood (shrimp and oysters) and egg whites.
Then had meal 2 (6PM), (a bit larger) of no fat seafood (cod more shrimp and egg whites with turmeric, curry powder and some sea greens and kale)
Filled me up great and killed my hunger until I went to bed around 9ish.....
Well I slept 9 hours..... bam! (got up for the bathroom twice to urinate & upon rising)
Well I woke up and felt great.... I looked and felt leaner....
When I was sleeping my body heated up and was warmer than usual.

Upon rising I dipped into my 45DF pool and did some outside movement and climbed a few trees. I followed it up with a cold shower....

Seems like this protein seafood based refeed did me good. I felt great all day, did a fasted weight session later and hit some PR's.

Today I ate normal epi keto diet..... craved some mac nuts so I had some of those as well...

So I will do another tomorrow and see how it goes. I may implement these 2-3 times a week as needed to get lean and feel great. Going to keep with testing them...

PS.... I also used a product called "KetoForce" to help keep in in Ketosis.....

Thanks for the questions
yewwei.tan

_**Protein Refeeds instead of Carb Refeeds

Listening to your interview with Dave Asprey reminded me about his recommendation to eat some starch (100-150g) every 3-7 days in this post: [http://www.bulletproofexec.com/the-b...safe-starches/](http://www.bulletproofexec.com/the-b...safe-starches/)

Dave claims to have experienced dry eyes, poor sleep, increased LDL, and even leaky gut, by not having enough carbs.

Question 1: Would protein refeeds work just as well as carb refeeds to prevent the symptoms that Dave experienced?

I ask because from what I know, refeeding with protein only would act on many of the same pathways as a carb refeed. In both cases, AMPk goes down, mTOR increases, leptin comes up a little (though more so with acute carb refeeds), and some would argue that the total insulin response that you can get from protein is comparable to carbs.

Even when it comes to thyroid health, overfeeding with protein seems to get T3 up and running again just as well as carbs: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC371281/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC371281/)

Question 2: If protein refeeds work, what sort of quantity would illicit a significant effect?

For example, let's say you are eating 120 grams of protein a day to stay in ketosis. What would happen when for one day a week, you increase that dose up to 220 grams?

Question 3: What sort of negative effects would a protein refeed have compared to a carb refeed? Or put another way, should one consider refeeding by the +100 grams once a week if inflammation is still high?

From the IF and Leptin blog post:

AMPk pathways requires really optimal leptin sensitivity and signaling to be occurring between the brain, liver, and muscles.
Question 4: When is the best timing for a refeed?

I would think first thing in the morning, and in the 4-6pm window would be best to overfeed with protein.

Thanks!**_

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-14T17:56:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105635>

great n=1.

I know I've experienced greats amts of energy when just eating seafood, particularly raw, for the day. love the way it makes me feel...

### yewwei.tan — 2014-01-15T01:22:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105694>

Haha, I asked that question because I was trying to figure if there is a way to maintain a really lean physique without compromising longevity.

I still have a bunch of questions that are yet to be answered of course, and need to think about them for awhile more.

For example, I was wondering if consuming less protein on other days is a good idea. I got this idea from Tim Ferriss's ['Geek to Freak' post](http://www.fourhourworkweek.com/blog/2007/04/29/from-geek-to-freak-how-i-gained-34-lbs-of-muscle-in-4-weeks/), and while I don't endorse almost anything in that post, he did talk about reducing calories by 50% for one day a week to prevent "protein uptake down-regulation". So his theory is that taking a low-protein day somehow re-sensitizes you to the to the effects of a protein surplus.

I was thinking that a really lean, but keto-adapted person, would have decreased amino acid requirements as a result. So the standard "eat more protein" as you get leaner may not actually have to be taken to extremes of the 3g/kg bodyweight recommendation that Martin Berkhan uses.

So what if said person eats 100-120g of protein a day for 5 days in the week, and then spikes that up to the 200-300g range for 2 days in a concentrated feeding window?

\---

Then there's the question about frequency -- how often to stuff your face with seafood? Common recommendations say that the leaner you get, the more re-feeding you need. Why is this?

\---

Anyway, I'll be experimenting as well. Just had basically a pound of grass-fed rump steak the other day that I get from a local farmer. Macadamia nuts are awesome too! Lots of these available here in Australia of course.

Thanks for sharing Dan!

### MeghanK — 2014-01-15T02:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105709>

what kind of Mac Nuts do you guys eat? I find the raw ones tasteless....

### yewwei.tan — 2014-01-15T03:16:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105713>

Only ever raw macadamias for me. I get them at this place near me for anywhere from $21.50 to $24 per KG (approx $10 - $10.90 per LBS) -- [http://www.rosssqualitynuts.com.au/](http://www.rosssqualitynuts.com.au/)

I find them incredibly sweet and tasty when raw, and actually like them better than the roasted ones. But this place I get them from is a wholesaler, and I'm pretty sure shipping them down from NSW or Queensland to VIC would only take 1-2 days, and the nuts would be pretty fresh as a result.

# Thread: CT and grounding effect...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danco3636 — 2014-01-14T06:57:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105617>

OK so it seems that CT is good not just for getting cold..... but also has a huge grounding effect. Water seems primordial in many ways so why not immersion for benefits?

With this I have been using water for a grounding effect and it seems to work great. Just washing my hands (I use water only, no soap) and running the cold water on my arms has a soothing grounding effect.... Why not right? The water that comes out should be grounded. At least at my place sense the water runs along pipes connected to my well pump and tank that sits on the ground slab. Anyway most piping makes it's way on the ground.....

So lately, and any given chance I rinse my hands and arms and splash my face with cold water. Anytime I go to the bathroom I rinse.... When I have been walking around with shoes for a long time I find time to rinse.... I especially enjoy washing the dishes by had as I feel it is a grounding effect. Even mild cold showers seems to have this effect.... Just being in water keeps me grounded....

Any one else notice this effect?
I am getting addicted to water, both drinking it and being in it.....
Perhaps this is why swimming (think Michael Phelps) is so great..... You are almost always grounded and eliminating most non native EMF's...?...?
My n=1 seems to point to getting wet often.

Any thoughts?

### Da-mo — 2014-01-14T07:35:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105618>

I work in a power station so grounding is a very important thing for me given the feilds that Im around. Some areas have feilds exceeding 500uT!!! Couple that with lots of artificial light and microwave transmitters everywhere for remote control of the grid and shiftwork . . . Its a wonder im still alive

My CT consists of a 15 minute walk through the bush each night after dinner to a waterfall currently around 14C (just over 55F). Currently have worked up to immersion up to my neck for 25 minutes. I do that walk barefoot for the grounding but also consider the waterfall immersion as very effective for grounding.

I have to remain shod when at work and have often wondered about grounding during the working day - thanks for the idea about water rinsing, I will try it although the water in my work environment is possibly affected as well.

### Inger — 2014-01-14T20:38:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105648>

I always wash dishes for hand too, since forever! Even if I have had a dishwasher, I did not used it. Now I know why... I always wash my dishes under running water too. I never have much dishes as I wash them right after use.

I loved to rinse my hands in cold water working as a waitress in the city full of EMF, it kind of gave me new energy! And I love to wash my feet under running water before I go to bed.. (if I do not shower before bed) and I love to shower, I often do twice a day (warm showers too) + a dip in the river.

I did not think about it like grounding before but sure it makes one feel good... yeah to get wet is nice - and rainy weather is nice too

### Jonathin — 2014-01-14T21:20:38+0200

### Da-mo — 2014-01-14T21:56:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105659>

Jonathin, I've wondered about placing the plants in a sandbed and pumping water back from the bottom of the sandbed to the swimming area of the pool. Then you'd bet the benefit of a sand filter that never needs backwashing as the plant could consume the sediment.

Im all for ways to reduce/eliminate chlorine from the pool. Here in NZ they have a product for hot tubs that is a block that automatically leaches things into the water to control pH, total dissolved solids and bacteria. The biocide used is ionic silver and they last about a year. They dont have one for swimming pools yet but I think they are working on it. Not sure if Im allowed to post a product link but .. . . [http://www.simplesilver.co.nz/](http://www.simplesilver.co.nz/)

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-28T22:49:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-107223>

I do the majority of my CT in a natural spring-fed pool. The water is about 40-degree F during the winter, and stays stable even during really cold periods. I find that I much prefer this natural CT and grounding session outdoors and in contact with the ground and rocks and water, than doing CT in my bathtub at home. I come away feeling exhilarated and connected, and it has become a really spiritual ritual for me. When I get out of my bath tub I just feel like, well, yep, that was cold, but don't feel that zingy feeling I get after sitting in the canyon.

# Thread: My Earthpulse N=1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arcole — 2014-01-14T18:28:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-105637>

After 6 months of hemming and hawing, I finally popped and purchased the Earthpulse. A number of factors entered into my decision to go that way versus the Magnetico, not the least of which was $$. After three nights with it, I thought I needed to start a journal for my own sake and for the possible edification of the rest of the community who might also be considering.

Participants include myself, my husband, and my 8 month old 5 pound Maltese dog who sleeps in his crate on a chair next to the bed.

I received the Earthpulse very quickly on Friday 1/10/14 but had left my printouts of the documentation at work. I didn't want to sleep on it without reading up carefully first. The next morning I retrieved my quickstart info and did the 1 hour Recovery entrainment which had me drooling out of the corners of my mouth in a state of deep relaxation alternating with sleep. Even the dog fell asleep. That led me to choose Recovery as our setting of choice for our first night.

Night 1 -- 1/11/14 -- Recovery
I, husband, and dog slept like we'd been knocked out by bricks. I woke easily and felt very refreshed.
No waking to pee either, which has gotten better for me since starting Melatonin and Magnesium, but I still generally wake once. I didn't even wake up.

Night 2 -- 1/12/14 -- Recovery
Another solid night for all of us.
No wake to pee. Easy waking and great daytime energy.

Night 3 -- 1/13/14 -- Recovery
Everybody was wakeful. I woke at 1:30 and went to the bathroom and again at 3:00 but didn't get up. Even the dog was wakeful and played in his crate. But I still went back to sleep after each waking very easily and woke after 8 hours at 5:30 with no desire to snooze the alarm (a big deal for me because I generally sleep 9 hours and still want to snooze). So far, daytime energy has been great. I did use the Earthpulse during the day at work on Alert setting on 1/13/14 and am using it again 1/14/14. I also had my regular chiropractic adjustment on 1/13/14. I am not sure if any of this made a difference for me personally.

So far the husband has said he's sleeping fine. The dog doesn't say much about it.

Resting breath hold times since 7/9/13 have held steady at between 1:00 and 1:05 with one check early on as low as 45 seconds.

After the first week, I will test again under the same circumstances and see what I'm getting.

Hope this helps! Holler if you have questions!

Arcole

Night 4 -- 1/14/14 --- Recovery

We were all a little wakeful, I'd say. However, I didn't get up to go pee and went right back to sleep after about 2-3 wakings. I did use the unit on Alert during the day as well. After 8 hours, I was alert and chirpy at 5:00. Energy levels today have been very good. My overall pain and stiffness rating is pretty good as well.

Night 5 -- 1/15/14--- Recovery setting

Woke once to pee at 1:00 but went right back to sleep and did great on waking after 8 hours at 5:00. No grogginess, no desire to snooze. Aches and pains are some better I'd say. Everyone else seemed to sleep well and husband said at breakfast he felt good and had slept well. Did NOT use ALERT setting the day before. I am wondering if Alert during the day has any hangover for me on sleep during the night. If so, I am sad because I like the Alert setting. I can't tell any huge difference, but I do feel good when running it. I am doing without again today to continue the experiment.

### Arcole — 2014-01-23T18:33:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-106776>

Multiple day update from my log.

Night 6 1/16/14 -- Sleep 3 -- poor sleep but the 8 year old grandson in the bed with us was the culprit. No alert setting because my daughter took the second unit back to school with her.

Night 7 1/17/14 -- Sleep 3 -- sleep 9/10! no wake to pee.

Night 8 1/18/14 -- Sleep 3 -- no wake; sleep 10/10!! Recovery might have been just a little too high for either me or my husband but Sleep 3 seems much better.

Night 9 1/19/14 -- Sleep 3 -- no wake, sleep 10+. Great energy during the day.

Night 10 1/20/14 -- sleep 3 -- woke once to pee, but went right back to sleep. sleep 9/10. Weight down 2 pounds since beginning!

Night 11 1/21/14 -- sleep 3 -- didn't get up to pee but was a little restless, sleep 7/10; forgot to take my Melatonin and Magnesium (which is also oxide and not malate because I ran out and picked up what I could get quick). Other slightly restless nights might also have been nights when I didn't take my pills. I will do more experimentation to see exactly which element makes the most difference in sleep, the melatonin or the magnesium.

Night 12 1/22/14 -- sleep 3 -- took my pills! Didn't wake at all, 10+ sleep quality. No grogginess.

Plus, my arms haven't gone to sleep at night in several nights and this had been a regular nightly occurrence despite chiropractic care. Also my waking joint pain has gotten significantly better. I'm trying to avoid wheat and corn completely since wheat and particular is an irritant to my joints, but even with some odd consumption, my morning stiffness is much improved. Sleep 3 appears to be the right setting at this time for me and my husband, so I'm not going to mess with that for a while. My daughter also had to back down from Recovery to Sleep 3 due to wakefulness.

When I first began sleeping on the earthpulse, my Resting Breath Hold time was :58, which is about the usual for the past eight months. However, my check today put me at :51 seconds, so no improvment in that quarter yet. However, I'm not fretting about it. I'll just keep doing what I'm doing and check it again in another week. The website documentation states I should see an improvement in two weeks and if not, to contact the inventor. I've talked to him by phone once already and was very pleased with the interaction. Dr. K, he knows you as the "cold water guy" and admitted to not having kept up with your blog for a couple of years. I told him he needed to check it out because you both talk about so many of the same issues with mitochondrial function and magnetism.

Hope this helps somebody!!

### Arcole — 2014-01-30T23:12:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-107494>

Multiple day update again:

Night 13 1/23/14 -- sleep 3, wake once to pee, morning pain levels improving; 10 sleep quality,

Night 14 1/24/14 -- sleep 3, no wake at all, sleep quality 10; magnesium only, no melatonin

Night 15 1/25/14 -- sleep 3, no waking but slightly restless night 8/10 sleep, magnesium only

Night 16 1/26/14 -- sleep 3, no waking, sleep 8+/10; magnesium only

Night 17 1/27/14 -- sleep 3, one waking to pee, sleep 10/10, magnesium only

Night 18 1/28/14 -- sleep 3, no waking, sleep 10/10, magnesium only

Night 19 1/29/14 -- sleep 3, woke once to pee, sleep 9+/10 due to vivid dreams about snow (wonder why? LOL), magnesium only

Summary so far: My morning waking stiffness and pain has gradually improved until I'm down to very little stiffness and almost no pain in the morning in my feet and ankles. This has been despite the occasional dietary slip-up which I know makes things worse. With 10 being excellent, no pain, I've gone from steady 7s to 9s and 9+s. My sleep quality overall has also improved dramatically over the last few weeks. I've gone more nights in without waking to go to the bathroom than I have in years. Also, any wakings I've had have been short and I've had almost no trouble going to sleep and staying asleep. Morning wakings have been much, much better than before the Earthpulse. I don't feel groggy or want to keep snoozing. This is a major improvment. Also, I have done much better of late with the magnesium only. I'm going to stay off the melatonin and see if my sleep continues to be good without it.

My daughter has also been reporting good sleep on sleep 3, but says she sometimes feels groggy in the mornings. At least part of hers, she admits is the fact that she's a college student and gets to bed much later than she should. But she reports that her sleep is very sound.

My dog had thirteen baby teeth pulled yesterday, slept great last night, and today is playing and eating like nothing happened. I can't tell for sure that sleeping near the Earthpulse helped him recover, but I can't rule it out either. My husband also reports really sound sleep and has not been tossing and turning nearly as much as he used to. In fact, my sleep may be so much better because his is!

Resting breath hold: 1:01 -- shows nice improvement

### Arcole — 2014-01-30T23:33:46+0200

When I upped my dosage recently, my dry skin went away on my hands, even in this awful cold weather.

### Arcole — 2014-02-18T17:18:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111000>

This is a long overdue update. I will sum up.

Nights 20-34 all look practically identical. Sleep 3, rare wakings and no more than one, great sleep 9-10.
Nights 35-37 look less fantastic but I did it to myself. On Feb 6, I fell prey to Girl Scout Cookies and early Valentine's candy and over the next few days managed to sugar bomb and stress my immune system into the toilet. I got a very mild cold on Feb 9, which a week later turned into a fatigue and gland issue I haven't managed to shake. So my sleep is suffering, my pain is up, and my overall fatigue levels are bad. Yet I somehow managed to pull off a 1:05 breath hold time. I figured I wouldn't make 45 seconds!

That tells me a lot! Despite being sick, my overall sleep has been better even the last few days than it ever was before the Earthpulse. During crazy stressful nights of being woke up at 2:00 a.m. with a crisis, spending one night with my husband in the hospital (he's fine), I was understandably wakeful, but my overall sleep quality was so restorative I managed to keep functioning.

If I hadn't trashed my system with sugar, I'd be doing fabulous right now. So no more sugar! No matter the temptation!!

Also, I have just gotten my results back from 23andme and ran them through Sterling's app at the MTHFRsupport site. I'm +/+ for C677T and 13 other snps in the Methylation section. So tomorrow afternoon I have my first consult with Dr. Tim Jackson. Maybe this is a sign of improvement to come!!

On a side note, dealing with Earthpulse's inventor Paul Becker has been fabulous!!! He responds so quickly to emailed questions and has really stood by his product with me. It has been a pleasure to do business with him. Once I get some of my MTHFR issues in line, I will touch base with Paul about improving my breath hold times even more. However, I feel pretty confident that these stupid snps are giving my body grief right now. I just don't know how much grief.

### sooperb — 2014-02-18T17:29:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111003>

Is your daughter's experience reflecting the same benefits, even though she perhaps abuses her sleep cycle by not going to sleep at a reasonable time? ### Arcole — 2014-02-18T17:39:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111008>

I would say my daughter does indeed abuse her sleep cycle and she knows it. She is trying to do better though since finding out she too has methylation issues. She is heterozygous for both C677T and A1298C as well as a number of others. But she does say that Sleep 3 has been a very good setting for her as well and she uses it nightly, which to me says a lot since college students aren't very good with consistency.

I'll get a real update from her soon and post. On the other front, my husband and dog are sleeping very well themselves. Now that my husband has changed some of his lifestyle habits, most especially with regard to diet and watching his blood pressure and blood sugar, I am hoping he will get even more benefit from it. It took a high blood pressure crisis to wake him up, but he's finally on the epi-paleo wagon with the rest of us. He's already lost 20 pounds too. Men. Sigh. All he had to do was give up the honey buns at last. I haven't had a stupid honey bun in seven years and can't lose 20 pounds.

### sooperb — 2014-02-18T17:57:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111019>

I sympathise.

We gave up carbs, mostly, two years ago this month. We both lost weight initially especially as we also did intermittent fasting. We slid backwards quite a lot after a few months, he'd lost over 21lbs and I lost about 14lbs, the most I'd lost in a long time. We needed to pull our socks up though but even being very low carb., no wheat, bread, potatoes, rice or pasta except once in a while, neither of us can lose and we both put most of the loss back. I started the reset but he isn't too interested but even so, 2 meals a day only, very few carbs, I managed to give up sugar completely but still no result. I made a start on CT, face dunking, abdomen icing and two baths so far and cold after warm showers. Still nothing doing. I am so surprised that cutting out sugar hasn't achieved any weight loss, I really thought it was going to be the winner but no. I haven't given up nor am I stoking myself with sugar laden food, it's do or die this time lol. It's a mystery to me although I'm sure Dr. Jack might have some sort of answer except he hasn't called into my journal yet lol.
