### Arcole — 2014-02-25T00:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111986>

Nights 38-42 --- My sleep for the past six weeks has been without a doubt deeper, longer, and more restorative than it has been in many years. Most nights I have slept like I did in my twenties. This has been a huge improvement. Lots of nights I don't wake at all and if I do, the waking is generally brief. Sometime I get up to go pee, sometimes not. Sometimes I sleep through 9 or 10 hours straight. In the mornings I am typically not groggy at all, which is also a huge improvement. The sleep 3 setting seems to knock us all out, me, husband, and dog, pretty much without fail. My husband in particular has stated that his sleep has dramatically improved. He used to have lots of sleepless nights, but no more.

My overall assessment personally of the Earthpulse is that I am not going to be sleeping without it, unless Dr. Kruse knows something awful about the way it works that I don't know. I believe that for me, it has filled in a piece of the puzzle with regard to sleep very nicely. I think it has some features to recommend it, price and portability springing to mind. I do not regret the purchase at all. I will update again for certain at 60 days and 90 days or if anything changes.

### sooperb — 2014-02-25T00:31:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111990>

Arcole I am more than pleased for you, sleep is so important and at last you're getting some quality rest. That alone for me would be worth the expense. I am grateful that you have taken the time to give us the benefit of your experience.

### Terminator — 2014-02-25T05:45:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-112052>

I love my earthpulse, I only use it when I travel and only sleep good in recover mode. I had to travel for Work and did not use earthpulse for first two nights and all except 1.5 hours of third night and my sleep was very bad except for 1.5 hour of earthpulse. Fourth night sleep was good. I cannot tolerate any sleep mode other than recover mode.

At home I don't use it. I don't have any wireless devices, shut down the power to entire house except Fridge & Furnace, I have 20 Gauss magnetico pads (bought used) on 13 inch mattress and sleep very good. I don't travel too often but next week I will travel to again and will use earthpulse every night in recover mode. I will try to report when i get back.

### Inger — 2014-01-14T20:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-the-military-from-a-former-agent.9338/post-105652>

This is all so sad.. it makes me mad. I called our electric company again today, as they seem to ignore me and my demanding them them to take the smart meter off. I sat and waited for so long at the telephone, and 3 times the person changed.. in the ends I got to tell my issue and she said she will forward it. WTH? I said I NEED TO TALK TO THEM WHO ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE METER AND WHO CAN PROMISE ME THEY COME AND TAKE IT OFF. Guess what she said to me? _There are no such person_. You just get stuck with them to the point they say they will call you back, and they do not call.

I just cannot believe this is possible at all in a country like Finland. But it is. If this was my house I would certainly kill the meter no doubt - but this is my parents house and I am scared to make a fire or kill the electricity all together in their house. After the talk with the company... I just cried. It is just so exhausting.. and it is just too sad. How can they do this? They are so weirdly polite... but they ignore you at the end. But I am not going to give up yet. I will keep bombarding them. Maybe... in the end I will take a sledgehammer. it is kind of calling my name in the garage

> so i guess no one is going to say to 'try' pregenenolone... i mean, im looking for another quick fix, i realise that, but im so effing desperate..

Ok, I say it would not hurt but you NEED to get to the bottom of why the cortisol is shot. It finally clicked for me when my last test of tests showed that my pregnenolone was tanked, my thyroid labs were still wonky and the progesterone was just marginally better. I have major pregnenolone steal going on to keep the adrenals and cortisol levels happy. I am taking meds and supplements for all three of the hormones but it is not addressing why I need to. I plan on calling my GI doctor today to get some stool sampling done and see if I have an infection in my gut (ready Misty’s stuff) plus I see my hormone doctor about my last set of labs today. We will see if he thinks I am right in needing to address the adrenals.

Hate to say it but there is no quick fix if your hormones are so screwed up. I did 3 rounds of HCG in the past 1 Â½ years and I lost a bunch of weight on it but it did not stick due to the hormones being wacked.

It is a process and hang in, it will be a bumpy ride.

oh and it would be worth it to become a gold member for a bit and listen to all of the webinars and QA sessions. Tons of great information in there. I have downloaded most of the audio versions and put in on an mp3 player to listen to on my commute.

Nicole

### margor_j — 2014-01-15T20:40:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pls-give-me-thoughts-on-dhea-pregnenolone.9341/post-105783>

hi nicole
thanks for that.. i was really stressed yesterday, its so frustrating.....
I had CDSA done, quite an advanced one (comprehensive diagnostic stool analysis) and my doc (the good doc, who understands all this stuff) said it was all fine.. but i wonder about my gut now, years of bulimia etc.. iv decided to start taking apple cider vinegar again before meals? iv been hitting kombucha tea, but thats made no dramatic change..
id have no idea WHY my cortisol is shot.. but i am sleeping better with a darker room, wearing blue blocking shades at nite.. i try to look at the sunrise etc. but i do still use melatonin or my chinese sleep herbs to sleep at nite.. i know melatonins not the best, but when youve had 4 years of CHRONIC insomnia, then .... desperation means you cant always heed advice. however, most nites, ive gotten away with having only half a one..

i had very stressful jobs this past year, which kicks the coffee habit off again - it mite only be one a day, but i start to feel myself unravel with all the stress etc.. plus i had jobs that meant i was getting up anywhere from 3 -5am to go to work.. hello adrenal stress!
as it is now, i dont sleep in per se, but i guess i just waike with the sun, which im fine with.

im not even sure where id start here in NZ to try HCG either.. and thats interesting your weight loss didnt stick.. hmm i guess some of the frustrating thng about all of this is exactly that! its just a constant whirlpool of deeper and deeper and deeper issues.. tying it all togethers basically a b\*tch! lol.

so maybe gold membership is a go.. and i think il just try pregnenolone.. it prob cant hurt me!
M

# Thread: Bedroom lighting solution - when the breaker is OFF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KatieO — 2014-01-14T23:23:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105676>

OK, so you've got the breaker off to your bedroom, and you're sleeping in complete darkness- great! But what happens when your 5yr old comes wandering in at 4am or you don't want to break your neck getting to the bathroom? I know I could have a candle, but I am not to be trusted with an open flame at 4am!

Here's what I did, and I hope this is helpful to someone...

Step 1- get go to IKEA and get a SUNNAN Led lamp:

[http://www.ikea.com/us/en/catalog/products/90154371/](http://www.ikea.com/us/en/catalog/products/90154371/)

Step 2- order some amber "correction gels" from Amazon:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006Z7M4G/ref=wms\_ohs\_product?ie=UTF8&psc=1](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006Z7M4G/ref=wms_ohs_product?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Step 3- cut the gel to cover the bulb area of the lamp, and glue or tape it on!

Viola- a little bit of light by your bed, no outlet needed, and you won't burn the house down, plus no blue light.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-14T23:30:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105677>

or you could just use a red headlamp...that's what we use [http://www.amazon.com/Princeton-Tec...1389734961&sr=8-7&keywords=red+light+headlamp](http://www.amazon.com/Princeton-Tec-Remix-Headlamp-5mm/dp/B002HMTNUW/ref=sr_1_7?ie=UTF8&qid=1389734961&sr=8-7&keywords=red+light+headlamp)

### KatieO — 2014-01-14T23:59:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105679>

Nice headlamp! I like that idea.
In our case, flashlights were hard to keep track of, so I like that this just sits right by the bed, and I know it's where I left it. But really, what I love about those lamps, is the rechargeable battery! When my daughter takes her panel out into the sun to charge, it really drives home the messages we are trying to send her about where energy comes from.

### caroline — 2014-01-15T00:00:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105681>

My eyes must be better because I have no trouble seeing in the dark .... Then again - I don't have a five year old!

# Thread: Reverse or slow progression of Alzheimer's?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2014-01-15T00:55:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105689>

Hi friends,

We just lost my grandfather to Parkinson's in July. Now my almost 83 year old grandmother might possibly be in the early stages of Alzheimer's. The doctor wasn't clear and it's obvious we need a second opinion. I was wondering what, if anything, could be done to reverse or slow the progression if she is officially diagnosed? She is diabetic and has high blood pressure, plus she takes pain medicine for a back condition. She is not very active because of her back and leg issues. She told me today she wakes up and her hands are numb or a few hours. I just want to help her the best I can.

### fitness@home — 2014-01-15T02:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105700>

I have read that adding coconut oil to the diet is helpful to those with Alzheimer's. Try Googling it for more information.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-15T02:52:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105704>

Annie...any improvements in activity, diet and environment could help...perhaps go back through the blogs with the key word of Alzheimers and see what Dr. K has mentioned over the past two years. Particularly some of the supplements which might be an easy place to start before suggesting bigger changes. Best of luck.

### AnnieW — 2014-01-15T03:34:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105717>

Thank you both for your replies. I've just started reading about coconut and MCT oils and how beneficial they can be for Alzheimer's. I'm also sloooooowly making my way through the blogs.

Thanks for the suggestions. I'd love to hear more if anyone has them!

[http://www.jackkruse.com/avoiding-fates-of-ronald-reagan-or-michael-j-fox/](http://www.jackkruse.com/avoiding-fates-of-ronald-reagan-or-michael-j-fox/)

My sympathies. I have similar issues in my family. I don't think you have to have an official diagnosis to help slow the progression of symptoms with Jack's ideas.

### Jude — 2014-01-15T23:14:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105817>

One small thing to note is coconut oil can tend to give older folk diarrhea....know of friends who started their mum, a 90yo, on a tablespoon daily with that result. She was in a nursing home and the staff then banned it! Start slowly!

### AnnieW — 2014-01-15T23:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105823>

Yes I found his posts Caroline, thanks for the suggestion. He has lots of knowledge so I will be following him. I am looking at ordering some MCT oil soon. My grandmother has LOTS of GI issues. I will be sure to tell my mother to start her slowly and add some into her tea maybe a teaspoonful at a time. Coconut oil even gives me diarrhea and I'm 31.

You know, I "fell off the wagon" a few months back, after losing 16 lbs in two weeks (what the hell???). I feel like total $hit. Nothing in life is ever a coincidence I think. I have been thinking about the way I have been living lately and if I keep it up I may not make it to 40. I think...are there limits? Is 350 lbs not enough? Back on the path to optimal for me. I made a deal with my grandma that she couldn't die until long after I have my first baby (that was when she almost died two years ago). I have been trying for three years with no luck. It's the weight and the PCOS and the massive block in my brain that is keeping me from being optimal. And of course, everyone around me is pregnant right now. Sometimes it bothers me but sometimes it doesn't because I know how horrible my health is. It would be very unjust and unfair for me to put myself or a baby through an obese pregnancy. Again, no coincidence. Anyway, I think God is saying to me...you are sucking right now and need to change. So I need to not just listen, but act on it. My poor grandparents' health was just so awful with both of them having diabetes and my grandfather suffering from Parkinson's for ten years, and now my grandma and her "possible" Alzheimer's. I just am feeling this pull again toward optimal and I need to go with it before my life passes me by and I have nothing left but health problems and empty arms.

Anyway, sorry for the side rant. Thanks for being a great community guys!

### caroline — 2014-01-16T00:28:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105830>

Annie - Dr. Tim talked about becoming pregnant while being suboptimal. It really struck with me.

He said you are subjecting your child to a horrible childhood of poor health \[or even much worse\] and not being able to enjoy their childhood.

Dr. K. has said several times - that, as much as it pained him, he has had to tell patients during a consult that they shoudn't be having kids. very painful to hear I am sure.
He has also talked of the stats of having an autistic child - seemingly simple things like having to go into hospital with the flu during your third trimester....etc. - really stacks the deck against you.
Dr. K. told us of a very good friend of his who he had to tell that to and they didn't listen and the nightmare of their life.

This is totally none of my business - but have you thought of adopting and giving yourself and your grandma the gift of a gorgeous health baby that needs you...

### AnnieW — 2014-01-16T01:32:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105839>

Yes I'm terrified of autism too. I know there is help out there for autistic children, but if me being suboptimal increases the risk of having a child who develops it of course I'm going to do whatever I can to prevent it from happening.

We are definitely open to adoption. The problem is that our finances are suboptimal too, but we are working on that as well.

My endocrinologist says I'm "just a kid" and that I still have time to conceive if I can get down to 200 lbs. I feel like PCOS is fighting against my clock though.

# Thread: Kia Ora from New Zealand
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T01:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105695>

Hello optimizers

I have been lurking around on this site for around 6 months and have been so impressed that I just last week joined up as a gold member. Firstly, I'd like to thanks Dr. Jack Kruse for sharing his genius so freely and helping people with seemingly intractable health problems. I have been able to refer many people to the information he has provided to show them there are solutions for things ranging from diabetes to crohn's.

So a bit about me . . .

I am fortunate enough to live on a 5 acre block in the country in a beautiful setting by a river with my lovely lady (high school sweethearts, 23 years married, 30 years together), two daughters (22 and 20), one son (17), two horses, two labradors and a weird looking goldfish who lives in the horse's drinking trough. There are bush clad ranges a few minutes walk away and a 15 minute walk brings us to a waterfall where we do our CT in preference to using our swimming pool.

As a child born of a Fijian mother and Scottish father, I grew up not far from here during the boom years of a local town. Food was much better back then and sports and outdoors were the norm rather than internet, xbox and playstation. Football (soccer), swimming, tennis, gymnastics and archery were all part of my life through to 20 years old.

From 20 to 35 years old I practiced Chinese internal martial arts. This was meditation based using Buddhist breathing techniques and combining Buddhist and Taoist principles. The martial aspect served to teach how to integrate mind and body - which is a mystical way of saying how to get out of your own way  . In a nutshell, this summed up to Chi follows Yi, which means that energy follows will/intent/focus/attention.

From 35 to 45 years old I fell into a very sedentary lifestyle, made bad health choices and developed osteoarthrosis in my knees. This was partly due to being born club footed, although corrective surgery as a baby enabled me to be a competitive sportsman regardless and go on to compete in gymnastics at a National level. It did however place abnormal stresses on my knees and ankles, resulting in 2 knee surgeries, until the final straws which were probably lack of activity combined with inflammatory foods.

At 45, when my left knee had been hurting constantly to the point it would wake me at night for over a year I decided enough was enough and something had to be done. After reading some information on this site I realised that the long term chronic pain was likely screwing with my cortisol and hormones as well as the disturbed sleep. Lots of physiotherapy and acupunture followed with good but not lasting results. Reading more, I eliminated the inflammatory foods, nightshades and grains from my diet and that was the breakthrough for the osteoarthrosis. My knees are now pain free and do not blow up like balloons anymore.

Such has been the dramatic effect of Dr. Kruse's information that I'm now hooked and have moved on to keep to an epi-paleo template and have added CT into the equation. At 45 I'm now facintated by this perspective of health from a brain signalling perspective and the positive effects are having some flow on effects on other members of my family and even to others I speak with about the profound ideas I have learned here.

My health continues to improve dramatically and I hope some of the damage that was done in my "lost" decade can be repaired. The idea of extending my telemere lengths and stem cell supply is an exciting prospect.

My current mission is to finally kick the smoking habit once and for all. I did stop for a couple of months late last year but that ended up with weight gain of 5kg. 3kg is enough to make a difference to my knees from pain free to constant pain - so it was kind of a choosing between the two evils . . . constant pain with cortisol and hormones and sleep disarray or smoking with all of its ill effects and dopamine problems. Im finding this site lacking in information that might help with some sort of dopamine reset for when I stop smoking. I expect this will depress my dopamine levels for awhile so am interested in learning the effects of low dopamine and also how dopamine levels might be returned towards optimal.

It would be so easy to just gut it out and stop like I did last time but again from reading here I have found out the effects on brain chemistry, leading to bad decision making and choices, fitted in with what I experienced with the weight gains. Finding a way to return to normal dopamine production while minimising bad choices caused by altered brain chemistry is what Im most interested in at this point.

A little about my working environment - its bad, real bad. I do have a plan in place to make it better by transitioning off shiftwork and minimising the time I spend in extremely high EMF (I do shift work as an operator in a power station). Meanwhile, eating well combined with CT and lots of grounding appears to be helping.

BTW - my username "Da-mo" was the name of a monk who crossed the Himalayas from India into China and he had an immense influence on the Chinese monks from whom my line of martial arts descended. Has was also known a Damo or Bodhidharmi and is credited with teaching the Shaolin monks how to integrate mind and body and also as the father of Zen Buddhism. I once learned a technique called the Damo fist set which is a set of postures involving manipulation of energy flow in the arms combined with meditative visualisations - the results were instantaneous and profound and further solidified my experience of mind/body integration. So when Dr. Kruse starts getting into quantum physics, things start to get really exciting.

I would have just used my name - Sandy - as my username but it was already taken.

I look forward to meeting you all in the forums and learning from your experiences.

Sandy (Male BTW, in case you missed that - since Sandy is a unisex kind of name)

I have noticed that my body can only handle so much extra energy as I age and that diet and environment are quite important. I still find it a challenge to recognize my resonant/internal training limit rather than my physical/external limit. The later always leaves me tired and the former always humbles me.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T03:09:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105710>

Thanks for the welcom fitness@home

Josh, yes I do still practice mostly on the meditative level - hardly on the physical level although I still do some as useful opportunity presents itself.For example, if I bang my shin or knee on something I might use some meditation to clear the pain and energy blockages. Since doing CT I've discovered I can instantly stop shivering if I drop into meditative breathing, its a fun novelty but I think there is a point to the shivering so dont prevent it for long.

I like the way you describe the internal versus external limits - it shows you have experienced exactly what I mean when I describe mind/body integration. During my 3rd grading which lasted 5 hours, the whole point was to first hit the physical limit then overcome it. SO when you hit the physical limit, you are instructing or willing yourself to continue b ut your body is sending all these pain signals back and your body s in effect arguing or battling with your mind. The resullt feels like a loss of co-ordination, emotional disturbances, loss of balance tec and exhaustion.

For me, getting through this barrier was achieved by a change of context, a paradigm shift. What I did was to visualise that I was holding onto a loved one dangling over a cliff and that there was no way I was going to willingly let go - my strength would have to fail first or my fingers or arms drop off. Shortly after this shift in context, my limbs felt lighter, co-ordination returned and so did energy along with a deeper meditative state in which I was more detached from the physical situation, more of an observer/controller.

I believe in some way that once the body stops protesting the mind/will, a lot of wasted energy that was used up in conflict between the body and mind is released to follow the will or intent - and my experience was that it is an immense amount of energy that can either work for you or be wasted on internal conflict. This was how I experienced mind/body integration and what it means to me.

My experience has caused me to believe that a universal truth applies universally - across all scales of magnitude, and I have read Dr. Kruse make reference to this fractal nature of physics etc. when comparing the quantum scales with the larger scales in biology. So, being that I believe truth and meaning and several layers or "depths" - on a lesser level the mind body integration can be demonstrated in a martial artist who has not learned to fully commit to the strike/block/movement because they have not learned to FULLY release the muscles opposing the movement - that there is also an example of wasted energy and reduced power.

This is kind of what I meant when I described mind/body integration as meaning "getting out of your own way".

We would use a lot of visualisations and often an individual needed a different visual for the same thing depending on what would make it more "real" for them. I the beginning your teacher guides you through these until you reach a point where you have learned enough to be creative and invent your own techniques and visuals.

Toanswer your first question last. I began it for self defence, then it grew into health, then philosohy. I found myself in the philosophy/religion section in the library often. One day I realised that I was disqualifying certain philosophies that I thought did not fit with my beliefs and after that forced myself to grab at least one book thought would be crap at each visit. This forced another paradigm shift where instead of focussing on the differences between them, I would focus on the common threads . . . and that was a huge breakthrough for me - pieces of the puzzle that previously didnt fit started to harmonise and form a bigger picture. It was about this time I started reading books on human perception, artificial intelligence and quantum effects relating to conciousness and sentience. I believe that right now the student is ready and the teacher (DK) has appeared.

If there is one meditation that I still do more than any other is is one that starts off pulling energy in from . . . its hard to describe breifly where but it is like a parallel dimension where all are one . . . . to your center and outward, clearing toxins as it goes before radiating further beyond the body and out into the world carrying compassion? connectedness? . . . When I was first taught this one my teacher warned me that people seem to be attracted to this energy like a magnet and some will unconciously suck it all up like vampires if you let them . . . . but otherwise, it can be really fun and rewarding to use it to light people up and make them feel good.

Apologies for the long posts, there are just some subjects that really get me excited

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T08:01:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105736>

**Hi Josh**

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> Welcome. Do you still practice on any level? What style did you practice, martial or for health and healing or both?

Dont know what happened to my previous reply but I'll have another go . . . .

Yes I do still practice some aspects. I do the breathing meditation and Chi manipulation - specifically:
1\. Filling the lake - increasing energy through the tricky gate (3rd eye position) into the Tantien (lower abdomen area)
2\. Small circulation- recycling through the governing vessel - pirenuem area between scrotum and anus.
3\. Ion arms
4\. Ion legs
5\. Ion back
6\. Grand circulation using Buddhist breathing - 4 stopping points at governing vessel, base of skull, solar plexus and Tantien as opposed to Taoist which only stops at base of skull and Tantein.
7\. 5 gate breathing through tricky gate (3rd eye position) palms of hands and equivalent part of feet.
8\. Talking to the immortal - an astro type meditation that connects with the divine spark
9\. Detox meditation morphing into compassion meditation.

Of these 9. is my favourite and unless Im having trouble I can go straight to that one - the others help buildup to that one if required. Each of these has a visualisation that was either taught to me or that I have modified to make work better for mysef. If you have the right teacher there comes a point at which you can begin to get creative and invent or discover things that were not taught to you. The reason 9. is my favourite is that the compassion meditation is where you radiate energy beyond your own body to the world at large. When I was taught this I was warned that people become subconciously drawn to the energy and some will suck it up like vampires if you let them. But the best thing about it is that you seem to be able to light people up with it which makes it fun and very rewarding.

I dont practise on any schedule nor do I devote time to it, it has become something that I do simultaneously while doing other things - for example if I bang my knee or shin I will use it to deal with the pain and use the pain to draw energy to the injury (pain is your freind when you can use it to train your focus onto an injury site) and again use some of the circulating meditations to clear energy blockages. If Im really onto it sometimes I might use it to give someone else a boost if they need it or if I need them to perform or communicate a little better. Prior to social occasions I often use 9. to charge up both myself and my wife and the result is that we can often light up the other people at a party or such. Once you get a few going they start vibing off eachother and energy levels skyrocket for everyone.

I've recently discovered I can instantly stop shivering during CT by just dropping into breathing based meditation - interesting effect but I think there are good reasons for shivering and so I only do it as a sort of curiousity for a few seconds at a time.

The practice was breathing based meditation and control of Chi energy with martial arts as a useful tool for mind/body integration. For me it started being about self defence, then health and finally philosophy. I view most social interactions as energy flows nowadays and that perspective makes for some interesting insights into how people share, compete for, amplify and diminish eachother's energy. The Celestine Prophecy novels are great books that provide an insightful template for viewing social interactions from this perspective.

When exploring the philosophical phase I would collect books from the library to read. One day I caught myself ruling out certain books because I did not think they would fit with my understanding. From then on I made myself select at least one book each visit that I thought I would disagree with. That small change caused me to start looking for the common threads in all the philosophies instead of the differences and a lot of the puzzle pieces that I had previoulsy discarded started falling into place. After this I started reading books on human perception, quantum effects and metaphysical effects on conciousness and sentience. Hence reading Dr. Kruse's material regarding quantum physics and energy states is very interesting for me.

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> I still find it a challenge to recognize my resonant/internal training limit rather than my physical/external limit. The later always leaves me tired and the former always humbles me.

Those words right there make it clear to me that you have an experience based understanding of the mind/body effect. My 3rd grading was 5 hours long. The whole point of it was to go to the point of physical exhaustion and overcome it. So 3.5 hours into it and the body is protesting loudly through the nervous system against the mind, co-ordination goes out the window as the signals from both battle for energy and right of way and physical collapse seems immenent.

It was at this point that I learned that a change in context, a paradigm shift, could be used to integrate the mind and body. My body was saying if you keep doing that your arm is going to fall off - my mind was saying dont be silly, of course it wont. I then visulaised holding a loved one dangling off a cliff and how I was not going to let go until my arm actually did fall off.

This caused my breathing to return from laboured to relaxed, my limbs to feel light, my co-ordination to return and a new influx of energy. Reflecting later, it seems that we waste HUGE amounts of energy on internal conflict and that if we can integrate mind and body to act congruently we can free up large amounts of energy and power.

If you consider as I do that the nature of the universe is fractal, I also believe that universal truths apply universally and fractally and that is why things can have many layers of meaning without contradiction. For example, a less advanced layer of meaning for this mind body integration is that often a martial artist does not develop anywhere near the power he is capable of in a movement, until he learns to fully commit to the movement and fully release all the muscles opposing the movement. So that is what I mean when I say that mind/body integration is really about learning how to get out of your own way.

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> Interesting how in the current world teachers are having trouble with students not being able to handle the practices. I figure it is a combination of the distorted resonant environment and all the rest. I have a friend in China whose teacher has gone to villages to find children that are in good shape from having been brought up on a good diet away from modern life to train in his system.

An interesting observation that I was not aware of. I have to say that my teacher had to beg and plead for months before his teacher would teach him as he was not interested in teaching anyone. I think that perhaps, in some cases, when the right student comes along it brings out the teacher in someone. That idea kind of fits with another thing I learned deeply and that was that focussing on the end goal can often prevent you from ever acheiving it, but focussing on the journey will often get you there. This proved to be a big block for breaking concrete slabs and clay bricks - the more you wanted to break it, the less likely that it would break, but shifting your intention to perfecting your technique and letting go of the result often ended in a seemingly effortless break.

My interest in horsemanship gives me another perspective on this as well. Many trainers discard horses as untrainable if they do not get the results they normally get given their methods. However, the really talented ones, the horse whisperers if you like, treat each horse as an individual coming from a different place and adjust their repertoire to fit the horse rather than trying a one size fits all approach. THese talented trainers are often able to get spectacular results from horses others have discarded as petfood.

The point is - maybe these teachers need to look to themselves to find the solution and make adjustments for what "is" instead of trying to recreate what "was".

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105702)
>
> I have noticed that my body can only handle so much extra energy as I age and that diet and environment are quite important.

Now you touch on something interesting that I have discovered recently. You are maybe viewing yourself as a storage tank or a fuel tank for energy and that your capacity has diminished. Where intially in 1. above "filling the lake" or chi-ing up I would do the same thing, nowadays I use chanelling more than storage. In other words, I have developed a visualisation technique where energy flows through me rather than to me or from me. Can you see the difference here? I'm not the tank anymore - Im the fuel line. It means that so long as I can draw energy in and allow it to flow through, I dont have to store it because the universe is my fuel tank.

Hope this is not too lengthy or out there - but I have an instinct you get this stuff and its not often I get to communicate with people that either get it or can be bothered with it.

Sandy

Very fascinating indeed! I loved the "energy flows through me" thing, instead of a storage tank. very cool.. When you think the energy flows fast it goes even more funny  I like that a lot!

I am sure our thoughts matter more than we can imagine. Because I so often realize how I think about things can make them the total opposite...???!! This is so weird and I catch myself thinking about it again and again when I realize it. It is very magic! ### SeaHorse — 2014-01-15T14:50:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105757>

> My interest in horsemanship gives me another perspective on this as well. Many trainers discard horses as untrainable if they do not get the results they normally get given their methods. However, the really talented ones, the horse whisperers if you like, treat each horse as an individual coming from a different place and adjust their repertoire to fit the horse rather than trying a one size fits all approach. THese talented trainers are often able to get spectacular results from horses others have discarded as petfood.
>
> The point is - maybe these teachers need to look to themselves to find the solution and make adjustments for what "is" instead of trying to recreate what "was".

That pricked my ears up....this is the world I operate in. I offer an approach to Equine Facilitated Learning that gives people an opportunity to tune into their body and emotional awareness by working with horses who are masters of reading their environment. It is all about becoming familiar with what "is" not applying technique or methods. There are so many misguided training methods out there it's scary and every one of them will "work" on some horses, but at what cost? Looking forward to hearing more about your work.....

### nonchalant — 2014-01-15T17:17:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105764>

Wow, Sandy, great post! So sorry the system is sitting on your previous post. It will probably show up in a few days.
I really like what you wrote. I never thought I'd be interested in martial arts, though I did take a class in Tai Chi and loved it. What you outline does seem to resonate with Dr. K's science.

> pain is your freind when you can use it to train your focus onto an injury site

Neat! I may try concentrating on a stubbed toe or whatever. It won't be a trained focus, but could be interesting to try.

> I view most social interactions as energy flows nowadays and that perspective makes for some interesting insights into how people share, compete for, amplify and diminish eachother's energy.

Dr. K has mentioned that a bit, and I experienced it after a night of grounding for the first time. I was literally vibrating with energy, and I went to an in-law family gathering around noon. The social experience was almost enjoyable for me, lol! I certainly wasn't vibrating with energy afterwards, though.

> I've recently discovered I can instantly stop shivering during CT by just dropping into breathing based meditation - interesting effect but I think there are good reasons for shivering and so I only do it as a sort of curiousity for a few seconds at a time.

I think you should continue to stop your shivering (during CT) because the energy is flowing better then. IMO.

> That idea kind of fits with another thing I learned deeply and that was that focussing on the end goal can often prevent you from ever acheiving it, but focussing on the journey will often get you there.

Very true. This is what most of us on Dr. K's site are doing.

> I'm not the tank anymore - Im the fuel line. It means that so long as I can draw energy in and allow it to flow through, I dont have to store it because the universe is my fuel tank.

Some of Dr. K's later blogs mention this. It is the movement of energy, like amps in electricity, that is what you need. Voltage is good, but it pretty much just sits there. Potential energy.

Thank you for the explanation and sharing your experience. I have touched most of these places and there are good reminders throughout. I am challenged to bridge the traditional energetic practice world with the conventional science world. This is one of my hopes for my journey here is to get myself as up to date as possible on technologies that will demonstrate effects you and I seem to agree are in a practical sense "real". Of course, the root of my quest remains to out my own health which is a life project. Currently I am wading through Gerald Pollock's 4th Phase of Water book. The new information feels foundational in almost everything that we touch on n the forum and many of the "energetic" anatomy effects I have experienced. I suspected Da-Mo was a Bodhidharma reference. For anyone who has not looked at his alleged life story, martial arts is just one of his contributions and it is good to understand the context that he presented them in. ### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T21:31:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105787>

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T23:31:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kia-ora-from-new-zealand.9344/post-105822>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=105768)
>
> I also liked hearing the term "tricky gate" for the pineal gland. I have all sorts of plans for mine, once I get all the fluoride-induced calcification gone.

We never really related the two - and never really referred to it as the 3rd eye either - I just used that term really to identify the location on the forehead.

So far as to why we used that and called it a tricky gate, it had to do with it being a gate most difficult to use which made it a safer option for the beginner in terms of not leaving it wide open to outside influence. By comparison, the gate we caled the bahui on top of the skull was one of the last we were made aware of because it can leave you wide open - I think this had some link to why some religions cover that part of the head.

The 5 gate breathing - that is the gate in the forehead, palms and feet - think about the crucifixion of Christ and which pathways were shut down with the crown of thorns and the nails (although more accurately the nails were driven into the wrists and not the palms). Regardless of whether one believes it actually happened or not - the symbolism is still there. There are further correlations across different philosophies/religions encompassing halos (auras) and group energy dynamics (regular gatherings in churches, mosques, temples etc.). Just like with seemingly contradictory results between warm or cold adapted biology - the total picture is not one or the other - it is both, but only makes sense when you know how they link with eachother.

# Thread: CT question - Shivering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T08:32:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105738>

I'm currently doing CT in a pool at the base of a waterfall immersed up to my neck at 14C for about 25 minutes.

I start shivering within 5 minutes of immersion and the worst of the shivering peaks during the 15 minute walk back from the waterfall and continues for 30minutes to an hour afterwards.

Should I be keeping the same duration, increasing duration or just going to the point where shivering is induced?

I do try to maximise the calorie burn and muscle workout from the shivering but wonder whether that should be the end point or not?

### nicld — 2014-01-15T16:40:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105760>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=105738)
>
> I'm currently doing CT in a pool at the base of a waterfall immersed up to my neck at 14C for about 25 minutes.
>
> I start shivering within 5 minutes of immersion and the worst of the shivering peaks during the 15 minute walk back from the waterfall and continues for 30minutes to an hour afterwards.
>
> Should I be keeping the same duration, increasing duration or just going to the point where shivering is induced?
>
> I do try to maximise the calorie burn and muscle workout from the shivering but wonder whether that should be the end point or not?

Shivering afterwards in normal. I asked jack once about shivering while in CT and he said that it was not really good and that when that happens to him, he gets out and has some shrimp or coconut oil then get's back in and it helps. Once in a while I get a little shivering while doing CT but not too bad. When it does get bad I get out. That's my N=1 and I do ALOT of long cold cold CT and it has got way better over the past 6 months.

### Dali Dula — 2014-01-15T20:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105780>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=105738)
>
> I'm currently doing CT in a pool at the base of a waterfall immersed up to my neck at 14C for about 25 minutes.
>
> I start shivering within 5 minutes of immersion and the worst of the shivering peaks during the 15 minute walk back from the waterfall and continues for 30minutes to an hour afterwards.
>
> Should I be keeping the same duration, increasing duration or just going to the point where shivering is induced?
>
> I do try to maximise the calorie burn and muscle workout from the shivering but wonder whether that should be the end point or not?

For the first year I did what you are doing and had the same experience. Now I stop full immersion when I begin to shiver whether that is just five minutes or longer. This also seems to prevent shivering post CT. Saves a lot of time as we are non functional during shivering.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T21:58:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105793>

Thanks for the responses. So if I understand correctly, I should end CT at the onset of shivering and over time the duration before that onset should increase?

I should probably say that the shivering is not uncontrollable - I can stop it at will if I want - just wonder whether I should . . . .

### caroline — 2014-01-15T22:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105795>

I really thought some shivering was a good thing .... am I wrong about that?

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T03:45:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105849>

What kind of shivering are we talking about? Can you talk normally? Are they fairly intense, or almost unnoticeable tremors running along your spine?

### Josh — 2014-01-16T03:51:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105850>

If you have not already, read CT 6, 7 and the second half of 11 where someone talks about their process; and the appendix of the book if you have it. These will frame your inquiry.

### nicld — 2014-01-16T04:19:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105854>

I think the small shivers are fine at least I hope so since I get them. The real issue may be the whole body shakes where your core body temperature may be dropping. When I get the whole body shaking then its time to get out.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T04:24:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105856>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=105854)
>
> I think the small shivers are fine at least I hope so since I get them. The real issue may be the whole body shakes where your core body temperature may be dropping. When I get the whole body shaking then its time to get out.

Yes, I agree totally.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-16T07:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105880>

Seems like there is no definitive answer so I'll take it to the Ask Jack section after checking the CT blogs again . . . . .

Next silly question . . . . Risk of reperfusion injury

After I get out of the waterfall pool it is a 15 minute walk back to the car. Given that I am wet and cold and shivering, and I feel it more in my arms and legs and between my shoulder blades - I dont really feel cold around the torso otherwise . . . . .

What risk am I running or a reperfusion injury where because of the walking I am not circulating cold blood from my extremeties and limbs back through the heart and lungs? . . . . .

this is the part of my CT where I feel the coldest - for the first part of the walk, by the second half of the walk I'm feeling warmer again.

I may take this question to the Ask Jack section as well. I know he will stand outside in the buff for awhile after getting out of the lake - but maybe he doesnt move around so much while he does this.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T18:52:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105922>

No idea about the reperfusion injury, but I like the double meaning in the second line of your sig.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-16T23:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105963>

I hadn't thought of that until you mentioned it nonchalant  But yes, you are right!

I sometimes ask people, chocloate or vanilla, how many choices do you have . . . most say two. I say you have four, chocolate, vanilla, both or none . . . .

My father extended this even further. Once when he was in hospital he was given a menu so he could tick in the box what foods he wanted. What he wanted was not on the list, so he wrote on that list what he wanted, drew a small box next to it and put a check mark in the box. Blow me down if they didnt turn up with the breakfast he chose

BTW, we saw an eel in the waterfall pool where we CT last night . . . dont think wife or daughter will be going back anytime soon unless I make some sort of cage for them to sit in . . . .

### Josh — 2014-01-24T06:52:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset.9350/post-106878>

On Gerald Pollack and 4th Phase of Water stuff.

1\. The book is great and easy to read, but it takes time to digest.
2\. He actually does not talk about human cells much in the book. For this I recommend the following which include much of the material from the book and more:

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JnGCMQ8TJ\_g](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JnGCMQ8TJ_g)

EU part 1

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hqHWueBp23c](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hqHWueBp23c)

EU part 2

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y--L6BoH3Ug](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y--L6BoH3Ug)

a lecture that covers the high points and gets to the point with the cell, but is not as comprehensive conceptually as the book. I watched it with my wife and she not only got it, but she got excited and started applying it everywhere. Not an everyday experience with her and physics....

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M3Sy1SZNebk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M3Sy1SZNebk)

with Ameer Rosic who asks some good and not so good questions, still much to be gathered on how to think about it.

Also the Thunderbolts Project Videos. All in all, if you watch them all, you get most of the book plus more related to biology. He is a good speaker and does not overwhelm. I would say that one could watch the videos and get what is needed to understand Dr. Kruse and go on.

You gotta love a man who readily says "we have no clue yet...." and "well, water has some potential for the future..."

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-25T17:31:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset.9350/post-107024>

Yes thanks Josh for the links, my book arrived today, so I'm ready to go .....

# Thread: Sandra's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 109
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sandra — 2014-01-16T01:07:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-105837>

Hi Everyone,

This is my first post on the forum though I have been lurking for a while and reading a lot about what Dr Kruse writes. I was interested when I saw the link to the 90 day re-set, so thought I should give it a go.

A bit about me ... I am from Australia, heading quickly towards 40 years of age, normal height and weight, have been eating a mostly paleo diet (no grain or sugar, but some dairy) for about 15 months, have had a magnetico mattress for around 8 weeks, and have installed an RO water filter in our home. I am trying to 'do all the right things' for me, as well as slowly transfer my husband and children to a more paleo life-style (all 3 are big grain eaters).

I don't have any major health issues (that I am aware of) and am hoping none arise, though from what I read, they can kind of sneak up on you!!

One thing that I do want to sort out is my tiredness. It's not chronic, doesn't stop me from doing anything, have had it for ages (I remember friends telling me I was always tired and yawning during my teenage years at school) and I am always yawning and not feeling totally energised. I thought that a paleo diet might change that, and that the magnetico might as well, but early days. I did have some tests done at the GP some time ago and I was iron deficient - not anaemic, but still low iron. I elected to try to eat more iron rich foods rather than to take supplements as the GP suggested. I didn't go back for a follow up test, but presume my iron is still low as I am still tired. Just talking to a naturopath today and she suggests that one reason for tiredness is that there might be some foods I am eating that react with my gut and do not allow the iron to be absorbed properly ... hopefully I'll know more next week after some tests. I am assuming it might be dairy as I was told 7 years ago by a kinesiologist (while he was testing my daughter for allergies) that I was intolerant to dairy. I didn't know enough about it then but the more I read about paleo and after discussions with the naturopath, it won't surprise me if that is it. So, I am hoping for an easy solution, otherwise it's onto round 2 to try to get to the bottom of it.

I have not had any other tests done that have been mentioned on the forum or by Dr Kruse ... maybe that's my next step. I've also not thought too much about CT - perhaps I should give that a go too??

That's all for now! I hope I have this in the right section, and looking forward to chatting with you.

Cheers

### Sandra — 2014-01-23T12:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106736>

Just home from an interesting trip to the Naturopath, who also does Kinesiology …

I'm still getting my head around the alternative medical practices, but the more I read and experience, the more I think there might be something in it.

To cut a long story short, she thinks my body reacts to gluten, corn, peanuts, soy (all at an amount of 10/10), dairy (5/10) and oysters (2/10). And she thinks I have candida, which apparently would explain my sub-optimal energy for many many years. She thinks that the candida has created a leaky gut which leads to bloating and inflammation in my body, and also the pain in my shoulders (from no apparent injury).

With any of these alternative therapies, I wonder if I divulged too much information about how I was feeling and certain ailments before the session, and was she just coming to the conclusions based on what I said … but I don't think so, as I could feel differences as she was testing the various vials.

So, I guess I have nothing to lose by giving it a go … a 1 month course to get rid of the candida, and then a 1 month course of pro-biotics to build up the gut flora again. I don't eat any grains, corn, peanuts and soy any more (for the last 12 months), and have a very small amount of dairy, so might cut that out totally for the next month and see how it goes.

I'd be interested to hear if anyone else has had experiences with kinesiology and what they thought of it. Thank you

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-23T13:47:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106740>

> [Sandra said:](/goto/post?id=106736)
>
> I'd be interested to hear if anyone else has had experiences with kinesiology and what they thought of it.

I remember over 25 years ago taking my eldest child to a kinesiologist because of hyperactivity problems, and he tested various foods and my son was put on a special diet. It worked brilliantly for a while, (physically at least, but emotionally very difficult), and then problems started again, and it was found at the second session that heâ€™d become â€œallergicâ€ to things he was OK with before. Each session cost a weekâ€™s wages we couldnâ€™t really spare to see this very respected naturopath/kinesiologist. What I've since learned is that it can tell me what, at that particular moment, is going on about certain things tested, but it doesnâ€™t give the whole picture, and most importantly - it doesn't tell me why. My sonâ€™s hyperactivity wasnâ€™t caused by a lack of the faddy diet I unfortunately put him on.  That set us back 2 years, and made things worse for a lot longer than that.

I've personally had machine bioresonance testing rather than kinesiology, but a machine still has limitations. For example I tested badly for milk, so I nipped up to the local shop and got some non-homogenised local full fat liquid milk, and the result was completely different. (Fortunately the therapist is a friend who is on my wavelength about experimentation and encourages people to bring things in they want testing.) The test vial was dried milk of unknown origin.

So although I would say that its fascinating that someone can experience the energetic difference when certain things are tested, I believe its a response to the contents of the vial, on the biologic/energetic terrain at that moment.

### caroline — 2014-01-24T09:31:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106885>

Sandra - personally I would say the GP is a D\*\*k. I have had probably 6 hsCRP done thru medicare. The ones they don't cover is rT3 and prenenolone and they are about $50.00 each.

Sad to say - you need to find another Doc. Did I tell you.... I found my Doc by asking the pharmacist at a compounding pharmacy. They are perfectly fine with giving you names of Docs who are writing scripts for BHRT... at least you will know they are interested in functional medicine. After - the pharmacist asked me for feedback because someone else had asked her for names. She was moving here from out of town.

You may also need to fib a little .... like make up a back history ....maybe a Mom Or Grandmother who had TD2 or thyroid issue or whatever. You need to think outside the box to get what you need.

The one test that is harder to get here is IGF1 .... medicare comes down hard on Docs if they ask for that test too often. I have had it done twice in the last 18 months. My Doc just has to make up his little story on the requisition to cover himself.

Your Doctor should be a partner - don't settle for anything less!

How are you? Say Hi to Nick!

### Sandra — 2014-01-24T12:37:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106897>

I was just reading a few of the earlier blogs. One spoke about inheriting genes from your mother and grandmother that affect mitochondria and metabolism, so thought I'd write it down ...

maternal grandmother
\- very strong and healthy all of her life
\- grew up as a farmers wife and worked hard
\- had the biggest fruit and vegetable patch I've ever seen
\- majority of fruits, vegetable and meats were home grown/raised, and meat would have been mainly grass fed, probably lots of organ meat (very little waste of anything as there was not a lot of money to go around and 7 kids in the family)
\- probably not much fish eaten
\- everything home baked, and there was bread, cakes and biscuits
\- very lean body composition in all photos I ever saw of her in the younger days, and until the end
\- walked a lot for daily exercise
\- died aged 87 from a stroke which they think was brought on as a side effect of an eye operation

mother
\- growing up with the food mentioned above
\- quite slim until after I was born
\- started to put on weight and become quite overweight, though not obese, from when I was around 10
\- now has thyroid problems (and is on medication), probably from diet as there is a lot of dairy, grains and sugar, though she still home-cooks most things. Also a lot of beer and no exercise.

### Sandra — 2014-01-24T13:43:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106903>

... my mum likes line dancing too!! ... you can take the girl out of the bush, but you can't take the bush out of the girl!!

I don't think the grains would have been locally milled - possibly, but not sure.

All the kids were really healthy early on, but now that they range from around 70+ to 50+, they are starting to have issues.

The eldest male, 72, had been going through chemo and radiation starting about 3 years ago for prostate cancer (clear now).

The next oldest female, 70, has been healthy all her life, married a farmer, home baked and grew fruit/veggies, walked every day for 10kms, and has recently developed high cholesterol - resisted medication for as long as she could, tried natural medication, but just within the last 12 months started to take something prescribed by the DR as it wouldn't come down (I don't know any more specifics than that, and when it was happening I didn't really know any of this stuff to help her out more).

Next child, male, 67, quite healthy, still lives on the farm, don't know much more bout him.

Next child, male, 65, slightly overweight, but still active, and don't know a lot about him either!

Next is my aunt who is 63. I think she's OK, but has been concerned lately with something in her eyes ... not cataracts or glaucoma, can't remember it!

Then it's my mum, 60, overweight, thyroid issues ...

And lastly my aunt, 58, periods of overweightedness but does Weight Watchers - otherwise I think OK.

I think my mum's pregnancies were all good, with me it was fine, natural delivery. I grew up on a farm too (a dairy farm!) - until I was about 18. Again, we had a big veggie patch, had cattle, sheep, pigs, chooks on the farm, so all meat was grass fed. Mum baked a lot too, so very little processed foods, but still dairy and grain. My 2 pregnancies were all fine, fell pregnant very easily and natural births. Actually, there was one thing that I never really got to the bottom of with #2 (girl, now turning 8) ....

... when I was pregnant with my daughter (2nd child) we went out to dinner at a seafood restaurant (have always loved seafood - esp prawns!!). I was about 6-8 months pregnant and ate a bowl full of the best seafood - fish, shell fish, crustaceans ... YUM!! The next day by entire baby bump was itchy and covered in a hives rash. It went away by the afternoon. I was concerned and went to the doctor who couldn't tell me anything except that I probably had a reaction to the seafood I'd eaten the night before (eventhough I'd eaten it all my life). I didn't eat shell fish or crustaceans for the rest of my pregnancy.

FAST FORWARD 6 months and my daughter has an allergic reaction to egg (which we initially thought was a heat rash ... itchy hives on chest, but went away on its own). Another 6 months later, and she has a reaction to peanut butter - same hives as the egg reaction, but this time was on chest, tummy, back and face. Raced her to our nearest doctor, 5 doors down the hill, and he watched as the rash went away within the hour with no effect on breathing. We then ordered specialist tests (standard scratch test on forearm) to which we were told she was allergic to eggs, nuts and shellfish!

So at 1 year of age, she had also had eczema all of her life. Doctors said not to worry about the eczema as she was still putting on weight and developing as per their charts (eventhough I thought it was strange she was vomitting a lot after I would breastfeed her). I questioned them weather it would be because of something I was eating, but they didn't think so. During this time of testing, we were not happy with the specialist really - yep, she's allergic to eggs, nuts and shell fish, so see you in 12 months for another test! It was now I had my first experience with a kinesiologist referred by a friend. He told us she was allergic to eggs, peanuts, shellfish, salicylates, dairy and grain - WOWSERS!!! I cried all the way home as we'd just booked a family trip to Italy! So, we took her off dairy and grain, and within 2 weeks her skin was perfect - no eczema. We continued this for 2 months and it was all good. This is back way before I'd even heard of paleo, and we thought "how can she live like this (no grain, no dairy)?" so we slowly started to add them back into her diet. Everything was 'fine' unless she overdosed on either and would get little hives in the insides of her elbows or on her ankles ... still does today.

She can eat eggs now, along with all other nuts, but not peanuts (and we haven't tried that one ... probably no need to now!). I suspect shellfish are OK too, but haven't actually fed any to her, though she has eaten oyster sauce in stir-frys. And the salicylates, dairy and grain are now back in her diet too. She is one that I would really like to get off grain, especially knowing what I do now!

I've always wondered whether it was my daughter reacting with the seafood I at while pregnant with her, and is it because of me that she's allergic, eventhough I'm not? It must be related somewhere, but no-one has ever said how or why ...

### sooperb — 2014-01-25T00:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106933>

When I was 8 months pregnant with my first I also grew a rash all over my bump that itched like mad. I was told by the doctor in the clinic that it was a general urticaria quite common in pregnancy and that it would go away after delivery. He was right it did and I didn't get it with my next two. Strangely though, that daughter developed chronic asthma at two and a half and has it to this day. She's had a host of other problems too. Her second daughter has eczema although she breast fed her for a long time and the first up indication of allergy problems was her first taste of kiwi at less than a year old which saw her face and ears swell alarmingly. Fortunately that went down on it's own while she was in the hospital.

Lots of things caused her problems, cows milk, cola drinks (both of which brought a red rash up round her mouth), sweets with bright colours. As soon as her mum put two and two together she started being very careful about what she let her eat so her eczema never got too bad but even now, at 14, too much of the wrong thing has her scratching at herself.

### Sandra — 2014-01-29T12:46:06+0200

I think my Leptin Reset essentially started today as I was starting to eat my BAB's within 1 hr of waking ... as of today, it's 1/2 an hour!!

So, some stats ...

38 year old female

weight - 65kg (143 pds)

height - 165cm (5ft 5in)

a bit of a tummy pudge but otherwise I'm OK with my body shape

2 kids, aged 9 and 7

I'd like to get to the bottom of my tiredness ... not critical, but less than optimal, and the more I read, the more I wonder if I have adrenal or hormone issues too. Again, I'm not feeling they are at a critical level, but something is off (and I guess this means something could be critical at some time in the future!)

no labs back yet ... tracked down some from a few years ago and had a few done last week - shall post results when I get them. I couldn't get all the ones I wanted due to the doctor not feeling they were in relation to my 'tiredness' which is what I went to see her about ... time for a new doc, I know - working on that.

Have been eating Paleo for about 12 months, including a little dairy, but have decided to remove all dairy now to see if that makes any difference.

My work and home life is reasonably stress-free, and although it is constant and I don't have a lot of down-time, I don't feel overwhelmed with anything. I work 2 days a week at home, 3 days away from home, have a few after school activities for the kids, husband works from home and life if pretty flexible and balanced.

Usual daily timeframe ...
5am - wake up
5:30am to 6am - brekky
6am to 3pm - work
11am - lunch
3:30pm - my dinner
3pm to 7pm - kids homework, activities, dinner prep
8:30/9pm - bed

Sleep is pretty good ... if I wake up during the night, I go back to sleep pretty quickly. Usually fall asleep alright and wake just before my 5am alarm. Always yawn and feel tired during the day, even if I get more sleep. I have a magnetico mattress but can't say I've noticed any great difference since having it (around 2 months now).

I use the blue-blocker glasses too, and doing it religiously now while on the Leptin Reset.

Is there anything else I should note?

### Sandra — 2014-01-30T02:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-107378>

I doubt I'm peri menopausal as I have hardly any of the symptoms (listed below) but if there's other things you think point to it, I'd love to hear it. I am still on the pill so could that mask it, and I'm 38 ... is that early?

Perhaps I should seek out an ASI test. I mentioned "adrenal" to the GP when I had my blood tests ordered last week and she told me that medicine doesn't deal with adrenal issues and I'd need to talk to the naturopath about that. I want to get the other test results back but really don't feel like I can go back and talk to her - I'm not going to get anywhere!

35 Symptoms of Perimenopause

Hot flashes, hot flushes, night sweats and/or cold flashes, clammy feeling
Irregular heart beat
Irritability
Mood swings, sudden tears
Trouble sleeping through the night (with or without night sweats)
Irregular periods; shorter, lighter periods; heavier periods, flooding; phantom periods, shorter cycles, longer cycles
Loss of libido
Vaginal dryness
Crashing fatigue
Anxiety, feeling ill at ease
Feelings of dread, apprehension, doom
Difficulty concentrating, disorientation, mental confusion
Disturbing memory lapses
Incontinence, especially upon sneezing, laughing; urge incontinence
Itchy, crawly skin
Aching, sore joints, muscles and tendons
Increased tension in muscles
Breast tenderness
Headache change: increase or decrease
Gastrointestinal distress, indigestion, flatulence, gas pain, nausea
Sudden bouts of bloat
Depression
Exacerbation of existing conditions
Increase in allergies
Weight gain
Hair loss or thinning, head, pubic, or whole body; increase in facial hair
Dizziness, vertigo, light-headedness, episodes of loss of balance
Changes in body odor
Electric shock sensation under the skin and in the head
Tingling in the extremities
Gum problems, increased bleeding
Burning tongue, burning roof of mouth, bad taste in mouth, change in breath odor
Osteoporosis (after several years)
Changes in fingernails: softer, crack or break easier
Tinnitus: ringing in ears, bells, 'whooshing,' buzzing etc.

### Sandra — 2014-02-06T21:20:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-108738>

Thought I'd add a mini timeline of events that might relate to some lab changes ...

Nov 2011 - first labs to go off ... while eating my 'normal' 37 year diet ... not overly bad I'd say, but still full of grains and dairy, though not a lot of sugar. Around June of 2011 I scared myself on the scales, and while I've never been overweight in my life, I was 69.9kgs ... and I swore I would no way be 70, so started to cut out snacking on junk then (so about 6 months before first labs)

pre-Oct 2012 - worked from home in our business, very flexible schedule, not a lot of computer time, lots of outside time in the veggie garden , also walked at least 5 times a week ... not power-walking, about 6kms in an hour, and did some swimming

Oct 2013 - started an office job 3 days a week, in front of a computer all day. much less exercise

Jan 2013 - started eating Paleo but still included some dairy, and I was snacking (I know that's not good now!)

June, July, August 2013 - trip to Europe, stuck 95% to eating Paleo, walked a lot, lost 3kgs - down to sub 62kgs (though back to 65kg pretty quickly ... maybe exercise??)

Aug 2013 - changed pill to a lower dose on Dr's advice due to risks associated with cancer and clotting on the one I was taking for so long (considering ditching it altogether now)

Nov 2013 - installed home and work RO water system, and got a 10 gauss magnetico mattress. Love drinking the water, and drink 4-5 Litres a day, but can't say I feel any noticeable difference from the magnetico yet (after 3 months)

Jan 25, 2014 - started Leptin Reset

Soooo, maybe the big change in my BUN/Creatine ratio could be do do with working in an office, change of pill, or lack of exercise???

Still sticking to the BAB's - no desire to snack, and happy with 2 meals a day. Not really feeling any different to before but was also on pretty low carb and no sugar

No pill (Levlen) as of Friday and I'll be getting some hormone testing done in about 6-8 weeks time, but now I have a headache ... I never get headaches

### Sandra — 2014-05-03T06:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124833>

It's been a while since i've been here but have been continuing on.

Still eating epi-paleo and stayed on the lepton reset for 4 weeks. No weight loss and no real physical change but I'm ok with that - 5kgs would have been nice though

Had some hormon blood test and they were all fine, in limits, with nothing the GP is concerned with.

Shoulder is still sore for no apparent reason so went off for another round of xrays ultrasounds and even a neck MRI. Nothing showed up except inflation in one of my shoulder bursas. They suggested a cortisone injection ... It's been sore for three years - so I went for it. Was great for a week or so then back to usual soreness again! Then Nick told me about the shoulder thread on here - whoa!!! That's me  so the next step ... Bone broth and CT - I want to fix this up and want to try anything that might work. The broth tastes awesome and is so filling. CT I'm working up to ... we have a swimming pool and it's 24 deg C at the moment. I know it's no 10 degrees but it's under body temp so I'm working with that at the moment. Have been I'm for 3 days straight for 1/2 hour each time. I shivered and shook the whole first time - was very weird, but never felt cold. 2nd time less shaking, and third time I swam around instead of reading and didn't shake at all. There was a temperature drop last night so hopefully it's getting colder. I'll keep on with the 10deg face dunks too and see what I notice over the next week. No change with my shoulder yet but having great sleeps and dreams the past three nights ... do things happen that quickly??

There's a quick recap and hopefully I'll be around more now - have a lot of reading to catch up on.

### Sandra — 2014-05-03T06:59:54+0300

### caroline — 2014-05-04T00:33:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124933>

Hellllooooo!

How did you make the cabbage?

### Sandra — 2014-05-04T01:36:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124935>

It was pretty easy actually ... just cabbage and salt, and lots of strong hands

### Sandra — 2014-05-04T01:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124936>

There are a few recipes floating around out there, but basically you slice the cabbage really thinly. For each kg of cabbage, sprinkle over 1 tablespoon of salt - best to do this in lots of 500g we found. I think we scrunched about 5kgs of cabbage in one sitting and filled about 8 1L Mason jars. I was lucky to have 2 sets of big man hands helping me too

Once you have sprinkled the salt on the cabbage, scrunch it with your hands. Really squeeze and squish it. After about 5-10 minutes, you'll notice the juice coming out of it. Once your cabbage has gone really soggy and you have lots of juice, time to jar it.

Sterilise your jars earlier, then (once the jars are cool) fill them with the sauerkraut. Leave about 2cm from the top of the jar and then fill with the juice. Don't seal it yet.
Then you need to ferment it for 3-10 days. I did 3 days as it was reasonably warm. Optimum temp is 20-24 deg C (or about 65-75 F). Push it down every few hours or use a weight to press it down. You'll see bubbles start to form - this is good. Sometimes there might be scum on top, so scoop this off (I didn't get scum, but I don't think it's a bad thing if you do). You can taste as you go too.

Once it's done, seal it up. If you live in a cold area you could store it on the shelf, but I didn't want to risk it, so put it in the fridge.

It's nice and crispy and great with grass fed sausages and mustard

I don't think this is the exact recipe I used, but looks familiar ...

[http://www.thekitchn.com/how-to-mak...on-jar-cooking-lessons-from-the-kitchn-193124](http://www.thekitchn.com/how-to-make-easy-homemade-sauerkraut-in-a-mason-jar-cooking-lessons-from-the-kitchn-193124)
