### Sandra — 2014-06-06T00:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-129878>

We were talking about the underwire bra issues a few days ago amongst some friends and the question was raised about rings (jewellery). I have been having issues with my fingers for about 6 months ... waking up in the mornings with stiff fingers. After a few minutes of stretching they seem OK, until the next morning. Nothing showed up in x-rays and MRIs of my neck, massage and physio on my arm and shoulders didn't seem to make a difference. Well, after our discussion 3 days ago, I have not been wearing my gold rings (on 3 fingers on one hand and 2 fingers on my left hand) ... and no more stiffness!!

Is that a bit weird, is it coincidence, or is there a good reason for the gold in my rings somehow causing my fingers to have these issues???

Interestingly I also wear a fairly heavy gold fob chain around my neck and I have started to have shoulder issues. I'm wondering if they are all related (amongst other things as well....).

So, I'm experimenting with wearing no jewellery during the day and none to bed anymore, and will take notice of how things go.

### Sandra — 2015-02-21T11:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156210>

a few strange observations lately ...
\- 'itchy' throat (inside)
\- itchy knees (on the front surface)

\- still tired and some bloating (situation normal!)

\- shoulders so much better (I put that down to the new latex mattress from last July)

\- started to take a magnesium supplement as advised by naturopath and am also taking some drops to flush out lymph nodes in the armpit area (which showed up on a test as being blocked - maybe due to deodorant or potential link to breast tissue)

\- currently waiting on results from a hair allergy test - not sure how accurate this will be but thought I'd give it a try to see if there are any more clues there. Naturopath thinks tiredness might be due to a food allergy and thinks my stomach area is very inflamed. I will also attempt to keep a food diary.

### caroline — 2015-02-22T00:09:37+0200

### Sandra — 2015-02-22T13:49:08+0200

It was great!! Jac has some pics which I'm sure Nick will post soon. I even ate prawn shells as Yew told me to  Lots of little clues just keep piecing together and I never would have worked it all out myself... so grateful to have found this forum and the wonderful people who are part of it.

### Sandra — 2015-02-22T14:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156278>

Just some other notes for me ...

\- feel tired tonight but can't sleep. Have been using blue blockers again regularly. Tonight is quite hot so have now put on the ceiling fan and I wonder if it might also have something to do with getting into the pool which was cooler than usual after all the rain??? I tend to get 'hot' after my limited attempt at CT - not that of class today as CT really - maybe it's just a hot night! ### caroline — 2015-02-22T23:02:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156313>

### Sandra — 2015-03-04T12:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-157403>

I've been keeping a good diary for around 10 days now. It's amazing shat you don't want to eat if you have to write it down!! Aside from no grain or sugar which is fairly normal for me now I am also not eating dairy or eggs. Something gives me tummy pains and bloating and I suspect it's eggs or dairy. The good thing is that I have lost cms and maybe even inches from my waist line!! I've never been one for weighing and measuring but when I put ony clothes and notice they are loose, and I feel good, then something must be right!

However I'm also taking some supplements and doing a bit of exercise so I don't know exactly what's working but I guess it's good anyway.

Just had 4 days in Rotorua and a funny observation re my fingers (which are usually a bit tight and sore every morning) ... they weren't in Rotorua! I did have a hot natural spa and sauna every night while away though ... wonder if that has anything to do with it. Also noticed that my period was very heavy just for day 1 and day 2 and then it was over - wonder if the hot spa had anything to do with that! (usually for last 12 months day 1 heavy and day 2-4 light) (and pre paleo and in the pill was always day 1-3 heavy and day 4-7 light).

Also been eating fresh sashimi every day or other day and two meals a day rather than 3 - just don't feel like I need to eat 3 and I seem to have more energy.

I'll keep noting and see what happens

### caroline — 2015-03-04T23:25:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-157452>

Good morning Sandra!

### Sandra — 2015-03-05T23:10:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-157572>

I also seem to be eating just two meals a day which is a bit odd for me Breakfast seems to be around 8 or 9 am and is quite a good size (3 sausages and some sauteed cabbage and veggies) and then lunch/dinner is around 3 or 4pm. I seem to have plenty of energy, am getting lots of sleep and feel great, so I guess that's OK. I'll just keep going down this path I think..

I've also noticed that perhaps I have a bit of nut issue going on too. I bake 'paleo' treats in waves, freeze them, eat them (not between meals as snacks but as part of the meal). When I don't do that I feel much better ... lots of little observations that might just add up to something big later on.

### Sandra — 2015-03-24T08:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-159343>

Confused ... I had an intolerance/allergy test done with a hair sample. Some people totally disagree with this testing and others seem to see merit in it, and even have the results line up with 'standard' medical tests.

So, I thought I'd give it a go and see it I found any more answers. I already had a couple of suspicions concerning dairy, eggs, red wine and chocolate. I was pretty sure about gluten.

I ran a little experiment on myself over the past few weeks after meeting Yew. I kept a food diary and waited for the results to arrive. During this time I avoided eggs, diary, chocolate and gluten. I was amazed with how I felt - lots more energy and not tired, as well as having far less abdominal bloating, and even some weight loss (about 3-4 kgs out of 69kgs)

I received the results today. I must admit I didn't really do a lot of research into what food types would be tested but just went on the naturopath's advice that perhaps it would be worth getting done.

These are the items that came as having 'tested reactive for your immune system and must be avoided' ...

\- celery
\- olives
\- shallots
\- avocado
\- cucumber
\- onion
\- tomato
\- cashews

\- lavish bread (no surprise there - gluten)
\- white bread (no surprise there - gluten)
\- wholemeal bread (no surprise there - gluten)

\- Jatz biscuits (no surprise there - gluten)
\- milk arrowroot biscuits (no surprise there - gluten)
\- são biscuits (no surprise there - gluten)
\- select chocolate creams (no surprise there - gluten)
\- vita wheat original (no surprise there - gluten)

\- pasta durham wheat (no surprise there - gluten) ... but not couscous - I thought that was made out of the same stuff and contained gluten?
\- white flour (no surprise there - gluten)
\- wholemeal flour (no surprise there - gluten)

\- chocolate (darrell lea brand)
\- chocolate (lindt brand)
\- chocolate (nestle brand)
\- chocolate (cadbury brand)
\- chocolate (dove brand) ..... assuming these commercial chocolates contain a soy product as carob and cocoa were fine
\- Licorice (no surprise there - gluten)

\- quite a few corn, oat, bran and wheat cereals - no major problem for me as I avoid these anyway ... but rice bran, rice bubbles, oat bran and polenta were OK

\- brown sugar
\- white sugar
\- raw sugar

\- beer (no loss)
\- red wine (what!!!!!!!)

\- apricots
\- bananas
\- kiwifruit
\- pawpaw

\- cola
\- drinking chocolate (cadbury)
\- fanta
\- ginger ale
\- milo (contains gluten)
\- ovaltine (contains gluten)

\- garlic
\- gelatine

\- shellfish (what!!!!!!)

\- ayam oyster sauce
\- soy sauce
\- tomato sauce
\- tomato paste
\- worcester sauce

\- butter
\- cows cheese
\- cows yoghurt
\- cream
\- eggs
\- icecream
\- full cream milk (but A2 is OK)
\- skim milk
\- soy milk

\- tofu
\- tempey

\- pork (no!!!! - I love bacon and crackling!!) - funnily enough, by avoiding eggs recently, I also avoided bacon ... and felt great

\- dove deodorant
\- mum deodorant

\- polyester (funny - I've always hated wearing this)

\- cigarette smoke (I've always hated this too - tried smoking once when I was 17 and vomited horribly ... never did it again and now can't stand the smell)

I'm wondering if anyone else has ever had this DNA Hair Allergy testing done, were the results in line with other tests you had done, did you feel better after avoiding the foods they suggested to avoid.

I'm also interested in what the connections are between the various foods, especially the fruit and vegetables. I thought eggplant might be off the list being a nightshade, as tomato is, but eggplant seemed to be fine.

I'm also wondering if this is a 'forever' thing, or if one day I'll be able to eat these foods again. I simply love shellfish and avocado ....

Thanks for any advice in advance.
Sandra

### Sandra — 2018-01-17T12:49:00+0200

How time flies in the blink of an eye ... nearly three years later and I'm still popping in from time to time to read up on things but rarely commenting. Loving all the inquisitive minds and advice here. Thank you everyone

And the good news is my breast MRI was all clear and I feel and see no effects from the contrast gadolinium.

### Sandra — 2024-02-29T02:31:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-332036>

Hi Strangers - it's been a while ... 6 years! Good to see this site is still here. It's like coming back to a wise neighbour. Funny how we put up with things to a point then need them solved. I am still having the tiredness issues - not stopping me doing anything but just not optimal. I have tracked down a functional GP who seems really good. Just one meeting so far. She started talking about circadian biology and I said I knew about that as I had followed a US neurosurgeon for years but just hadn't really implemented it all practically. She asked who it was ... so I saved her telling me all about Uncle Jack - yay!! I felt she'd be the right one after that. So here I go again ... but now incorporating every sunrise with barefoot grounding - 7 days in a row now. She looked at all my symptoms and said thyroid issues for sure so is running the full thyroid panel, as well as hormones. I'm tracking everything I do and eat and how I feel.

I have a question ... if it is my thyroid, and since thyroid controls the hormones, and if I'm getting a big energy slump on day 1-3 of my cycle, how do I fix this and feel better? I just feel like I need to eat something to get some energy, but when I look at all the food I'm eating (100-120g protein a day, good fat, some salad vegetables, the odd piece of fruit), I can't possibly be hungry. Do I actually need energy? And in the past when I felt like this and I ate, it didn't fix the feeling of that energy slump. So if it's hormones, how do I feel better and get rid of the low energy feeling. I've emailed the new doctor but she works on her own and may not get back to me quickly. Next appt is end of April. Any ideas here would be great to manage me through. Thanks

### Sandra — 2024-02-29T02:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-332037>

Day 1 of new work from home office ... outdoor grounding

### Sandra — 2024-03-23T07:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-332775>

It's my birthday today ... new year, new me. Started with sunrise on the beach and a swim. Total bliss! I'm reading a book "Younger Next Year" ... mid life crisis time coming up and I'm doing what I can to avoid that! I'm making improvements in my health, redox/circadian biology, food, lifestyle - my gift to me is to continuously improve this. I'm starting to feel better, people are starting to notice - it will be a great year! Whilst I have big gaps in my time spent on this site, I am so grateful for you all for sharing your stories and advice to create an amazing resource, and to Jack for being the light!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-23T18:17:41+0200

### Nick — 2014-01-29T10:10:55+0200

We have just bought a 10G Magnetico for him and will give the 7yo the 5G version. I am very keen to see what more progress we can make while he is still so young. We just had a terrific trip to Canada, a holiday of a lifetime, he survived that including many days of ski lessons which was a big relief. No problems with any of our energy levels! I have attached a photo of him, hopefully it works.

Sandra is great to talk to and is very smart, I am not sure about co opting her journal though, lol!!!!

We broke him after the pregnancy I think, he was born very healthy in all respects but developed Allergies and Eczema at 6 months. In that time up to when he was three we had him sitting in a 12MGauss field from a fridge through the wall plus he was also next to 2 iPads, 2 iPhones and a wireless home phone right on the other side of the wall to his bed. It was only because of the info from JK about EMF that we cottoned onto this. Up until then the testing was progressively worse every time and the best the Dr's could recommend was to use yet another steroid cream or use wet wraps. My wife is part of a FB eczema group and there are so many kids that are getting worse all the time with many mothers now using chemo to try and reverse the eczema. You can see why I am so grateful to Jack. We have avoid this, managed to fix a fair bit up quickly plus improved the health of the entire family along the way. We don't spend much time with regrets about what we did, can't change that now, but we feel very responsible for coming up for a solution as best as we can.

# Thread: Hair falling out and dizziness

**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eliza — 2014-01-16T10:32:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-105885>

Hi all,

I've been on the leptin reset for about 2 weeks now and CT for about a week. I'm a 28 year old female. Since starting the reset I'm finding on occasion I'm quite dizzy despite putting salt on all my food. I've got bone broth cooking as we speak. My hair is falling out more than normal too and my skin is dry.

Nobody looks at me and says I'm overweight, but according to numerous BIA I have 38% fat mass. I feel very overweight.

Can someone tell me what might be causing the dizziness? And whether the leptin reset with CT will allow me to drop back to a much lower fat mass and reverse the insulin resistance?

My history: I'm being treated with metformin for mild insulin resistance. Other relevant labs I've had done so far (for the others I will try to get the Dr to agree to):
Vitamin D: 65nM/L (currently on supplementation)
Free T4: 14.4pmol/L
TSH: 1.8 mU/L
Free T3: 3.2 pmol/L
Testosterone: <0.5nmol/L
SHBG: 93 nmol/L
Free testosterone: <4
FAI: <0.5L
DHEAS: 4umol/L
ESR: 16mm/hr
Cholesterol: 6.9 mmol/L
HDL: 2.3mmol/L
LDL: 4.3 mmol/L
Trigs: 0.76mmol/L
Magnesium: 0.79mmol/L

Thank you so much for your help in advance. This is the first site I've seen in years that's offering real light at the end of the tunnel for so many people.

### caroline — 2014-01-16T12:05:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-105887>

Hi Eliza and welcome! Sorry - I can't help with labs but someone will come along as soon as they have time.

Ask your Doc to do a hsCRP ..... that indicates how much inflammation you have in your body.

Have you had a saliva ASI done? you need to know your cortisol levels and DHEA and Pg/E2 etc.

There is a free 90 Day optimal reset starting soon. Please sign up on the home page. There is a yellow banner right at the top of the page where you can register. Dr. K. is going to cover all the basics and he will have guests - Dr. Jackson is one and Ameer Rosic etc.

Dr. Kruse and Misty have done a webinar about getting labs. You should get a link when you register.

Also - Dr. Tim Jackson did a free webinar about hormones - you can track that down on the home page.

Do you have Dr.K's book The Epi Paleo RX .... it has a great section on labs and gives you a great overview of all of this and what Dr. Kruse is all about.

What is your non native EMF exposure? at home? at work? this is critical.
Are you able to get outside for grounding and sunlight? Are you drinking spring water?

Please start a journal in the journal section - Dr. K. likes journals - he needs context. Tell us where you live...in a city? and more of your health history. A journal is great for keeping track and interacting with the rest of us!

There is also a section for posting labs - they might not get seen here quite as readily.

Please keep reading and asking questions......

This is a great journey to Optimal health - so glad that you have found Dr. K.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-16T19:15:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-105923>

You really ought to get a full thyroid panel done which includes antibodies and rT3 as this can be linked to hair loss.

Also, B12 deficiency can cause hair loss. I remember watching a vid about a women who went bald because of it. Unfortunately I can't seem to get the video to load but it used to be here

"Janette first appeared in the BBC InsideOut documentary in October 2006. Since then the NHS has forced her doctor to withdraw B12 replacement therapy on a number of occasions, and she tells of her struggles with having B12 and then having it taken away.

She has bravely agreed to be filmed without her usual wig, but her memory is playing up because it is so long since her last injection.

Janette also appears in the BBC Documentary, filmed 4 years earlier. In the meantime, she's had her treatment stopped on two occasions by the local health board."

[https://www.b12d.org/pernicious-anemia-and-hair-loss](https://www.b12d.org/pernicious-anemia-and-hair-loss)

### cantweight — 2014-01-16T19:27:07+0200

### caroline — 2014-01-19T02:05:28+0200

Hi Eliza! I get a saliva ASI done thru ZRT labs in the U.S. - You can order them yourself.

Are you having 50gr of protein for your BAB? I didn't quite understand. You said you are eating 3 meals a day but never hungry? Some people think that 50gr is volume......

You can ground your laptop - there is a video on how to do it on the home page.

Some threads are moderated and if it is the weekend - they may not get approved until Monday.....prevents spam etc. Don't take it personally ..lol

If you ever have a problem tho .... go to the bottom of the page where it says "contact us"

My Doc is great - he is very interested in all of this but doesn't have time to do all the reading - so we have great discussions. I am very careful to respect his opinions and he does the same for me and we decide on a course of action between us.

I look forward to your journal and getting to know you

Don't forget the webinar on Monday!

### Jude — 2014-01-19T13:18:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-106192>

> [Eliza said:](/goto/post?id=105885)
>
> Hi all,
>
> I've been on the leptin reset for about 2 weeks now and CT for about a week. I'm a 28 year old female. Since starting the reset I'm finding on occasion I'm quite dizzy despite putting salt on all my food. I've got bone broth cooking as we speak. My hair is falling out more than normal too and my skin is dry.
>
> Nobody looks at me and says I'm overweight, but according to numerous BIA I have 38% fat mass. I feel very overweight.
>
> Can someone tell me what might be causing the dizziness? And whether the leptin reset with CT will allow me to drop back to a much lower fat mass and reverse the insulin resistance?
>
> My history: I'm being treated with metformin for mild insulin resistance. Other relevant labs I've had done so far (for the others I will try to get the Dr to agree to):
> Vitamin D: 65nM/L (currently on supplementation)
> Free T4: 14.4pmol/L
> TSH: 1.8 mU/L
> Free T3: 3.2 pmol/L
> Testosterone: <0.5nmol/L
> SHBG: 93 nmol/L
> Free testosterone: <4
> FAI: <0.5L
> DHEAS: 4umol/L
> ESR: 16mm/hr
> Cholesterol: 6.9 mmol/L
> HDL: 2.3mmol/L
> LDL: 4.3 mmol/L
> Trigs: 0.76mmol/L
> Magnesium: 0.79mmol/L
>
> Thank you so much for your help in advance. This is the first site I've seen in years that's offering real light at the end of the tunnel for so many people.

Hi Eliza, not sure what is causing your dissyness but in response to your second question about using the leptin reset/ct/etc to effect weight loss and basically resolve insulin resistance is that.... yes it will...and in a fashion completely belonging to you, which really means no one can predict how fast or slow that may take

Are you getting a Biochemistry and hormone tests as well?
Your Vit D is low at 65....my Aussie Dr likes it round 120.. you need Vit D3 and probably 10,000u for a couple of months and recheck.
DHEAS is low as well (you didn't include ref ranges)
Trigs ok
HDL is lower than LDL indicating a leaky gut..(which epi paleo covers)
Your T3 is a bit low.
Looks like ?pregnenolone steal syndrome...heaps on here about this..Hormones 101 to start.
Have fun!

### Eliza — 2014-01-20T09:00:39+0200

I am not concerned about my weight, but am really concerned about my fat mass. I don't think 38% is particularly normal. Most people can't believe it when I tell them but that doesn't change what it is!

I've started having more fat in my BAB and I think my hair loss is perhaps slowing a little? Anyway, the dizziness is gone, so I suspect that was probably a combination of dehydration plus not enough fat/calories.

### Eliza — 2014-01-20T09:15:38+0200

Hi Jude,

I didn't know that about the connection between cholesterol and leaky gut! There's so much more reading that I need to do. I do suffer from reflux on occasion (and my mum has really severe GERD).

### caroline — 2014-01-20T11:33:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-106272>

BPC = Bullet proof Coffee. You add a couple of TBSP of coconut oil and the same in GF butter to your coffee and blitz.
It makes it so much easier to get more fat in. You can do it to green tea too.
But this has to be in context - maybe you shouldn't be having coffee? if you have adrenal issues?

remember that gut issues and hormones are inter related. Everything is connected.

are you sure your fat mass index number is correct? If it is a scale you have at home - my understanding is they aren't all that accurate.

It doesn't sound right to me......

### caroline — 2014-01-20T11:45:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-106273>

There are a fair few Aussies here! We have pretty specific issues here because of the donut hole in the ozone. Dr. Kruse goes into a lot of detail for us in that thread I mentioned.

There is a lot of stuff to get thru here and some of it is pretty tough going unless you have a science degree.

As Dr. K. has told me .... you don't have to understand Chinese to enjoy Chinese food.

Just make sure you are doing epi paleo and C/T ... and limiting your non native EMF exposure and getting a lot of sunshine and grounding. It is the concepts that you want to get a good grasp of and your understanding will improve down the track.
I read something now that I read a year or two ago and go ... so that's what he meant! It happens to all of us. Remember that Dr. K. is 8-10 years ahead of us,

Have you listened to the free podcasts? they are a huge help.......and the webinars and follow up Q&A....

### healthman — 2014-01-16T12:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-105889>

Is a colonoscopy the only way to test for ulcer colitis & crohn's disease?

### sooperb — 2014-01-16T22:01:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-105945>

[http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/Crohns-disease/Pages/Diagnosis.aspx](http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/Crohns-disease/Pages/Diagnosis.aspx)

### Destiny — 2014-01-17T02:59:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-105977>

There is a test called fecal Calprotectin - Intestinal Inflammation Index. It might be a good first step before a colonoscopy.

[http://www.calprotectintest.com/](http://www.calprotectintest.com/)

### Eugenia — 2014-01-18T03:33:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-106084>

You don't have to be afraid of colonoscopy. As long as they put you under (not complete anesthesia, there's a middle path for it), you shouldn't feel a thing. In some countries they don't offer that though..

# Thread: Dairy - beta casein - A1 vs A2 milk.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Da-mo — 2014-01-16T23:38:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105966>

Having read some of the Leptin and Epi-paleo blogs, I see JK seeming to advocated limiting or eliminating dairy unless you can get it from France or New Zealand - I presume that has to do with being pasture fed. I've also seen him mention the beta-casein issue which I interpret as being about the A1 and A2 milk differences.

Here in New Zealand, yes our dairy and indeed most food animals are pasture fed. However, most of the dairy herds produce A1 milk.

Luckily, we can get A2 milk here and our family switched over to it several years ago. Initially it was very expensive compared with non-A2 milk but has now come down to the price of the A1. I read somewhere that the dairy industry here is switching over to A2 herds but that will likely take a decade or so.

As for the effects on my family. . . . the most noticeable was that prior to switching to A2 milk, both my daughters would get debilitating hayfever in the summer to the point they could hardly function without antihistamines up to the eyeballs. Since making the switch, their hayfever is no more noticeable than anyone else that may get dust or pollen in their eyes. Both also were on asthma medication (Ventolin) but since switching to A2 neither has needed any at all.

Unfortunately the other dairy derived products here (cheese, yoghurt etc.) do not distinguish between A1 and A2 feedstocks so are probably still A1 derived.

Thoughts/Comments/Opinion/Discussion?

### margor_j — 2014-01-16T23:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105967>

thats interesting..
i recently listened to a podcast by sean croxton (underground wellness) where he was interviewing a dairy farmer in NZ, who said most of our milk here is A2, so i kept taking the raw milk (iv since stopped)
im using a 'red 8' whey protein - trying it.. now im wondering if im using the wrong one, confusion starts all over again.. but to get 50gm of protein at b'fast AND cut back on eggs leaves less choice (where i am right now, its not so easy to get up and cook up 3 steaks etc..)

so youre saying that alot of our milk is A1 now? hmm this guy being interviewed (sorry, dont know name) was saying we are one of the lucky countries, because we have mainly A2...

MG

### Da-mo — 2014-01-17T00:15:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105968>

Last I read, Fonterra was switching the herds over to A2, while simultaneouly refuting any claims that A1 was harmful . . . hahaha!!

THe A2 is now the same price as most A1 so Im sticking with the A2 labelled stuff.

Still reading whats in the forum about it and saw something about no dairy until FBG <85?, need to research that a little more and start thinking about getting some labs done.

Was interesting reading some of your introduction - had a freind who went to Antartica with the Air Force. He and a freind from the Army got in trouble for talking a pool toy, an inflatable seal, out onto the ice and baiting the greenies by beating it with broomsticks at a distance where it looked real.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-17T02:40:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105972>

Keith Woodford. Devil in the milk. Also I spoke in some blog comments in the BG series with a Bob Smith about BCM-7 and the casein link to DPPIV in the intestine. I am sure someone has a link to the discussion. It was deep.

### margor_j — 2014-01-17T03:59:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105982>

oh thats funny re antarctica.. that wouldnt have gone down well! yeah, its a great place, but i wonder now about the deeper effects. funny, EVERY summer ppl just balloon and i always assumed it was the carbs and the beer, maybe its more than that. i know 90% of folk there find 'some' issue with sleep.
so look at JKs comment.. sounds like hes not a fan.. :-/ (feck, i have whey protein! i thought it was the right stuff to take.) thats in the brain gut series - we got some homework to do bro! haha

### Sean Waters — 2017-08-02T17:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-dr-kruse-use-crystals-in-surgery.9367/post-220724>

I was always sceptical of healing crystals, but since putting the pieces together with Iron and Magnetism, it started to make sense that some of them could deflect or absorb EMF.

I carry Lodeston and Hematite tumble stones, about 10 altogether, in my pocket at all times and I put 2 stones at each corner of my bed before sleep, that is apparently creating an EMF protective tent over the bed.

I was looking to buy some more Lodestone, or Pyrite or Hematite - as I read this article: [http://hibiscusmooncrystalacademy.com/how-to-lower-your-emf-risks-with-crystal-healing/](http://hibiscusmooncrystalacademy.com/how-to-lower-your-emf-risks-with-crystal-healing/)

She explains how some crystals are used for electronics, silicates, and these actually resonate with these frequencies. So quartz and the like are a No-no. But the iron containing stones: Pyrite, Hematite and Lodestone would be beneficial due to their Ferromagnetic properties.

I decided to check the forum and now I need to look into Amethyst Geodes... Either way, for anyone reading - the Iron stones work quite well (my n=1).

### Sean Waters — 2017-08-02T18:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-dr-kruse-use-crystals-in-surgery.9367/post-220729>

Yeah I did some more research and I need to Caveat a few parts of the above:
1\. Hematite are often sold as bracelets or in tumblestones (black and shiny), they are always "magnetic", but according to this site: [http://geology.com/minerals/hematite.shtml](http://geology.com/minerals/hematite.shtml), they aren't supposed to be. So they are apparently fakes.
2\. Hematite, pure specimens of the rock are used to provide protection against radiation due to the high iron content.. coincidentally, they can't be found on amazon (UK), it's all the shiny black magnetic bracelets - which I'm not sure about now. However, Ebay (UK) has raw specimens that are about £10 each.
3\. Pyrite is much cheaper than Hematite or Lodestone/Magnetite, but consequently it is only 46% Iron, whereas Hematite is 70% and Lodestone/Magnetite 72.4%. If the Iron (transition metal) is the key player in absorbing the EMF, then we want the latter two rather than Pyrite. It really is Fool's Gold.

Take away (from my research so far):
\- Buy Lodestone tumble stones for your pockets
\-Buy Magnetite/Lodestone and Hematite Raw Rock Specimens for your home

# Thread: Calories - to count or not?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-01-17T12:57:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106002>

So im doing LR and need a bit of clarity!
iv seen things throughout my daily trawl through the site for info that have confused me -
JK says 1) calories dont matter for LR - timing is more important.
2) BAB includes both x amount of protein AND fat (saturated, predominantly)
3) Are we for or against dairy? im in NZ and believe we have A2 milk here. im currently trying no milk (was using raw milk) but the protein powder i have is whey - not much junk in it, NZ made.. havent noticed any nasty symptoms.. and im also trying to cut down the eggs i eat, just incase - i dont want to get a bad reaction to them. i try to force a tin of fish down somedays (hello! catfood!) ugh.. its a struggle, but also, its not so easy where i am now to just get up and use the kitchen, a shake is really easy for me WITH coconut oil
4) can i take some coconut oil before / after / during each meal? im thinking yes? (im trying no butter, i have ghee if i want it..)
5) i tried dave aspreys BP diet - the fatty coffees, admittedly, it wasnt his coffee per se, but during those weeks, i swelled right up. could have been the dessicated thyroid i was on (not taking anymore).. but now im too scared to ever do that diet again.

6) is anyone taking less than 50gm of protein for BAB and 'getting away with it'? thats not the point, right?!
cheers guys

### sooperb — 2014-01-17T14:35:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106004>

I sympathise with your confusion. 50gs of protein seems huge to me as well as having to try and take on board some saturated fat alongside. For breakfast today I had one sausage, two eggs, 2 rashers of bacon, one tomato and some mushrooms followed by some prawns and some tea. I really don't want any more than that. I have just had the BPC with a tbs of raw butter and a tbs of coconut oil. I won't feel hungry again today but I will eat a protein rich meal later with greens of some sort or other veg. but no carbs. I have followed this for nearly 2 weeks with absolutely no sugar and have lost .................... nothing. I've managed the face dunking several times followed by a progressively cooler to cold shower. I even managed to sit with the icepacks on my abdomen. None of this is working for me  I look six months pregnant most of the time.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-01-17T16:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106014>

1) The community seems to be split in both the ones that think that a calorie is not a calorie and that calories don't matter if they come from the correct choices, and the ones that believe that in the end it always prove to be that a negative calorie balance is needed to lose weight, period. Well, I myself changed my point of view in this subject many times. As for now, what I've found is that:

... you can loose weight no matter the macro compositions if you stay pretty under caloric and are diligent over time
... yet this does not mean that a diet such like that will be healthy
... you can lose weight more easily if you adopt a more LCHF style like the epi-paleo template, wich optimizes your hormones and neurotransmitters so that this will allow it to happen
... but you still have to have a look at your total food intake because, no matter how clean, low carb you eat, if you overeat, that will somehow turn out as weight gain unless you have really really optimal metabolic status (I bet JK would say great leptin sensititvity that pairs with proper working internal circadian clock tunning with perfect PPP working that leads to UC1 and UC3 uncoupling proteins to do its work of getting rid of energy surplus as heat when you don't need it) but if that was your case, you wouldn't be here, would you?

But the great thing about this is that in order to acomplish the goal of the LR you don't need to care right now about calories because you're not pursuing immediate weight loss but to trigger the right signals via your gut-brain axis that regulate your hunger so you can skip the junk food you'd be eating at night if doing a more low fat grain based diet which kills the reward status on your brain... so in the end you'll fall to eat the calories you need, and being mostly from protein and fat, with few carbs that could maximize your ability to store fat, you'll eventually regain leptin/insulin sensitivity as you get rid of body fat (and thus inflammation that halts your ferrari to work properly).

So yes calories might still have a role but you don't need to care right now because you're working on how to regain the ability to naturally sense your optimal need for them.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-01-17T16:51:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106017>

3) The LR are general guidelines that will work for most people. In an initial scenario, if you got to need the LR it implies that you might be having some gut issues (because the gut and the liver are first defense lines before inflammation gets to the brain via a leaky blood brain barrier and help develop the LR first and the IR second) so in this case, chances are that the proteins in dairy could make it to the blood via the leaky gut that allows food to cross the gut ligning, increasing the change of your immune system getting triggered to sense these counfounders and to release cytokines that will lead you to an inflammatory state that lead to more LR, IR, obesity and neolithic diseases. So, assuming that you fall in this category, it seems a good bet to avoid dairy until you have your gut issues fixed. Dairy is not indispensable by any means, so it makes sense to err on the side of caution and to suggest avoiding it. If you feel well having dairy, I guess there will be no problem at all. In fact, the dairy fat has great components for helping you getting the gut fixed and your brain working (like the K2 vitamin and butyrate for ketone generation), that's why pastured hard cheese and butter are recommended and not milk.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-01-17T16:56:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106018>

4) I bet JK would say that lots of coconut oil is what you need and yes, in whataver meal you want. I'm personally a little bit skeptical about this but but won't say a thing against. Coconut oil is expensive and over time I tend to eat less and less... feel no difference at all, but your milleage may vary. Turmeric, garlic, onions and ginger are also very good anti-bacterial, anti-inflammatory foods that can work well for similar purposes and are more affordable. I tend to mix all of this so I can minimize a little bit both my money expenditure and my fat intake if I were relying too much on coconut oil or butter/ghee (not agaist fat but since I allow for quality carbs I feel safer to down-regulate fat abuse so I do not end eating just too much beyond my needs).

### nonchalant — 2014-01-17T17:02:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106019>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=106004)
>
> I have followed this for nearly 2 weeks with absolutely no sugar and have lost .................... nothing. I've managed the face dunking several times followed by a progressively cooler to cold shower. I even managed to sit with the icepacks on my abdomen. None of this is working for me  I look six months pregnant most of the time.

Sooperb, sounds like you are doing well. One thing I have learned here is that what we are doing is making ourselves more healthy. I came here wanting to lose weight, and I found that my body had other plans for all the new energy it was getting. Instead of losing all of the fat, I lost the scoliosis in my back, and the plantar faciitis in my feet. My brain started working like it used to in my teens and my eyesight sharpened. I stopped getting sick. I obtained the energy to do daily tasks with ease.

Your body has a laundry list of things it wants to get done. Life threatening things it wants to fix. Like repairing damage from artificial EMF and lengthening telomeres. Unfortunately (or fortunately for that matter) losing fat (your body's insurance policy) is probably low on the list. From what I've seen, men in general are able to lose weight easier, probably because less is "wrong" with their bodies. Their hormones are less complex, and they haven't been walking around for decades with less myelination on their neurons, soaking up all the deranged environmental signals.

I hope you continue to stay the course. I think you will be surprised at all the health benefits you will see. Things you didn't know could even be fixed.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-01-17T17:03:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106020>

Like in 3), the 50 gr of protein is a general rule that will work for the most part of people. The real idea is that your vagus nerve can send the proper signal to the hipothalamus so your hunger is killed for the most part of the day that can be, the more, the better. It's all about helping you killing the cravings that will lead you to unhealthy food choices (mainly high palatable salty or sweet carbs with unhealthy fats). Protein is good at doing this, and depending on how mesed one is more or less of it will be needed. Your milleage might vary so your best bet I think is to do trial-error and see on how much protein you can get to go until 6-7pm without having an urge to eat. Once you find your sweet spot you then follow it over time until you reach your goal... then see if you can change it for a late protein meal at the evening and a mixed breakfast as per the post-leptin RX recommendations and stay on the track.

>
> So im doing LR and need a bit of clarity!
> iv seen things throughout my daily trawl through the site for info that have confused me -
> JK says 1) calories dont matter for LR - timing is more important.
> 2) BAB includes both x amount of protein AND fat (saturated, predominantly)
> 3) Are we for or against dairy? im in NZ and believe we have A2 milk here. im currently trying no milk (was using raw milk) but the protein powder i have is whey - not much junk in it, NZ made.. havent noticed any nasty symptoms.. and im also trying to cut down the eggs i eat, just incase - i dont want to get a bad reaction to them. i try to force a tin of fish down somedays (hello! catfood!) ugh.. its a struggle, but also, its not so easy where i am now to just get up and use the kitchen, a shake is really easy for me WITH coconut oil
> 4) can i take some coconut oil before / after / during each meal? im thinking yes? (im trying no butter, i have ghee if i want it..)
> 5) i tried dave aspreys BP diet - the fatty coffees, admittedly, it wasnt his coffee per se, but during those weeks, i swelled right up. could have been the dessicated thyroid i was on (not taking anymore).. but now im too scared to ever do that diet again.
>
> 6) is anyone taking less than 50gm of protein for BAB and 'getting away with it'? thats not the point, right?!
> cheers guys

What Albert said. Keep in mind this is a protocol. Do it as instructed and it will most likely work. Literally put your scale in the closet or the trash. At this point it is a meaningless distraction. If you follow the protocols your body is going to change for like two years and you will feel better and look better as time passes. Muscle will replace fat so your weight is not a reliable indicator of progress. Enjoy the path.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-17T17:33:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106024>

> [Dali Dula said:](/goto/post?id=106022)
>
> Muscle will replace fat so your weight is not a reliable indicator of progress. Enjoy the path.

Yes, it is hard to believe, but exercise isn't the only way of putting on muscle.

### Inger — 2014-01-17T18:03:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106029>

I never count calories. But I just put what I had today into a calorimeter to just check what comes out (hope I did not forgot something...) I am 166 cm, and... maybe 55 kg? No idea since I do not weight lol but at totally normal weight with quite some muscles I think (almost no exercise, I just started a few weeks ago with 1 or 2 times / week, 30- 45 minutes)

2850 kcal

211 g fat
173 g protein
9 g carbohydrates

I eat quite a bit! I probably should gain on so many calories but I do not really... and I have been eating like this for years already

I always eat a BAB. I feel I still need it, I had bulemia for many years so that might be why. I had more than 400 grams of raw grassfed ground beef + coconut oil for BAB today.

Be careful with coffee. Might stop you from losing weight. Jack says, if you want to lose weight coffee is not a good idea. Make your own N=1  If your adrenals are not healthy coffee might too be a bad idea. I drink very little coffee... and when I do, I have it at lunchtime not at breakfast and then I sip the small cup for quite a bit of time, not all in once. That way I feel I tolerate it better! And only very good quality of course.

HereÂ´s about what I had

450 g raw grassfed beef
some couple of tbsp raw virgin coconut oil
a few tbsp raw EVOO
maybe 1 or 2 tbsp natural balsamic vinegar from grapes only
some pieces of raw grassfed beef fat
250 g cooked mussels
1 tbsp organic 100% cocoapowder
1 raw duck egg
1 small cup organic coffee, black
150 g raw sheep milk cheese, Roquefort
1 big tbsp french butter
lots of seaweed water

I think that was it? I ate all this in a 6 hour window, I like to eat when it is light outside so that is how long the sun is up, about, here now.

I usually have no dairy, but once in a while, maybe a few times / month, I buy me a piece of Roquefort and eat it with or without some butter... a treat for me, so yummy!

### Inger — 2014-01-17T18:32:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106031>

IDK how I would do if I ate sausages and regular (not pasture or own eggs) eggs... I eat very clean, mostly plain meat and fish, fresh. I do not eat bacon ever, the stuff I can get here is full of crap. If it was pastured and no additives I would sure eat it but it is not.....
I make great effort to search for clean food sources, it is a never ending project lol but I like it, it is like hunting.. And I get so happy when I find something great to add to my shopping list!

Some people lose best on just clean beef, fat and seafood and water. No veggies, no coffee, no dairy. I would do that if I had an weight issue. Not count calories

### nicld — 2014-01-17T18:46:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106033>

My N=1.

If your hormones are off, not matter how little or how much you eat your weight will not go down. I have been eating with hunger for 9 months now is it seems to be working. Besides making sure I get the BAB breakfast right away, I do not eat until I am hungry again (usually 7-8 hours later) and then stop when satisfied, not still a bit hungry and not stuffed. It does take some practice to really identify true hunger and when truly full. I use to control my food intake obsessively and it got me now where. I eat breakfast around 6:30 am then a smaller 2nd meal between 1-3 pm and then if I am hungry when I get home a small meal with the hubby or sometimes just coconut crack (coconut oil, coconot, dark coco powder and a little stevia) for supper.

I say try to get in at least 50g (not weight) of protein and fats right away in the morning but if you still feel a bit hungry eat more.

Nicole

### margor_j — 2014-01-17T19:42:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106037>

thanks for all the input, guys!
hmm see now i wonder about my gut and the whey protein. but its such an easy option for me... ( i was also bulimic, for years)
however, i moved house / city this week, and now im having lunch again! tho- this is a cup of bone broth with seaweed and some butter / coconut oil and it keeps me going until dinner. im not 'starving' when i have it, and part of that is to fit in with my mum an dad (altho iv explained to them what im doing, well, started to!) but i think this is also with the whey protein, cos if i eat 50gms of fish / eggs / bacon / meat with fat, then i can easily go til dinner time.. so
i guess i need to play around with the ratios / and / try a fleshier breakfast most days..

albert- you really helped me understand a lot! thanks! thanks again to you all for your help.

### caroline — 2014-01-17T22:58:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106058>

Everyone has given you such great advice Mags ...... remember we are all searching for our own n=1. It is different for all of us.

I totally agree with Inger - I need to eat very clean and very simply and for me raw is best. I also do best if I stretch out dinner over a couple of hours ... usually between 3:30 and 5:00pm. I just munch away! Sometimes it is breakfast that I stretch out. It all depends on hunger and what I am doing. It is all about our own personal context.

Right now you are trying to fix yourself ......Do as Jack's protocols suggest to fix your circadian cycle, your gut and your hormones ...they are all interactive. Just fixing one doesn't really work.

This definitely isn't a quick fix .... even tho most of us came here for exactly that! I wouldn't trade this journey for anything. I have learned so much and will keep learning .... and I have wound up in the most amazing place! and I can't even imagine where I will be a year from now....

This really is about the journey......

I think you need to turn off your thinking machine for a bit and follow the protocols exactly ......in my opinion of course!
I don't ever mean to put words in Jack's mouth....

### margor_j — 2014-01-17T23:17:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106060>

thanks caroline,
i think you make a lot of sense! its remarkable how caught up in 'results' i get and then i completely unravel myself (like last nite!) to the point i cant switch off and i dream about asking questions on the forum! lol. i have an amazing beach ten mins walk away, and im LOVING going and spending time on it, then i lie on the massive, damp grass of the field nearby - also, i can look at the sun as its rising and then do 'lines' (lol) of oxytocin - i have it in a nasal spray.
and iv taken to sitting on the lawn outside, to read, in the evenings. admittedly, im using an ipad for my books BUT, its on airplane mode and i have the light turned right down, plus i use blu blockers.. its gotta all help, right?

il look at having less whey to. maybe il buy pea protein next. tastes like arse, but its vital for me to have on hand..
theres a big yoga group that do yoga on the local beach! there was about 40 ppl there today, im gonna give that a whirl to! gotta remember the relaxation stuff, right!

Mags

### caroline — 2014-01-17T23:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calories-to-count-or-not.9368/post-106063>

Mags - now you are getting it .... good girl! Your beach sounds awesome .... and you do need oxy twice a day - either a partner or a BOB ... to activate the great pharmacy in your brain - JK calls it a freebie! lol

I think you are still pretty young? have you thought about changing careers? something you love? you could maybe teach yoga or something on the beach? you will be able to C/T all winter too!

Also remember - you need some testing/labs to see what you need to fix - use that as your roadmap ...then work the protocols and then retest and see where you are and what you need to do next.

Did you see the video that Dr. K. and Misty just did?

It is so great to be learning all of this - thanks JK ....and then work on our own personal masterpiece ... our one life to live ....and I want to get it more right than wrong.

So that is my personal choice - I am going to only get out of this what I am willing to put in....that alone sets me on fire!

Was I always like this? maybe a little .....but Jack gave me confidence to know that I am entitled to a healthy life but need to do the work - not depend on my Doc .... and I have always been lucky enough to have good docs.

### caroline — 2014-01-17T23:57:54+0200

THis is due to Ijen being the largest naturally formed lake of Sulfuric acid (approx ph 3) in the world....this rests in the crater......further up the crater they mine loads of sulfur which is spewing out of the crater regularly. I have been thre a few times collected the sulfur and also the water. The blue colour is due to this excessive amounts of sulfur.

They would have taken these photos at night or in a very low light environment....as this is what sulfur does

# Thread: Any advice on giving advice?

### cantweight — 2014-01-17T16:37:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106013>

My Uncle was diagnosed with Shy Drager syndrome....a neurodegenerative disease similar to parkinsons. He had a triple bypass many years ago and it looks like lowfat living coupled with some illness (Lyme) has taken a toll. I can't sit by and watch him wither away without saying something. He was one of the first people I remember that professed the importance of a positive outlook on life and I care for him very much. Any suggestions on approaching this subject to someone that will likely balk at the idea of fat in the diet?

### nicld — 2014-01-17T18:48:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106034>

Buy him Dr. Kruse's book and let him start learning. Only he can make the change.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-01-17T20:20:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106039>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=106034)
>
> Buy him Dr. Kruse's book and let him start learning. Only he can make the change.

Excellent advice .... and remember people will not change until they are READY. And for most people, they are so stuck in their own stuff, they never become ready.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-17T20:21:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106040>

what Nicole and ATL paleo said...

### cantweight — 2014-01-17T20:35:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106043>

Ah! Great idea! So simple. I pray he is not too far gone to read it. Oh thanks so much. It gives me some relief from that horrible feeling of doing nothing. Thanks guys

### nicld — 2014-01-17T21:18:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106046>

Good luck, I keep telling my hubby that when he eats carbs and sugar he is killing himself but he still does it  He is not willing to change yet either.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-17T21:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106048>

Show them the Volek video I just posted on here last night. BOOM

### caroline — 2014-01-18T00:31:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106070>

I was having lunch the other day with a couple of very dear friends - and I was particularly struck, even more than usual, at how they are so mired in Dogma - they are up to their eyeballs and about to drown for goodness sake!

Alternate opinions aren't even welcome ... and in my case - it is very visible what all this has done for me .....

Don't you just want to scream "wake up and smell the coffee"

I have found lately it is way better to say nothing and wait for them to ask questions....

My oldest daughter needs to know all of this and act - desperately - but wouldn't dream of it and she has two beautiful little boys that she needs to be around for

It is like we are Charlatans and trying to feed people a load of bull - even tho results are clearly evident.

### sooperb — 2014-01-18T12:39:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-advice-on-giving-advice.9370/post-106106>

Maybe you can have him to stay for a while and take over his food supply? Even a week of introducing him to fresh, wholesome food might serve to improve how he feels and make him think a little. In his situation he has nothing to lose even if he chewed through a mountain of fatty food topped off with coconut oil etc.  I think the best demonstration is the taste test, take his favourite food and cook it for him paleo style. Add Dr. Stephen Sinatra's book "Reverse Heart Disease Now" to his reading list, it's a mine of information

# Thread: Finasteride (Proscar) Hormonal Damage . How Can I Recover?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finasteride-proscar-hormonal-damage-how-can-i-recover.9372/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KJ55 — 2014-01-17T17:41:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finasteride-proscar-hormonal-damage-how-can-i-recover.9372/post-106028>

I have been taking finasteride (5mg/day) for the last 2 years to slow down my male pattern baldness. The drug has worked great for my receding hairline, but I have some negative symptoms that I am very worried about. I was aware that finasteride lowered DHT (Dihydrotestosterone), but I was unaware of the effects of the hormonal imbalances. My two side effects are decrease in semen production, and severe brain fog. I stopped taking the drug 2 weeks ago.

Iâ€™m really at a loss on how to help my body through this. I would really appreciate your opinion on what I can do to regulate my hormones and return to my normal self.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-17T21:57:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finasteride-proscar-hormonal-damage-how-can-i-recover.9372/post-106052>

you should read my Hair loss blog and you will need to hire a really good anti aging doc to assess how bad your HPA axis has become from the drugs.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finasteride-proscar-hormonal-damage-how-can-i-recover.9372/post-109883>

### persistence — 2014-03-10T02:09:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finasteride-proscar-hormonal-damage-how-can-i-recover.9372/post-114427>

> [KJ55 said:](/goto/post?id=106028)
>
> I have been taking finasteride (5mg/day) for the last 2 years to slow down my male pattern baldness. The drug has worked great for my receding hairline, but I have some negative symptoms that I am very worried about. I was aware that finasteride lowered DHT (Dihydrotestosterone), but I was unaware of the effects of the hormonal imbalances. My two side effects are decrease in semen production, and severe brain fog. I stopped taking the drug 2 weeks ago.
>
> Iâ€™m really at a loss on how to help my body through this. I would really appreciate your opinion on what I can do to regulate my hormones and return to my normal self.

KJ55, why would you take 5mg Finasteride? The studies were against 1/5th that dose, 1mg. Brand name for the 1mg dose is Propecia? Here is what I have found: I can take one Finasteride and divide into EIGHT parts (so effective dose around 0.625 gm per day, and this gives me the maximum benefit. That's for me, you can test. I see no reason to provoke hormonal imbalances.

No doubt even 0.625 of this drug is pretty horrible. I dislike doing this to my body. But this is one of the only treatments for type 2 DHT in the hair follicle.

I assume you are taking Rogaine twice a day as well? That works well.

I will share another secret: topical urea 35% or 40% improves hair quality and density for me dramatically. It stopped all of the scalp itching and hair loss I was getting from male pattern baldness. Parts of my head with thick hair intact is starting to look better than it did in my 20s. Topical urea is not prescribed currently for male pattern baldness. I used it for some skin lesions and discovered the scalp benefit on my own. My theory on this is that the urea is forcing rapid turnover of the scalp, thus freeing up the sebum loaded with type 1 DHT, and allowing the trapped follicles to grow. There is no published science to back up that theory. This is my own personal speculation. Note that Finasteride does NOT treat type 1 DHT. There is no treatment that I know of for type 1 DHT in the sebum at top of scalp.

# Thread: Sighâ€¦â€¦.. I think I need a new hormone doctor
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-01-17T18:36:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/post-106032>

I went and saw him on Wednesday and he was a little bit helpful. He read my labs and noticed that I was low on magnesium which concerns me since I am on 3 forms of magnesium and the more expensive ones. I need to figure out how low magnesium relates to other hormones being off now.

My Albumin level (gm/dL) (main protein of plasma) is lower than the limits and not sure why. He asked if a ate protein and besides fat, that is the main food in my diet so it is not related to eating. Another one to figure out.

He said that it looks like I have some microplasma biofilms in my body since my M.pneumoniae Ab IgG and IgM came back positive. He suggested a supplement to try but I cannot remember but I need to dig deeper into what this is too. I need to find out why I have this which for me, means GI stool testing. I will be seeing which doctor to have it ordered from depending on if one ordering clinic gets a better discount than the other. I also showed Parvo antibodies (no current infection) and he is not sure how to deal with this either

And then there was the whole pregnenolone steal issue. I am 99% sure that I have this since my pregnenolone levels are in the basement, thyroid was low, dhea was low and Pg was low before supplementing. He had never really heard of this and when I described it, he was not really sure about it. He had nothing to offer me and did not think it was the adrenals even though I had told him about my crash many years ago and my years of compulsive exercise (which also stressed the adrenals). He did want to see me start exercising and see how I handle it. **_I am hesitant right now in this because if it is my adrenals that are wonky, wouldn't exercise make them work harder and be a less than ideal thing?_**

Basically, I think I have learned more than my doctor and he cannot longer help me if he does not really understand it and I get the feeling he really did not want to learn about it. He is not a bad doctor just not what I need right now.

Right now, I do not think that my GP would be able to help either since he did take a look at my tests and did not see the big picture of what was going on but he probably does not have the time to see the big picture since he is part of big medicine.

I have to do my melatonin test again this weekend and am still waiting for my 23 and me results. I will try getting the GI testing next week and then I think I should have enough information to see what the bleep is going on.

So, I will be patiently waiting for Dr. Tim to announce the plans for this year’s consults.

Oh and if anyone knows of a Dr Kruse protocol doctor in the Minneapolis MN area, let me know.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-01-17T20:18:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/post-106038>

IIRC, magnesium is very difficult to measure through standard bloodwork.

The adrenals and thyroid are closedly linked. Thus, your intution about not exercising seems correct.

Low thyroid is often linked to adrenal issues. I would focus on the thyroid issue since other hormonal issues are difficult to resolve with thyroid and/or adrenal issues.

8 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

# Thread: Free Fatty Acids and Mitochondrial Respiration
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-fatty-acids-and-mitochondrial-respiration.9375/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-17T20:35:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-fatty-acids-and-mitochondrial-respiration.9375/post-106044>

Dr. Kruse has convinced me that mitochondria really are the keys to health and longevity and for that reason, I've delved full force into studying the bioenergetics of mitochondria. I've been trying to find how mitochondrial bioenergetics react to different substrates like free fatty acids and glucose. Considering Dr. Kruse's epi-paleo, ketotic diet would result in a substantial rise in blood free fatty acids, I thought I'd post some of my thoughts/questions here. I hope I can get a Kruse-ian interpretation of these studies I've been looking in to.

First, this study [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6279918](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6279918) seems to suggest the opposite of what we would desire from free fatty acid metabolism. Mitochondrial repsiration appered to decrease in response to increase free fatty acid content in the blood. Nicotonic acid was used to reverse these effects and reactivated proper mitochondrial respiration by supressing the rise of free fatty acids in the blood. The author states that "These results suggest that high plasma FFA itself, whether it may be exogenous or endogenous, may impair the oxidative phosphorylation of the mitochondria isolated from normal and ischemic hearts." This worries me as I want fully functioning, high mitochondrial respiration.

Another study I found analyzes how free fatty acid beta-oxidation affects heart function, [http://cardiovascres.oxfordjournals.org/content/59/1/143.abstract](http://cardiovascres.oxfordjournals.org/content/59/1/143.abstract) Essentially, it shows that inhibiting free fatty aicd utilization by heart cells increased glucose oxidation, decreased lactate production (good, bad? I'm not fully sure here.), decreased glycogen depletion and resulted in stronger and more efficient regional contractile power in the heart. This suggests that fatty acid oxidation is not the substrate that provides the optimal bioenergetic state for the heart and glucose seems to make hearts stronger/more efficient. Do you guys have any thoughts in this regard? Similarly, this study [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3071466/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3071466/) concluded that a high fat diet causes greater oxygen consumption in heart cells, seemingly due to the increase of uncoupling proteins in the heart's mitochondria reducing the efficiency of ATP generation (uncoupling caused by the fatty acids makes the mitochondria burn more fuel to produce the same amount of energy). I'm not sure of the implications here so your thoughts would be greatly appreciated.

Last but not least, this study [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6867023](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/6867023) claims that "oxidation of fat is one of the fundamental responses to stress" and free fatty acids are released in response to stress. FFAs seem to supress full glucose oxidation and abort the process at lactate instead of full oxidation to carbon dioxide. Does this mean FFAs are necessarily stressful? Not necessrily, but compounds released in synchrony with FFAs during stress, like cortisol and adrenaline, are things we do not want to have in high concentrations during our non-stressed state.

I'm not trying to make any claims here, I'm just genuinely trying to understand the maze of bioenergetics that surround mitochondria function. I really do hope somebody can explain these findings!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-17T21:53:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-fatty-acids-and-mitochondrial-respiration.9375/post-106051>

You need to review the Volek video I posted and read up on how complex 1 can be affected by drugs that block it. Complex I dysfunction leads to intracellular lipid accumulation in the tissues whose mitochondria are damaged. .For example......metformin, rotenone, bisphenyl A,isoniazid, flunarizine, and atrazine are all complex 1 blockers but only metformin works on weight loss because it affects the DIRECTION of the current of flow electrons in and out of ETC. Metformin is unusual because it does not actively block ROS production on a chronic basis. It can under a shorter time frame but this drug is never given acutely. It is given long term to T2D who have a trashed complex one. So in T2D with chronic reverse flow of electrons metformin signals itself from the pack of complex one blockers. In all the links you post not one looks that details of QED effects. Have a look here at one that does.
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16732470](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16732470)

The story is always in the details......and when you begin to realize quantum actions tie to specifics of atomic structure you begin to see why the literature is useless in many cases. They never control for these small changes that appear to make huge differences in how mitochondria are designed to work.

### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-18T06:13:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-fatty-acids-and-mitochondrial-respiration.9375/post-106093>

Wait, wait, wait, I'm a bit confused now. You seem to suggest that reverse electron flow is bad and that metformin inhibits this, thereby being beneficial. But according to Peter over at hyperlipid, we need superoxide generated by reverse electron flow in order to generate physiological insulin resistance and spare glucose for our brains. In fact, high fat diets are extremely good at generating reverse electron flow and physiological insulin resistance. If metformin inhibits this reverse electron flow, wouldn't it increase insulin sensitivity? Isn't this something we want to avoid on a high fat diet? What causes this disparity between your position and Peter's?

### QiGuy1997 — 2014-01-18T06:30:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-fatty-acids-and-mitochondrial-respiration.9375/post-106096>

I have a feeling I'm totally misunderstanding this reverse electron flow in the mitochondria. Oh well, I have a no school on Monday so this will be a perfect weekend to read through your blog posts on the topic and Peter's at hyperlipid. Any particular posts you recommend? Also, any books at go into the deep biochemistry of mitochondria that you suggest? I started Power, Sex, Suicide tonight but I don't think it goes quite this deep into everything.

# Thread: total knee surgery prep
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/total-knee-surgery-prep.9376/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### motivatedhealthseeker — 2014-01-17T20:58:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/total-knee-surgery-prep.9376/post-106045>

Hello
My mom that is in her early 70s is having total knee surgery in a week. Is there any steps she can take now to prepare for surgery? I read Dr kruse's pre ope blog section. I know he discussed more about the surgeries associated with osteoporosis. So does the packing the knee for 45 mins with ice still a good plan for the total knee surgery? She stays cold most of the time and bundled up so I dont know if she could tolerate this but she is willing to try I know. Low thyroid plus the ratio of omega 3 to 6 is probably off as well.

Also I understand vit c aids with healing. Any contraindications with taking vit c before surgery for normal orthopods ..I know Dr K is an exception? I hope it doesnt thin the blood and cause bleeding issues. He also recommends the reduced version of coQ10 300 mg but the nurse stated to get off the omacor (prescription fish oil), coq10, and krill oil 1 wk before surgery.

I m a bit concerned about her iron levels. When she tries to supplement iron it mostly goes towards ferritin which signals inflamm. Shes been taking 2000 mg of krill oil and the ferritin remains high.

Also I remember Dr K discussing the use of progesterone to bring people out of anesthesia faster. She doesnt use progesterone normally but should she use transdermal progesterone for a few days post surgery?
Also which anesthesia is best please..general or spinal? Spinal would be good except she had l4/l5 issues about 10 yrs ago and hasnt had an mri since then. I know it was mentioned that it can be given at an area above or below but i think she had a bulge at l4/l5 with minor involvements at l3/l4 as well? Any advise as to how a spinal can be given without causing any further discal injury?

Here are her latest lab results. Your input is greatly appreciated.

Free t3 3.4 (2.0-4.4) Shes had chronic constipation issues for atleast 10 yrs that I can remember esp after chemo was done. She takes magnesium citrate powder at night to offset this. She is very dependent on this.
Free t4 .82 (.82-1.77)
TSH 1.16 (.45-4.5) She was on 25 mcg of t3 when blood drawn..has increased dose by 6.25 mcg
since then.

Serum iron 73 (35-155) she unfortunately didnt have time to fast off the vit C before testing. she was on NO iron though
TIBC 298 (250-450)
UIBC 225 (150-375)
iron sat 24 (15-55)
ferritin 183 (15-150)

HA1c 6.4 (this is increased from last time at 5.8 because when overseas diet unfortunately involved a vegetarian carb diet) This is also after taking 500 mg metformin taken twice at lunch and dinner. She is back to a strict diet now.

vit D 50.7 (30-100)
C reactive protein cardiac .58 (0-3)
b12 1174
BUN 33 (8-27)
BUN/creatinine ratio 28 ( 11-26) does this mean shes dehydrated?
creatinine 1.18 (.57-1.00)
sodium 141 (134-144)
potassium 4.4 (3.5-5.2)
HOMOCYSTEINE had been high when checked about 3 months ago at 13.8.

CAN SHE SAFELY SUPPLEMENT IRON LEADING UP TO SURGERY? My main concern is her high ferritin levels and that supplemented iron might increase her ferritin. But she is obviously low in percent saturation and will go lower after surgery. So how to supplement and keep iron from going to ferritin? Is turmeric ok to increase before surgery?
THank you in advance!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-17T21:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/total-knee-surgery-prep.9376/post-106049>

vitamin C is fine pre op. So is turmeric. If you mom wants to pop her redox potential she should visit a natural path for some Meyer's cocktails the week before the surgery and maybe talk to them about HBO dives then too to increase her tissue O2 content.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-17T21:43:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/total-knee-surgery-prep.9376/post-106050>

HbA1C is a bit high......tighten the dietary noose and CT is smart.

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2014-01-17T22:13:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/total-knee-surgery-prep.9376/post-106055>

Thanks Dr Kruse! Is lactoferrin ok to use before and after surgery?

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2014-01-30T01:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/total-knee-surgery-prep.9376/post-107368>

Hello
As per Dr kruse's pre and post surgical protocol, Vit C is recommended highly for healing. If the MD has put one on coumadin, is it ok still to take vit c as it is one of things that has an interaction with coumadin. How should that be handled..to take vit c or not to take post total knee replacement?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-30T04:26:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/total-knee-surgery-prep.9376/post-107409>

vitamin c is not recommended unless the redox potential is crashed.......then how you get it become critical. IV Meyers cocktails is it. Oral vitamin C is actually bad for you. EE12 will show you why
